And after the house of TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.Israel should be scattered they should be TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered together again; or, in fine, after the 1 Ne. 13:42; D&C 14:10.Gentiles had received the fulness of the TG Gospel.Gospel, the natural branches of the 1 Ne. 15:7.olive tree, or the TG Israel, Remnant of.remnants of the house of TG Israel, Twelve Tribes of.Israel, should be grafted in, or 1 Ne. 19:15 (14–17).come to the knowledge of the true Messiah, their Lord and their Redeemer.

And after this manner of language did my father prophesy and speak unto my brethren, and also many more things which I do not write in this book; for I have written as many of them as were expedient for me in mine 1 Ne. 1:17 (16–18).other book.
And all these things, of which I have spoken, were done as my father dwelt in a 1 Ne. 2:15 (15–16).tent, in the valley of Lemuel.

And it came to pass after I, Nephi, having heard all the Enos 1:3; Alma 36:17 (17–18).words of my father, concerning the things which he saw in a 1 Ne. 8:2.vision, and also the things which he spake by the power of the Holy Ghost, which power he received by faith on the Son of God—and the Son of God was the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messiah who should come—I, Nephi, was 2 Ne. 4:24.desirous also that I might see, and hear, and know of these things, by the power of the 2 Pet. 1:21.Holy Ghost, which is the TG God, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Gift of.gift of God unto Moro. 7:36; 10:7 (4–5, 7, 19).all those who diligently seek him, as well in times of D&C 20:26.old as in the time that he should manifest himself unto the children of men.

For he is the Heb. 13:8; Morm. 9:9 (9–11); D&C 20:12. TG God, Perfection of.same yesterday, today, and forever; and the way is prepared for all men from the foundation of the world, if it so be that they repent and come unto him.

For he that diligently TG Objectives.seeketh shall find; and the TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries of God shall be unfolded unto them, by the power of the TG Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.Holy Ghost, as well in these times as in times of old, and as well in times of old as in times to come; wherefore, the Alma 7:20; 37:12; D&C 3:2; 35:1. TG God, Eternal Nature of.course of the Lord is one eternal round.
Therefore remember, O man, for all thy doings thou shalt be brought into Eccl. 12:14; Ezek. 33:20. TG Judgment, the Last.judgment.

Wherefore, if ye have sought to do Ezek. 33:9.wickedly in the days of your TG Probation.probation, then ye are found 1 Cor. 6:9 (9–10); Morm. 7:7; D&C 76:62 (50–62); 138:20, 37; Moses 6:57.unclean before the judgment-seat of God; and no unclean thing can dwell with God; wherefore, ye must be cast off forever.
And the Holy Ghost giveth TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.authority that I should speak these things, and deny them not.

div.p: Nephi sees the Spirit of the Lord and is shown in vision the tree of life—He sees the mother of the Son of God and learns of the condescension of God—He sees the baptism, ministry, and crucifixion of the Lamb of God—He sees also the call and ministry of the Twelve Apostles of the Lamb. About 600–592 B.C.

chapter.verse: For it came to pass after I had desired to know the things that my father had seen, and believing that the Lord was able to make them known unto me, as I sat D&C 76:19. TG Meditation.pondering in mine heart I was Dan. 8:2; 2 Cor. 12:2 (1–4); Rev. 21:10; 2 Ne. 4:25; Moses 1:1.caught away in the Spirit of the Lord, yea, into an exceedingly high Ex. 24:13 (12–13); Deut. 10:1; Ether 3:1.mountain, which I never had before seen, and upon which I never had before set my foot.
And the Spirit said unto me: Behold, what Zech. 4:2 (1–6).desirest thou?
And I said: I desire to behold the things which my father 1 Ne. 8:2.saw.
And the Spirit said unto me: Mosiah 5:1 (1–2).Believest thou that thy father saw the 1 Ne. 8:10 (10–12); 15:22 (21–22).tree of which he hath spoken?
And I said: Yea, thou knowest that I 1 Ne. 2:16.believe all the words of my father.

And when I had spoken these words, the Spirit cried with a loud voice, saying: Hosanna to the Lord, the most high God; for he is God over all the Ex. 9:29; Deut. 10:14; 2 Ne. 29:7; 3 Ne. 11:14; D&C 55:1; Moses 6:44.earth, yea, even above all. And blessed art thou, Nephi, because thou TG Believe.believest in the Son of the most high God; wherefore, thou shalt behold the things which thou hast desired.

And behold this thing shall be given unto thee for a TG Signs.sign, that after thou hast beheld the tree which bore the fruit which thy father tasted, thou shalt also behold a man descending out of heaven, and him shall ye witness; and after ye have witnessed him ye shall TG Testimony; Witness.bear record that it is the Son of God.

And it came to pass that the Spirit said unto me: Look! And I looked and beheld a tree; and it was like unto the 1 Ne. 8:10.tree which my father had seen; and the TG Beauty.beauty thereof was far beyond, yea, exceeding of all beauty; and the 1 Ne. 8:11.whiteness thereof did exceed the whiteness of the driven snow.
And it came to pass after I had seen the tree, I said unto the Spirit: I behold thou hast shown unto me the tree which is 1 Ne. 11:22 (22–25).precious above all.
And he said unto me: What desirest thou?

And I said unto him: To know the Gen. 40:8.interpretation thereof—for I spake unto him as a man speaketh; for I beheld that he was in the TG Spirit Body.form of a man; yet nevertheless, I knew that it was the Spirit of the Lord; and he spake unto me as a man speaketh with another.
And it came to pass that he said unto me: Look! And I looked as if to look upon him, and I saw him not; for he had gone from before my presence.

And it came to pass that I looked and beheld the great city of Jerusalem, and also other cities. And I beheld the city of Nazareth; and in the city of Matt. 2:23.Nazareth I beheld a Luke 1:27 (26–27); Alma 7:10.virgin, and she was exceedingly fair and white.
And it came to pass that I saw the Ezek. 1:1; 1 Ne. 1:8 (6–11).heavens open; and an angel came down and stood before me; and he said unto me: Nephi, what beholdest thou?
And I said unto him: A virgin, most beautiful and fair above all other virgins.
And he said unto me: Knowest thou the 1 Ne. 11:26. TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.condescension of God?
And I said unto him: I know that he loveth his children; nevertheless, I do not know the meaning of all things.

And he said unto me: Behold, the Luke 1:34 (34–35). TG Foreordination; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.virgin whom thou seest is the Matt. 1:16; Mosiah 3:8; Alma 19:13. TG Jesus Christ, Birth of.mother of the Son of God, after the manner of the flesh.
And it came to pass that I beheld that she was carried away in the Spirit; and after she had been carried away in the Matt. 1:20.Spirit for the space of a time the angel spake unto me, saying: Look!
And I looked and beheld the virgin again, bearing a Luke 2:16.child in her arms.

And the angel said unto me: Behold the TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God.Lamb of God, yea, even the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son of the Eternal TG God the Father, Elohim.Father! Knowest thou the meaning of the 1 Ne. 8:10; Alma 5:62.tree which thy father saw?

And I answered him, saying: Yea, it is the TG God, Love of.love of God, which Moro. 8:26.sheddeth itself abroad in the hearts of the children of men; wherefore, it is the 1 Ne. 11:9.most desirable above all things.
And he spake unto me, saying: Yea, and the most TG Joy.joyous to the soul.

And after he had said these words, he said unto me: Look! And I looked, and I beheld the Son of God Luke 4:14 (14–21).going forth among the children of men; and I saw many fall down at his feet and worship him.

And it came to pass that I beheld that the Rev. 2:27; JST Rev. 2:27 (Bible Appendix).rod of iron, which my father had seen, was the 1 Ne. 8:19.word of God, which TG Guidance, Divine.led to the fountain of TG Living Water.living waters, or to the Gen. 2:9; Prov. 11:30 (22–30); Moses 4:28 (28, 31).tree of life; which waters are a representation of the love of God; and I also beheld that the tree of life was a representation of the love of God.
And the angel said unto me again: Look and behold the 1 Ne. 11:16. TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.condescension of God!

And I looked and 2 Ne. 25:13.beheld the Redeemer of the world, of whom my father had spoken; and I also beheld the Mal. 3:1; Matt. 11:10; John 1:6 (6–7); 1 Ne. 10:7 (7–10); 2 Ne. 31:4 (4–18); D&C 35:4.prophet who should prepare the way before him. And the Lamb of God went forth and was TG Jesus Christ, Baptism of.baptized of him; and after he was baptized, I beheld the heavens open, and the Holy Ghost come down out of heaven and abide upon him in the form of a TG Holy Ghost, Dove, Sign of.dove.

And I beheld that he went forth ministering unto the people, in D&C 138:26.power and great glory; and the multitudes were gathered together to hear him; and I beheld that they cast him out from among them.

And I also beheld 1 Ne. 12:9; 13:40 (24–26, 40). TG Apostles.twelve others following him. And it came to pass that they were 1 Ne. 14:30.carried away in the Spirit from before my face, and I saw them not.

And it came to pass that the angel spake unto me again, saying: Look! And I looked, and I beheld the heavens open again, and I saw TG Angels.angels descending upon the children of men; and they did minister unto them.

And he spake unto me again, saying: Look! And I looked, and I beheld the Lamb of God going forth among the children of men. And I beheld multitudes of people who were TG Sickness.sick, and who were afflicted with all manner of diseases, and with Mark 5:15 (15–20); 7:30; Mosiah 3:6 (5–7); Morm. 9:24.devils and TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.unclean spirits; and the angel spake and showed all these things unto me. And they were TG Heal.healed by the power of the Lamb of God; and the devils and the unclean spirits were cast out.

And it came to pass that the angel spake unto me again, saying: Look! And I looked and beheld the Lamb of God, that he was TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.taken by the people; yea, the Son of the everlasting God was Mark 15:19 (17–20); Luke 9:44 (44–45).judged of the world; and I saw and bear record.

And I, Nephi, saw that he was Luke 18:31; 2 Ne. 10:3; Mosiah 3:9 (9–10); 3 Ne. 27:14.lifted up upon the cross and TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Death of.slain for the sins of the world.

And after he was slain I saw the multitudes of the earth, that they were gathered together to Mark 13:13; 1 Cor. 4:9 (6–13).fight against the apostles of the Lamb; for thus were the twelve called by the angel of the Lord.

And the multitude of the earth was gathered together; and I beheld that they were in a large and spacious 1 Ne. 8:26; 12:18.building, like unto the building which my father saw. And the angel of the Lord spake unto me again, saying: Behold the world and the wisdom thereof; yea, behold the house of Israel hath gathered together to Micah 3:5; D&C 121:38.fight against the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

And it came to pass that I saw and bear record, that the great and spacious building was the TG Pride.pride of the world; and it TG Earth, Cleansing of; World, End of.fell, and the fall thereof was exceedingly great. And the angel of the Lord spake unto me again, saying: Thus shall be the destruction of all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people, that shall fight against the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

div.p: Nephi sees in vision the land of promise; the righteousness, iniquity, and downfall of its inhabitants; the coming of the Lamb of God among them; how the Twelve Disciples and the Twelve Apostles will judge Israel; and the loathsome and filthy state of those who dwindle in unbelief. About 600–592 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that the angel said unto me: Look, and behold thy seed, and also the seed of thy brethren. And I looked and beheld the TG Promised Lands.land of promise; and I beheld multitudes of people, yea, even as it were in number as many as the Gen. 22:17 (17–18); 1 Kgs. 4:20.sand of the sea.

And it came to pass that I beheld multitudes gathered together to battle, one against the other; and I beheld Enos 1:24; Morm. 8:7 (7–8). TG War.wars, and rumors of wars, and great slaughters with the sword among my people.
And it came to pass that I beheld many generations pass away, after the manner of wars and contentions in the land; and I beheld many cities, yea, even that I did not number them.

And it came to pass that I saw a 1 Ne. 19:12; Hel. 14:28 (20–28); 3 Ne. 10:11.mist of 1 Ne. 19:10; 2 Ne. 26:3.darkness on the face of the land of promise; and I saw lightnings, and I heard thunderings, and earthquakes, and all manner of tumultuous noises; and I saw the earth and the rocks, that they rent; and I saw mountains tumbling into pieces; and I saw the plains of the earth, that they were Gen. 7:11.broken up; and I saw many cities that they were 3 Ne. 8:14.sunk; and I saw many that they were burned with fire; and I saw many that did tumble to the earth, because of the quaking thereof.

And it came to pass after I saw these things, I saw the 3 Ne. 8:20.vapor of darkness, that it passed from off the face of the earth; and behold, I saw multitudes who had not fallen because of the great and terrible judgments of the Lord.
And I saw the heavens open, and the 2 Ne. 26:1 (1, 9); 3 Ne. 11:8 (3–17).Lamb of God descending out of heaven; and he came down and 2 Ne. 32:6; Alma 7:8; 16:20.showed himself unto them.
And I also saw and bear record that the Holy Ghost fell upon 3 Ne. 11:22; 12:1.twelve others; and they were ordained of God, and chosen.
And the angel spake unto me, saying: Behold the twelve disciples of the Lamb, who are chosen to minister unto thy seed.

And he said unto me: Thou rememberest the Matt. 10:1; Luke 6:13; John 6:70; 1 Ne. 11:29; 13:40 (24–26, 40).twelve apostles of the Lamb? Behold they are they who shall Matt. 19:28; D&C 29:12. TG Judgment, the Last.judge the twelve tribes of Israel; wherefore, the twelve ministers of thy seed shall be judged of them; for ye are of the house of Israel.

And these 3 Ne. 27:27; Morm. 3:18–19.twelve ministers whom thou beholdest shall judge thy seed. And, behold, they are righteous forever; for because of their faith in the Lamb of God their Rev. 7:14; 19:8; Alma 5:21 (21–27); 13:11 (11–13); 3 Ne. 27:19 (19–20); D&C 88:85.garments are made white in his blood.

And the angel said unto me: Look! And I looked, and beheld 2 Ne. 26:9 (9–10); 3 Ne. 27:32 (30–32).three generations pass away in righteousness; and their garments were white even like unto the Lamb of God. And the angel said unto me: These are made white in the blood of the Lamb, because of their faith in him.
And I, Nephi, also saw many of the 2 Ne. 26:9; Alma 45:12 (10–12); Hel. 13:10 (5, 9–10); 3 Ne. 27:32; 4 Ne. 1:14 (14–41).fourth generation who passed away in righteousness.
And it came to pass that I saw the multitudes of the earth gathered together.
And the angel said unto me: Behold thy seed, and also the seed of thy brethren.
And it came to pass that I looked and beheld the people of my seed gathered together in multitudes Morm. 6:7 (1–22).against the seed of my brethren; and they were gathered together to battle.

And the angel spake unto me, saying: Behold the fountain of TG Filthiness.filthy water which thy father saw; yea, even the 1 Ne. 8:13 (13–14); 15:27 (26–29).river of which he spake; and the depths thereof are the depths of TG Hell.hell.

And the 2 Pet. 2:17; 1 Ne. 8:23; 15:24 (23–24).mists of darkness are the temptations of the devil, which TG Apostasy of Individuals.blindeth the eyes, and hardeneth the hearts of the children of men, and leadeth them away into Prov. 4:14; Luke 13:24.broad roads, that they perish and are lost.

And the large and spacious 1 Ne. 8:26; 11:35 (35–36).building, which thy father saw, is vain Jer. 7:24; 9:14.imaginations and the TG Haughtiness; Pride.pride of the children of men. And a great and a terrible Luke 16:26; 1 Ne. 15:28 (28–30).gulf divideth them; yea, even the word of the TG God, Justice of.justice of the Eternal God, and the Messiah who is the Lamb of God, of whom the Holy Ghost beareth record, from the beginning of the world until this time, and from this time henceforth and forever.

And while the angel spake these words, I beheld and saw that the seed of my brethren did contend against my seed, according to the word of the angel; and because of the pride of my seed, and the TG Temptation.temptations of the devil, I beheld that the seed of my brethren did Jarom 1:10; W of M 1:2 (1–2).overpower the people of my seed.
And it came to pass that I beheld, and saw the people of the seed of my brethren that they had overcome my seed; and they went forth in multitudes upon the face of the land.
And I saw them gathered together in multitudes; and I saw Morm. 8:8; Moro. 1:2.wars and rumors of wars among them; and in wars and rumors of wars I saw 2 Ne. 1:18.many generations pass away.
And the angel said unto me: Behold these shall 1 Ne. 15:13; 2 Ne. 26:15.dwindle in unbelief.

And it came to pass that I beheld, after they had dwindled in unbelief they became a 2 Ne. 26:33.dark, and loathsome, and a 2 Ne. 5:22 (20–25). TG Filthiness.filthy people, full of TG Idleness.idleness and all manner of abominations.

div.p: Nephi sees in vision the church of the devil set up among the Gentiles, the discovery and colonizing of America, the loss of many plain and precious parts of the Bible, the resultant state of gentile apostasy, the restoration of the gospel, the coming forth of latter-day scripture, and the building up of Zion. About 600–592 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that the angel spake unto me, saying: Look! And I looked and beheld many nations and kingdoms.
And the angel said unto me: What beholdest thou? And I said: I behold many TG Kings, Earthly; Nations.nations and kingdoms.
And he said unto me: These are the nations and kingdoms of the Gentiles.
And it came to pass that I saw among the nations of the TG Gentiles.Gentiles the formation of a 1 Ne. 13:26 (26, 34); 14:10 (3, 9–17).great church.

And the angel said unto me: Behold the formation of a 2 Ne. 10:16.church which is most abominable above all other churches, which Rev. 17:6 (3–6); 18:24; 1 Ne. 14:13.slayeth the saints of God, yea, and tortureth them and bindeth them down, and yoketh them with a Jer. 28:14 (10–14). TG Bondage, Spiritual.yoke of iron, and bringeth them down into captivity.
And it came to pass that I beheld this D&C 88:94.great and TG Devil, Church of.abominable church; and I saw the 1 Ne. 22:23 (22–23). TG Devil.devil that he was the founder of it.
And I also saw Morm. 8:37 (36–38).gold, and silver, and silks, and scarlets, and fine-twined Ether 10:24.linen, and all manner of precious clothing; and I saw many harlots.

And the angel spake unto me, saying: Behold the gold, and the silver, and the silks, and the scarlets, and the fine-twined linen, and the precious clothing, and the harlots, are the Rev. 18:19 (10–24); Morm. 8:37 (36–41).desires of this great and abominable church.
And also for the Morm. 8:38.praise of the world do they Rev. 13:7 (4–7).destroy the saints of God, and bring them down into captivity.
And it came to pass that I looked and beheld many waters; and they divided the Gentiles from the seed of my brethren.
And it came to pass that the angel said unto me: Behold the wrath of God is upon the seed of thy brethren.

And I looked and beheld a man among the Gentiles, who was separated from the seed of my brethren by the many waters; and I beheld the Spirit of God, that it came down and TG Guidance, Divine.wrought upon the man; and he went forth upon the many waters, even unto the seed of my brethren, who were in the promised land.
And it came to pass that I beheld the Spirit of God, that it wrought upon other Gentiles; and they went forth out of captivity, upon the many waters.

And it came to pass that I beheld many 2 Ne. 1:11; Morm. 5:19 (19–20).multitudes of the Gentiles upon the TG Promised Lands.land of promise; and I beheld the wrath of God, that it was upon the seed of my brethren; and they were 1 Ne. 22:7. TG Israel, Scattering of.scattered before the Gentiles and were smitten.

And I beheld the Spirit of the Lord, that it was upon the Gentiles, and they did prosper and Morm. 5:19.obtain the 2 Ne. 10:19.land for their inheritance; and I beheld that they were white, and exceedingly fair and 2 Ne. 5:21; 4 Ne. 1:10; Morm. 9:6.beautiful, like unto my people before they were Morm. 6:19 (17–22).slain.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, beheld that the Gentiles who had gone forth out of captivity did humble themselves before the Lord; and the power of the Lord was D&C 101:80.with them.
And I beheld that their mother Gentiles were gathered together upon the waters, and upon the land also, to battle against them.
And I beheld that the power of God was with them, and also that the wrath of God was upon all those that were gathered together 1 Ne. 17:35.against them to battle.
And I, Nephi, beheld that the Gentiles that had gone out of captivity were 2 Ne. 10:10 (10–14); 3 Ne. 21:4; Ether 2:12.delivered by the power of God out of the hands of all other nations.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, beheld that they did prosper in the land; and I beheld a 1 Ne. 13:41 (3–41); 14:23.book, and it was carried forth among them.
And the angel said unto me: Knowest thou the meaning of the book?
And I said unto him: I know not.

And he said: Behold it proceedeth out of the mouth of a Jew. And I, Nephi, beheld it; and he said unto me: The 1 Ne. 13:38; 2 Ne. 29:6 (4–12).book that thou beholdest is a TG Scriptures, Preservation of.record of the 2 Ne. 3:12.Jews, which contains the covenants of the Lord, which he hath made unto the house of Israel; and it also containeth many of the prophecies of the holy prophets; and it is a record like unto the engravings which are upon the 1 Ne. 5:10 (10–22); 19:22.plates of brass, save there are not so many; nevertheless, they contain the covenants of the Lord, which he hath made unto the house of Israel; wherefore, they are of great worth unto the Gentiles.

And the angel of the Lord said unto me: Thou hast beheld that the 2 Ne. 29:3.book proceeded forth from the mouth of a Jew; and when it proceeded forth from the mouth of a Jew it contained the fulness of the gospel of the Lord, of whom the twelve apostles bear record; and they bear record according to the truth which is in the Lamb of God.
Wherefore, these things go forth from the 2 Ne. 29:4 (4–6); D&C 3:16. TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jews in purity unto the TG Gentiles.Gentiles, according to the truth which is in God.

And after they go forth by the Luke 1:1 (1–4); 2 Tim. 4:13.hand of the twelve apostles of the Lamb, from the Jews Matt. 21:43.unto the Gentiles, thou seest the formation of that 1 Ne. 13:4 (4–6); 14:10 (3, 9–17).great and abominable TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.church, which is most abominable above all other churches; for behold, they have Morm. 8:33; Moses 1:41. TG False Doctrine; Scriptures, Lost.taken away from the gospel of the Lamb many parts which are 1 Ne. 14:21 (20–26); A of F 1:8.plain and most precious; and also many covenants of the Lord have they taken away.
And all this have they done that they might pervert the right ways of the Lord, that they might blind the eyes and harden the hearts of the children of men.

Wherefore, thou seest that after the book hath gone forth through the hands of the great and abominable church, that there are many plain and 1 Ne. 14:23.precious things taken away from the book, which is the book of the Lamb of God.

And after these plain and precious things were 2 Pet. 3:16.taken away it goeth forth unto all the nations of the Gentiles; and after it goeth forth unto all the nations of the Gentiles, yea, even across the many waters which thou hast seen with the Gentiles which have gone forth out of captivity, thou seest—because of the many plain and precious things which have been taken out of the book, which were plain unto the understanding of the children of men, according to the plainness which is in the Lamb of God—because of these things which are taken away out of the gospel of the Lamb, an exceedingly great many do stumble, yea, insomuch that Satan hath great power over them.

Nevertheless, thou beholdest that the Gentiles who have gone forth out of captivity, and have been lifted up by the power of God above all other nations, upon the face of the land which is choice above all other lands, which is the land that the Lord God hath covenanted with thy father that his seed should have for the TG Lands of Inheritance; Promised Lands.land of their inheritance; wherefore, thou seest that the Lord God will not suffer that the Gentiles will utterly destroy the 2 Ne. 29:12 (12–13); Alma 45:14 (10–14); D&C 3:17.mixture of thy 2 Ne. 3:3.seed, which are among thy brethren.
Neither will he suffer that the Gentiles shall 2 Ne. 4:7; 10:18 (18–19); Jacob 3:6 (5–9); Hel. 15:12 (10–17); 3 Ne. 16:8 (4–13); Morm. 5:20 (20–21).destroy the seed of thy brethren.

Neither will the Lord God suffer that the Gentiles shall forever remain in that awful state of blindness, which thou beholdest they are in, because of the plain and most precious parts of the gospel of the Lamb which have been kept back by that TG Devil, Church of.abominable church, whose formation thou hast seen.
Wherefore saith the Lamb of God: I will be Isa. 42:1 (1, 3–4).merciful unto the Gentiles, unto the visiting of the remnant of the house of Israel in great judgment.

And it came to pass that the angel of the Lord spake unto me, saying: Behold, saith the Lamb of God, after I have D&C 124:8; Abr. 1:17.visited the TG Israel, Joseph, People of.remnant of the house of Israel—and this remnant of whom I speak is the seed of thy father—wherefore, after I have visited them in judgment, and smitten them by the hand of the Gentiles, and after the Gentiles do 1 Ne. 14:1 (1–3); 2 Ne. 26:20.stumble exceedingly, because of the most plain and precious parts of the TG Gospel.gospel of the Lamb which have been kept back by that abominable church, which is the mother of harlots, saith the Lamb—I will be merciful unto the TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.Gentiles in that day, insomuch that I will TG Scriptures, Lost.bring forth unto them, in mine own power, much of my D&C 10:62. TG Restoration of the Gospel.gospel, which shall be plain and precious, saith the Lamb.

For, behold, saith the Lamb: I will manifest myself unto thy seed, that they shall write many things which I shall minister unto them, which shall be plain and precious; and after thy seed shall be destroyed, and dwindle in unbelief, and also the seed of thy brethren, behold, 1 Ne. 14:7; 22:8; 2 Ne. 27:26 (6–26); 29:1 (1–2). TG Book of Mormon.these things shall be hid up, to come forth unto the Gentiles, by the gift and power of the Lamb.
And in them shall be written my 3 Ne. 27:21.gospel, saith the Lamb, and my 3 Ne. 11:39 (38–39). TG Rock.rock and my salvation.

And Jacob 5:75 (70–76); D&C 21:9. TG Israel, Mission of; Mission of Latter-day Saints.blessed are they who shall seek to bring forth my TG Zion.Zion at that day, for they shall have the TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.gift and the Luke 24:49; 1 Ne. 14:14; D&C 38:38 (32–38).power of the Holy Ghost; and if they 3 Ne. 27:16. TG Endure; Perseverance; Steadfastness.endure unto the end they shall be James 4:10.lifted up at the last day, and shall be saved in the everlasting TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.kingdom of the Lamb; and whoso shall Isa. 52:7 (7–10); Mark 13:10; 3 Ne. 20:40.publish peace, yea, tidings of great joy, how beautiful upon the mountains shall they be.

And it came to pass that I beheld the remnant of the seed of my brethren, and also the 1 Ne. 13:23; 2 Ne. 29:6 (4–6).book of the Lamb of God, which had proceeded forth from the mouth of the Jew, that it came forth from the Gentiles Morm. 5:15; 7:8 (8–9).unto the remnant of the seed of my brethren.

And after it had come forth unto them I beheld D&C 9:2.other TG Scriptures to Come Forth.books, which came forth by the power of the Lamb, from the Gentiles unto them, unto the Ezek. 37:17 (15–20); 1 Ne. 14:2 (1–5).convincing of the Gentiles and the remnant of the seed of my brethren, and also the Jews who were scattered upon all the face of the earth, that the records of the prophets and of the twelve apostles of the Lamb are 1 Ne. 14:30.true.

And the angel spake unto me, saying: These 2 Ne. 26:17 (16–17); 27:6 (6–26); 29:12. TG Book of Mormon.last records, which thou hast seen among the Gentiles, shall TG Scriptures, Value of.establish the truth of the See the title page of the Book of Mormon. TG Bible.first, which are of the 1 Ne. 11:29; 12:9.twelve apostles of the Lamb, and shall make known the plain and precious things which have been taken away from them; and shall make known to all kindreds, tongues, and people, that the Lamb of God is the Son of the Eternal Father, and the Moses 1:6.Savior of the world; and that all men must come unto him, or they cannot be saved.

And they must come according to the words which shall be established by the mouth of the Lamb; and the words of the Lamb shall be made known in the records of thy seed, as well as in the 1 Ne. 13:20 (20–28).records of the twelve apostles of the Lamb; wherefore they both shall be established in Ezek. 37:17.one; for there is Deut. 6:4; 2 Ne. 31:21.one God and one TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd.Shepherd over all the earth.

And the time cometh that he shall manifest himself unto all nations, both unto the D&C 18:6, 26; 19:27; 21:12; 90:9 (8–9); 107:33; 112:4.Jews and also unto the Gentiles; and after he has manifested himself unto the Jews and also unto the Gentiles, then he shall manifest himself unto the Gentiles and also unto the Jews, and the Jacob 5:63; Ether 13:12.last shall be first, and the Luke 13:30; 1 Ne. 10:14; 15:13 (13–20); D&C 14:10.first shall be last.

div.p: An angel tells Nephi of the blessings and cursings to fall upon the Gentiles—There are only two churches: the Church of the Lamb of God and the church of the devil—The Saints of God in all nations are persecuted by the great and abominable church—The Apostle John will write concerning the end of the world. About 600–592 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it shall come to pass, that if the 1 Ne. 22:9 (8–9); 2 Ne. 30:3; 3 Ne. 16:6 (6–13). TG Gentiles.Gentiles shall hearken unto the Lamb of God in that day that he shall manifest himself unto them in word, and also in 1 Thes. 1:5; 1 Ne. 14:14; Jacob 6:2 (2–3).power, in very deed, unto the Ether 12:27.taking away of their Isa. 57:14; Ezek. 7:19; 1 Cor. 1:23; 1 Ne. 13:34 (29, 34); 2 Ne. 26:20.stumbling blocks—

And harden not their hearts against the Lamb of God, they shall be numbered among the seed of thy father; yea, they shall be Gal. 3:7 (7, 29); 2 Ne. 10:18 (18–19); 3 Ne. 16:13; 21:6 (6, 22); Abr. 2:10 (9–11).numbered among the house of Israel; and they shall be a 2 Ne. 6:12; 10:10 (8–14); 3 Ne. 16:6 (6–7); 20:27; Morm. 5:19.blessed people upon the TG Israel, Deliverance of; Israel, Restoration of; Lands of Inheritance.promised land forever; they shall be no more brought down into captivity; and the house of Israel shall no more be confounded.

And that great Ps. 57:6; Matt. 7:2 (1–2); 1 Ne. 22:14 (13–14); D&C 10:26 (25–27); 109:25.pit, which hath been digged for them by that great and abominable church, which was founded by the devil and his children, that he might lead away the souls of men down to hell—yea, that great pit which hath been digged for the destruction of men shall be filled by those who digged it, unto their utter destruction, saith the Lamb of God; not the destruction of the soul, save it be the casting of it into that Alma 19:29. TG Damnation; Hell.hell which hath no end.
For behold, this is according to the TG Bondage, Spiritual.captivity of the devil, and also according to the justice of God, upon all those who will work wickedness and abomination before him.

And it came to pass that the angel spake unto me, Nephi, saying: Thou hast beheld that if the Gentiles repent it shall be 1 Ne. 13:39 (34–42); 22:9.well with them; and thou also knowest concerning the covenants of the Lord unto the house of Israel; and thou also hast heard that whoso TG Repent.repenteth not must perish.
Therefore, 2 Ne. 28:32.wo be unto the Gentiles if it so be that they harden their hearts against the Lamb of God.

For the time cometh, saith the Lamb of God, that I will work a great and a Isa. 29:14; 1 Ne. 13:35; 22:8; 2 Ne. 27:26; 29:1 (1–2); D&C 4:1. TG Restoration of the Gospel.marvelous work among the children of men; a TG God, Works of.work which shall be everlasting, either on the one hand or on the other—either to the convincing of them unto TG Peace; Peace of God.peace and Jer. 21:8. TG Eternal Life.life eternal, or unto the deliverance of them to the hardness of their hearts and the blindness of their minds unto their being brought down into captivity, and also into destruction, both temporally and spiritually, according to the 2 Ne. 2:29 (26–29); Alma 12:11 (9–11).captivity of the devil, of which I have spoken.

And it came to pass that when the angel had spoken these words, he said unto me: Rememberest thou the TG Abrahamic Covenant; Israel, Mission of.covenants of the Father unto the house of Israel? I said unto him, Yea.

And it came to pass that he said unto me: Look, and behold that great and abominable church, which is the mother of abominations, whose founder is the 1 Ne. 15:35; D&C 1:35. TG Devil, Church of.devil.

And he said unto me: Behold there are save 1 Ne. 22:23; 2 Ne. 26:20; Morm. 8:28 (25–41). TG Church.two churches only; the one is the church of the Lamb of God, and the 1 Ne. 13:4 (4–6), 26 (26, 34).other is the church of the TG Devil, Church of; False Prophets.devil; wherefore, 2 Ne. 10:16.whoso belongeth not to the church of the Lamb of God belongeth to that great church, which is the mother of abominations; and she is the Rev. 17:15 (5, 15).whore of all the earth.

And it came to pass that I looked and beheld the whore of all the earth, and she sat upon many Jer. 51:13 (12–14).waters; and she had dominion over D&C 35:11.all the earth, among all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people.

And it came to pass that I beheld the church of the Lamb of God, and its numbers were Matt. 7:14; Jacob 5:70; 3 Ne. 14:14; D&C 138:26.few, because of the wickedness and abominations of the whore who sat upon many waters; nevertheless, I beheld that the church of the Lamb, who were the saints of God, were also upon D&C 90:11.all the face of the earth; and their dominions upon the face of the earth were small, because of the wickedness of the great whore whom I saw.

And it came to pass that I beheld that the great mother of abominations did gather together multitudes upon the face of all the earth, among all the nations of the Gentiles, to Rev. 17:6 (1–6); 18:24; 1 Ne. 13:5.fight against the Lamb of God.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, beheld the power of the Lamb of God, that it descended upon the saints of the church of the Lamb, and upon the covenant people of the Lord, who were scattered upon all the face of the earth; and they were TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.armed with TG Deliver; Protection, Divine.righteousness and with the Luke 24:49; 1 Ne. 13:37; 14:1; Jacob 6:2 (2–3); D&C 38:38 (32–38).power of God in great glory.

And it came to pass that I beheld that the wrath of God was D&C 115:6 (5–6).poured out upon that great and abominable church, insomuch that there were wars and rumors of wars among all the Mark 13:8; D&C 87:6.nations and kindreds of the earth.

And as there began to be 1 Ne. 22:13 (13–14); Morm. 8:30. TG War.wars and rumors of wars among all the nations which belonged to the mother of abominations, the angel spake unto me, saying: Behold, the wrath of God is upon the mother of harlots; and behold, thou seest all these things—

And when the TG Last Days.day cometh that the 1 Ne. 21:26; 22:16 (15–16); 3 Ne. 20:20 (19–21).wrath of God is poured out upon the mother of harlots, which is the great and abominable church of all the earth, whose founder is the devil, then, at that day, the 3 Ne. 21:26 (7, 20–29). TG Israel, Restoration of.work of the Father shall commence, in preparing the way for the fulfilling of his Morm. 8:21 (21, 41). TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenants, which he hath made to his people who are of the house of Israel.
And it came to pass that the angel spake unto me, saying: Look!
And I looked and beheld a man, and he was dressed in a white robe.
And the angel said unto me: Behold Rev. 1:1 (1–3); 1 Ne. 14:27.one of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
Behold, he shall Rev. 1:1.see and 1 Ne. 13:24 (20–40); A of F 1:8.write the Rev. 4:1.remainder of these things; yea, and also many things which have been.
And he shall also write concerning the end of the world.

Wherefore, the things which he shall write are just and true; and behold they are written in the 1 Ne. 13:20 (20–24); Morm. 8:33; Ether 4:16.book which thou beheld proceeding out of the mouth of the Jew; and at the time they proceeded out of the mouth of the Jew, or, at the time the book proceeded out of the mouth of the Jew, the things which were written were plain and pure, and most 1 Ne. 13:28 (28–32).precious and easy to the understanding of all men.
And behold, the things which this Ether 4:16.apostle of the Lamb shall write are many things which thou hast seen; and behold, the remainder shalt thou see.
But the things which thou shalt see hereafter thou shalt not write; for the Lord God hath ordained the apostle of the Lamb of God that he should John 20:30 (30–31); 21:25; Rev. 1:19.write them.

And also others who have been, to them hath he shown all things, and they have TG Scriptures, Writing of.written them; and they are Dan. 12:9; 2 Ne. 27:10 (6–23); 30:17; Ether 3:21 (21–27); 4:5 (4–7); 12:21; D&C 35:18; JS—H 1:65.sealed up to come forth in their purity, according to the truth which is in the Lamb, in the own due time of the Lord, unto the house of Israel.
And I, Nephi, heard and bear record, that the name of the apostle of the Lamb was Rev. 1:1 (1–3).John, according to the word of the angel.

And behold, I, Nephi, am forbidden that I should write the remainder of the things which I saw and heard; wherefore the things which I have written sufficeth me; and I have written but a small part of the things which I saw.
And I bear record that I saw the things which my 1 Ne. 8:2 (2–35).father saw, and the angel of the Lord did make them known unto me.

And now I make an end of speaking concerning the things which I saw while I was 1 Kgs. 18:12; 1 Ne. 11:29 (19, 29).carried away in the Spirit; and if all the things which I saw are not written, the things which I have written are 1 Ne. 13:39; 2 Ne. 25:20.true. And thus it is. Amen.

div.p: Lehi’s seed are to receive the gospel from the Gentiles in the latter days—The gathering of Israel is likened unto an olive tree whose natural branches will be grafted in again—Nephi interprets the vision of the tree of life and speaks of the justice of God in dividing the wicked from the righteous. About 600–592 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that after I, Nephi, had been carried away in the Spirit, and seen all these things, I returned to the tent of my father.
And it came to pass that I beheld my brethren, and they were disputing one with another concerning the things which my father had spoken unto them.

For he truly spake many great things unto them, which were hard to be 1 Cor. 2:11 (10–12). TG Hardheartedness; Understanding.understood, save a man should inquire of the Lord; and they being hard in their hearts, therefore they did not look unto the Lord as they ought.

And now I, Nephi, was grieved because of the hardness of their hearts, and also, because of the things which I had seen, and knew they must unavoidably come to pass because of the great wickedness of the children of men.

And it came to pass that I was overcome because of my afflictions, for I considered that mine Moses 7:44 (41–44).afflictions were great above all, because of the Enos 1:13; Morm. 6:1.destruction of my people, for I had beheld their fall.
And it came to pass that after I had received Moses 1:10; JS—H 1:20, 48.strength I spake unto my brethren, desiring to know of them the cause of their disputations.
And they said: Behold, we cannot understand the words which our father hath spoken concerning the natural branches of the 1 Ne. 10:14 (2–15).olive tree, and also concerning the Gentiles.
And I said unto them: Have ye 2 Ne. 1:25 (24–27); Mosiah 10:14. TG Problem-Solving.inquired of the Lord?
And they said unto me: D&C 58:33.We have not; for the Lord maketh no such thing known unto us.
Behold, I said unto them: How is it that ye do not keep the commandments of the Lord? How is it that ye will TG Apostasy of Individuals.perish, because of the hardness of your hearts?

Do ye not remember the things which the Lord hath said?—If ye will not harden your hearts, and James 1:5 (5–6). TG Prayer.ask me in 1 Ne. 2:19 (18–21); 7:12 (9–13).faith, believing that ye shall receive, with diligence in keeping my commandments, surely these things shall be made known unto you.

Behold, I say unto you, that the house of Israel was compared unto an olive tree, by the Spirit of the Lord which was in our father; and behold are we not broken off from the house of Israel, and are we not a Gen. 49:22 (22–26); 1 Ne. 10:12 (12–14); 19:24. TG Israel, Joseph, People of.branch of the house of Israel?

And now, the thing which our father meaneth concerning the grafting in of the natural branches through the fulness of the Gentiles, is, that in the latter days, when our seed shall have 1 Ne. 12:22 (22–23); 2 Ne. 26:15.dwindled in unbelief, yea, for the space of many years, and many generations after the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messiah shall be manifested in body unto the children of men, then shall the fulness of the TG Gospel; Mission of Latter-day Saints.gospel of the Messiah come unto the Gentiles, and from the 1 Ne. 13:42; 22:9 (5–10); D&C 14:10. TG Gentiles.Gentiles unto the remnant of our seed—

And at that day shall the remnant of our 2 Ne. 10:2; 3 Ne. 5:23 (21–26); 21:7 (4–29).seed 2 Ne. 3:12; 30:5; Morm. 7:9 (1, 9–10).know that they are of the house of Israel, and that they are the TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenant people of the Lord; and then shall they know and Jacob 3:6.come to the D&C 3:18 (16–20). See also title page of the Book of Mormon.knowledge of their forefathers, and also to the knowledge of the gospel of their Redeemer, which was ministered unto their fathers by him; wherefore, they shall come to the knowledge of their Redeemer and the very points of his doctrine, that they may know how to come unto him and be saved.

And then at that day will they not rejoice and give praise unto their everlasting God, their TG Rock.rock and their salvation? Yea, at that day, will they not receive the strength and nourishment from the true Gen. 49:11; John 15:1.vine? Yea, will they not come unto the true fold of God?
Behold, I say unto you, Yea; they shall be remembered again among the house of Israel; they shall be Jacob 5:54 (1–77).grafted in, being a natural branch of the olive tree, into the true olive tree.

And this is what our father meaneth; and he meaneth that it will not come to pass until after they are scattered by the Gentiles; and he meaneth that it shall come by way of the Gentiles, that the Lord may show his power unto the Gentiles, for the very cause that he shall be TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of; Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.rejected of the Jews, or of the house of Israel.

Wherefore, our father hath not spoken of our seed alone, but also of all the house of Israel, pointing to the covenant which should be fulfilled in the latter days; which covenant the Lord made to our father Abraham, saying: In thy Gen. 12:3 (1–3); Abr. 2:11 (6–11). TG Seed of Abraham.seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be TG Israel, Mission of.blessed.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, spake much unto them concerning these things; yea, I spake unto them concerning the Isa. 42:22 (22–23); 1 Ne. 19:15. TG Israel, Gathering of; Israel, Judah, People of.restoration of the Jews in the latter days.

And I did rehearse unto them the words of 1 Ne. 19:23.Isaiah, who spake Isa. 40:9.concerning the TG Israel, Restoration of.restoration of the Jews, or of the house of Israel; and after they were restored they should no more be confounded, neither should they be scattered again. And it came to pass that I did speak many words unto my brethren, that they were pacified and did 1 Ne. 16:5 (5, 24, 39).humble themselves before the Lord.
And it came to pass that they did speak unto me again, saying: What meaneth this thing which our father saw in a dream? What meaneth the 1 Ne. 8:10 (10–12).tree which he saw?
And I said unto them: It was a representation of the 1 Ne. 11:4; Moses 3:9.tree of life.
And they said unto me: What meaneth the 1 Ne. 8:19 (19–24).rod of iron which our father saw, that led to the tree?

And I said unto them that it was the 1 Ne. 8:19. TG Gospel.word of God; and whoso would hearken unto the word of God, and would Prov. 4:13.hold fast unto it, they would never perish; neither could the 1 Ne. 8:23. TG Temptation.temptations and the fiery Eph. 6:16; D&C 3:8; 27:17.darts of the TG Devil.adversary overpower them unto blindness, to lead them away to destruction.

Wherefore, I, Nephi, did exhort them to give D&C 11:2; 32:4; 84:43 (43–44). TG Scriptures, Study of.heed unto the word of the Lord; yea, I did exhort them with all the energies of my soul, and with all the W of M 1:18.faculty which I possessed, that they would give heed to the word of God and remember to keep his commandments always in all things.
And they said unto me: What meaneth the 1 Ne. 8:13.river of water which our father saw?

And I said unto them that the 1 Ne. 12:16 (16–18).water which my father saw was TG Filthiness.filthiness; and so much was his mind swallowed up in other things that he beheld not the filthiness of the water.
And I said unto them that it was an awful Luke 16:26; 1 Ne. 12:18; 2 Ne. 1:13.gulf, which separated the wicked from the tree of life, and also from the saints of God.
And I said unto them that it was a representation of that awful 1 Ne. 8:32 (13–14, 32). TG Hell.hell, which the angel said unto me was prepared for the wicked.

And I said unto them that our father also saw that the TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice of God did also divide the wicked from the righteous; and the brightness thereof was like unto the brightness of a flaming Num. 11:1 (1, 10); 2 Ne. 26:6.fire, which ascendeth up unto God forever and ever, and hath no end.

And they said unto me: Doth this thing mean the torment of the body in the days of TG Probation.probation, or doth it mean the final state of the soul after the Alma 40:11 (6–26).death of the temporal body, or doth it speak of the things which are temporal?

And it came to pass that I said unto them that it was a representation of things both temporal and spiritual; for the day should come that they must be judged of their TG Good Works.works, yea, even the works which were done by the temporal body in their days of TG Probation.probation.

Wherefore, if they should Ezek. 18:26; Mosiah 15:26; Moro. 10:26.die in their wickedness they must be Alma 12:16; 40:26.cast off also, as to the things which are spiritual, which are pertaining to righteousness; wherefore, they must be brought to stand before God, to be TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment, the Last.judged of their Ps. 33:15 (13–15); 3 Ne. 27:25 (23–27).works; and if their works have been filthiness they must needs be 2 Ne. 9:16; D&C 88:35.filthy; and if they be filthy it must needs be that they cannot Ps. 15:1 (1–5); 24:3 (3–4); Mosiah 15:23; Alma 11:37; D&C 76:62 (50–70); Moses 6:57 (55–59).dwell in the kingdom of God; if so, the kingdom of God must be filthy also.

But behold, I say unto you, the kingdom of God is not filthy, and there cannot any unclean thing enter into the kingdom of God; wherefore there must needs be a place of TG Filthiness.filthiness prepared for that which is filthy.

And there is a place prepared, yea, even that 2 Ne. 9:19; Mosiah 26:27.awful TG Hell.hell of which I have spoken, and the 1 Ne. 14:9; D&C 1:35.devil is the preparator of it; wherefore the final state of the souls of men is to dwell in the kingdom of God, or to be cast out because of that TG Justice.justice of which I have spoken.

Wherefore, the wicked are rejected from the righteous, and also from that Gen. 2:9; 1 Ne. 8:11; 2 Ne. 2:15.tree of life, whose fruit is most precious and most 1 Ne. 8:12.desirable above all other fruits; yea, and it is the Hel. 5:8.greatest of all the D&C 14:7. TG God, Gifts of.gifts of God. And thus I spake unto my brethren. Amen.

div.p: The wicked take the truth to be hard—Lehi’s sons marry the daughters of Ishmael—The Liahona guides their course in the wilderness—Messages from the Lord are written on the Liahona from time to time—Ishmael dies; his family murmurs because of afflictions. About 600–592 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that after I, Nephi, had made an end of speaking to my brethren, behold they said unto me: Thou hast declared unto us hard things, more than we are able to bear.

And it came to pass that I said unto them that I knew that I had spoken Acts 7:54; 2 Ne. 33:5; Enos 1:23; W of M 1:17. TG Chastening.hard things against the wicked, according to the truth; and the righteous have I justified, and testified that they should be lifted up at the last day; wherefore, the John 3:20 (19–21); 7:7; Hel. 13:24 (24–27).guilty taketh the Prov. 15:10; 2 Ne. 1:26; 9:40.truth to be hard, for it Acts 5:33; Mosiah 13:7; Moses 6:37.cutteth them to the very center.

And now my brethren, if ye were righteous and were willing to hearken to the truth, and give heed unto it, that ye might D&C 5:21. TG Walking with God.walk uprightly before God, then ye would not murmur because of the truth, and say: Thou speakest hard things against us.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did exhort my brethren, with all diligence, to keep the commandments of the Lord.

And it came to pass that they did 1 Ne. 15:20; 16:24 (24, 39); 18:4.humble themselves before the Lord; insomuch that I had joy and great hopes of them, that they would walk in the paths of righteousness.
Now, all these things were said and done as my father dwelt in a tent in the 1 Ne. 2:14 (8, 14); 9:1.valley which he called Lemuel.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, took one of the 1 Ne. 7:1 (1, 19); 18:19 (19–20).daughters of Ishmael to TG Marriage, Marry.wife; and also, my brethren took of the 1 Ne. 7:6.daughters of Ishmael to wife; and also 1 Ne. 4:35; 2 Ne. 5:6 (5–6).Zoram took the eldest daughter of Ishmael to wife.
And thus my father had fulfilled all the 1 Ne. 3:18; 5:21; 7:2.commandments of the Lord which had been given unto him. And also, I, Nephi, had been blessed of the Lord exceedingly.
And it came to pass that the voice of the Lord spake unto my father by night, and commanded him that on the morrow he should take his Omni 1:16.journey into the wilderness.

And it came to pass that as my father arose in the morning, and went forth to the tent door, to his great astonishment he beheld upon the ground a round 1 Ne. 16:16; Alma 37:38 (38–47).ball of curious workmanship; and it was of fine brass. And within the ball were two spindles; and the one Ex. 13:21.pointed the way whither we should go into the wilderness.

And it came to pass that we did gather together whatsoever things we should carry into the wilderness, and all the remainder of our provisions which the Lord had given unto us; and we did take 1 Ne. 8:1; 18:6; Ether 1:41; 2:3.seed of every kind that we might carry into the wilderness.
And it came to pass that we did take our tents and depart into the wilderness, across the river Laman.

And it came to pass that we traveled for the space of four days, nearly a south-southeast direction, and we did pitch our tents again; and we did call the name of the place HEB twisting, intertwining.Shazer.

And it came to pass that we did take our bows and our arrows, and go forth into the wilderness to slay food for our families; and after we had slain food for our families we did return again to our families in the wilderness, to the place of Shazer. And we did go forth again in the wilderness, following the same direction, keeping in the most fertile parts of the wilderness, which were in the borders near the 1 Ne. 2:5; D&C 17:1.Red Sea.
And it came to pass that we did travel for the space of many days, Alma 17:7.slaying food by the way, with our bows and our arrows and our stones and our slings.
And we did follow the 1 Ne. 16:10 (10, 16, 26); 18:12 (12, 21); 2 Ne. 5:12; Alma 37:38 (38–47); D&C 17:1.directions of the ball, which led us in the more fertile parts of the wilderness.
And after we had traveled for the space of many days, we did pitch our tents for the space of a time, that we might again rest ourselves and obtain food for our families.

And it came to pass that as I, Nephi, went forth to slay food, behold, I did break my bow, which was made of fine 2 Sam. 22:35; Ps. 18:34.steel; and after I did break my bow, behold, my brethren were angry with me because of the loss of my bow, for we did obtain no food.
And it came to pass that we did return without food to our families, and being much fatigued, because of their journeying, they did suffer much for the want of food.

And it came to pass that Laman and Lemuel and the sons of Ishmael did begin to murmur exceedingly, because of their sufferings and afflictions in the wilderness; and also my father began to murmur against the Lord his God; yea, and they were all exceedingly sorrowful, even that they did TG Murmuring.murmur against the Lord.

Now it came to pass that I, Nephi, having been afflicted with my brethren because of the loss of my bow, and their bows having lost their Gen. 49:24.springs, it began to be exceedingly difficult, yea, insomuch that we could obtain no food.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did speak much unto my brethren, because they had hardened their hearts again, even unto Ex. 16:8; Num. 11:1 (1–2); D&C 29:19.complaining against the Lord their God.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did TG Initiative.make out of wood a bow, and out of a straight stick, an arrow; wherefore, I did arm myself with a bow and an arrow, with a sling and with stones. And I said unto my TG Honoring Father and Mother.father: Whither shall I go to obtain food?

And it came to pass that he did TG Guidance, Divine; Prayer.inquire of the Lord, for they had 1 Ne. 15:20; 16:5.humbled themselves because of my words; for I did say many things unto them in the energy of my soul.

And it came to pass that the voice of the Lord came unto my father; and he was truly Ether 2:14. TG Chastening; Repent.chastened because of his murmuring against the Lord, insomuch that he was brought down into the depths of sorrow.
And it came to pass that the voice of the Lord said unto him: Look upon the ball, and behold the things which are written.
And it came to pass that when my father beheld the things which were TG Warn.written upon the ball, he did fear and tremble exceedingly, and also my brethren and the sons of Ishmael and our wives.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, beheld the pointers which were in the ball, that they did work according to the Alma 37:40. TG Faith.faith and diligence and heed which we did give unto them.

And there was also written upon them a new writing, which was plain to be read, which did give us TG Understanding.understanding concerning the ways of the Lord; and it was written and changed from time to time, according to the faith and diligence which we gave unto it. And thus we see that by 2 Kgs. 5:13; James 3:4; Alma 37:6 (6–8, 41); D&C 123:16.small means the Lord can bring about great things.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did go forth up into the top of the mountain, according to the TG Guidance, Divine.directions which were given upon the ball.
And it came to pass that I did slay wild Gen. 9:3.beasts, insomuch that I did obtain food for our families.

And it came to pass that I did return to our tents, bearing the beasts which I had slain; and now when they beheld that I had obtained 2 Ne. 1:24. TG Food; Thanksgiving.food, how great was their joy! And it came to pass that they did humble themselves before the Lord, and did give thanks unto him.

And it came to pass that we did again take our journey, traveling nearly the same course as in the beginning; and after we had traveled for the space of many days we did pitch our tents again, that we might tarry for the space of a time.
And it came to pass that 1 Ne. 7:2 (2–6, 19).Ishmael died, and was buried in the place which was called HEB probably “consolation,” from verb naham, “be sorry, console oneself.”Nahom.

And it came to pass that the daughters of Ishmael did TG Mourning.mourn exceedingly, because of the loss of their father, and because of their TG Affliction.afflictions in the wilderness; and they did TG Murmuring.murmur against my father, because he had brought them out of the land of Jerusalem, saying: Our father is dead; yea, and we have wandered much in the wilderness, and we have suffered much affliction, hunger, thirst, and fatigue; and after all these sufferings we must perish in the wilderness with hunger.
And thus they did murmur against my father, and also against me; and they were desirous to Num. 14:4 (1–5).return again to Jerusalem.

And Laman said unto Lemuel and also unto the sons of Ishmael: Behold, let us 1 Ne. 17:44; 2 Ne. 1:24. TG Murder.slay our father, and also our brother Nephi, who has taken it upon him to be our Gen. 37:10 (9–11); Num. 16:13; 1 Ne. 2:22; 18:10.ruler and our teacher, who are his elder brethren.

Now, he says that the Lord has talked with him, and also that 1 Ne. 3:30 (30–31); 4:3.angels have ministered unto him. But behold, we know that he lies unto us; and he tells us these things, and he worketh many things by his cunning arts, that he may deceive our eyes, thinking, perhaps, that he may lead us away into some strange wilderness; and after he has led us away, he has thought to make himself a king and a ruler over us, that he may do with us according to his will and pleasure. And after this manner did my brother Laman TG Provoking.stir up their hearts to TG Anger.anger.

And it came to pass that the Lord was with us, yea, even the voice of the Lord came and did speak many words unto them, and did TG Chastening.chasten them exceedingly; and after they were chastened by the voice of the Lord they did turn away their anger, and did repent of their sins, insomuch that the Lord did bless us again with food, that we did not perish.

div.p: Nephi is commanded to build a ship—His brethren oppose him—He exhorts them by recounting the history of God’s dealings with Israel—Nephi is filled with the power of God—His brethren are forbidden to touch him, lest they wither as a dried reed. About 592–591 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that we did again take our journey in the wilderness; and we did travel nearly eastward from that time forth. And we did travel and Ps. 69:2 (1–2, 14).wade through much affliction in the wilderness; and our TG Woman.women did bear children in the wilderness.

And so great were the TG Blessing.blessings of the Lord upon us, that while we did live upon 1 Ne. 17:12.raw Ex. 16:13 (12–13); 1 Ne. 18:6. TG Meat.meat in the wilderness, our women did give plenty of suck for their children, and were strong, yea, even like unto the men; and they began to bear their journeyings without murmurings.

And thus we see that the commandments of God must be fulfilled. And if it so be that the children of men keep the commandments of God he doth nourish them, and Ex. 1:19; Ezra 8:22 (22–23); Isa. 45:24; Mosiah 2:41; Alma 26:12. TG Strength.strengthen them, and provide means whereby they can accomplish the thing which he has commanded them; wherefore, he did Gen. 18:14; 1 Ne. 3:7.provide means for us while we did sojourn in the wilderness.
And we did sojourn for the space of many years, yea, even eight years in the wilderness.

And we did come to the land which we called Alma 22:29 (29–33).Bountiful, because of its much fruit and also wild honey; and all these things were prepared of the Lord that we might not perish. And we beheld the sea, which we called Irreantum, which, being interpreted, is many waters.

And it came to pass that we did pitch our tents by the seashore; and notwithstanding we had suffered many 2 Ne. 4:20.afflictions and much difficulty, yea, even so much that we cannot write them all, we were exceedingly rejoiced when we came to the seashore; and we called the place Bountiful, because of its much fruit.

And it came to pass that after I, Nephi, had been in the land of Bountiful for the space of many days, the voice of the Lord came unto me, saying: Ezek. 3:22 (22–27).Arise, and get thee into the mountain. And it came to pass that I arose and went up into the mountain, and cried unto the Lord.

And it came to pass that the Lord spake unto me, saying: Thou shalt Gen. 6:14 (14–16).construct a ship, after the Ex. 25:40; 1 Chr. 28:12 (11–12, 19); 1 Ne. 18:2.manner which I shall show thee, that I may carry thy people across these waters.

And I said: Lord, whither shall I go that I may find ore to molten, that I may make Deut. 8:9; 1 Kgs. 6:7; 1 Chr. 22:3 (3, 14); Job 28:2; Isa. 44:12.tools to construct the ship after the manner which thou hast shown unto me?
And it came to pass that the Lord told me whither I should go to find ore, that I might make tools.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did make a bellows wherewith to Isa. 54:16.blow the fire, of the skins of beasts; and after I had made a bellows, that I might have wherewith to blow the fire, I did smite two stones together that I might make fire.
For the Lord had not hitherto suffered that we should make much fire, as we journeyed in the wilderness; for he said: I will make thy food become sweet, that ye 1 Ne. 17:2.cook it not;

And I will also be your Alma 5:37 (37–38); D&C 88:66.light in the wilderness; and I will prepare the way before you, if it so be that ye shall keep my commandments; wherefore, inasmuch as ye shall keep my commandments ye shall be led towards the 1 Ne. 2:20; 4:14; Jacob 2:12.promised land; and ye shall Ex. 6:7; 13:21. TG Guidance, Divine.know that it is by me that ye are led.

Yea, and the Lord said also that: After ye have arrived in the promised land, ye shall 2 Ne. 1:4. TG God, Knowledge about; Testimony.know that I, the Lord, am D&C 5:2.God; and that I, the Lord, did TG Deliver.deliver you from destruction; yea, that I did bring you out of the land of Jerusalem.
Wherefore, I, Nephi, did strive to keep the 1 Kgs. 2:3; Prov. 7:2.commandments of the Lord, and I did Acts 14:22; Titus 2:15; Heb. 3:13.exhort my brethren to faithfulness and diligence.
And it came to pass that I did TG Skill.make tools of the ore which I did molten out of the rock.

And when my brethren saw that I was about to 1 Ne. 17:49 (8, 49–51); 18:1 (1–6).build a ship, they began to TG Murmuring.murmur against me, saying: Our brother is a fool, for he thinketh that he can build a ship; yea, and he also thinketh that he can cross these great waters.

And thus my brethren did 1 Ne. 3:28; 7:6 (6–19); 18:10 (9–22).complain against me, and were desirous that they might not labor, for they did not TG Unbelief.believe that I could build a ship; neither would they believe that I was instructed of the Lord.

And now it came to pass that I, Nephi, was exceedingly sorrowful because of the hardness of their hearts; and now when they saw that I began to be sorrowful they were glad in their hearts, insomuch that they did TG Mocking; Persecution.rejoice over me, saying: We knew that ye could not construct a ship, for we knew that ye were lacking in judgment; wherefore, thou canst not accomplish so great a work.

And thou art like unto our father, led away by the foolish 1 Ne. 2:11; 5:4 (2–4).imaginations of his heart; yea, he hath led us out of the land of Jerusalem, and we have wandered in the wilderness for these many years; and our women have toiled, being big with child; and they have borne children in the wilderness and suffered all things, save it were death; and it would have been better that they had died before they came out of Jerusalem than to have suffered these afflictions.
Behold, these many years we have suffered in the wilderness, which time we might have enjoyed our possessions and the land of our inheritance; yea, and we might have been happy.

And we know that the people who were in the land of Jerusalem were a 1 Ne. 1:19 (4, 13, 18–20).righteous people; for they kept the statutes and judgments of the Lord, and all his commandments, according to the law of Moses; wherefore, we know that they are a righteous people; and our father hath judged them, and hath led us away because we would hearken unto his words; yea, and our brother is like unto him. And after this manner of language did my brethren murmur and complain against us.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, spake unto them, saying: Do ye believe that our fathers, who were the children of Israel, would have been led away out of the hands of the Ex. 20:2; Ps. 80:8; Moses 1:26.Egyptians if they had not hearkened unto the words of the Lord?

Yea, do ye suppose that they would have been led out of bondage, if the Lord had not commanded Moses that he should Ex. 3:10 (2–10); Hosea 12:13 (12–14); 1 Ne. 19:10; 2 Ne. 3:9; 25:20.lead them out of bondage?

Now ye know that the children of Israel were in Gen. 15:13 (13–14); Mosiah 11:21; D&C 101:79. TG Israel, Bondage of, in Egypt.bondage; and ye know that they were laden with Ex. 1:11 (10–11); 2:11; 1 Ne. 20:10.tasks, which were grievous to be borne; wherefore, ye know that it must needs be a good thing for them, that they should be Ex. 5:1.brought out of bondage.

Now ye know that Josh. 24:6; Jer. 2:2; Acts 7:27 (22–39).Moses was commanded of the Lord to do that great work; and ye know that by his Ex. 14:21 (19–31); Josh. 2:10; Neh. 9:11; 1 Ne. 4:2; Mosiah 7:19; Hel. 8:11; D&C 8:3; Moses 1:25. TG Israel, Deliverance of.word the waters of the Red Sea were divided hither and thither, and they passed through on dry ground.
But ye know that the Egyptians were Josh. 24:6.drowned in the Red Sea, who were the armies of Pharaoh.
And ye also know that they were fed with Ex. 16:15 (4, 14–15, 35); Num. 11:7 (7–8); Deut. 8:3; Neh. 9:20; Hosea 13:6 (5–8); John 6:49; Mosiah 7:19.manna in the wilderness.

Yea, and ye also know that Moses, by his word according to the power of God which was in him, Ex. 17:6; Num. 20:11; Deut. 8:15; Neh. 9:15; 1 Ne. 20:21; 2 Ne. 25:20.smote the rock, and there came forth water, that the children of Israel might quench their thirst.

And notwithstanding they being led, the Lord their God, their Redeemer, going before them, Ex. 13:18 (18, 20).leading them by day and giving light unto them by night, and doing all things for them which were D&C 18:18; 88:64 (64–65).expedient for man to receive, they hardened their hearts and blinded their minds, and Ex. 32:8; Num. 14:11 (11–12); Ezek. 20:13 (13–16); D&C 84:24 (23–25).reviled against Moses and against the true and living God.

And it came to pass that according to his word he did Num. 26:65.destroy them; and according to his word he did Ex. 15:13; 1 Ne. 5:15; D&C 103:16 (16–18).lead them; and according to his word he did do all things for them; and there was not any thing done save it were by his word.

And after they had crossed the river Jordan he did make them mighty unto the Ex. 34:11; Num. 33:52 (52–53); Josh. 11:6; 24:8.driving out of the children of the land, yea, unto the scattering them to destruction.
And now, do ye suppose that the children of this land, who were in the land of promise, who were driven out by our fathers, do ye suppose that they were righteous? Behold, I say unto you, Nay.
Do ye suppose that our fathers would have been more choice than they if they had been righteous? I say unto you, Nay.

Behold, the Lord esteemeth all Acts 10:15 (15, 34); Rom. 2:11; 2 Ne. 26:33 (23–33).flesh in one; he that is Ps. 55:22; John 15:10; 1 Ne. 22:17.righteous is 1 Sam. 2:30; 1 Kgs. 2:3; Ps. 97:10; 145:20 (1–21); Alma 13:4; 28:13; D&C 82:10 (8–10).favored of God. But behold, this Gen. 15:16; Ex. 23:31 (28–31); Deut. 7:10; Josh. 2:24.people had rejected every word of God, and they were ripe in iniquity; and the fulness of the wrath of God was upon them; and the Lord did curse the land against them, and bless it unto our fathers; yea, he did curse it against them unto their destruction, and he did bless it unto our fathers unto their obtaining power over it.

Behold, the Lord hath created the Gen. 1:28 (26–28); Jer. 27:5; Moses 1:29. TG Earth, Purpose of; Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father; Man, Physical Creation of.earth that it should be Isa. 45:18.inhabited; and he hath created his children that they should possess it.
And he Ps. 1:6; Prov. 14:34; Isa. 45:1 (1–3); 1 Ne. 4:13; Ether 2:10; D&C 98:32 (31–32); 117:6.raiseth up a righteous nation, and destroyeth the nations of the wicked.
And he leadeth away the righteous into precious TG Lands of Inheritance.lands, and the wicked he Lev. 20:22.destroyeth, and curseth the land unto them for their sakes.
He ruleth high in the heavens, for it is his throne, and this earth is his Isa. 66:1; Lam. 2:1; D&C 38:17; Abr. 2:7.footstool.

And he loveth those who will have him to be their God. Behold, he loved our TG Israel, Origins of.fathers, and he TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenanted with them, yea, even Abraham, Gen. 21:12; D&C 27:10.Isaac, and Gen. 28:4 (1–5).Jacob; and he remembered the covenants which he had made; wherefore, he did bring them out of the land of Deut. 4:37 (37–38).Egypt.

And he did straiten them in the wilderness with his rod; for they 2 Kgs. 17:7 (7–23).hardened their hearts, even as ye have; and the Lord straitened them because of their iniquity. He sent fiery flying Num. 21:6 (4–9); Deut. 8:15; Alma 33:19 (18–22).serpents among them; and after they were bitten he prepared a way that they might be Hosea 11:3; John 3:14; 2 Ne. 25:20.healed; and the labor which they had to perform was to look; and because of the Alma 37:46 (44–47); Hel. 8:15.simpleness of the way, or the easiness of it, there were many who perished.

And they did harden their hearts from time to time, and they did Ex. 32:23; Num. 14:2 (1–12). TG Reviling.revile against D&C 84:23.Moses, and also against God; nevertheless, ye know that they were led forth by his matchless power into the land of promise.

And now, after all these things, the time has come that they have become wicked, yea, nearly unto ripeness; and I know not but they are at this day about to be Hosea 7:13. TG Israel, Scattering of.destroyed; for I know that the day must surely come that they must be destroyed, save a few only, who shall be led away into captivity.

Wherefore, the Lord 1 Ne. 2:2 (1–2).commanded my father that he should depart into the wilderness; and the Jews also sought to take away his life; yea, and 1 Ne. 16:37.ye also have sought to take away his life; wherefore, ye are murderers in your hearts and ye are like unto them.

Ye are Mosiah 13:29.swift to do iniquity but slow to remember the Lord your God. Ye have seen an 1 Ne. 4:3.angel, and he spake unto you; yea, ye have heard his voice from time to time; and he hath spoken unto you in a still small voice, but ye were Acts 17:27; Eph. 4:19; 1 Ne. 2:14.past feeling, that ye could not feel his words; wherefore, he has spoken unto you like unto the voice of thunder, which did cause the earth to shake as if it were to divide asunder.

And ye also know that by the Hel. 12:10 (6–18).power of his almighty word he can cause the earth that it shall pass away; yea, and ye know that by his word he can cause the rough places to be made smooth, and smooth places shall be broken up. O, then, why is it, that ye can be so hard in your hearts?

Behold, my soul is rent with anguish because of you, and my heart is pained; I fear lest ye shall be cast off forever. Behold, I am Micah 3:8.full of the Spirit of God, insomuch that my frame has Dan. 10:8 (8, 17); 1 Ne. 1:7; 19:20.no strength.

And now it came to pass that when I had spoken these words they were angry with me, and were desirous to throw me into the depths of the sea; and as they came forth to lay their hands upon me I spake unto them, saying: In the name of the Almighty God, I command you that ye Mosiah 13:3.touch me not, for I am filled with the 2 Ne. 1:27 (26–27). TG God, Power of; Priesthood, Power of.power of God, even unto the consuming of my flesh; and whoso shall lay his hands upon me shall 1 Kgs. 13:4 (4–7); Moses 1:11; 6:47.wither even as a dried reed; and he shall be as naught before the power of God, for God shall smite him.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, said unto them that they should murmur no more against their father; neither should they withhold their labor from me, for God had commanded me that I should 1 Ne. 17:17; 18:1 (1–6).build a ship.

And I said unto them: Philip. 4:13; 1 Ne. 3:7; D&C 24:13.If God had commanded me to do all things I could do them. If he should command me that I should say unto this water, be thou earth, it should be earth; and if I should say it, it would be done.
And now, if the Lord has such great power, and has wrought so many miracles among the children of men, how is it that he cannot Gen. 6:14 (14–16); 1 Ne. 18:1.instruct me, that I should build a ship?

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, said many things unto my brethren, insomuch that they were IE ashamed, overawed.confounded and could not contend against me; neither durst they lay their hands upon me nor touch me with their fingers, even for the space of many days. Now they durst not do this lest they should wither before me, so powerful was the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God; and thus it had wrought upon them.

And it came to pass that the Lord said unto me: Stretch forth thine hand again unto thy brethren, and they shall not wither before thee, but I will IE cause to shake or tremble; see vv. 54–55.shock them, saith the Lord, and this will I do, that they may know that I am the Lord their God.
And it came to pass that I stretched forth my hand unto my brethren, and they did not wither before me; but the Lord did shake them, even according to the word which he had spoken.

And now, they said: We know of a surety that the Lord is Ex. 3:12; Alma 38:4.with thee, for we know that it is the power of the Lord that has shaken us. And they fell down before me, and were about to Dan. 2:46; Acts 14:15 (11–15).worship me, but I would not suffer them, saying: I am thy brother, yea, even thy younger brother; wherefore, worship the Lord thy God, and honor thy father and thy mother, that thy Ex. 20:12; Prov. 9:11; Mosiah 14:10; Hel. 7:24; D&C 5:33.days may be long in the land which the Lord thy God shall give thee.

div.p: The ship is finished—The births of Jacob and Joseph are mentioned—The company embarks for the promised land—The sons of Ishmael and their wives join in revelry and rebellion—Nephi is bound, and the ship is driven back by a terrible tempest—Nephi is freed, and by his prayer the storm ceases—The people arrive in the promised land. About 591–589 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that they did 1 Ne. 17:55.worship the Lord, and did go forth with me; and we did work timbers of curious TG Art.workmanship. And the Lord did show me from time to time after what manner I should work the timbers of the 1 Ne. 17:49 (8, 17, 49–51).ship.

Now I, Nephi, did not work the timbers after the manner which was learned by men, neither did I build the ship after the manner of men; but I did build it after the manner which the Lord had shown unto me; wherefore, it was not after the manner of men.
And I, Nephi, did go into the mount oft, and I did Jer. 33:3.pray oft unto the Lord; wherefore the Lord TG Guidance, Divine.showed unto me 2 Ne. 1:24.great things.

And it came to pass that after I had finished the ship, according to the word of the Lord, my brethren beheld that it was good, and that the workmanship thereof was exceedingly fine; wherefore, they did 1 Ne. 16:5.humble themselves again before the Lord.
And it came to pass that the voice of the Lord came unto my father, that we should arise and go down into the ship.

And it came to pass that on the morrow, after we had prepared all things, much fruits and 1 Ne. 17:2.meat from the wilderness, and honey in abundance, and provisions according to that which the Lord had commanded us, we did go down into the ship, with all our loading and our 1 Ne. 8:1; 16:11.seeds, and whatsoever thing we had brought with us, every one according to his age; wherefore, we did all go down into the Gen. 7:7.ship, with our wives and our children.
And now, my father had begat two sons in the wilderness; the elder was called 2 Ne. 2:1.Jacob and the younger 2 Ne. 3:1.Joseph.

And it came to pass after we had all gone down into the ship, and had taken with us our provisions and things which had been commanded us, we did put forth into the Ps. 8:8; 2 Ne. 10:20.sea and were driven forth before the wind towards the 1 Ne. 2:20; 5:5 (5, 22). TG Promised Lands.promised land.

And after we had been Ether 6:5.driven forth before the wind for the space of many days, behold, my brethren and the sons of Ishmael and also their wives began to make themselves merry, insomuch that they began to dance, and to sing, and to speak with much 2 Ne. 1:2. TG Rioting and Reveling.rudeness, yea, even that they did forget by what power they had been brought thither; yea, they were lifted up unto exceeding rudeness.

And I, Nephi, began to fear exceedingly lest the Lord should be angry with us, and smite us because of our iniquity, that we should be swallowed up in the depths of the sea; wherefore, I, Nephi, began to speak to them with much soberness; but behold they were 1 Ne. 17:18 (17–55); 2 Ne. 4:13 (13–14).angry with me, saying: We will not that our younger brother shall be a Gen. 37:10 (9–11); 1 Ne. 16:37 (37–38); 2 Ne. 1:25 (25–27).ruler over us.

And it came to pass that Laman and Lemuel did take me and 1 Ne. 7:16 (16–20).bind me with cords, and they did treat me with much harshness; nevertheless, the Lord did suffer it that he might show forth his power, unto the fulfilling of his word which he had Ex. 23:7; Ps. 37:9 (8–13); Alma 14:11.spoken concerning the wicked.

And it came to pass that after they had bound me insomuch that I could not move, the 1 Ne. 16:16 (10, 16, 26); 2 Ne. 5:12; Alma 37:38 (38–47); D&C 17:1.compass, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work.

Wherefore, they knew not whither they should steer the ship, insomuch that there arose a great Jonah 1:4; Matt. 8:24.storm, yea, a great and terrible tempest, and we were Mosiah 1:17.driven back upon the waters for the space of three days; and they began to be frightened exceedingly lest they should be drowned in the sea; nevertheless they did not loose me.
And on the fourth day, which we had been driven back, the tempest began to be exceedingly sore.

And it came to pass that we were about to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea.
