And after we had been driven back upon the waters for the space of four days, my brethren began to Hel. 12:3.see that the judgments of God were upon them, and that they must perish save that they should repent of their iniquities; wherefore, they came unto me, and loosed the bands which were upon my wrists, and behold they had swollen exceedingly; and also mine ankles were much swollen, and great was the soreness thereof.

Nevertheless, I did look unto my God, and I did Ezra 3:11 (11–13); 2 Ne. 9:49; Mosiah 2:20 (20–21); Alma 36:28; D&C 136:28.praise him all the day long; and I did not murmur against the Lord because of mine afflictions.

Now my father, Lehi, had said many things unto them, and also unto the sons of 1 Ne. 7:4 (4–20).Ishmael; but, behold, they did breathe out much threatenings against anyone that should speak for me; and my parents being Gen. 24:1.stricken in years, and having TG Suffering.suffered much grief because of their TG Family, Children, Duties of; Honoring Father and Mother.children, they were brought down, yea, even upon their sick-beds.

Because of their grief and much sorrow, and the iniquity of my brethren, they were brought near even to be carried out of this time to meet their God; yea, their Gen. 42:38.grey hairs were about to be brought down to lie low in the dust; yea, even they were near to be cast with sorrow into a watery grave.

And Jacob and Joseph also, being young, having need of much nourishment, were grieved because of the afflictions of their mother; and also 1 Ne. 7:19; 16:7.my wife with her tears and prayers, and also my children, did not soften the hearts of my brethren that they would loose me.

And there was nothing save it were the power of God, which threatened them with destruction, could soften their TG Hardheartedness.hearts; wherefore, when they saw that they were about to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea they repented of the thing which they had done, insomuch that they loosed me.

And it came to pass after they had loosed me, behold, I took the compass, and it did work whither I desired it. And it came to pass that I Jonah 1:6.prayed unto the Lord; and after I had prayed the winds did cease, and the storm did cease, and there was a great calm.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did guide the ship, that we sailed again towards the promised land.

And it came to pass that after we had sailed for the space of many days we did arrive at the Mosiah 10:13. TG Promised Lands.promised land; and we went forth upon the land, and did pitch our tents; and we did call it the promised land.

And it came to pass that we did begin to till the earth, and we began to plant seeds; yea, we did put all our 1 Ne. 8:1.seeds into the earth, which we had brought from the land of Jerusalem. And it came to pass that they did grow exceedingly; wherefore, we were blessed in abundance.

And it came to pass that we did find upon the land of promise, as we journeyed in the wilderness, that there were Enos 1:21.beasts in the forests of every kind, both the cow and the ox, and the ass and the horse, and the goat and the wild goat, and all manner of wild animals, which were for the use of men. And we did find all manner of 2 Ne. 5:15 (14–16).ore, both of Deut. 33:16 (13–17).gold, and of silver, and of copper.

div.p: Nephi makes plates of ore and records the history of his people—The God of Israel will come six hundred years from the time Lehi left Jerusalem—Nephi tells of His sufferings and crucifixion—The Jews will be despised and scattered until the latter days, when they will return unto the Lord. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that the Lord commanded me, wherefore I did make plates of ore that I might engraven upon them the TG Plate; Record Keeping.record of my people. And upon the plates which I made I did TG Scribe.engraven the record of my 1 Ne. 1:17 (16–17); 6:1 (1–3); Jacob 7:26 (26–27).father, and also our journeyings in the wilderness, and the prophecies of my father; and also many of mine own prophecies have I engraven upon them.

And I knew not at the time when I made them that I should be commanded of the Lord to make 2 Ne. 5:30; Jacob 3:14.these plates; wherefore, the record of my father, and the genealogy of his fathers, and the more part of all our proceedings in the wilderness are engraven upon those first plates of which I have spoken; wherefore, the things which transpired before I made 1 Ne. 9:2 (1–5); Omni 1:1.these plates are, of a truth, more particularly made mention upon the first plates.

And after I had made these plates by way of commandment, I, Nephi, received a commandment that the ministry and the prophecies, the more plain and precious parts of them, should be written upon 1 Ne. 10:1; Jacob 1:1 (1–4); 3:13 (13–14); 4:1 (1–4).these plates; and that the things which were written should be kept for the instruction of my people, who should possess the land, and also for other 1 Ne. 9:5 (4–5); W of M 1:7; D&C 3:19 (19–20); 10:38 (1–51).wise purposes, which purposes are known unto the Lord.

Wherefore, I, Nephi, did make a record upon the 1 Ne. 9:4 (2–5); 2 Ne. 5:33.other plates, which gives an account, or which gives a greater account of the wars and contentions and destructions of my people. And this have I done, and commanded my people what they should do after I was gone; and that these plates should be handed down from one generation to another, or from one prophet to another, until further commandments of the Lord.

And an account of my 2 Ne. 5:30 (28–33).making these plates shall be given hereafter; and then, behold, I proceed according to that which I have spoken; and this I do that the more sacred things may be TG Scriptures, Preservation of.kept for the knowledge of my people.

Nevertheless, I do not TG Scriptures, Writing of.write anything upon plates save it be that I think it be See title page of the Book of Mormon. TG Sacred.sacred. And now, if I do err, even did they err of old; not that I would excuse myself because of other men, but because of the Morm. 8:17 (13–17); Ether 12:23 (23–28).weakness which is in me, according to the flesh, I would excuse myself.

For the things which some men esteem to be of great worth, both to the body and soul, others set at Num. 15:31 (30–31); 2 Ne. 33:2; Jacob 4:14; D&C 3:7 (4–13).naught and trample under their feet. Yea, even the very God of Israel do men Ezek. 34:19; D&C 76:35. TG Blaspheme; Sacrilege.trample under their feet; I say, trample under their feet but I would speak in other words—they set him at naught, and TG Disobedience; Prophets, Rejection of.hearken not to the voice of his counsels.

And behold he TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of; Jesus Christ, Birth of; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.cometh, according to the words of the angel, in 1 Ne. 2:4; 10:4 (4–11); 2 Ne. 25:19.six hundred years from the time my father left Jerusalem.

And the world, because of their iniquity, shall judge him to be a thing of naught; wherefore they scourge him, and he suffereth it; and they smite him, and he suffereth it. Yea, they Isa. 50:6 (5–6); Matt. 27:30.spit upon him, and he suffereth it, because of his loving TG Kindness.kindness and his TG Forbear.long-suffering towards the children of men.

And the 2 Ne. 10:3; 26:12; Mosiah 7:27; 27:31 (30–31); Alma 11:39 (38–39); 3 Ne. 11:14 (14–15).God of our fathers, who were Gen. 15:14 (13–14); Ex. 3:10 (2–10); 6:6; 1 Ne. 5:15; 17:24 (24, 31, 40); 2 Ne. 25:20; D&C 136:22.led out of Egypt, out of bondage, and also were preserved in the wilderness by him, yea, the Gen. 32:9; Matt. 22:32; Mosiah 7:19; D&C 136:21. TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.yieldeth himself, according to the words of the angel, as a man, into the hands of TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.wicked men, to be 3 Ne. 27:14; 28:6.lifted up, according to the words of BD Lost books. See also Alma 33:15; 34:7; Hel. 8:20 (19–20); 3 Ne. 10:16 (15–16).Zenock, and to be 2 Ne. 6:9; Mosiah 3:9. TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified, according to the words of Neum, and to be buried in a Matt. 27:60; Luke 23:53; 2 Ne. 25:13.sepulchre, according to the words of Jacob 5:1; 6:1; Hel. 15:11.Zenos, which he spake concerning the three days of 1 Ne. 12:4 (4–5); Hel. 14:27 (20, 27); 3 Ne. 8:19 (3, 19–23); 10:9.darkness, which should be a sign given of his death unto those who should inhabit the isles of the sea, more especially given unto those who are of the 3 Ne. 16:1 (1–4).house of Israel.

For thus spake the prophet: The Lord God surely shall D&C 5:16.visit all the house of Israel at that day, some with his 3 Ne. 9:1 (1–22).voice, because of their righteousness, unto their great joy and salvation, and others with the Hel. 14:21 (20–27); 3 Ne. 8:6 (5–23).thunderings and the lightnings of his power, by tempest, by fire, and by Gen. 19:28; Ex. 19:18; Morm. 8:29 (29–30); D&C 45:41 (40–41).smoke, and 1 Ne. 12:5.vapor of Luke 23:44 (44–45). TG Darkness, Physical.darkness, and by the opening of the Num. 16:32; 2 Ne. 26:5.earth, and by 3 Ne. 10:13 (13–14).mountains which shall be carried up.

And Hel. 14:28 (20–28); 3 Ne. 10:11.all these things must surely come, saith the prophet Jacob 5:1.Zenos. And the Matt. 27:51 (51–54).rocks of the earth must rend; and because of the Moses 7:56 (48–56).groanings of the earth, many of the kings of the isles of the sea shall be wrought upon by the Spirit of God, to exclaim: The God of nature suffers.

And as for those who are at Jerusalem, saith the prophet, they shall be Matt. 23:38 (37–39); Luke 23:28 (27–30).scourged by all people, because they crucify the God of Israel, and turn their hearts aside, rejecting signs and wonders, and the power and glory of the God of Israel.

And because they turn their hearts aside, saith the prophet, and have Ps. 22:6; Mosiah 14:3 (3–6).despised the Holy One of Israel, they shall wander in the flesh, and perish, and become a Jer. 24:9; 3 Ne. 29:8 (8–9). TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.hiss and a Deut. 28:37; 1 Kgs. 9:7 (6–7); Joel 2:17; 3 Ne. 16:9 (8–9).byword, and be 2 Ne. 10:6; 25:15. TG Hate.hated among all nations.

Nevertheless, when that day cometh, saith the prophet, that they 1 Ne. 15:19; 22:12 (11–12).no more TG Israel, Restoration of.turn aside their hearts against the Holy One of Israel, then will he remember the TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenants which he made to their fathers.

Yea, then will he remember the 1 Ne. 22:4; 2 Ne. 10:21.isles of the sea; yea, and all the people who are of the house of Israel, will I Isa. 49:22 (20–22); 60:4. TG Israel, Gathering of.gather in, saith the Lord, according to the words of the prophet Zenos, from the four quarters of the earth.
Yea, and all the earth shall Isa. 40:5 (4–5).see the salvation of the Lord, saith the prophet; every nation, kindred, tongue and people shall be blessed.
And I, Nephi, have written these things unto my people, that perhaps I might persuade them that they would Mosiah 13:29.remember the Lord their Redeemer.
Wherefore, I speak unto all the house of Israel, if it so be that they should obtain Enos 1:16; Morm. 5:12; 7:9 (9–10). TG Israel, Restoration of.these things.

For behold, I have workings in the spirit, which doth Dan. 10:8 (8–12); 1 Ne. 1:7; Alma 27:17; Moses 1:10 (9–10).weary me even that all my joints are weak, for those who are at Jerusalem; for had not the Lord been merciful, to show unto me concerning them, even as he had prophets of old, I should have perished also.

And he surely did show unto the 2 Kgs. 17:13; Amos 3:7. TG Prophets, Mission of.prophets of old all things 3 Ne. 10:16 (16–17).concerning them; and also he did show unto many concerning us; wherefore, it must needs be that we know concerning them for they are written upon the plates of brass.

Now it came to pass that I, Nephi, did teach my brethren these things; and it came to pass that I did read many things to them, which were engraven upon the 1 Ne. 13:23; 22:1.plates of brass, that they might know concerning the doings of the Lord in other lands, among people of old.

And I did read many things unto them which were written in the Ex. 17:14; 1 Ne. 5:11; Moses 1:41 (40–41).books of Moses; but that I might more fully persuade them to believe in the Lord their Redeemer I did read unto them that which was written by the prophet Isa. 1:1; 1 Ne. 15:20; 2 Ne. 25:5 (2–6); 3 Ne. 23:1.Isaiah; for I did TG Scriptures, Value of.liken all scriptures unto us, that it might be for our 2 Ne. 4:15.profit and learning.

Wherefore I spake unto them, saying: Hear ye the words of the prophet, ye who are a 2 Kgs. 19:31.remnant of the house of Israel, a Gen. 49:22 (22–26); 1 Ne. 15:12 (12, 16); 2 Ne. 3:5 (4–5).branch who have been broken off; TG Scriptures, Study of.hear ye the words of the prophet, which were written unto all the house of Israel, and liken them unto yourselves, that ye may have hope as well as your brethren from whom ye have been broken off; for after this manner has the prophet written.

div.p: The Lord reveals His purposes to Israel—Israel has been chosen in the furnace of affliction and is to go forth from Babylon—Compare Isaiah 48. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 48:1 (1–22).Hearken and hear this, O house of Jacob, who are called by the name of Israel, and are come forth out of the waters of Judah, or out of the waters of TG Baptism; Conversion.baptism, who Deut. 6:13.swear by the name of the Lord, and make mention of the God of Israel, yet they swear Jer. 4:2; 5:2.not in truth nor in righteousness.

Nevertheless, they call themselves of the Isa. 52:1. TG Jerusalem.holy city, but they do TG Hypocrisy; Prophets, Rejection of.not stay themselves upon the God of Israel, who is the Lord of Hosts; yea, the Lord of Hosts is his name.
Behold, I have declared the Isa. 42:9; 46:10 (9–10). TG God, Foreknowledge of.former things from the beginning; and they went forth out of my mouth, and I showed them. I did show them suddenly.
And I did it because I knew that thou art obstinate, and thy TG Stiffnecked.neck is an iron sinew, and thy brow brass;

And I have even from the beginning declared to thee; before it came to pass I TG God, Omniscience of; Idolatry.showed them thee; and I showed them for fear lest thou shouldst say—Mine idol hath done them, and my graven image, and my molten image hath commanded them.
Thou hast seen and heard all this; and will ye 1 Cor. 9:16.not declare them? And that I have showed thee new things from this time, even hidden things, and thou didst not know them.
They are created now, and not from the beginning, even before the day when thou heardest them not they were declared unto thee, lest thou shouldst say—Behold I knew them.

Yea, and thou heardest not; yea, thou knewest not; yea, from that time thine ear was not opened; for I knew that thou wouldst deal very treacherously, and wast called a Ps. 58:3.transgressor from the womb.
Nevertheless, for my 1 Sam. 12:22; Ps. 23:3; 1 Jn. 2:12.name’s sake will I defer mine anger, and for my praise will I refrain from thee, that I cut thee not off.
For, behold, I have refined thee, I have chosen thee in the furnace of Ex. 1:11 (10–11); 1 Ne. 17:25. TG Affliction.affliction.
For mine own sake, yea, for mine own sake will I do this, for I will not suffer my Jer. 44:26.name to be polluted, and I will Isa. 42:8; Moses 4:1 (1–4).not give my glory unto another.
Hearken unto me, O Jacob, and Israel my called, for I am he; I am the Rev. 1:17; 22:13. TG Jesus Christ, Firstborn; Jesus Christ, Jehovah.first, and I am also the last.

Mine hand hath also Ps. 102:25. TG God the Father, Jehovah; Jesus Christ, Creator.laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand hath spanned the heavens. I Ps. 148:8 (5–10).call unto them and they stand up together.

All ye, assemble yourselves, and hear; who among them hath declared these things unto them? The Lord hath loved him; yea, and he will 1 Kgs. 8:56; D&C 64:31; 76:3.fulfil his word which he hath declared by them; and he will do his pleasure on TG Babylon.Babylon, and his arm shall come upon the Chaldeans.
Also, saith the Lord; I the Lord, yea, I have spoken; yea, I have called Isa. 45:1 (1–4).him to declare, I have brought him, and he shall make his way prosperous.
Come ye near unto me; I have not spoken in Isa. 45:19.secret; from the beginning, from the time that it was declared have I spoken; and the Lord God, and his TG God, Spirit of.Spirit, hath sent me.

And thus saith the Lord, thy TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel; I have sent him, the Lord thy God who teacheth thee to profit, who TG Guidance, Divine.leadeth thee by the way thou shouldst go, hath done it.
O that thou hadst hearkened to my Eccl. 8:5.commandments—then had thy TG Israel, Blessings of; Peace of God.peace been as a river, and thy righteousness as the waves of the sea.

Thy Gen. 22:17 (15–19); Isa. 48:19 (18–22); Hosea 1:10.seed also had been as the sand; the offspring of thy bowels like the gravel thereof; his name should not have been cut off nor destroyed from before me.

Jer. 51:6 (6, 44–45); D&C 133:5 (5–14).Go ye forth of Babylon, flee ye from the TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.Chaldeans, with a voice of singing declare ye, tell this, utter to the end of the earth; say ye: The Lord hath redeemed his Isa. 44:1 (1–2, 21); 45:4.servant Jacob.

And they Ps. 107:33 (33–37); Isa. 41:18 (17–20).thirsted not; he led them through the deserts; he caused the waters to flow out of the Ex. 17:6; Num. 20:11; 1 Ne. 17:29; 2 Ne. 25:20; D&C 133:26 (26–30).rock for them; he clave the rock also and the waters gushed out.
And notwithstanding he hath done all this, and greater also, there is no Rom. 3:17. TG Peace of God.peace, saith the Lord, unto the wicked.

div.p: The Messiah will be a light to the Gentiles and will free the prisoners—Israel will be gathered with power in the last days—Kings will be their nursing fathers—Compare Isaiah 49. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 49:1 (1–26).And again: Hearken, O ye house of Israel, all ye that are broken off and are driven out because of the wickedness of the pastors of my people; yea, all ye that are broken off, that are scattered abroad, who are of my people, O house of Israel. Listen, O 1 Ne. 22:4; 2 Ne. 10:21 (20–22).isles, unto me, and hearken ye people from D&C 1:1.far; the Lord hath called me from the womb; from the bowels of my mother hath he made mention of my name.
And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword; in the shadow of his hand hath he hid me, and made me a polished shaft; in his quiver hath he hid me;
And said unto me: Thou art my Lev. 25:55; Isa. 41:8; D&C 93:46 (45–46).servant, O Israel, in whom I will be glorified.
Then I said, I have labored in Isa. 55:2 (1–2).vain, I have spent my strength for naught and in vain; surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.

And now, saith the Lord—that Isa. 44:24.formed me from the womb that I should be his servant, to bring Jacob again to him—though Israel be not gathered, yet shall I be glorious in the eyes of the Lord, and my God shall be my TG Strength.strength.

And he said: It is a light thing that thou shouldst be my servant to raise up the TG Israel, Twelve Tribes of.tribes of Jacob, and to restore the preserved of Israel. I will also give thee for a Ezek. 5:5; D&C 103:9 (8–9); Abr. 2:11 (6–11).light to the 3 Ne. 21:11. TG Israel, Mission of.Gentiles, that thou mayest be my salvation unto the ends of the earth.

Thus saith the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to him whom man despiseth, to him whom the nations abhorreth, to servant of rulers: Kings shall see and arise, princes also shall worship, because of the Lord that is faithful.

Thus saith the Lord: In an acceptable time have I heard thee, O isles of the sea, and in a day of salvation have I helped thee; and I will preserve thee, and give thee 2 Ne. 3:11 (6–15); 3 Ne. 21:11 (8–11); Morm. 8:16 (16, 25).my servant for a covenant of the people, to establish the earth, to cause to inherit the desolate heritages;

That thou mayest say to the TG Salvation for the Dead; Spirits in Prison.prisoners: Go forth; to them that sit in 2 Ne. 3:5.darkness: Show yourselves. They shall feed in the ways, and their Ezek. 34:14; 1 Ne. 22:25.pastures shall be in all high places.
They shall not hunger nor thirst, neither shall the heat nor the sun smite them; for he that hath mercy on them shall lead them, even by the springs of water shall he guide them.
And I will make all my mountains a way, and my Isa. 62:10; D&C 133:27 (23–32). TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.highways shall be exalted.
And then, O house of Israel, behold, Isa. 43:5 (5–7).these shall come from far; and lo, these from the north and from the west; and these from the land of Sinim.

Isa. 44:23.Sing, O heavens; and be joyful, O earth; for the feet of those who are in the east shall be established; and TG Earth, Renewal of.break forth into singing, O mountains; for they shall be smitten no more; for the Lord hath comforted his people, and will have mercy upon his 2 Sam. 22:28; Ps. 18:27; Isa. 49:13.afflicted.
But, behold, Zion hath said: The Lord hath forsaken me, and my Lord hath forgotten me—but he will show that he hath not.

For can a TG Woman.woman forget her sucking child, that she should not have Ps. 103:13.compassion on the son of her womb? Yea, they may 2 Kgs. 17:38; Isa. 41:17 (15–17); Alma 46:8; D&C 61:36; 133:2.forget, yet will I not forget thee, O house of Israel.
Behold, I have graven thee upon the Zech. 13:6.palms of my hands; thy walls are continually before me.
Thy children shall make haste against thy destroyers; and they that made thee 3 Ne. 21:13 (12–20).waste shall go forth of thee.

Lift up thine eyes round about and behold; all these Micah 4:11 (11–13).gather themselves together, and they shall come to thee. And as I live, saith the Lord, thou shalt surely clothe thee with them all, as with an ornament, and bind them on even as a bride.
For thy waste and thy desolate places, and the land of thy destruction, shall even now be too narrow by reason of the inhabitants; and they that swallowed thee up shall be far away.
The children whom thou shalt have, after thou hast lost the first, shall TG Israel, Gathering of.again in thine ears say: The place is too strait for me; give place to me that I may dwell.

Then shalt thou say in thine heart: Who hath begotten me these, seeing I have lost my children, and am Isa. 54:1; Gal. 4:27.desolate, a captive, and removing to and fro? And who hath brought up these? Behold, I was left alone; these, where have they been?

Thus saith the Lord God: Behold, I will lift up mine hand to the Isa. 66:19 (18–20). TG Israel, Mission of.Gentiles, and set up my Isa. 11:12 (10–12); 18:3; Zech. 9:16.standard to the people; and they shall bring thy sons in their 1 Ne. 22:8; 2 Ne. 10:8 (8–9).arms, and thy daughters shall be carried upon their shoulders.

And Isa. 60:16 (14–16).kings shall be thy 1 Ne. 22:6.nursing fathers, and their queens thy nursing mothers; they shall bow down to thee with their face towards the earth, and lick up the dust of thy feet; and thou shalt know that I am the Lord; for they shall not be ashamed that Gen. 49:18; Prov. 27:18; 2 Ne. 6:13; D&C 98:2; 133:11 (10–11, 45).wait for me.

For shall the prey be taken from the mighty, or the IE the covenant people of the Lord. See also v. 25. JST Isa. 49:25 reads: “But thus saith the Lord; even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered; for the mighty God shall deliver his covenant people …”lawful captives delivered?

But thus saith the Lord, even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered; for I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children.

And I will 1 Ne. 14:17 (15–17); 22:13 (13–14); 2 Ne. 6:14 (14–18).feed them that oppress thee with their own flesh; they shall be drunken with their own blood as with sweet wine; and all flesh shall Ezek. 26:6; Mosiah 11:22 (20–22).know that I, the Lord, am thy TG Jesus Christ, Savior.Savior and thy Redeemer, the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Mighty One of Jacob.

div.p: Israel will be scattered upon all the face of the earth—The Gentiles will nurse and nourish Israel with the gospel in the last days—Israel will be gathered and saved, and the wicked will burn as stubble—The kingdom of the devil will be destroyed, and Satan will be bound. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that after I, Nephi, had read these things which were engraven upon the 1 Ne. 19:22; 2 Ne. 4:2.plates of brass, my brethren came unto me and said unto me: What TG Interpretation.meaneth these things which ye have read? Behold, are they to be understood according to things which are TG Spiritual.spiritual, which shall come to pass according to the spirit and not the flesh?

And I, Nephi, said unto them: Behold they were 2 Pet. 1:21 (19–21).manifest unto the prophet by the voice of the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit; for by the Spirit are all things made known unto the TG Prophecy.prophets, which shall come upon the children of men according to the flesh.

Wherefore, the things of which I have read are things pertaining to things both D&C 29:34 (31–34).temporal and spiritual; for it appears that the house of Israel, sooner or later, will be 1 Ne. 10:12 (12–14); 2 Ne. 25:15 (14–16). TG Israel, Scattering of.scattered upon all the face of the earth, and also TG Inspiration.among all nations.

And behold, there are many who are already lost from the knowledge of those who are at Jerusalem. Yea, the more part of all the TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.tribes have been 2 Ne. 10:22.led away; and they are Ps. 107:4; Zech. 2:6.scattered to and fro upon the Isa. 51:5; 1 Ne. 21:1; 2 Ne. 10:8 (8, 20).isles of the sea; and whither they are none of us knoweth, save that we know that they have been led away.

And since they have been led away, these things have been prophesied concerning them, and also concerning all those who shall hereafter be scattered and be confounded, because of the Holy One of Israel; for against him will they TG Hardheartedness.harden their hearts; wherefore, they shall be scattered among all nations and shall be Luke 23:28–31; 1 Ne. 19:14.hated of all men.

Nevertheless, after they shall be 1 Ne. 21:23.nursed by the TG Gentiles.Gentiles, and the Lord has lifted up his hand upon the Gentiles and set them up for a standard, and their 1 Ne. 15:13; 2 Ne. 30:3 (1–7).children have been carried in their arms, and their daughters have been carried upon their shoulders, behold these things of which are spoken are temporal; for thus are the covenants of the Lord with our fathers; and it meaneth us in the days to come, and also all our brethren who are of the house of Israel.

And it meaneth that the time cometh that after all the house of Israel have been scattered and confounded, that the Lord God will raise up a mighty nation among the 3 Ne. 20:27.Gentiles, yea, even upon the face of this land; and by them shall our seed be Isa. 18:7; 1 Ne. 13:14 (12–14); 2 Ne. 1:11.scattered.

And after our seed is scattered the Lord God will proceed to do a Isa. 29:14; 1 Ne. 14:7; 2 Ne. 27:26. TG Restoration of the Gospel.marvelous work among the 2 Ne. 10:10; 3 Ne. 16:6 (4–7); Morm. 5:19.Gentiles, which shall be of great 1 Ne. 15:14 (13–18); Jacob 3:6; 3 Ne. 5:23 (21–26); 21:7 (4–29).worth unto our seed; wherefore, it is likened unto their being nourished by the TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.Gentiles and being carried in their arms and upon their shoulders.

And it shall also be of 1 Ne. 14:5 (1–5); 2 Ne. 28:2.worth unto the Gentiles; and not only unto the Gentiles but 1 Ne. 15:13 (13–17); 2 Ne. 30:3 (1–7).unto all the 2 Ne. 29:14 (13–14).house of Israel, unto the making known of the Deut. 4:31.covenants of the Father of heaven unto Abraham, saying: In thy TG Abrahamic Covenant; Seed of Abraham.seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be Gen. 12:2; 3 Ne. 20:25 (25, 27).blessed.
And I would, my brethren, that ye should know that all the kindreds of the earth cannot be blessed unless he shall make Isa. 52:10.bare his arm in the eyes of the nations.

Wherefore, the Lord God will proceed to make bare his arm in the eyes of all the TG Israel, Mission of.nations, in bringing about his covenants and his gospel unto those who are of the house of Israel.

Wherefore, he will Ps. 80:19 (17–19); D&C 35:25.bring them again out of 1 Ne. 21:25 (24–25).captivity, and they shall be TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered together to the lands of their TG Lands of Inheritance.inheritance; and they shall be TG Israel, Restoration of.brought out of obscurity and out of TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness; and they shall know that the 1 Ne. 19:15; 2 Ne. 6:11 (10–15).Lord is their TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Jesus Christ, Savior.Savior and their Redeemer, the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Mighty One of Israel.

And the blood of that great and Rev. 17:16 (16–17). TG Devil, Church of.abominable church, which is the whore of all the earth, shall turn upon their own heads; for they shall 1 Ne. 14:16 (3, 15–17); 2 Ne. 6:15. TG War.war among themselves, and the sword of their 1 Ne. 21:26 (24–26).own hands shall fall upon their own heads, and they shall be drunken with their own blood.

And every Luke 21:10.nation which shall war against thee, O house of Israel, shall be turned one against another, and they shall Ps. 7:15; Prov. 26:27; 28:10; Isa. 60:12; Zech. 12:9; 1 Ne. 14:3; 2 Ne. 28:8; D&C 109:25.fall into the pit which they digged to ensnare the people of the Lord. And all that 2 Ne. 10:13; 27:3 (2–3); Morm. 8:41 (40–41); D&C 136:36. TG Protection, Divine.fight against Zion shall be destroyed, and that great whore, who hath perverted the right ways of the Lord, yea, that great and abominable church, shall tumble to the Isa. 25:12.dust and great shall be the fall of it.

For behold, saith the prophet, the time cometh speedily that Satan shall have no more power over the hearts of the children of men; for the day soon cometh that all the proud and they who do wickedly shall be as Isa. 5:23–24; Nahum 1:10; Mal. 4:1; 2 Ne. 15:24; 26:6 (4–6); D&C 64:24 (23–24); 133:64.stubble; and the day cometh that they must be Ps. 21:9 (8–10); 3 Ne. 25:1; D&C 29:9. TG Earth, Cleansing of.burned.

For the time soon cometh that the fulness of the 1 Ne. 14:17; 3 Ne. 20:20 (19–21).wrath of God shall be poured out upon all the children of men; for he will not suffer that the wicked shall destroy the righteous.

Wherefore, he will 2 Ne. 30:10; 3 Ne. 22:13 (13–17); Moses 7:61. TG Protection, Divine.preserve the Ps. 55:22; 1 Ne. 17:35 (33–38).righteous by his power, even if it so be that the fulness of his wrath must come, and the righteous be preserved, even unto the destruction of their enemies by fire. Wherefore, the righteous need not fear; for thus saith the prophet, they shall be saved, even if it so be as by fire.

Behold, my brethren, I say unto you, that these things must shortly come; yea, even blood, and fire, and vapor of smoke must come; and it must needs be upon the face of this earth; and it cometh unto men according to the flesh if it so be that they will harden their hearts against the Holy One of Israel.
For behold, the righteous shall not perish; for the time surely must come that all they who fight against Zion shall be cut off.

And the Lord will surely TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.prepare a way for his people, unto the fulfilling of the words of Moses, which he spake, saying: A John 4:19; 7:40.prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you. And it shall come to pass that all those who will not hear that prophet shall be D&C 133:63.cut off from among the people.

And now I, Nephi, declare unto you, that this Deut. 18:15 (15–19); Acts 3:22 (20–23); 1 Ne. 10:4; 3 Ne. 20:23; Moses 1:6. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.prophet of whom Moses spake was the Holy One of Israel; wherefore, he shall execute Ps. 98:9; Moses 6:57. TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment in righteousness.

And the righteous need not fear, for they are those who shall not be confounded. But it is the kingdom of the devil, which shall be built up among the children of men, which kingdom is established among them which are in the flesh—

For the time speedily shall come that all 1 Ne. 14:10 (9–10); 2 Ne. 26:20. TG Covet; Priestcraft.churches which are built up to get gain, and all those who are built up to get power over the flesh, and those who are built up to become Luke 6:26; Alma 1:3.popular in the eyes of the world, and those who seek the lusts of the flesh and the things of the world, and to do all manner of iniquity; yea, in fine, all those who belong to the kingdom of the 1 Ne. 13:6.devil are they who need fear, and tremble, and 2 Ne. 28:19.quake; they are those who must be brought low in the dust; they are those who must be Zeph. 1:2 (2–3); 2 Ne. 26:6.consumed as stubble; and this is according to the words of the prophet.

And the time cometh speedily that the righteous must be led up as Amos 6:4; Mal. 4:2; 3 Ne. 25:2.calves of the stall, and the Holy One of Israel must reign in dominion, and might, and power, and great TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory.

And he Isa. 43:6 (5–7); Eph. 1:10. TG Israel, Gathering of.gathereth his children from the four quarters of the earth; and he numbereth his TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd; Sheep; Shepherd.sheep, and they know him; and there shall be one fold and one shepherd; and he shall feed his sheep, and in him they shall find 1 Ne. 21:9.pasture.

And because of the TG Millennium; Righteousness.righteousness of his people, Rev. 20:2; Alma 48:17 (16–17); D&C 43:31; 45:55; 88:110; 101:28. TG Devil.Satan has no power; wherefore, he cannot be loosed for the space of Jacob 5:76.many years; for he hath no power over the hearts of the people, for they dwell in righteousness, and the Holy One of Israel TG Jesus Christ, Millennial Reign.reigneth.
And now behold, I, Nephi, say unto you that all these IE these things pertain to this mortal world.things must come according to the flesh.
But, behold, all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people shall dwell safely in the Holy One of Israel if it so be that they will TG Forgive; Repent.repent.
And now I, Nephi, make an end; for I durst not speak further as yet concerning these things.

Wherefore, my brethren, I would that ye should consider that the things which have been written upon the 1 Ne. 19:22; 2 Ne. 4:2.plates of brass are true; and they testify that a man must be obedient to the commandments of God.

Wherefore, ye need not suppose that I and my father are the only ones that have testified, and also taught them. Wherefore, if ye shall be obedient to the Matt. 19:17. TG Commandments of God.commandments, and endure to the end, ye shall be saved at the last day. And thus it is. Amen.

div.p: An account of the death of Lehi. Nephi’s brethren rebel against him. The Lord warns Nephi to depart into the wilderness. His journeyings in the wilderness, and so forth.
Lehi prophesies of a land of liberty—His seed will be scattered and smitten if they reject the Holy One of Israel—He exhorts his sons to put on the armor of righteousness. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that after I, Nephi, had made an end of teaching my brethren, our TG Patriarch.father, Lehi, also spake many things unto them, and rehearsed unto them, how great things the Lord had done for them in bringing them out of the land of Jerusalem.

And he spake unto them concerning their Isa. 65:2 (1–5); 1 Ne. 18:9 (9–20); Alma 18:38.rebellions upon the waters, and the mercies of God in sparing their lives, that they were not swallowed up in the sea.
And he also spake unto them concerning the land of promise, which they had obtained—how Gen. 19:16.merciful the Lord had been in TG Warn.warning us that we should flee out of the land of Jerusalem.

For, behold, said he, I have 1 Ne. 17:14.seen a TG Vision.vision, in which I know that Jer. 26:18 (17–19); 1 Ne. 1:4 (4–18); Hel. 8:20. TG Jerusalem.Jerusalem is Jer. 44:2.destroyed; and had we remained in Jerusalem we should also have Alma 9:22.perished.

But, said he, notwithstanding our afflictions, we have obtained a TG Promised Lands.land of promise, a land which is Ether 2:10 (7–12).choice above all other lands; a land which the Lord God hath TG Vow.covenanted with me should be a land for the inheritance of my seed. Yea, the Lord hath TG Covenants.covenanted this land unto me, and to my children forever, and also all those who should be Ezra 8:22.led out of other countries by the hand of the Lord.
Wherefore, I, Lehi, prophesy according to the workings of the Spirit which is in me, that there shall 2 Ne. 10:22.none come into this land save they shall be brought by the hand of the Lord.

Wherefore, this Mosiah 29:32; Alma 46:10 (10–28, 34).land is consecrated unto him whom he shall bring. And if it so be that they shall serve him according to the commandments which he hath given, it shall be a land of 2 Ne. 10:11. TG Liberty.liberty unto them; wherefore, they shall never be brought down into captivity; if so, it shall be because of iniquity; for if iniquity shall abound Alma 45:16 (10–14, 16); Morm. 1:17; Ether 2:11 (8–12).cursed shall be the land for their sakes, but unto the righteous it shall be blessed forever.

And behold, it is wisdom that this land should be 3 Ne. 5:20.kept as yet from the knowledge of other TG Nations.nations; for behold, many nations would overrun the land, that there would be no place for an inheritance.

Wherefore, I, Lehi, have obtained a Jacob 1:5.promise, that 2 Ne. 4:4; Alma 9:13.inasmuch as those whom the Lord God shall bring out of the land of Jerusalem shall keep his commandments, they shall Deut. 29:9; 30:9.prosper upon the face of this land; and they shall be kept from all other nations, that they may possess this land unto themselves. And if it so be that they shall TG Obedience.keep his commandments they shall be blessed upon the face of this land, and there shall be none to molest them, nor to take away the land of their TG Inheritance.inheritance; and they shall dwell safely forever.

But behold, when the time cometh that they shall dwindle in TG Unbelief.unbelief, after they have received so great blessings from the hand of the Lord—having a knowledge of the creation of the earth, and all men, knowing the great and marvelous works of the Lord from the creation of the world; having power given them to do all things by faith; having all the commandments from the beginning, and having been brought by his infinite goodness into this precious land of promise—behold, I say, if the day shall come that they will reject the Holy One of Israel, the true TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messiah, their Redeemer and their God, behold, the judgments of him that is TG Justice.just shall rest upon them.

Yea, he will bring 1 Ne. 13:14 (12–20); Morm. 5:19 (19–20).other nations unto them, and he will give unto them power, and he will take away from them the lands of their possessions, and he will cause them to be 1 Ne. 22:7.scattered and smitten.

Yea, as one generation passeth to another there shall be Morm. 1:11 (11–19); 4:1 (1–23); D&C 87:6 (1–6).bloodsheds, and great visitations among them; wherefore, my sons, I would that ye would remember; yea, I would that ye would hearken unto my words.

O that ye would awake; awake from a deep TG Sleep.sleep, yea, even from the sleep of TG Damnation.hell, and shake off the awful Isa. 58:6; Alma 12:11 (9–11). TG Bondage, Spiritual.chains by which ye are bound, which are the chains which bind the children of men, that they are carried away captive down to the eternal 1 Ne. 12:18; 15:28 (28–30); Alma 26:20 (19–20); Hel. 3:29.gulf of misery and woe.

Awake! and arise from the dust, and hear the words of a trembling TG Family, Love within.parent, whose limbs ye must soon lay down in the cold and silent TG Death.grave, from whence no traveler can Job 10:21.return; a few more Gen. 47:29 (28–29); Jacob 1:9.days and I go the Josh. 23:14; 1 Kgs. 2:2.way of all the earth.

But behold, the Lord hath Alma 36:28. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.redeemed my soul from hell; I have beheld his Ex. 24:16; Lev. 9:6 (6, 23); Ether 12:6 (6–18). TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory, and I am encircled about eternally in the Isa. 59:16; Jacob 6:5; Alma 5:33; 3 Ne. 9:14.arms of his Rom. 8:39. TG God, Love of.love.

And I desire that ye should remember to observe the Deut. 4:6 (5–8); Ezek. 20:11; 2 Ne. 5:10 (10–11).statutes and the judgments of the Lord; behold, this hath been the anxiety of my soul from the beginning.

My heart hath been weighed down with sorrow from time to time, for I have feared, lest for the hardness of your hearts the Lord your God should come out in the fulness of his 1 Ne. 2:23; 2 Ne. 5:21 (21–24); Alma 3:6 (6–19). TG God, Indignation of.wrath upon you, that ye be Gen. 6:13; 1 Ne. 17:31; Mosiah 12:8; 3 Ne. 9:9.cut off and destroyed forever;

Or, that a TG Curse.cursing should come upon you for the space of 1 Ne. 12:21 (20–23).many generations; and ye are visited by sword, and by famine, and are hated, and are led according to the will and captivity of the Rev. 12:9 (7–9); Moses 1:12. TG Devil.devil.

O my sons, that these things might not come upon you, but that ye might be a choice and a TG Peculiar People.favored people of the Lord. But behold, his will be done; for his Hosea 14:9.ways are righteousness forever.

And he hath said that: Jarom 1:9; Omni 1:6; Mosiah 1:7; Alma 9:13 (13–14); 36:30; 37:13; 3 Ne. 5:22.Inasmuch as ye shall keep my Lev. 26:3 (3–14); Joel 2:25 (23–26); Amos 5:4 (4–8); Mosiah 26:30.commandments ye shall Ps. 67:6; Prov. 22:4 (4–5); Mosiah 2:24 (21–25).prosper in the land; but inasmuch as ye will not keep my commandments ye shall be cut off from my presence.

And now that my soul might have joy in you, and that my heart might leave this world with gladness because of you, that I might not be brought down with grief and sorrow to the grave, arise from the dust, my sons, and be 1 Sam. 4:9; 1 Kgs. 2:2.men, and be determined in Moses 7:18.one mind and in one heart, united in all things, that ye may not come down into captivity;

That ye may not be TG Curse.cursed with a sore cursing; and also, that ye may not incur the displeasure of a D&C 3:4. TG Justice.just God upon you, unto the destruction, yea, the eternal destruction of both soul and body.
Awake, my sons; put on the armor of TG Righteousness.righteousness. Shake off the TG Bondage, Spiritual.chains with which ye are bound, and come forth out of obscurity, and arise from the dust.

Rebel no more against your brother, whose views have been 1 Ne. 18:3.glorious, and who hath kept the commandments from the time that we left Jerusalem; and who hath been an instrument in the hands of God, in bringing us forth into the land of promise; for were it not for him, we must have perished with 1 Ne. 16:32.hunger in the wilderness; nevertheless, ye sought to 1 Ne. 16:37.take away his life; yea, and he hath suffered much sorrow because of you.

And I exceedingly fear and tremble because of you, lest he shall suffer again; for behold, ye have 1 Ne. 15:8 (8–11); Mosiah 10:14.accused him that he sought power and Gen. 37:10 (9–11); 1 Ne. 2:22.authority over you; but I know that he hath not sought for power nor authority over you, but he hath sought the glory of God, and your own eternal welfare.

And ye have murmured because he hath been plain unto you. Ye say that he hath used Prov. 15:10; 1 Ne. 16:2.sharpness; ye say that he hath been angry with you; but behold, his W of M 1:17; Moro. 9:4; D&C 121:43 (41–43).sharpness was the sharpness of the power of the word of God, which was in him; and that which ye call anger was the truth, according to that which is in God, which he could not restrain, manifesting boldly concerning your iniquities.

And it must needs be that the 1 Ne. 17:48.power of God must be with him, even unto his commanding you that ye must obey. But behold, it was not he, but it was the D&C 121:43.Spirit of the Lord which was in him, which D&C 33:8.opened his mouth to utterance that he could not shut it.

And now my son, Laman, and also Lemuel and Sam, and also my sons who are the sons of Ishmael, behold, if ye will hearken unto the voice of Nephi ye shall not perish. And if ye will hearken unto him I leave unto you a TG Birthright.blessing, yea, even my first blessing.
But if ye will not hearken unto him I take away my Gen. 49:3 (3–4); D&C 68:17; Abr. 1:3.first blessing, yea, even my blessing, and it shall rest upon him.

And now, Zoram, I speak unto you: Behold, thou art the 1 Ne. 4:20 (20, 35).servant of Laban; nevertheless, thou hast been brought out of the land of Jerusalem, and I know that thou art a true TG Friendship.friend unto my son, Nephi, forever.

Wherefore, because thou hast been faithful thy seed shall be blessed 2 Ne. 5:6.with his seed, that they dwell in prosperity long upon the face of this land; and nothing, save it shall be iniquity among them, shall harm or disturb their prosperity upon the face of this land forever.
Wherefore, if ye shall keep the commandments of the Lord, the Lord hath consecrated this land for the security of thy seed with the seed of my son.

div.p: Redemption comes through the Holy Messiah—Freedom of choice (agency) is essential to existence and progression—Adam fell that men might be—Men are free to choose liberty and eternal life. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, Jacob, I speak unto you: Thou art my 1 Ne. 18:7 (7, 19).firstborn in the days of my tribulation in the wilderness. And behold, in thy childhood thou hast suffered afflictions and much sorrow, because of the rudeness of thy brethren.
Nevertheless, Jacob, my firstborn in the wilderness, thou knowest the greatness of God; and he shall consecrate thine Micah 4:13; 2 Ne. 32:9. TG Affliction.afflictions for thy gain.

Wherefore, thy soul shall be blessed, and thou shalt dwell safely with thy brother, Nephi; and thy days shall be Enos 1:1.spent in the service of thy God. Wherefore, I know that thou art redeemed, because of the righteousness of thy Redeemer; for thou hast 2 Ne. 11:3.beheld that in the TG Fulness.fulness of time he cometh to bring salvation unto men.

And thou hast 2 Ne. 11:3; Jacob 7:5. TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Antemortal.beheld in thy youth his glory; wherefore, thou art blessed even as they unto whom he shall minister in the flesh; for the Spirit is the same, yesterday, today, and forever. And the way is prepared from the fall of man, and Jude 1:3.salvation is TG Grace.free.

And men are instructed sufficiently that they Moro. 7:16.know good from evil. And the Gal. 2:16; 3:2; Mosiah 13:28 (27–28).law is given unto men. And by the law no flesh is Rom. 3:20 (20–24); 7:5; 2 Ne. 25:23; Alma 42:14 (12–16). TG Justification.justified; or, by the law men are Lev. 7:20 (20–21); 1 Ne. 10:6; 2 Ne. 9:6 (6–38); Alma 11:42 (40–45); 12:16 (16, 24, 36); 42:7 (6–11); Hel. 14:16 (15–18).cut off. Yea, by the temporal law they were cut off; and also, by the spiritual law they perish from that which is good, and become miserable forever.

Wherefore, 1 Ne. 10:6; 2 Ne. 25:20; Mosiah 16:5 (4–5); Alma 12:22 (22–25). TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Redemption.redemption cometh in and through the TG Holiness.Holy TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messiah; for he is full of John 1:17 (14, 17); Alma 13:9; Moses 1:6. TG Grace.grace and truth.

Behold, he offereth himself a TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Sacrifice; Self-Sacrifice.sacrifice for sin, to answer the ends of the law, unto all those who have a broken heart and a contrite spirit; and unto 1 Sam. 2:2 (1–10).none else can the Rom. 10:4.ends of the law be answered.

Wherefore, how great the importance to make these things known unto the inhabitants of the earth, that they may know that there is no flesh that can dwell in the presence of God, 2 Ne. 25:20; 31:21; Mosiah 4:8; 5:8; Alma 21:9; 38:9.save it be through the merits, and mercy, and grace of the Holy Messiah, who TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.layeth down his life according to the flesh, and taketh it again by the power of the Spirit, that he may bring to pass the 1 Cor. 15:20; Mosiah 13:35; Alma 7:12; 12:25 (24–25); 42:23. TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.resurrection of the dead, being the first that should rise.

Wherefore, he is the firstfruits unto God, inasmuch as he shall make Isa. 53:12 (1–12); Mosiah 14:12; 15:8; Moro. 7:28 (27–28). TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.intercession for all the children of men; and they that believe in him shall be saved.

And because of the intercession for Ps. 65:2. TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.all, all men come unto God; wherefore, they stand in the presence of him, to be TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judged of him according to the truth and TG Holiness.holiness which is in him. Wherefore, the ends of the law which the Holy One hath given, unto the inflicting of the TG Punish.punishment which is affixed, which punishment that is affixed is in opposition to that of the happiness which is affixed, to answer the ends of the 2 Ne. 9:26 (7, 21–22, 26); Alma 22:14; 33:22; 34:9 (8–16). TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement—

For it must needs be, that there is an Job 2:10; Matt. 5:45; D&C 29:39; 122:7 (5–9); Moses 6:55. TG Adversity; Agency; Mortality; Opposition.opposition in all things. If not so, my firstborn in the wilderness, righteousness could not be brought to pass, neither wickedness, neither holiness nor misery, neither good nor bad. Wherefore, all things must needs be a compound in one; wherefore, if it should be one body it must needs remain as dead, having no life neither death, nor corruption nor incorruption, happiness nor misery, neither sense nor insensibility.

Wherefore, it must needs have been created for a thing of naught; wherefore there would have been no D&C 88:25. TG Earth, Purpose of.purpose in the end of its creation. Wherefore, this thing must needs destroy the wisdom of God and his eternal purposes, and also the power, and the mercy, and the TG God, Justice of.justice of God.

And if ye shall say there is Rom. 4:15; 5:13; 2 Ne. 9:25; 11:7.no law, ye shall also say there is no sin. If ye shall say there is no sin, ye shall also say there is no righteousness. And if there be no righteousness there be no happiness. And if there be no righteousness nor happiness there be no punishment nor misery. And if these things are not Alma 42:13.there is no God. And if there is no God we are not, neither the earth; for there could have been no creation of things, neither to act nor to be acted upon; wherefore, all things must have vanished away.

And now, my sons, I speak unto you these things for your profit and TG Learn.learning; for there is a God, and he hath TG Creation; God, Creator; Jesus Christ, Creator.created all things, both the heavens and the earth, and all things that in them are, both things to act and things to be D&C 93:30.acted upon.

And to bring about his eternal Isa. 45:18 (17–18); Matt. 5:48; Rom. 8:17 (14–21); Eph. 3:11 (7–12); Alma 42:26; D&C 29:43 (42–44); Moses 1:31, 39. TG Earth, Purpose of.purposes in the end of man, after he had TG Man, Physical Creation of.created our first parents, and the beasts of the field and the Gen. 1:20.fowls of the air, and in fine, all things which are created, it must needs be that there was an opposition; even the Gen. 2:17 (16–17); Moses 3:17.forbidden Gen. 3:6; Mosiah 3:26; Alma 12:22 (21–23).fruit in TG Opposition.opposition to the Gen. 2:9; 1 Ne. 15:36 (22, 28, 36); Alma 12:26 (21, 23, 26); 32:40.tree of life; the one being sweet and the other bitter.

Wherefore, the Lord God gave unto man that he should Alma 12:31. TG Initiative.act for himself. Wherefore, man could not 2 Ne. 10:23. TG Agency.act for himself save it should be that he was D&C 29:39 (39–40).enticed by the one or the other.

And I, Lehi, according to the things which I have read, must needs suppose that an TG Council in Heaven.angel of God, according to that which is written, had Isa. 14:12; 2 Ne. 9:8; Moses 4:3 (3–4); Abr. 3:28 (27–28). TG Sons of Perdition.fallen from heaven; wherefore, he became a TG Adversary; Devil; Lucifer; Satan.devil, having sought that which was evil before God.

And because he had fallen from heaven, and had become miserable forever, he Luke 22:31; Rev. 13:7; 2 Ne. 28:20 (19–23); 3 Ne. 18:18; D&C 10:22 (22–27); 50:3; 76:29.sought also the misery of all mankind. Wherefore, he said unto Eve, yea, even that old serpent, who is the devil, who is the father of all 2 Ne. 28:8; Moses 4:4.lies, wherefore he said: Partake of the forbidden fruit, and ye shall not die, but ye shall be as God, Gen. 3:5; Mosiah 16:3; Alma 29:5; Moro. 7:16 (15–19).knowing good and evil.
And after Adam and Eve had Gen. 2:17 (16–17); Alma 12:31. TG Fall of Man.partaken of the forbidden fruit they were driven out of the garden of TG Eden.Eden, to till the earth.
And they have brought forth children; yea, even the 1 Cor. 15:45 (45–48); D&C 27:11; 138:38; Moses 1:34. TG Adam.family of all the earth.

And the days of the children of Job 14:1; Alma 12:24; Moses 4:23 (22–25).men were prolonged, according to the TG God, Will of.will of God, that they might Alma 34:32. TG Repent.repent while in the flesh; wherefore, their state became a state of TG Mortality; Probation.probation, and their time was lengthened, according to the commandments which the Lord God gave unto the children of men. For he gave commandment that all men must repent; for he showed unto all men that they were Jacob 7:12.lost, because of the transgression of their parents.

And now, behold, if Adam had not transgressed he would not have fallen, but he would have remained in the garden of Eden. And all things which were created must have remained in the same state in which they were after they were created; and they must have remained forever, and had no end.

And they would have had no Gen. 3:16; Moses 5:11. TG Family; Marriage, Motherhood.children; wherefore they would have remained in a state of innocence, having no TG Joy.joy, for they knew no misery; doing no good, for they knew no TG Sin.sin.
But behold, all things have been done in the wisdom of him who TG God, Foreknowledge of; God, Intelligence of; God, Omniscience of.knoweth all things.
TG Adam.Adam Moses 6:48. TG Fall of Man.fell that men might be; and men TG Mortality.are, that they might have Moses 5:10. TG Joy; Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.joy.

And the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messiah cometh in the fulness of time, that he may TG Salvation, Plan of.redeem the children of men from the fall. And because that they are TG Redemption.redeemed from the fall they have become Gal. 5:1; Alma 41:7; 42:27; Hel. 14:30.free forever, knowing good from evil; to act for themselves and not to be acted upon, save it be by the punishment of the TG God, Law of.law at the great and last day, according to the commandments which God hath given.

Wherefore, men are Gal. 5:1; Hel. 14:30 (29–30); Moses 6:56.free according to the TG Mortality.flesh; and 2 Ne. 26:24; Jacob 5:41; Alma 26:37.all things are Alma 29:8. TG Talents.given them which are expedient unto man. And they are free to TG Initiative; Opposition.choose TG Liberty.liberty and eternal Deut. 30:15.life, through the great Mediator of all men, or to choose captivity and death, according to the captivity and power of the devil; for he seeketh that all men might be D&C 10:22.miserable like unto himself.

And now, my sons, I would that ye should look to the great TG Jesus Christ, Mediator.Mediator, and hearken unto his great commandments; and be faithful unto his words, and choose eternal life, according to the will of his Holy Spirit;

And not choose eternal death, according to the will of the flesh and the TG Evil; Sin.evil which is therein, which giveth the spirit of the devil power to Rom. 6:14 (14–18); 1 Ne. 14:7; Alma 12:11 (9–11). TG Bondage, Spiritual.captivate, to bring you down to TG Hell.hell, that he may reign over you in his own kingdom.

I have spoken these few words unto you all, my sons, in the last days of my probation; and I have chosen the good part, according to the words of the prophet. And I have none other object save it be the everlasting TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.welfare of your souls. Amen.

div.p: Joseph in Egypt saw the Nephites in vision—He prophesied of Joseph Smith, the latter-day seer; of Moses, who would deliver Israel; and of the coming forth of the Book of Mormon. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now I speak unto you, Joseph, my 1 Ne. 18:7 (7, 19).last-born. Thou wast born in the wilderness of mine afflictions; yea, in the days of my greatest sorrow did thy mother bear thee.

And may the Lord consecrate also unto thee this 1 Ne. 2:20. TG Promised Lands.land, which is a most precious land, for thine inheritance and the inheritance of thy seed with thy brethren, for thy security forever, if it so be that ye shall keep the commandments of the Holy One of Israel.

And now, Joseph, my last-born, whom I have brought out of the wilderness of mine afflictions, may the Lord bless thee forever, for thy Gen. 45:7; 1 Ne. 13:30.seed shall not utterly be Amos 9:8; 2 Ne. 9:53; 25:21.destroyed.

For behold, thou art the fruit of my loins; and I am a descendant of Gen. 39:2; 45:4; 49:22 (22–26); Ps. 77:15; 1 Ne. 5:14 (14–16).Joseph who was carried Gen. 37:36 (29–36).captive into Egypt. And great were the Amos 5:15.covenants of the Lord which he made unto Joseph.

Wherefore, Joseph truly JST Gen. 50:24–38 (Bible Appendix); 2 Ne. 3:22; 4:2 (1–32).saw our day. And he obtained a TG Promise.promise of the Lord, that out of the fruit of his loins the Lord God would raise up a Jacob 2:25.righteous Gen. 45:7 (5–7); 49:22 (22–26); 1 Ne. 15:12 (12, 16); 19:24; 2 Ne. 14:2. TG Vineyard of the Lord.branch unto the house of Israel; not the Messiah, but a branch which was to be broken off, nevertheless, to be remembered in the covenants of the Lord that the Messiah should be made 2 Ne. 6:14; D&C 3:18 (16–20).manifest unto them in the latter days, in the spirit of power, unto the bringing of them out of Isa. 42:16; 1 Jn. 2:8; 1 Ne. 21:9.darkness unto light—yea, out of hidden darkness and out of captivity unto freedom.

For Joseph truly testified, saying: A 3 Ne. 21:11 (8–11); Morm. 8:16 (16, 25); Ether 3:28 (21–28). TG Seer.seer shall the Lord my God raise up, who shall be a choice seer unto the fruit of my D&C 132:30.loins.

Yea, Joseph truly said: Thus saith the Lord unto me: A choice TG Joseph Smith.seer will I TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.raise up out of the fruit of thy loins; and he shall be esteemed highly among the fruit of thy loins. And unto him will I give commandment that he shall do a work for the fruit of thy loins, his brethren, which shall be of great worth unto them, even to the bringing of them to the TG Book of Mormon.knowledge of the covenants which I have made with thy fathers.
And I will give unto him a commandment that he shall do D&C 24:9 (8–9).none other work, save the work which I shall command him. And I will make him great in mine eyes; for he shall do my work.
And he shall be great like unto Moses 1:41.Moses, whom I have said I would raise up unto you, to Ex. 3:10 (7–10); 1 Ne. 17:24.deliver my 2 Ne. 29:14.people, O house of Israel.
And TG Foreordination.Moses will I raise up, to deliver thy people out of the land of Egypt.

But a 1 Ne. 21:8; 3 Ne. 21:11 (8–11); Morm. 8:16 (16, 25). TG Prophets, Mission of.seer will I raise up out of the fruit of thy loins; and unto him will I give D&C 5:4 (3–4).power to TG Scriptures to Come Forth.bring forth my word unto the seed of thy loins—and not to the bringing forth my word only, saith the Lord, but to the convincing them of my word, which shall have already gone forth among them.

Wherefore, the fruit of thy loins shall TG Book of Mormon.write; and the fruit of the loins of 1 Ne. 13:23 (23–29); 2 Ne. 29:12.Judah shall TG Scriptures, Preservation of; Scriptures, Writing of.write; and that which shall be written by the fruit of thy loins, and also that which shall be written by the fruit of the loins of Judah, shall grow together, unto the Ezek. 37:17 (15–20); 1 Ne. 13:39 (38–41); 2 Ne. 29:8; 33:10 (10–11).confounding of TG False Doctrine.false doctrines and laying down of contentions, and establishing TG Peacemakers.peace among the fruit of thy loins, and Moro. 1:4.bringing them to the 1 Ne. 15:14; 2 Ne. 30:5; 3 Ne. 5:23; Morm. 7:9 (1, 5, 9–10).knowledge of their fathers in the latter days, and also to the knowledge of my covenants, saith the Lord.
And out of weakness he shall be made strong, in that day when my work shall commence among all my people, unto the restoring thee, O house of Israel, saith the Lord.

And thus prophesied Joseph, saying: Behold, that seer will the Lord bless; and they that seek to destroy him shall be confounded; for this promise, which I have obtained of the Lord, of the fruit of my loins, shall be fulfilled. Behold, I am sure of the fulfilling of this promise;

And his D&C 18:8.name shall be called after me; and it shall be after the JS—H 1:3.name of his father. And he shall be D&C 28:2.like unto me; for the thing, which the Lord shall bring forth by his hand, by the power of the Lord shall bring Enos 1:13 (12–18); Alma 37:19 (1–20).my people unto TG Scriptures, Value of.salvation.
Yea, thus prophesied Joseph: I am sure of this thing, even as I am sure of the promise of Moses; for the Lord hath said unto me, I will Gen. 45:7 (1–8); D&C 107:42.preserve thy seed forever.

And the Lord hath said: I will raise up a Moses; and I will give power unto him in a rod; and I will give judgment unto him in writing. Yet I will not loose his tongue, that he shall speak much, for I will not make him mighty in speaking. But I will Deut. 10:2 (2, 4); Moses 2:1. TG Scriptures, Writing of.write unto him my law, by the finger of mine own hand; and I will make a Ex. 4:16 (14–16).spokesman for him.

And the Lord said unto me also: I will raise up unto the fruit of thy loins; and I will make for him a spokesman. And I, behold, I will give unto him that he shall write the writing of the fruit of thy loins, unto the fruit of thy loins; and the spokesman of thy loins shall declare it.

And the words which he shall write shall be the words which are expedient in my wisdom should go forth unto the D&C 28:8.fruit of thy loins. And it shall be as if the fruit of thy loins had cried unto them Isa. 29:4; 2 Ne. 27:13; 33:13 (13–15); Morm. 9:30; Moro. 10:27.from the dust; for I know their faith.

And they shall 2 Ne. 26:16; Morm. 8:23 (23, 26).cry from the TG Book of Mormon.dust; yea, even repentance unto their brethren, even after many generations have gone by them. And it shall come to pass that their cry shall go, even according to the simpleness of their words.

Because of their faith their 2 Ne. 29:2.words shall proceed forth out of my mouth unto their brethren who are the fruit of thy loins; and the weakness of their words will I make strong in their faith, unto the remembering of my covenant which I made unto thy fathers.
And now, behold, my son Joseph, after this manner did my father of old 2 Ne. 3:5.prophesy.
Wherefore, because of this covenant thou art TG Birthright.blessed; for thy seed shall not be destroyed, for they shall TG Obedience.hearken unto the words of the book.

And there shall rise up TG Joseph Smith.one mighty among them, who shall do much good, both in word and in deed, being an instrument in the hands of God, with exceeding faith, to work mighty wonders, and do that thing which is great in the sight of God, unto the bringing to pass much TG Dispensations; Israel, Restoration of; Restoration of the Gospel.restoration unto the house of Israel, and unto the seed of thy brethren.

And now, blessed art thou, Joseph. Behold, thou art little; wherefore hearken unto the words of thy brother, Nephi, and it shall be done unto thee even according to the words which I have spoken. Remember the words of thy dying father. Amen.

div.p: Lehi counsels and blesses his posterity—He dies and is buried—Nephi glories in the goodness of God—Nephi puts his trust in the Lord forever. About 588–570 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, I, Nephi, speak concerning the prophecies of which my father hath spoken, concerning Gen. 39:2.Joseph, who was carried into Egypt.

For behold, he truly prophesied concerning all his seed. And the 2 Ne. 3:5.prophecies which he wrote, there are not many greater. And he prophesied concerning us, and our future generations; and they are written upon the 1 Ne. 22:30; 2 Ne. 5:12.plates of brass.

Wherefore, after my father had made an end of speaking concerning the prophecies of Joseph, he called the children of Laman, his sons, and his daughters, and said unto them: Behold, my sons, and my daughters, who are the sons and the daughters of my TG Firstborn.firstborn, I would that ye should give ear unto my words.

For the Lord God hath said that: 2 Ne. 1:9; Alma 9:13.Inasmuch as ye shall keep my commandments ye shall prosper in the land; and inasmuch as ye will not keep my commandments ye shall be cut off from my presence.

But behold, my sons and my daughters, I cannot go down to my grave save I should leave a TG Family, Patriarchal; Patriarchal Blessings.blessing upon you; for behold, I know that if ye are TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.brought up in the Prov. 22:6.way ye should go ye will not depart from it.
Wherefore, if ye are 1 Ne. 2:23.cursed, behold, I leave my blessing upon you, that the TG Curse.cursing may be taken from you and be answered upon the D&C 68:25 (25–29).heads of your parents.

Wherefore, because of my blessing the Lord God will 1 Ne. 22:7 (7–8); 2 Ne. 30:3 (3–6); Jacob 1:5. TG Book of Mormon.not suffer that ye shall perish; wherefore, he will be 1 Ne. 13:31; 2 Ne. 10:18 (18–19); Jacob 3:6 (5–9); Hel. 15:12 (10–17); Morm. 5:20 (20–21).merciful unto you and unto your seed forever.
And it came to pass that after my father had made an end of speaking to the sons and daughters of Laman, he caused the sons and daughters of Lemuel to be brought before him.

And he spake unto them, saying: Behold, my sons and my daughters, who are the sons and the daughters of my second son; behold I leave unto you the same blessing which I left unto the sons and daughters of Laman; wherefore, thou shalt not utterly be destroyed; but in the end thy seed shall be blessed.
And it came to pass that when my father had made an end of speaking unto them, behold, he spake unto the sons of 1 Ne. 7:6.Ishmael, yea, and even all his household.

And after he had made an end of speaking unto them, he spake unto Sam, saying: Blessed art thou, and thy Jacob 1:14 (12–14).seed; for thou shalt inherit the land like unto thy brother Nephi. And thy seed shall be numbered with his seed; and thou shalt be even like unto thy brother, and thy seed like unto his seed; and thou shalt be blessed in all thy days.

And it came to pass after my father, Lehi, had Gen. 49:1 (1–27).spoken unto all his household, according to the feelings of his heart and the Spirit of the Lord which was in him, he waxed TG Old Age.old. And it came to pass that he died, and was buried.

And it came to pass that not many days after his death, Laman and Lemuel and the sons of Ishmael were 1 Ne. 7:6 (6–19); 17:18 (17–55); 18:10 (9–22); 2 Ne. 5:2 (1–25). TG Anger.angry with me because of the admonitions of the Lord.

For I, Nephi, was constrained to speak unto them, according to his word; for I had spoken many things unto them, and also my father, before his death; many of which sayings are written upon mine 1 Ne. 1:17 (16–17); 9:4; 2 Ne. 5:33 (29–33); D&C 10:42.other plates; for a more history part are written upon mine other plates.

And upon 1 Ne. 6:1 (1–6).these I TG Scriptures, Writing of.write the things of my soul, and many of the scriptures which are engraven upon the plates of brass. For my soul Ps. 119:24; Moses 6:59.delighteth in the scriptures, and my heart TG Meditation; Scriptures, Study of.pondereth them, and writeth them for the 1 Ne. 19:23. TG Scriptures, Value of.learning and the profit of my children.
Behold, my TG Spirituality; Thanksgiving.soul delighteth in the things of the Lord; and my TG Heart.heart pondereth continually upon the things which I have seen and heard.

Nevertheless, notwithstanding the great Ex. 34:6 (5–7); 2 Ne. 9:10; D&C 86:11.goodness of the Lord, in showing me his great and marvelous works, my heart exclaimeth: O Rom. 7:24.wretched man that I am! Yea, my heart TG Poor in Spirit; Repent; Sorrow.sorroweth because of my flesh; my soul grieveth because of mine iniquities.
I am encompassed about, because of the temptations and the sins which do so easily Rom. 7:21 (15–25); Heb. 12:1; Alma 7:15.beset me.
And when I desire to rejoice, my heart groaneth because of my sins; nevertheless, I know in whom I have TG Trust in God.trusted.
My God hath been my 2 Cor. 4:16.support; he hath led me through mine 1 Ne. 17:6. TG Affliction; Comfort.afflictions in the wilderness; and he hath preserved me upon the waters of the great deep.
He hath filled me with his TG God, Love of.love, even unto the D&C 84:33.consuming of my flesh.
He hath confounded mine Ps. 3:7 (7–8).enemies, unto the causing of them to quake before me.
Behold, he hath heard my cry by day, and he hath given me TG Knowledge.knowledge by 2 Chr. 26:5. TG Dream; Vision.visions in the night-time.
And by day have I waxed bold in mighty James 5:16; 1 Ne. 1:5 (5–8); 10:17.prayer before him; yea, my voice have I sent up on high; and angels came down and ministered unto me.

And upon the wings of his Spirit hath my body been 2 Cor. 12:2 (1–4); 1 Ne. 11:1 (1–36); Moses 1:1.carried away upon exceedingly high mountains. And mine eyes have beheld great things, yea, even too great for man; therefore I was bidden that I should not write them.

O then, if I have seen so great things, if the Lord in his condescension unto the children of men hath Ex. 3:16; Alma 9:21; Morm. 1:15.visited men in so much TG Compassion; God, Mercy of.mercy, Ps. 43:5.why should my TG Heart.heart weep and my soul linger in the valley of sorrow, and my flesh waste away, and my strength slacken, because of mine afflictions?

And why should I Rom. 6:13 (10–16).yield to sin, because of my flesh? Yea, why should I give way to TG Temptation.temptations, that the evil one have place in my heart to destroy my TG Contentment; Peace; Peace of God.peace and afflict my soul? Why am I TG Self-Mastery.angry because of mine enemy?
Awake, my soul! No longer Ps. 42:11.droop in sin. Rejoice, O my heart, and give place no more for the TG Adversary; Enemies.enemy of my soul.
Do not TG Anger.anger again because of mine enemies. Do not slacken my strength because of mine afflictions.
Rejoice, O my TG Heart.heart, and cry unto the Lord, and say: O Lord, I will praise thee forever; yea, my soul will rejoice in thee, my God, and the 1 Cor. 3:11 (9–13). TG Rock.rock of my salvation.
O Lord, wilt thou Ps. 16:10.redeem my soul? Wilt thou deliver me out of the hands of mine enemies? Wilt thou make me that I may shake at the appearance of Rom. 12:9; Alma 13:12; 37:32. TG Sin.sin?

May the gates of hell be shut continually before me, because that my TG Contrite Heart.heart is broken and my spirit is contrite! O Lord, wilt thou not shut the gates of thy righteousness before me, that I may TG Walking with God.walk in the path of the low valley, that I may be strict in the plain road!

O Lord, wilt thou encircle me around in the robe of thy TG Righteousness.righteousness! O Lord, wilt thou make a way for mine escape before mine Lev. 26:7 (1–13); D&C 44:5.enemies! Wilt thou make my path straight before me! Wilt thou not place a stumbling block in my way—but that thou wouldst clear my way before me, and hedge not up my way, but the ways of mine enemy.

O Lord, I have TG Trustworthiness.trusted in thee, and I will TG Trust in God.trust in thee forever. I will not put my Ps. 33:16; 44:6 (6–8). TG Trust Not in the Arm of Flesh.trust in the arm of flesh; for I know that cursed is he that putteth his Prov. 14:16; Jer. 17:5; Morm. 3:9; 4:8.trust in the arm of flesh. Yea, cursed is he that putteth his trust in man or maketh flesh his arm.

Yea, I know that God will give James 1:5. TG Abundant Life.liberally to him that asketh. Yea, my God will give me, if I TG Prayer.ask Hel. 10:5.not amiss; therefore I will lift up my voice unto thee; yea, I will cry unto thee, my God, the Deut. 32:4.rock of my Ps. 4:1.righteousness. Behold, my voice shall forever ascend up unto thee, my rock and mine everlasting God. Amen.

div.p: The Nephites separate themselves from the Lamanites, keep the law of Moses, and build a temple—Because of their unbelief, the Lamanites are cut off from the presence of the Lord, are cursed, and become a scourge unto the Nephites. About 588–559 B.C.

chapter.verse: Behold, it came to pass that I, Nephi, did cry much unto the Lord my God, because of the 2 Ne. 4:13; Jacob 7:24; Enos 1:20; Mosiah 10:12, 15.anger of my brethren.
But behold, their 1 Ne. 7:6 (6–19); 17:18 (17–55); 18:10 (9–22); 2 Ne. 4:13 (13–14).anger did increase against me, insomuch that they did seek to take away my life.

Yea, they did murmur against me, saying: Our younger brother thinks to Num. 16:13; 1 Ne. 16:37 (37–38); Mosiah 10:15.rule over us; and we have had much trial because of him; wherefore, now let us slay him, that we may not be afflicted more because of his words. For behold, we will not have him to be our ruler; for it belongs unto us, who are the elder brethren, to Alma 54:17.rule over this people.
Now I do not write upon these plates all the words which they murmured against me. But it sufficeth me to say, that they did seek to take away my life.
And it came to pass that the Lord did TG Guidance, Divine.warn me, that I, Mosiah 10:13.Nephi, should depart from them and flee into the wilderness, and all those who would go with me.

Wherefore, it came to pass that I, Nephi, did take my family, and also 1 Ne. 4:35; 16:7; 2 Ne. 1:31 (30–32).Zoram and his family, and Sam, mine elder brother and his family, and Jacob and Joseph, my younger brethren, and also my sisters, and all those who would go with me. And all those who would go with me were those who believed in the TG Warn.warnings and the revelations of God; wherefore, they did hearken unto my words.

And we did take our tents and whatsoever things were possible for us, and did journey in the wilderness for the space of many days. And after we had journeyed for the space of many days we did pitch our tents.

And my people would that we should call the name of the place Omni 1:12 (12, 27); Mosiah 7:1 (1–7, 21); 9:1 (1–6, 14); 28:1 (1, 5); Alma 2:24; 20:1; 50:8 (8, 11).Nephi; wherefore, we did call it Nephi.
And all those who were with me did take upon them to call themselves the Jacob 1:14.people of Nephi.
And we did observe to keep the judgments, and the Ezek. 20:11; 2 Ne. 1:16 (16–17).statutes, and the commandments of the Lord in all things, according to the 2 Ne. 11:4. TG Law of Moses.law of Moses.
And the Lord was with us; and we did Matt. 6:33.prosper exceedingly; for we did sow seed, and we did reap again in abundance. And we began to raise flocks, and herds, and animals of every kind.

And I, Nephi, had also brought the records which were engraven upon the 2 Ne. 4:2; Mosiah 1:3 (3–4).plates of brass; and also the Mosiah 1:16.ball, or 1 Ne. 16:16 (10, 16, 26); 18:12 (12, 21); Alma 37:38 (38–47); D&C 17:1.compass, which was prepared for my father by the hand of the Lord, according to that which is written.
And it came to pass that we began to prosper exceedingly, and to multiply in the land.

And I, Nephi, did take the 1 Ne. 4:9; Jacob 1:10; W of M 1:13; Mosiah 1:16; D&C 17:1.sword of Laban, and after the manner of it did make many Jarom 1:8; Mosiah 10:8; Alma 2:12; Hel. 1:14; 3 Ne. 3:26.swords, lest by any means the people who were now called Lamanites should come upon us and destroy us; for I knew their TG Hate.hatred towards me and my children and those who were called my people.

And I did teach my people to TG Skill.build buildings, and to TG Art.work in all Jarom 1:8.manner of wood, and of Josh. 8:31; 1 Ne. 18:25; Jacob 2:12 (12–13); Hel. 6:9 (9–11); Ether 9:17; 10:23 (12, 23); Moses 5:46.iron, and of copper, and of Gen. 4:22.brass, and of steel, and of Ex. 31:4 (4–5); 1 Kgs. 6:21 (21–22); D&C 124:26 (26–27).gold, and of silver, and of precious ores, which were in great abundance.

And I, Nephi, did 2 Chr. 3:1 (1–17); D&C 84:5 (5, 31); 124:31 (25–55).build a 1 Kgs. 5:5; Jacob 1:17; Mosiah 1:18; 7:17; 11:10; Alma 16:13; Hel. 3:14 (9, 14); 3 Ne. 11:1. TG Temple.temple; and I did construct it after the manner of the temple of 1 Kgs. 6:2.Solomon save it were not built of so many 1 Kgs. 5:17.precious things; for they were not to be found upon the land, wherefore, it could not be built like unto Solomon’s 1 Kgs. 9:1.temple. But the manner of the construction was like unto the temple of 1 Chr. 18:8.Solomon; and the workmanship thereof was exceedingly fine.
And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did cause my people to be TG Industry; Work, Value of.industrious, and to TG Labor.labor with their Prov. 31:13.hands.

And it came to pass that they would that I should be their 2 Ne. 6:2; Jacob 1:9 (9, 11, 15); Jarom 1:7 (7, 14); Mosiah 1:10.king. But I, Nephi, was desirous that they should have no king; nevertheless, I did for them according to that which was in my power.

And behold, the words of the Lord had been fulfilled unto my brethren, which he spake concerning them, that I should be their 2 Ne. 1:25 (25–27).ruler and their teacher. Wherefore, I had been their ruler and their TG Teacher.teacher, according to the commandments of the Lord, until the time they sought to take away my life.

Wherefore, the word of the Lord was fulfilled which he spake unto me, saying that: Inasmuch as they will 1 Ne. 8:18.not hearken unto thy words they shall be 1 Ne. 8:35 (35–36).cut off from the presence of the Lord. And behold, they were 1 Ne. 2:21; Alma 9:14 (13–15); 38:1.cut off from his presence.

And he had caused the TG Curse.cursing to come upon them, yea, even a sore cursing, because of their iniquity. For behold, they had hardened their hearts against him, that they had become like unto a flint; wherefore, as they were white, and exceedingly fair and Gen. 24:16; 1 Ne. 13:15; 4 Ne. 1:10; Morm. 9:6.delightsome, that they might not be TG Marriage, Temporal.enticing unto my people the Lord God did cause a 2 Ne. 30:6; 3 Ne. 2:15 (14–16).skin of 2 Ne. 26:33; Moses 7:8.blackness to come upon them.
And thus saith the Lord God: I will cause that they shall be 1 Ne. 12:23.loathsome unto thy people, save they shall repent of their iniquities.
And cursed shall be the seed of him that TG Marriage, Interfaith.mixeth with their seed; for they shall be cursed even with the same cursing. And the Lord spake it, and it was done.
And because of their TG Curse.cursing which was upon them they did become an Alma 22:28. TG Idleness.idle people, full of mischief and subtlety, and did seek in the wilderness for beasts of prey.

And the Lord God said unto me: They shall be a scourge unto thy seed, to 1 Ne. 2:24.stir them up in remembrance of me; and inasmuch as they will not remember me, and hearken unto my words, they shall scourge them even unto destruction.
