And it came to pass that I, Nephi, did Lev. 16:32; Jacob 1:18 (18–19); Mosiah 23:17. TG Priesthood, Authority.consecrate Jacob and Joseph, that they should be TG Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.priests and TG Teacher.teachers over the land of my people.
And it came to pass that we lived after the manner of Alma 50:23.happiness.
And thirty years had passed away from the time we left Jerusalem.
And I, Nephi, had kept the TG Record Keeping.records upon my plates, which I had made, of my people thus far.

And it came to pass that the Lord God said unto me: 1 Ne. 19:5 (1–6); Jacob 3:14.Make other plates; and thou shalt engraven many things upon them which are good in my sight, for the profit of thy people.
Wherefore, I, Nephi, to be obedient to the commandments of the Lord, went and made 1 Ne. 19:3; Jacob 1:1.these plates upon which I have engraven these things.
And I engraved that which is pleasing unto God. And if my people are pleased with the things of God they will be pleased with mine engravings which are upon these plates.
And if my people desire to know the more particular part of the history of my people they must search mine 1 Ne. 1:17 (16–17); 2 Ne. 4:14; D&C 10:42.other 1 Ne. 19:4; Jacob 1:3.plates.
And it sufficeth me to say that forty years had passed away, and we had already had wars and contentions with our brethren.

div.p: Jacob recounts Jewish history: The Babylonian captivity and return; the ministry and crucifixion of the Holy One of Israel; the help received from the Gentiles; and the Jews’ latter-day restoration when they believe in the Messiah. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: The 2 Ne. 11:1; Jacob 2:1.words of Jacob, the brother of Nephi, which he spake unto the people of Nephi:

Behold, my beloved brethren, I, Jacob, having been called of God, and ordained after the manner of his holy TG Priesthood, Melchizedek.order, and having been consecrated by my brother Nephi, unto whom ye look as a 2 Ne. 5:18; Jacob 1:9 (9, 11, 15); Jarom 1:7 (7, 14); Mosiah 1:10.king or a protector, and on whom ye depend for safety, behold ye know that I have spoken unto you exceedingly many things.

Nevertheless, I speak unto you again; for I am desirous for the Jacob 2:3; Mosiah 25:11.welfare of your souls. Yea, mine anxiety is great for you; and ye yourselves know that it ever has been. For I have exhorted you with all diligence; and I have taught you the words of my father; and I have spoken unto you concerning all things which are TG Scriptures, Value of.written, from the creation of the world.

And now, behold, I would speak unto you concerning things which are, and which are to come; wherefore, I will read you the words of 3 Ne. 23:1 (1–3).Isaiah. And they are the words which my brother has desired that I should speak unto you. And I speak unto you for your sakes, that ye may learn and glorify the name of your God.

And now, the words which I shall read are they which Isaiah spake concerning all the house of Israel; wherefore, they may be IE applied.likened unto you, for ye are of the house of Israel. And there are many things which have been spoken by Isaiah which may be likened unto you, because ye are of the house of Israel.

And now, these are the words: Isa. 49:22 (22–23); 2 Ne. 10:9.Thus saith the Lord God: Behold, I will lift up mine hand to the Gentiles, and set up my TG Ensign.standard to the people; and they shall bring thy sons in their arms, and thy daughters shall be carried upon their shoulders.

And Isa. 60:16.kings shall be thy nursing fathers, and their queens thy nursing mothers; they shall bow down to thee with their faces towards the earth, and lick up the dust of thy feet; and thou shalt know that Isa. 44:8; 45:5 (5–22); 46:9; 3 Ne. 24:6; Moses 1:6.I am the Lord; for they shall not be ashamed that Lam. 3:25 (25–26); D&C 133:45.wait for me.

And now I, Jacob, would speak somewhat concerning these words. For behold, the Lord has shown me that those who were at Esth. 2:6; 1 Ne. 7:13; 10:3; 2 Ne. 25:6, 10; Omni 1:15; Hel. 8:20 (20–21).Jerusalem, from whence we came, have been Ezek. 23:25 (24–29).slain and 2 Kgs. 24:14 (10–16); 25:11 (1–12); Jer. 13:19 (19, 24). TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.carried away captive.

Nevertheless, the Lord has shown unto me that they should Jer. 29:10 (9–10); 1 Ne. 10:3.return again. And he also has shown unto me that the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel, should manifest himself unto them in the flesh; and after he should manifest himself they should TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.scourge him and 1 Ne. 19:10 (10, 13); Mosiah 3:9; 3 Ne. 11:14 (14–15). TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucify him, according to the words of the angel who spake it unto me.

And after they have TG Hardheartedness.hardened their hearts and TG Stiffnecked.stiffened their necks against the Holy One of Israel, behold, the Matt. 27:25 (24–25).judgments of the Holy One of Israel shall come upon them. And the day cometh that they shall be smitten and afflicted.

Wherefore, after they are driven to and fro, for thus saith the angel, many shall be afflicted in the flesh, and shall not be suffered to Amos 9:8 (8–9); 2 Ne. 20:20 (20–21).perish, because of the prayers of the faithful; they shall be scattered, and smitten, and hated; nevertheless, the Lord will be merciful unto them, that 1 Ne. 22:12 (11–12); 2 Ne. 9:2 (1–2).when they shall come to the Hosea 3:5; D&C 113:10. TG Israel, Restoration of.knowledge of their Redeemer, they shall be TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered together again to the TG Lands of Inheritance.lands of their inheritance.

And blessed are the 1 Ne. 14:2 (1–5); 2 Ne. 10:10 (8–14, 18).Gentiles, they of whom the prophet has written; for behold, if it so be that they shall repent and fight not against Zion, and do not unite themselves to that great and TG Devil, Church of.abominable church, they shall be saved; for the Lord God will fulfil his TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenants which he has made unto his children; and for this cause the prophet has written these things.

Wherefore, they that fight against Zion and the covenant people of the Lord shall lick up the dust of their feet; and the people of the Lord shall not be Joel 2:26 (26–27); 3 Ne. 22:4; D&C 90:17.ashamed. For the people of the Lord are they who Gen. 49:18; Ps. 25:5; Prov. 20:22; 27:18; Isa. 40:31; 1 Ne. 21:23; D&C 98:2; 133:11, 45.wait for him; for they still wait for the coming of the Messiah.

And behold, according to the words of the prophet, the Messiah will set himself again the 2 Ne. 21:11; 25:17; 29:1.second time to recover them; wherefore, he will 2 Ne. 3:5; D&C 3:18 (16–20).manifest himself unto them in power and great glory, unto the 1 Ne. 21:26 (24–26); 22:13 (13–14).destruction of their enemies, when that day cometh when they shall believe in him; and none will he destroy that believe in him.

And they that believe not in him shall be 1 Ne. 22:13 (13–23); 2 Ne. 10:16 (15–16); 28:15 (15–32); 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–15); Ether 2:9 (8–11). TG Last Days.destroyed, both by Joel 1:19 (19–20); Jacob 5:77; 6:3.fire, and by tempest, and by earthquakes, and by TG Blood, Shedding of.bloodsheds, and by Luke 21:11 (10–13); Mosiah 12:4; D&C 97:26 (22–26). TG Plague.pestilence, and by TG Drought.famine. And they shall know that the Lord is God, the Holy One of Israel.
Isa. 49:24 (24–26); 2 Ne. 11:2.For shall the prey be taken from the mighty, or the HEB righteous captive; i.e., the covenant people of the Lord, as stated in v. 17.lawful captive delivered?

But thus saith the Lord: Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered; 1 Ne. 21:25.for the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Mighty God shall 2 Kgs. 17:39; D&C 105:8. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Jesus Christ, Savior.deliver his covenant people. For thus saith the Lord: I will contend with them that contendeth with thee—

And I will feed them that oppress thee, with their own flesh; and they shall be drunken with their own blood as with sweet wine; and all flesh shall know that I the Lord am thy Savior and thy TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.Redeemer, the Gen. 49:24; Ps. 132:2; Isa. 1:24; 60:16.Mighty One of Jacob.

div.p: Jacob continues reading from Isaiah: Isaiah speaks messianically—The Messiah will have the tongue of the learned—He will give His back to the smiters—He will not be confounded—Compare Isaiah 50. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 50:1 (1–11); 2 Ne. 8:1.Yea, for thus saith the Lord: Have I put thee away, or have I cast thee off forever? For thus saith the Lord: Where is the Jer. 3:8.bill of your mother’s TG Divorce.divorcement? To whom have I put thee away, or to which of my 2 Kgs. 4:1; Matt. 18:25.creditors have I Judg. 4:2; Isa. 52:3. TG Apostasy of Israel.sold you? Yea, to whom have I sold you? Behold, for your iniquities have ye sold yourselves, and for your transgressions is your mother put away.

Wherefore, when I came, there was no man; when I Prov. 1:24 (24–27); Isa. 65:12; Alma 5:37.called, yea, there was none to answer. O house of Israel, is my hand shortened at all that it cannot redeem, or have I no power to deliver? Behold, at my rebuke I Nahum 1:4.dry up the Ex. 14:21 (1–31); Ps. 106:9; D&C 133:68.sea, I make their Josh. 3:16 (15–16).rivers a wilderness and their Ex. 7:21 (17–21).fish to stink because the waters are dried up, and they die because of thirst.
I clothe the heavens with Ex. 10:21.blackness, and I make Rev. 6:12.sackcloth their covering.

The Lord God hath given me the Luke 21:15.tongue of the learned, that I should know how to speak a word in season unto thee, O house of Israel. When ye are weary he waketh morning by morning. He waketh mine ear to hear as the learned.
The Lord God hath opened mine D&C 58:1.ear, and I was not rebellious, neither turned away back.
I gave my back to the Isa. 53:4; Matt. 27:26; 2 Ne. 9:5 (4–7).smiter, and my cheeks to them that plucked off the hair. I hid not my face from TG Shame.shame and spitting.
For the Lord God will help me, therefore shall I not be confounded. Therefore have I set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be Rom. 9:33.ashamed.

And the Lord is near, and he Rom. 8:33 (32–34).justifieth me. Who will contend with me? Let us stand together. Who is mine adversary? Let him come near me, and I will Isa. 11:4.smite him with the strength of my mouth.
For the Lord God will help me. And all they who shall Rom. 8:31.condemn me, behold, all they shall Ps. 102:26.wax old as a garment, and the moth shall eat them up.
Who is among you that feareth the Lord, that obeyeth the D&C 1:38.voice of his servant, that TG Walking in Darkness.walketh in darkness and hath no light?

Behold all ye that kindle fire, that compass yourselves about with sparks, walk in the light of Deut. 12:8; Judg. 17:6.your fire and in the sparks which ye have kindled. D&C 133:70.This shall ye have of mine hand—ye shall lie down in sorrow.

div.p: Jacob continues reading from Isaiah: In the last days, the Lord will comfort Zion and gather Israel—The redeemed will come to Zion amid great joy—Compare Isaiah 51 and 52:1–2. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 51:1 (1–23); 2 Ne. 7:1.Hearken unto me, ye that follow after righteousness. Look unto the IE Abraham and Sarah; see v. 2. TG Rock.rock from whence ye are hewn, and to the hole of the pit from whence ye are digged.
Look unto Abraham, your Gen. 17:4 (1–8); D&C 109:64; 132:49.father, and unto Gen. 24:36.Sarah, she that bare you; for I called him alone, and blessed him.

For the Lord shall TG Israel, Restoration of.comfort TG Zion.Zion, he will comfort all her waste places; and he will make her Isa. 35:2 (1–2, 6–7). TG Israel, Blessings of.wilderness like TG Earth, Renewal of; Eden.Eden, and her desert like the garden of the Lord. Joy and gladness shall be found therein, thanksgiving and the voice of melody.

Hearken unto me, my people; and give ear unto me, O my nation; for a Isa. 2:3. TG God, Law of.law shall proceed from me, and I will make my judgment to rest for a TG Light [noun].light for the people.

My righteousness is near; my TG Jesus Christ, Savior; Salvation.salvation is gone forth, and mine arm shall TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge the people. The 2 Ne. 10:20.isles shall wait upon me, and on mine arm shall they trust.

Lift up your eyes to the 2 Pet. 3:10.heavens, and look upon the earth beneath; for the heavens shall Isa. 13:13.vanish away like smoke, and the earth shall Ps. 102:26 (25–28).wax old like a garment; and they that dwell therein shall die in like manner. But my salvation shall be forever, and my righteousness shall not be abolished.

Hearken unto me, ye that know righteousness, the people in whose heart I have written my law, Deut. 1:17; Ps. 56:4 (4, 11); 118:6; D&C 122:9. TG Peer Influence.fear ye not the TG Reproach.reproach of men, neither be ye afraid of their TG Hate.revilings.
For the Isa. 50:9.moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool. But my righteousness shall be forever, and my salvation from generation to generation.

Isa. 52:1.Awake, awake! Put on D&C 113:8 (7–8). TG Israel, Restoration of.strength, O arm of the Lord; awake as in the ancient days. Art thou not he that hath cut Ps. 89:10; Isa. 27:1.Rahab, and wounded the Ezek. 29:3.dragon?
Art thou not he who hath dried the sea, the waters of the great deep; that hath made the depths of the sea a Isa. 35:8 (8–10).way for the ransomed to pass over?

Therefore, the TG Israel, Restoration of.redeemed of the Lord shall TG Israel, Gathering of.return, and come with Isa. 35:10; Jer. 31:12 (12–13).singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy and holiness shall be upon their heads; and they shall obtain gladness and joy; sorrow and Rev. 21:4 (2–5).mourning shall flee away.

D&C 133:47; 136:22.I am he; yea, I am he that comforteth you. Behold, who art thou, that thou shouldst be Jer. 1:8 (7–8).afraid of man, who shall die, and of the son of man, who shall be made like unto Isa. 40:6 (6–8); 1 Pet. 1:24 (24–25).grass?

And Jer. 23:27 (27–39).forgettest the Lord thy maker, that hath Job 9:8.stretched forth the heavens, and laid the foundations of the earth, and hast feared continually every day, because of the fury of the IE Israel’s captors, typifying evil rulers who oppress the righteous; see v. 14. TG Oppression.oppressor, as if he were ready to destroy? And where is the fury of the oppressor?
The Isa. 52:2.captive exile hasteneth, that he may be loosed, and that he should not die in the pit, nor that his bread should fail.
But I am the Lord thy God, whose 1 Ne. 4:2.waves roared; the Lord of Hosts is my name.

And I have TG Israel, Mission of; Prophets, Mission of.put my words in thy mouth, and have covered thee in the shadow of mine hand, that I may plant the heavens and lay the foundations of the earth, and say unto Zion: Behold, thou art my 1 Kgs. 8:51; 2 Ne. 3:9; 29:14.people.
Awake, awake, stand up, O Jerusalem, which hast drunk at the hand of the Lord the Jer. 25:15.cup of his Luke 21:24 (22–24).fury—thou hast drunken the dregs of the cup of trembling wrung out—
And none to guide her among all the sons she hath brought forth; neither that taketh her by the hand, of all the sons she hath brought up.
These two Rev. 11:3 (3–12).sons are come unto thee, who shall be sorry for thee—thy desolation and destruction, and the famine and the sword—and by whom shall I comfort thee?
Thy sons have fainted, save these two; they lie at the head of all the streets; as a wild bull in a net, they are full of the fury of the Lord, the rebuke of thy God.
Therefore hear now this, thou afflicted, and 2 Ne. 27:4.drunken, and not with wine:

Thus saith thy Lord, the Lord and thy God Jer. 50:34.pleadeth the cause of his people; behold, I have taken out of thine hand the cup of trembling, the dregs of the cup of my fury; thou shalt no more drink it again.

But Joel 3:16 (9–16); Zech. 12:9 (2–3, 8–9); 14:12 (3, 12–15).I will put it into the hand of them that afflict thee; who have said to thy soul: Bow down, that we may go over—and thou hast laid thy body as the ground and as the street to them that went over.

Isa. 52:1 (1–2).Awake, awake, put on thy D&C 113:8 (7–8). TG Strength.strength, O TG Zion.Zion; put on thy beautiful garments, O Jerusalem, the holy city; for henceforth there shall Joel 3:17; Zech. 14:21.no more come into thee the uncircumcised and the unclean.
Shake thyself from the dust; arise, sit down, O Jerusalem; loose thyself from the D&C 113:10 (9–10).bands of thy neck, O captive daughter of Zion.

div.p: Jacob explains that the Jews will be gathered in all their lands of promise—The Atonement ransoms man from the Fall—The bodies of the dead will come forth from the grave, and their spirits from hell and from paradise—They will be judged—The Atonement saves from death, hell, the devil, and endless torment—The righteous are to be saved in the kingdom of God—Penalties for sins are set forth—The Holy One of Israel is the keeper of the gate. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, my beloved brethren, I have read these things that ye might know concerning the TG Abrahamic Covenant; Israel, Mission of.covenants of the Lord that he has covenanted with all the house of Israel—

That he has spoken unto the Jews, by the mouth of his holy prophets, even from the beginning down, from generation to generation, until the time comes that they shall be 2 Ne. 6:11 (10–15); 10:7 (5–9). TG Israel, Restoration of; Restoration of the Gospel.restored to the true church and fold of God; when they shall be TG Israel, Gathering of; Mission of Latter-day Saints.gathered home to the TG Lands of Inheritance.lands of their inheritance, and shall be established in all their lands of promise.

Behold, my beloved brethren, I speak unto you these things that ye may rejoice, and Ps. 24:7 (7–10).lift up your heads forever, because of the blessings which the Lord God shall bestow upon your children.

For I know that ye have searched much, many of you, to know of things to come; wherefore I know that ye know that our Gen. 6:3; Moses 8:17.flesh must waste away and die; nevertheless, in our Job 19:26; Alma 11:41 (41–45); 42:23; Hel. 14:15 (15–18); Morm. 9:13. TG Body, Sanctity of.bodies we shall see God.

Yea, I know that ye know that in the body he shall show himself unto those at Jerusalem, from whence we came; for it is expedient that it should be among them; for it behooveth the great TG Jesus Christ, Creator.Creator that he 2 Ne. 7:6 (4–9).suffereth himself to become Mark 10:44 (43–44).subject unto man in the flesh, and TG Jesus Christ, Death of; Jesus Christ, Mission of.die for John 12:32; 2 Ne. 26:24; 3 Ne. 27:14 (14–15).all men, that all men might become subject unto him.

For as Eccl. 8:8 (6–8).death hath passed upon all men, to fulfil the merciful TG Salvation, Plan of.plan of the great Creator, there must needs be a power of TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.resurrection, and the resurrection must needs come unto man by reason of the TG Fall of Man.fall; and the fall came by reason of TG Transgress.transgression; and because man became fallen they were 2 Ne. 2:5 (5–8); Alma 11:42 (40–45).cut off from the TG God, Presence of.presence of the Lord.

Wherefore, it must needs be an Alma 34:10.infinite Matt. 26:54 (52–56). TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement—save it should be an infinite atonement this corruption could not put on incorruption. Wherefore, the Mosiah 16:4 (4–7); Alma 11:45; 12:36; 42:6 (6, 9, 14).first judgment which came upon man must needs have Mosiah 15:19.remained to an endless duration. And if so, this flesh must have laid down to rot and to crumble to its mother earth, to rise no more.

O the Job 12:13 (7–25); Abr. 3:21. TG God, Wisdom of.wisdom of God, his TG God, Mercy of.mercy and TG Grace.grace! For behold, if the D&C 93:34.flesh should rise no more our spirits must become subject to that angel who Isa. 14:12; 2 Ne. 2:17; Moses 4:3 (3–4); Abr. 3:28 (27–28).fell from before the presence of the Eternal God, and became the TG Devil.devil, to rise no more.

And our spirits must have become 3 Ne. 29:7.like unto him, and we become devils, Jacob 3:11; Alma 5:25, 39; Moro. 9:13.angels to a 2 Cor. 11:14 (13–15).devil, to be Rev. 12:9 (7–9).shut out from the presence of our God, and to remain with the father of TG Lying.lies, in misery, like unto himself; yea, to that being who Gen. 3:13 (1–13); Mosiah 16:3; Ether 8:25; Moses 4:19 (5–19).beguiled our first parents, who Rev. 16:14 (13–14); Alma 30:53.transformeth himself nigh unto an D&C 129:8.angel of light, and TG Motivations.stirreth up the children of men unto TG Secret Combinations.secret combinations of murder and all manner of secret works of darkness.

O how great the Ex. 34:6 (5–7); 2 Ne. 4:17; D&C 86:11.goodness of our God, who prepareth a way for our TG Death, Power over.escape from the grasp of this awful monster; yea, that monster, Mosiah 16:8 (7–8); Alma 42:15 (6–15).death and TG Hell.hell, which I call the death of the body, and also the death of the spirit.

And because of the way of TG Deliver.deliverance of our God, the Holy One of Israel, this TG Death.death, of which I have spoken, which is the temporal, shall deliver up its dead; which death is the grave.

And this TG Death, Spiritual, First.death of which I have spoken, which is the spiritual death, shall deliver up its dead; which spiritual death is D&C 76:84.hell; wherefore, death and hell must D&C 138:18.deliver up their dead, and hell must deliver up its TG Bondage, Spiritual; Spirits in Prison.captive TG Spirits, Disembodied.spirits, and the grave must deliver up its captive TG Body, Sanctity of.bodies, and the bodies and the TG Spirit Body.spirits of men will be TG Resurrection.restored one to the other; and it is by the power of the resurrection of the Holy One of Israel.

O how great the TG Salvation, Plan of.plan of our God! For on the other hand, the D&C 138:19. TG Paradise.paradise of God must deliver up the spirits of the righteous, and the grave deliver up the body of the righteous; and the spirit and the body is Alma 11:43; 40:23; 41:4 (3–5); D&C 138:17.restored to itself again, and all men become incorruptible, and TG Immortality.immortal, and they are living souls, having a TG Perfection.perfect Eccl. 9:10; D&C 130:18.knowledge like unto us in the flesh, save it be that our knowledge shall be perfect.

Wherefore, we shall have a Mosiah 3:25.perfect Isa. 59:12; Alma 5:18; 11:43.knowledge of all our TG Guilt.guilt, and our TG Uncleanness.uncleanness, and our Gen. 2:25; Ex. 32:25; Moses 4:13 (13, 16–17).nakedness; and the righteous shall have a perfect knowledge of their enjoyment, and their TG Righteousness.righteousness, being Prov. 31:25.clothed with TG Purity.purity, yea, even with the D&C 109:76.robe of righteousness.

And it shall come to pass that when all men shall have passed from this first death unto life, insomuch as they have become immortal, they must appear before the TG Judgment, the Last.judgment-seat of the Holy One of Israel; and then cometh the Job 34:12; Ps. 19:9; 2 Ne. 30:9.judgment, and then must they be judged according to the holy judgment of God.

And assuredly, as the Lord liveth, for the Lord God hath spoken it, and it is his eternal 1 Kgs. 8:56; Ps. 33:11; D&C 1:38 (37–39); Moses 1:4.word, which cannot D&C 56:11.pass away, that they who are righteous shall be righteous still, and they who are Prov. 22:8. TG Filthiness.filthy shall be 1 Ne. 15:33 (33–35); Alma 7:21; Morm. 9:14; D&C 88:35.filthy still; wherefore, they who are filthy are the TG Devil.devil and his angels; and they shall go away into Mosiah 27:28.everlasting fire, prepared for them; and their TG Punish.torment is as a Rev. 19:20; 21:8; 2 Ne. 28:23; D&C 63:17; 76:36.lake of fire and brimstone, whose flame ascendeth up forever and ever and has no end.
O the greatness and the TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice of our God! For he executeth all his words, and they have gone forth out of his mouth, and his law must be Ezek. 24:14.fulfilled.

But, behold, the Ps. 5:12. TG Righteousness.righteous, the TG Saints.saints of the Holy One of Israel, they who have believed in the Holy One of Israel, they who have endured the Luke 14:27.crosses of the world, and despised the shame of it, they shall Col. 1:12; D&C 45:58 (57–58); 84:38 (33–38).inherit the TG Exaltation.kingdom of God, which was prepared for them Alma 13:3 (3, 5, 7–9).from the foundation of the world, and their Matt. 5:12 (11–12).joy shall be full TG Eternal Life.forever.

O the greatness of the mercy of our God, the Holy One of Israel! For he Job 23:7; D&C 108:8.delivereth his saints from that 1 Ne. 15:35.awful monster the devil, and death, and TG Hell.hell, and that lake of fire and brimstone, which is endless torment.

O how great the TG Holiness.holiness of our God! For he Mosiah 24:12; Alma 26:35. TG God, Foreknowledge of; God, Intelligence of; God, Omniscience of.knoweth Prov. 5:21; D&C 38:2 (1–2).all things, and there is not anything save he knows it.

And he cometh into the world that he may TG Salvation.save all men if they will hearken unto his voice; for behold, he suffereth the pains of all men, yea, the D&C 18:11; 19:18 (15–18). TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.pains of every living creature, both men, women, and children, who belong to the family of D&C 107:54 (53–56); 128:21. TG Adam.Adam.
And he suffereth this that the resurrection might pass upon all men, that all might stand before him at the great and judgment day.

And he commandeth all men that they must TG Repent.repent, and be Mosiah 26:22; D&C 76:52 (50–52); 84:74; 137:6. TG Baptism, Essential.baptized in his name, having perfect TG Baptism, Qualifications for; Faith.faith in the Holy One of Israel, or they cannot be saved in the kingdom of God.

And if they will not repent and believe in his TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name, and be baptized in his name, and TG Perseverance.endure to the end, they must be TG Damnation.damned; for the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel, has spoken it.

Wherefore, he has given a TG God, Law of.law; and where there is Rom. 4:15; 5:13; 2 Ne. 2:13.no John 15:22 (22–24); Acts 17:30; Rom. 5:13; James 4:17; Alma 42:17 (12–24). TG Accountability.law given there is no TG Punish.punishment; and where there is no punishment there is no condemnation; and where there is no condemnation the mercies of the Holy One of Israel have claim upon them, because of the atonement; for they are delivered by the power of him.

For the Lev. 4:20; Neh. 10:33; 2 Ne. 2:10. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement satisfieth the demands of his TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice upon all those who Mosiah 3:11; Alma 9:16 (15–16); 42:21.have not the Mosiah 15:24; D&C 137:7. TG Ignorance.law given to them, that they are TG Death, Power over.delivered from that awful monster, death and TG Spirits in Prison.hell, and the devil, and the lake of fire and brimstone, which is endless torment; and they are restored to that God who gave them Gen. 2:7; 6:17; Mosiah 2:21; D&C 77:2; 93:33; Abr. 5:7 (7–8).breath, which is the Holy One of Israel.

But wo unto him that has the Luke 12:47 (47–48). TG God, Law of.law given, yea, that has all the commandments of God, like unto us, and that TG Disobedience.transgresseth them, and that TG Idleness; Procrastination; Waste.wasteth the days of his TG Probation.probation, for awful is his state!

O that cunning Alma 28:13.plan of the evil one! O the Job 11:12 (11–12); Isa. 9:9 (9–10). TG Vanity.vainness, and the frailties, and the Eccl. 4:5; 10:12 (1–3, 12); 2 Ne. 19:17; D&C 35:7. TG Foolishness.foolishness of men! When they are Luke 16:15; 2 Ne. 26:20; 28:4 (4, 15). TG Education; Worldliness.learned they think they are Prov. 14:6; Jer. 8:8 (8–9); Rom. 1:22. TG Pride; Wisdom.wise, and they TG Walking in Darkness.hearken not unto the Prov. 15:22; Jacob 4:10; Alma 37:12. TG Counsel.counsel of God, for they set it aside, supposing they know of themselves, wherefore, their Prov. 23:4; Eccl. 8:17 (16–17); Ezek. 28:5 (4–5); D&C 76:9. TG Knowledge.wisdom is foolishness and it profiteth them not. And they shall perish.
But to be D&C 67:6. TG Learn.learned is good if they 2 Ne. 28:26. TG Submissiveness.hearken unto the Jacob 4:10. TG Counsel.counsels of God.

But wo unto the Jer. 17:11; Luke 12:34; D&C 56:16.rich, who are Matt. 19:23.rich as to the things of the TG World.world. For because they are rich they despise the TG Poor.poor, and they persecute the meek, and their TG Hardheartedness.hearts are upon their treasures; wherefore, their TG Treasure.treasure is their god. And behold, their Prov. 27:24.treasure shall perish with them also.
And wo unto the deaf that will not Ezek. 33:31 (30–33); Matt. 11:15; 13:14; Heb. 5:11 (11–14); Mosiah 26:28; D&C 1:14 (2, 11, 14); Moses 6:27.hear; for they shall perish.
Wo unto the TG Apathy; Spiritual Blindness.blind that will not see; for they shall perish also.
Wo unto the Rom. 2:29 (27–29).uncircumcised of heart, for a knowledge of their iniquities shall smite them at the last day.
Wo unto the Prov. 19:9. TG Gossip; Honesty; Lying.liar, for he shall be thrust down to TG Hell.hell.
Wo unto the Num. 35:16 (16–25).murderer who deliberately Deut. 19:11; 2 Sam. 12:9; Mosiah 13:21.killeth, for he shall TG Capital Punishment.die.
Wo unto them who commit 3 Ne. 12:27 (27–32). TG Chastity; Whore.whoredoms, for they shall be thrust down to hell.
Yea, wo unto those that Isa. 41:24 (21–24). TG Idolatry.worship idols, for the devil of all devils delighteth in them.
And, in fine, wo unto all those who die in their Ezek. 18:24. TG Sin.sins; for they shall Alma 40:11.return to God, and behold his face, and remain in their sins.

O, my beloved brethren, remember the awfulness in TG Transgress.transgressing against that Holy God, and also the awfulness of yielding to the enticings of that 2 Ne. 28:21 (20–22); 32:8; Mosiah 2:32; 4:14; Alma 30:42 (42, 53).cunning one. Remember, to be Rom. 8:6. TG Carnal Mind.carnally-minded is TG Death; Death, Spiritual, First; Hell.death, and to be Prov. 15:24. TG Spirituality.spiritually-minded is Prov. 11:19.life TG Eternal Life.eternal.

O, my beloved brethren, give ear to my words. Remember the greatness of the Holy One of Israel. Do not say that I have spoken hard things against you; for if ye do, ye will TG Reviling.revile against the Prov. 15:10; Mosiah 13:7. TG Truth.truth; for I have spoken the words of your Maker. I know that the words of truth are 1 Ne. 16:2; 2 Ne. 28:28; 33:5.hard against all TG Uncleanness.uncleanness; but the Prov. 28:1.righteous fear them not, for they love the truth and are not shaken.

O then, my beloved brethren, 1 Ne. 6:4; Jacob 1:7; Omni 1:26 (25–26); Alma 29:2; 3 Ne. 21:20; Morm. 9:27; Ether 5:5; Moro. 10:30 (30–32).come unto the Lord, the Holy One. Remember that his paths are righteous. Behold, the Ex. 33:13 (12–13); 2 Ne. 31:21 (17–21); Alma 37:46; D&C 132:22 (22, 25).way for man is Luke 13:24; 2 Ne. 33:9; Jacob 6:11; Hel. 3:29 (29–30).narrow, but it lieth in a straight course before him, and the keeper of the 2 Ne. 31:9 (9, 17–18); 3 Ne. 14:14 (13–14); D&C 22:4; 43:7; 137:2.gate is the Holy One of Israel; and he employeth no servant there; and there is none other way save it be by the gate; for he cannot be deceived, for the Lord God is his name.

And whoso TG Objectives; Study.knocketh, to him will he open; and the Matt. 11:25. TG Wisdom.wise, and the learned, and they that are rich, who are puffed up because of their TG Knowledge; Learn; Worldliness.learning, and their Ezek. 28:5 (4–5).wisdom, and their riches—yea, they are they whom he despiseth; and save they shall cast these things away, and consider themselves 1 Cor. 3:18 (18–21).fools before God, and come down in the depths of TG Humility; Teachable.humility, he will not open unto them.
But the things of the wise and the TG Prudence.prudent shall be 1 Cor. 2:14 (9–16).hid from them forever—yea, that happiness which is prepared for the saints.

O, my beloved brethren, remember my words. Behold, I take off my garments, and I shake them before you; I pray the God of my salvation that he view me with his Jacob 2:10.all-searching eye; wherefore, ye shall know at the last day, when all men shall be judged of their works, that the God of Israel did witness that I Jacob 1:19.shook your iniquities from my soul, and that I stand with brightness before him, and am Jacob 2:2 (2, 16); Mosiah 2:28; D&C 61:34.rid of your blood.
O, my beloved brethren, turn away from your sins; shake off the 2 Ne. 28:22; Alma 36:18.chains of him that would bind you fast; come unto that God who is the TG Rock.rock of your salvation.

Prepare your souls for that glorious day when TG God, Justice of.justice shall be administered unto the righteous, even the day of TG Judgment, the Last.judgment, that ye may not shrink with awful fear; that ye may not remember your awful Mosiah 3:25.guilt in perfectness, and be constrained to exclaim: Holy, holy are thy judgments, O Lord God Gen. 48:3; 1 Ne. 1:14; 3 Ne. 4:32; Moses 2:1.Almighty—but I know my guilt; I transgressed thy law, and my transgressions are mine; and the devil hath TG Apostasy of Individuals.obtained me, that I am a prey to his awful misery.

But behold, my brethren, is it expedient that I should awake you to an awful reality of these things? Would I harrow up your souls if your minds were pure? Would I be plain unto you according to the plainness of the truth if ye were freed from sin?

Behold, if ye were holy I would speak unto you of holiness; but as ye are not holy, and ye look upon me as a 2 Ne. 5:26. TG Teacher; Teaching.teacher, it must needs be expedient that I Deut. 33:10; 2 Chr. 15:3 (1–4); 17:9. TG Prophets, Mission of.teach you the consequences of sin.
Behold, my soul abhorreth sin, and my heart TG Desire; Motivations.delighteth in righteousness; and I will Ezra 3:11 (11–13); 1 Ne. 18:16; Alma 36:28.praise the holy name of my God.

Come, my brethren, every one that Isa. 44:3; 55:1 (1–2).thirsteth, come ye to the TG Living Water.waters; and he that hath no Alma 5:34; 42:27.money, come buy and eat; yea, come buy wine and milk without money and without price.

Wherefore, do not spend money for that which is of no worth, nor your Isa. 55:2. TG Work, Value of.labor for that which cannot Eccl. 1:3.satisfy. Hearken diligently unto me, and remember the words which I have spoken; and come unto the Holy One of Israel, and Prov. 13:25; Enos 1:4; 3 Ne. 12:6.feast upon that which perisheth not, neither can be corrupted, and let your soul delight in fatness.
Behold, my beloved brethren, remember the words of your God; pray unto him continually by day, and give TG Thanksgiving.thanks unto his holy name by night. Let your hearts Deut. 26:11.rejoice.

And behold how great the TG Covenants.covenants of the Lord, and how great his TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.condescensions unto the children of men; and because of his greatness, and his TG Grace.grace and TG Compassion; God, Mercy of.mercy, he has promised unto us that our seed shall not utterly be destroyed, according to the flesh, but that he would TG Protection, Divine.preserve them; and in future generations they shall become a righteous TG Vineyard of the Lord.branch unto the house of Israel.
And now, my brethren, I would speak unto you more; but on the morrow I will declare unto you the remainder of my words. Amen.

div.p: Jacob explains that the Jews will crucify their God—They will be scattered until they begin to believe in Him—America will be a land of liberty where no king will rule—Reconcile yourselves to God and gain salvation through His grace. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now I, Jacob, speak unto you again, my beloved brethren, concerning this righteous 1 Ne. 15:12 (12–20); 2 Ne. 3:5; Jacob 5:45 (43–45); Alma 46:24 (24–25).branch of which I have spoken.

For behold, the 1 Ne. 22:8 (8–12); 3 Ne. 5:23 (21–26); 21:7 (4–29). TG Promise.promises which we have obtained are promises unto us according to the flesh; wherefore, as it has been shown unto me that many of our children shall perish in the flesh because of TG Doubt.unbelief, nevertheless, God will be merciful unto many; and our children shall be TG Restoration of the Gospel.restored, that they may come to that which will give them the true knowledge of their Redeemer.

Wherefore, as I said unto you, it must needs be expedient that Christ—for in the last night the 2 Ne. 2:4; 11:3; 25:19; Jacob 7:5; Moro. 7:22.angel spake unto me that this should be his name—should TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.come among the TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jews, among those who are the more wicked part of the world; and they shall Luke 18:31; 1 Ne. 11:33; Mosiah 3:9 (9–10).crucify him—for thus it behooveth our God, and there is none other nation on earth that would Matt. 27:22; Luke 22:2; 23:23 (20–24).crucify their 1 Ne. 19:10 (7, 10); 2 Ne. 26:12.God.
For should the mighty TG Miracle.miracles be wrought among other nations they would repent, and know that he be their God.

But because of Matt. 27:20 (11–26); Luke 22:2; John 11:47 (47–53). TG Apostasy of Israel; Priestcraft.priestcrafts and iniquities, they at Jerusalem will TG Stiffnecked.stiffen their necks against him, that he be TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified.

Wherefore, because of their iniquities, destructions, famines, TG Plague.pestilences, and bloodshed shall come upon them; and they who shall not be destroyed shall be 1 Ne. 19:14 (13–14); 2 Ne. 25:15. TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.scattered among all nations.

But behold, thus saith the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord God: 2 Ne. 9:2 (1–2); 25:16 (16–17).When the day cometh that they shall believe in me, that I am Christ, then have I covenanted with their fathers that they shall be Gen. 49:10. TG Israel, Restoration of.restored in the flesh, upon the earth, unto the TG Lands of Inheritance.lands of their inheritance.

And it shall come to pass that they shall be TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered in from their long dispersion, from the Isa. 51:5; 1 Ne. 22:4; 2 Ne. 29:7; D&C 133:8.isles of the sea, and from the four parts of the earth; and the nations of the Gentiles shall be great in the eyes of me, saith God, in Isa. 11:14; 1 Ne. 21:22; 22:8.carrying them forth to the lands of their inheritance.

Isa. 49:22 (22–23); 2 Ne. 6:6.Yea, the kings of the Gentiles shall be nursing fathers unto them, and their queens shall become nursing mothers; wherefore, the 1 Ne. 22:8 (1–9); Alma 9:24; D&C 3:20.promises of the Lord are great unto the Gentiles, for he hath spoken it, and who can dispute?
But behold, this land, said God, shall be a land of thine inheritance, and the 2 Ne. 6:12; 3 Ne. 16:6 (4–7).Gentiles shall be blessed upon the land.
And this land shall be a land of TG Liberty.liberty unto the Gentiles, and there shall be no 2 Ne. 1:7; Mosiah 29:32.kings upon the land, who shall raise up unto the Gentiles.
And I will fortify this land 1 Ne. 13:19.against all other nations.
And he that 1 Ne. 22:14 (14, 19).fighteth against Zion shall Isa. 60:12.perish, saith God.

For he that raiseth up a TG Kings, Earthly.king against me shall perish, for I, the Lord, the Josh. 2:11; Ps. 44:4; Matt. 2:2; Alma 5:50; D&C 20:17; 38:21 (21–22); 128:22 (22–23); Moses 7:53.king of heaven, will be their king, and I will be a TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World.light unto them forever, that hear my words.

Wherefore, for this cause, that my TG Abrahamic Covenant; Covenants.covenants may be fulfilled which I have made unto the children of men, that I will do unto them while they are in the flesh, I must needs destroy the Lev. 19:26; Hel. 3:23; 7:25 (4–5, 21, 25). TG Secret Combinations.secret works of TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness, and of murders, and of abominations.

Wherefore, he that TG Protection, Divine.fighteth against TG Zion.Zion, both Jew and Gentile, both bond and free, both male and female, Isa. 41:11 (11–12).shall perish; for 1 Ne. 13:5.they are they who are the TG Devil, Church of; Whore.whore of all the earth; for 1 Ne. 14:10.they who are 1 Ne. 22:13 (13–23); 2 Ne. 6:15; 28:15 (15–32); 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–15); Ether 2:9 (8–11).not for me are Matt. 12:30.against me, saith our God.
For I will 1 Kgs. 8:56; D&C 1:38; 101:64.fulfil my TG Promise.promises which I have made unto the children of men, that I will do unto them while they are in the flesh—

Wherefore, my beloved brethren, thus saith our God: I will afflict thy seed by the hand of the Gentiles; nevertheless, I will 1 Ne. 13:31; 2 Ne. 4:7; Jacob 3:6 (5–9); Hel. 15:12 (10–17); Morm. 5:20 (20–21).soften the hearts of the Matt. 8:11 (11–12); 12:21; Luke 13:29 (28–30); Acts 10:45; D&C 45:9 (7–30).Gentiles, that they shall be like unto a father to them; wherefore, the Gentiles shall be Eph. 3:6 (1–7); 2 Ne. 33:9; 3 Ne. 21:14.blessed and Gal. 3:7 (7, 29); 1 Ne. 14:2; 3 Ne. 16:13; 21:6 (6, 22, 25); 30:2; Abr. 2:10 (9–11).numbered among the house of Israel.

Wherefore, I will 1 Ne. 13:15.consecrate this land unto thy seed, and them who shall be numbered among thy seed, forever, for the land of their inheritance; for it is a choice land, saith God unto me, above all other lands, wherefore I will have all men that dwell thereon that they shall worship me, saith God.

And now, my beloved brethren, seeing that our merciful God has given us so great knowledge concerning these things, let us remember him, and lay aside our sins, and not hang down our heads, for we are not cast off; nevertheless, we have been 1 Ne. 1:20 (18–20); 2:2 (1–4).driven out of the land of our inheritance; but we have been led to a 1 Ne. 2:20. TG Promised Lands.better land, for the Lord has made the sea our Ps. 8:8; 1 Ne. 18:8 (5–23).path, and we are upon an Isa. 11:11 (11–12); 42:4; 51:5; Ezek. 26:15 (3, 6–7, 15); 39:6; 2 Ne. 8:5.isle of the sea.

But great are the promises of the Lord unto them who are upon the Isa. 49:1; 1 Ne. 19:16; 21:1; 22:4.isles of the sea; wherefore as it says isles, there must needs be more than this, and they are inhabited also by our brethren.

For behold, the Lord God has 1 Ne. 22:4 (4–5); 2 Ne. 1:6. TG Israel, Scattering of; Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.led away from time to time from the house of Israel, according to his will and pleasure. And now behold, the Lord remembereth all them who have been broken off, wherefore he remembereth us also.

Therefore, TG Cheerful.cheer up your hearts, and remember that ye are TG Agency.free to 2 Ne. 2:16.act for yourselves—to Deut. 30:19 (15, 19).choose the way of everlasting death or the way of eternal life.

Wherefore, my beloved brethren, TG Reconciliation.reconcile yourselves to the TG God, Will of.will of God, and not to the will of the devil and the flesh; and remember, after ye are reconciled unto God, that it is only in and through the TG Grace.grace of God that ye are TG Salvation; Salvation, Plan of.saved.

Wherefore, may God TG Death, Power over; Resurrection.raise you from death by the power of the resurrection, and also from everlasting death by the power of the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement, that ye may be received into the TG Eternity.eternal kingdom of God, that ye may praise him through grace divine. Amen.

div.p: Jacob saw his Redeemer—The law of Moses typifies Christ and proves He will come. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, 2 Ne. 6:1 (1–10).Jacob spake many more things to my people at that time; nevertheless only these things have I caused to be 2 Ne. 31:1.written, for the things which I have written sufficeth me.

And now I, Nephi, write 2 Ne. 6:16 (16–18).more of the words of 3 Ne. 23:1.Isaiah, for my soul delighteth in his words. For I will liken his words unto my people, and I will send them forth unto all my children, for he verily 2 Ne. 16:1. TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Antemortal.saw my TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Redeemer, even as I have seen him.

And my brother, Jacob, also has 2 Ne. 2:3 (3–4); 10:3; Jacob 7:5. TG God, Privilege of Seeing.seen him as I have seen him; wherefore, I will send their words forth unto my children to prove unto them that my words are true. Wherefore, by the words of 2 Ne. 27:12 (12–14); Ether 5:3 (2–4); D&C 5:11 (11, 15).three, God hath said, I will establish my word. Nevertheless, God sendeth more TG Book of Mormon; Witness.witnesses, and he proveth all his words.

Behold, my soul delighteth in 2 Ne. 31:2.proving unto my people the truth of the Jacob 4:5; Jarom 1:11; Alma 25:16 (15–16); Ether 12:19 (18–19).coming of Christ; for, for this end hath the 2 Ne. 5:10.law of Moses been given; and all things which have been given of God from the beginning of the world, unto man, are the TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Law of Moses.typifying of him.

And also my soul delighteth in the TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenants of the Lord which he hath made to our fathers; yea, my soul delighteth in his TG Benevolence; Grace.grace, and in his justice, and power, and mercy in the great and eternal plan of TG Deliver; Jesus Christ, Atonement through.deliverance from death.
And my soul delighteth in proving unto my people that Mosiah 3:15.save Christ should come all men must perish.

For if there be 2 Ne. 2:13 (13–14).no Christ there be no God; and if there be no God we are not, for there could have been no Heb. 3:4 (3–4). TG Creation; God, Creator.creation. But there is a God, and TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.he is Christ, and he cometh in the fulness of his own time.

And now I write See the Latter-day Saint edition of the King James Version of the Bible for other notes and cross-references on these chapters from Isaiah.some of the words of Isaiah, that whoso of my people shall see these words may lift up their hearts and rejoice for all men. Now these are the words, and ye may liken them unto you and unto all men.

div.p: Isaiah sees the latter-day temple, gathering of Israel, and millennial judgment and peace—The proud and wicked will be brought low at the Second Coming—Compare Isaiah 2. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 2:1 (1–22).The word that Isaiah, the son of Amoz, saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:

And it shall come to pass in the last days, Comparison with the King James Bible in English shows that there are differences in more than half of the 433 verses of Isaiah quoted in the Book of Mormon, while about 200 verses have the same wording as KJV.when the TG Zion.mountain of the Lord’s 3 Ne. 24:1.house shall be established in the top of the Gen. 49:26; D&C 49:25; 109:61; 133:31 (29–31).mountains, and shall be exalted above the hills, and all nations shall flow unto it.

And many Zech. 8:22.people shall go and say, Come ye, and let us go up to the Joel 2:1; 2 Ne. 30:15 (12–18); D&C 133:13.mountain of the Lord, to the Ps. 122:1.house of the God of Jacob; and he will teach us of his ways, and we will TG Walking with God.walk in his paths; for out of Zion shall go forth the law, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.

And he shall 2 Ne. 21:3 (2–5, 9).judge among the nations, and shall rebuke many people: and they shall beat their swords into plow-shares, and their spears into pruning-hooks—nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
O house of Jacob, come ye and let us walk in the light of the Lord; yea, come, for ye have all 2 Ne. 28:14; Mosiah 14:6; Alma 5:37.gone astray, every one to his Isa. 53:6.wicked ways.

Therefore, O Lord, thou hast forsaken thy people, the house of Jacob, because they be replenished from the east, and hearken unto TG Sorcery.soothsayers like the Gen. 10:14.Philistines, and they please themselves in the children of strangers.
Their land also is full of silver and gold, neither is there any end of their TG Treasure.treasures; their land is also full of horses, neither is there any end of their chariots.
Their land is also full of Jer. 2:28. TG Idolatry.idols; they worship the work of their own hands, that which their own fingers have made.
And the mean man Ex. 34:8; Isa. 2:9.boweth IE unto God; he worships idols instead.not down, and the great man humbleth himself not, therefore, forgive him not.
O ye wicked ones, enter into the rock, and Amos 9:3; Rev. 6:15 (15–16); Alma 12:14.hide thee in the dust, for the fear of the Lord and the glory of his majesty shall smite thee.
And it shall come to pass that the 2 Ne. 15:15 (15–16).lofty looks of man shall be humbled, and the haughtiness of men shall be bowed down, and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day.

For the TG Day of the Lord.day of the Lord of Hosts soon cometh upon all nations, yea, upon every one; yea, upon the Job 40:11; Mal. 4:1; 2 Ne. 23:11; D&C 64:24.proud and lofty, and upon every one who is lifted up, and he shall be brought low.
Yea, and the day of the Lord shall come upon all the Isa. 37:24; Ezek. 31:3; Zech. 11:1 (1–2).cedars of Lebanon, for they are high and lifted up; and upon all the oaks of Bashan;
And upon all the Isa. 30:25.high mountains, and upon all the hills, and upon all the nations which are lifted up, and upon every people;
And upon every 3 Ne. 21:15 (15, 18).high tower, and upon every fenced wall;

And upon all the ships of the The Greek (Septuagint) has “ships of the sea.” The Hebrew has “ships of Tarshish.” The Book of Mormon has both, showing that the brass plates had lost neither phrase.sea, and upon all the ships of Tarshish, and upon all pleasant pictures.

And the loftiness of man shall be bowed down, and the TG Haughtiness.haughtiness of men shall be made low; and the Lord alone shall be exalted in IE the day of the Lord’s coming in glory; see vv. 17–21.that day.
And the idols he shall utterly abolish.

And they shall go into the holes of the rocks, and into the caves of the earth, for the fear of the Lord shall come upon them and the TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory of his majesty shall smite them, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
In that day a man shall cast his idols of silver, and his idols of gold, which he hath made for himself to worship, to the moles and to the bats;

To go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rocks, for the fear of the Lord shall come upon them and the majesty of his glory shall smite them, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
Cease ye from man, whose breath is in his nostrils; for wherein is he to be accounted of?

div.p: Judah and Jerusalem will be punished for their disobedience—The Lord pleads for and judges His people—The daughters of Zion are cursed and tormented for their worldliness—Compare Isaiah 3. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 3:1 (1–26).For behold, the Lord, the Lord of Hosts, doth take away from Jerusalem, and from Judah, the stay and the staff, the whole staff of bread, and the whole stay of water—
The 2 Kgs. 24:14.mighty man, and the man of 1 Chr. 28:3.war, the judge, and the prophet, and the TG Prudence.prudent, and the ancient;
The captain of fifty, and the honorable man, and the counselor, and the cunning artificer, and the eloquent orator.
And I will give children unto them to be their princes, and babes shall rule over them.

And the people shall be TG Oppression.oppressed, every one by another, and every one by his neighbor; the child shall behave himself TG Haughtiness.proudly against the ancient, and the base against the honorable.
When a man shall take hold of his brother of the house of his father, and shall say: Thou hast clothing, be thou our ruler, and let not this Isa. 3:6.ruin come under thy hand—
In that day shall he swear, saying: I will not be a healer; for in my house there is neither bread nor clothing; make me not a ruler of the people.

For Jerusalem is Isa. 1:7; Jer. 9:11; Ezek. 36:17 (16–20).ruined, and Judah is Lam. 1:3 (1–3).fallen, because their Ps. 52:2.tongues and their doings have been against the Lord, to TG Provoking.provoke the eyes of his glory.

The show of their countenance doth witness against them, and doth declare their TG Apostasy of Israel.sin to be even as Gen. 18:20 (20–21); 19:5, 24 (24–25); 2 Ne. 23:19. TG Homosexual Behavior.Sodom, and they cannot hide it. Wo unto their souls, for they have rewarded evil unto themselves!
Say unto the righteous that it is Deut. 12:28.well with them; for they shall Ps. 128:2.eat the fruit of their doings.
Wo unto the wicked, for they shall perish; for the reward of their hands shall be upon them!
And my people, children are their oppressors, and women rule over them. O my people, they who Isa. 9:16. TG Leadership.lead thee cause thee to err and destroy the way of thy paths.
The Lord standeth up to Micah 6:2.plead, and standeth to judge the people.

The Lord will enter into TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment with the ancients of his people and the princes thereof; for ye have eaten up the Isa. 5:7.vineyard and the spoil of the Ezek. 18:12; 2 Ne. 28:13 (12–13); Hel. 4:12 (11–13).poor in your houses.
What mean ye? Ye Micah 3:3 (2–3); 2 Ne. 26:20.beat my people to pieces, and grind the faces of the poor, saith the Lord God of Hosts.

Moreover, the Lord saith: Because the daughters of Zion are TG Haughtiness.haughty, and TG Walking in Darkness.walk with stretched-forth necks and wanton eyes, walking and mincing as they go, and making a tinkling with their feet—
Therefore the Lord will smite with a Deut. 28:27.scab the crown of the head of the daughters of Zion, and the Lord will Jer. 13:22; Nahum 3:5.discover their secret parts.
In that TG Day of the Lord.day the Lord will take away the bravery of their tinkling ornaments, and cauls, and round tires like the moon;
The chains and the bracelets, and the mufflers;
The bonnets, and the ornaments of the legs, and the headbands, and the tablets, and the ear-rings;
The rings, and nose jewels;
The changeable suits of apparel, and the mantles, and the wimples, and the crisping-pins;
The glasses, and the fine linen, and hoods, and the veils.

And it shall come to pass, instead of sweet smell there shall be stink; and instead of a girdle, a rent; and instead of well set hair, Isa. 22:12; Micah 1:16.baldness; and instead of a stomacher, a girding of sackcloth; 2 Ne. 14:4.burning instead of Lam. 1:6 (4–6).beauty.
Thy men shall fall by the sword and thy mighty in the war.
And her Jer. 14:2.gates shall lament and Lam. 1:4 (4–6).mourn; and she shall be desolate, and shall Lam. 2:10.sit upon the ground.

div.p: Zion and her daughters will be redeemed and cleansed in the millennial day—Compare Isaiah 4. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 4:1 (1–6).And in that day, seven women shall take hold of one man, saying: We will eat our own bread, and wear our own apparel; only let us be called by thy name to take away our TG Reproach.reproach.
In that day shall the Isa. 60:21; 61:3; 2 Ne. 3:5; Jacob 2:25.branch of the Lord be beautiful and glorious; the fruit of the earth excellent and comely to them that are escaped of Israel.
And it shall come to pass, they that are Matt. 13:43 (41–43).left in Zion and remain in Jerusalem shall be called holy, every one that is written among the living in Jerusalem—

When the Lord shall have 2 Ne. 13:24 (16–26). TG Wash.washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, and shall have purged the blood of Jerusalem from the midst thereof by the spirit of judgment and by the spirit of Mal. 3:2; 4:1.burning.

And the Isa. 60:20 (1–3, 19–21). TG God, Presence of.Lord will create upon every dwelling-place of mount Zion, and upon her assemblies, a Ex. 13:21; Zech. 2:5.cloud and smoke by day and the shining of a flaming fire by night; for upon all the glory of Zion shall be a defence.
And there shall be a tabernacle for a shadow in the daytime from the heat, and for a place of Isa. 25:4. TG Refuge.refuge, and a covert from storm and from rain.

div.p: The Lord’s vineyard (Israel) will become desolate, and His people will be scattered—Woes will come upon them in their apostate and scattered state—The Lord will lift an ensign and gather Israel—Compare Isaiah 5. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 5:1 (1–30).And then will I sing to my well-beloved a song of my beloved, touching his TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard. My well-beloved hath a vineyard in a very fruitful hill.

And he fenced it, and gathered out the stones thereof, and planted it with the choicest Jer. 2:21.vine, and built a tower in the midst of it, and also made a wine-press therein; and he looked that it should bring forth grapes, and it brought forth wild grapes.
And now, O inhabitants of Jerusalem, and men of Judah, judge, I pray you, betwixt me and my vineyard.
What could have been done more to my vineyard that I have not done in it? Wherefore, when I looked that it should bring forth grapes it brought forth wild grapes.

And now go to; I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard—I will Ps. 80:12 (8–15).take away the hedge thereof, and it shall be eaten up; and I will break down the wall thereof, and it shall be trodden down;

And I will lay it waste; it shall not be pruned nor digged; but there shall come up Isa. 7:23 (23–24); 32:13.briers and thorns; I will also command the clouds that they Lev. 26:4; Jer. 3:3.rain no rain upon it.

For the TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard of the Lord of Hosts is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah his pleasant plant; and he looked for Amos 5:24.judgment, and behold, TG Oppression.oppression; for righteousness, but behold, a cry.
Wo unto them that join Micah 2:2.house to house, till there can be no place, that they may be placed alone in the midst of the earth!
In mine ears, said the Lord of Hosts, of a truth many houses shall be desolate, and great and fair cities without inhabitant.
Yea, ten acres of vineyard shall yield one BD Weights and measures. See also Ezek. 45:11.bath, and the seed of a homer shall yield an ephah.
Wo unto them that rise up early in the morning, that they may Prov. 23:30 (29–32).follow strong drink, that continue until night, and TG Drunkenness; Word of Wisdom.wine inflame them!

And the harp, and the Amos 6:5 (5–6).viol, the tabret, and pipe, and wine are in their feasts; but they Ps. 28:5. TG Rebellion.regard not the work of the Lord, neither consider the operation of his hands.

Therefore, my people are gone into Lam. 1:3 (1–3).captivity, because they have no Isa. 1:3; Hosea 4:6. TG Knowledge.knowledge; and their honorable men are famished, and their multitude dried up with thirst.
Therefore, hell hath enlarged herself, and opened her mouth without measure; and their glory, and their multitude, and their pomp, and he that rejoiceth, shall descend into it.
And the mean man shall be Isa. 2:17 (11, 17).brought down, and the 2 Ne. 12:11.mighty man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the TG Haughtiness.lofty shall be humbled.
But the Lord of Hosts shall be exalted in TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment, and God that is holy shall be sanctified in righteousness.
Then shall the lambs feed after their manner, and the waste places of the Isa. 10:16.fat ones shall strangers eat.
Wo unto them that draw iniquity with cords of TG Vanity.vanity, and sin as it were with a cart rope;
That say: Let him Jer. 17:15.make speed, TG Haste.hasten his work, that we may TG Sign Seekers.see it; and let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw nigh and come, that we may know it.
Wo unto them that D&C 64:16; 121:16.call Moro. 7:14 (14, 18).evil good, and good evil, that put 1 Jn. 1:6.darkness for light, and light for darkness, that put bitter for sweet, and sweet for bitter!
Wo unto the Prov. 3:7 (5–7); 2 Ne. 28:15.wise in their own eyes and TG Prudence.prudent in their own sight!
Wo unto the mighty to drink Prov. 31:4 (3–9).wine, and men of strength to mingle strong drink;
Who justify the wicked for TG Bribe.reward, and take away the righteousness of the righteous from him!

Therefore, as the Obad. 1:18; 2 Ne. 20:17; 3 Ne. 20:16.fire devoureth the Joel 2:5; 1 Ne. 22:15 (15, 23); 2 Ne. 26:6 (4, 6); D&C 64:24 (23–24); 133:64.stubble, and the flame consumeth the Luke 3:17; Mosiah 7:30 (29–31).chaff, their Job 18:16 (16–21).root shall be rottenness, and their blossoms shall go up as dust; because they have cast away the law of the Lord of Hosts, and 2 Sam. 12:9 (7–9).despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.

Therefore, is the Deut. 32:21; D&C 63:32; Moses 6:27.anger of the Lord kindled against his people, and he hath stretched forth his hand against them, and hath smitten them; and the hills did tremble, and their carcasses were torn in the midst of the streets. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

And he will lift up an TG Ensign.ensign to the TG Nations.nations from far, and will hiss unto them from the 2 Ne. 29:2.end of the earth; and behold, they shall TG Israel, Gathering of.come with speed swiftly; none shall be weary nor stumble among them.
None shall slumber nor sleep; neither shall the girdle of their loins be loosed, nor the latchet of their shoes be broken;
Whose arrows shall be sharp, and all their bows bent, and their horses’ hoofs shall be counted like flint, and their wheels like a whirlwind, their roaring like a lion.
They shall roar like young 3 Ne. 21:12 (12–13).lions; yea, they shall roar, and lay hold of the prey, and shall carry away safe, and none shall deliver.

And in that TG Day of the Lord.day they shall roar against them like the roaring of the sea; and if they look unto the land, behold, darkness and sorrow, and the light is darkened in the heavens thereof.

div.p: Isaiah sees the Lord—Isaiah’s sins are forgiven—He is called to prophesy—He prophesies of the rejection by the Jews of Christ’s teachings—A remnant will return—Compare Isaiah 6. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 6:1 (1–13).In the IE about 750 B.C.year that king Uzziah died, I John 12:41; 2 Ne. 11:2.saw also the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up, and his train filled the temple.
Above it stood the TG Cherubim. BD Seraphim.seraphim; each one had six wings; with twain he covered his face, and with twain he covered his feet, and with twain he did fly.
And one cried unto another, and said: Holy, holy, holy, is the Lord of Hosts; the whole earth is full of his Ps. 72:19 (19–20). TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory.
And the posts of the door moved at the voice of him that cried, and the house was filled with smoke.

Then said I: Wo is unto me! for I am undone; because I am a man of unclean lips; and I dwell in the midst of a people of unclean lips; for mine eyes have TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Antemortal.seen the King, the Lord of Hosts.
Then flew one of the seraphim unto me, having a live coal in his hand, which he had taken with the tongs from off the altar;
And he laid it upon my mouth, and said: Lo, this has touched thy lips; and thine TG Cleanse; Remission of Sins.iniquity is taken away, and thy sin purged.
Also I heard the voice of the Lord, saying: TG Called of God.Whom shall I send, and who will go for us? Then I said: Here am I; send me.
And he said: Go and tell this people—Hear ye indeed, but they understood not; and see ye indeed, but they perceived not.

Make the heart of this people fat, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes—lest they see with their eyes, and Matt. 13:14 (14–15); John 12:40; Acts 28:26–27; Rom. 11:8.hear with their ears, and understand with their Prov. 2:2.heart, and be converted and be healed.
Then said I: Lord, how long? And he said: Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land be utterly desolate;
And the Lord have 2 Kgs. 17:18 (18, 20); 25:21.removed men far away, for there shall be a great forsaking in the midst of the land.

But yet there shall be a tenth, and they shall return, and shall be eaten, as a teil tree, and as an oak whose substance is in them when they cast their leaves; so the Ezra 9:2.holy seed shall be the substance thereof.

div.p: Ephraim and Syria wage war against Judah—Christ will be born of a virgin—Compare Isaiah 7. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 7:1 (1–25).And it came to pass in the days of 2 Kgs. 16:5; 2 Chr. 28:5 (5–6).Ahaz the son of 2 Kgs. 15:32.Jotham, the son of Uzziah, king of Judah, that 2 Kgs. 15:37 (36–38).Rezin, king of Syria, and 2 Kgs. 15:25.Pekah the son of Remaliah, king of Israel, went up toward Jerusalem to war against it, but could not prevail against it.
And it was told the house of David, saying: Syria is confederate with Ephraim. And his heart was moved, and the heart of his people, as the trees of the wood are moved with the wind.
Then said the Lord unto Isaiah: Go forth now to meet Ahaz, thou and Shearjashub thy son, at the end of the 2 Kgs. 18:17; Isa. 36:2.conduit of the upper pool in the highway of the fuller’s field;
And say unto him: Take heed, and be quiet; fear not, neither be faint-hearted for the two tails of these smoking firebrands, for the fierce anger of Rezin with Syria, and of the son of Remaliah.
Because Syria, Ephraim, and the son of Remaliah, have taken evil counsel against thee, saying:
Let us go up against Judah and vex it, and let us make a breach therein for us, and set a king in the midst of it, yea, the son of Tabeal.
Thus saith the Lord God: Prov. 21:30; Isa. 8:10 (9–10).It shall not stand, neither shall it come to pass.
For the head of Syria is Damascus, and the head of Damascus, Rezin; and within threescore and five years shall Ephraim be TG Israel, Scattering of.broken that it be not a people.
And the head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is Remaliah’s son. If ye will 2 Chr. 20:20. TG Unbelief.not believe surely ye shall not be established.
Moreover, the Lord spake again unto Ahaz, saying:
Ask thee a Judg. 6:39 (36–40). TG Signs.sign of the Lord thy God; ask it either in the depths, or in the heights above.
But Ahaz said: I will not ask, neither will I IE test, try, or prove.tempt the Lord.
And he said: Hear ye now, O house of David; is it a small thing for you to weary men, but will ye weary my God also?
Therefore, the Lord himself shall give you a sign—Behold, a Isa. 7:14.virgin shall conceive, and shall bear a son, and shall call his name Isa. 8:8; 2 Ne. 18:8, 10.Immanuel.
Butter and 2 Sam. 17:29.honey shall he eat, that he may know to refuse the evil and to choose the good.
For Isa. 8:4; 2 Ne. 18:4.before the child shall know to refuse the evil and choose the good, the land that thou abhorrest shall be forsaken of 2 Kgs. 15:30; 16:9.both her kings.

The Lord shall 2 Chr. 28:19 (19–21).bring upon thee, and upon thy people, and upon thy father’s house, days that have not come from the day that 1 Kgs. 12:19 (16–19).Ephraim departed from Judah, the king of Assyria.
And it shall come to pass in that day that the Lord shall hiss for the fly that is in the uttermost part of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.
And they shall come, and shall rest all of them in the desolate valleys, and in the holes of the rocks, and upon all thorns, and upon all bushes.

In the same day shall the Lord shave with a 2 Kgs. 16:7 (7–8); 2 Chr. 28:20 (20–21).razor that is hired, by them beyond the river, by the king of Assyria, the head, and the hair of the feet; and it shall also consume the beard.
And it shall come to pass in that day, a man shall nourish a young cow and two sheep;
And it shall come to pass, for the abundance of milk they shall give he shall eat butter; for butter and honey shall every one eat that is left in the land.
And it shall come to pass in that day, every place shall be, where there were a thousand vines at a thousand silverlings, which shall be for briers and thorns.
With arrows and with bows shall men come thither, because all the land shall become briers and thorns.
And all hills that shall be digged with the mattock, there shall not come thither the fear of briers and thorns; but it shall be for the sending forth of oxen, and the treading of lesser cattle.

div.p: Christ will be as a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense—Seek the Lord, not peeping wizards—Turn to the law and to the testimony for guidance—Compare Isaiah 8. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Moreover, the word of the Lord said unto me: Take thee a great Isa. 8:1 (1–22).roll, and write in it with a man’s pen, concerning HEB To speed to the spoil, he hasteneth the prey.Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
And I took unto me faithful TG Witness.witnesses to record, Uriah the priest, and Zechariah the son of Jeberechiah.
And I went unto the prophetess; and she conceived and bare a son. Then said the Lord to me: 2 Ne. 18:18.Call his name, Maher-shalal-hash-baz.

For behold, 2 Ne. 17:16.the child shall Isa. 8:4.not have knowledge to cry, My father, and my mother, before the riches of Damascus and the 2 Kgs. 15:29 (29–30).spoil of 2 Kgs. 17:6.Samaria shall be taken away before the king of 2 Kgs. 16:7 (7–18); 2 Ne. 20:12. TG Israel, Scattering of.Assyria.
The Lord spake also unto me again, saying:
Forasmuch as this people refuseth the waters of Neh. 3:15; John 9:7.Shiloah that go softly, and rejoice in Isa. 7:1 (1–6).Rezin and Remaliah’s son;

Now therefore, behold, the Lord bringeth up upon them the waters of the river, strong and many, even the king of Isa. 10:12.Assyria and all his glory; and he shall come up over all his channels, and go over all his banks.
And he shall pass through Judah; he shall overflow and go over, he shall Isa. 30:28.reach even to the neck; and the stretching out of his wings shall fill the breadth of thy land, O Immanuel.

Joel 3:9 (9–14).Associate yourselves, O ye people, and ye shall be broken in pieces; and give ear all ye of far countries; gird yourselves, and ye shall be broken in pieces; gird yourselves, and ye shall be broken in pieces.
Take counsel together, and it shall come to naught; speak the word, and it shall not stand; for God is with us.
For the Lord spake thus to me with a strong hand, and instructed me that I should not walk in the way of this people, saying:
Say ye not, A confederacy, to all to whom this people shall say, A Isa. 31:1 (1–3).confederacy; neither fear ye their fear, nor be afraid.
Sanctify the Lord of Hosts himself, and let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.

And he shall be for a sanctuary; but for a Rom. 9:33 (32–33). TG Cornerstone; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.stone of Isa. 8:14 (13–15); Luke 2:34; 1 Pet. 2:8 (4–8); Jacob 4:15.stumbling, and for a TG Rock.rock of Luke 7:23.offense to both the houses of Israel, for a gin and a Mosiah 7:29.snare to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
And many among them shall Matt. 21:44.stumble and fall, and be broken, and be snared, and be taken.
Dan. 12:9.Bind up the testimony, seal the law among my disciples.
And I will wait upon the Lord, that Isa. 54:8.hideth his face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for him.
Behold, I and the children whom the Lord hath given me are for 2 Ne. 18:3 (1–3).signs and for wonders in Israel from the Lord of Hosts, which dwelleth in Mount Zion.

And when they shall say unto you: Seek unto them that have Moro. 10:30. TG Sorcery.familiar spirits, and unto Lev. 20:6.wizards that peep and mutter—1 Sam. 28:11 (8–20).should not a people seek unto their God for the living to hear from the dead?
To the Luke 16:29 (29–31).law and to the testimony; and if they speak not according to this word, it is because there is no light in them.
And they shall pass through it hardly bestead and hungry; and it shall come to pass that when they shall be hungry, they shall fret themselves, and curse their king and their God, and look upward.
And they shall look unto the earth and behold trouble, and Isa. 5:30.darkness, dimness of anguish, and shall be driven to darkness.

div.p: Isaiah speaks messianically—The people in darkness will see a great light—Unto us a child is born—He will be the Prince of Peace and will reign on David’s throne—Compare Isaiah 9. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 9:1 (1–21).Nevertheless, the dimness shall not be such as was in her vexation, when at first he lightly afflicted the Matt. 4:15 (15–16).land of Josh. 19:10 (10–16).Zebulun, and the land of Josh. 19:33 (32–39).Naphtali, and afterwards did more grievously afflict by the way of the Red Sea beyond Jordan in Galilee of the nations.
The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light; they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined.
Thou hast multiplied the nation, and Isa. 9:3.increased the joy—they joy before thee according to the joy in harvest, and as men rejoice when they divide the spoil.
For thou hast broken the yoke of IE Israel, the nation mentioned in v. 3.his burden, and the staff of his shoulder, the rod of his TG Oppression.oppressor.
For every battle of the warrior is with confused noise, and garments rolled in blood; but Mal. 4:1.this shall be with burning and fuel of fire.

For unto us a Isa. 7:14; Luke 2:11.child is born, unto us a son is given; and the Matt. 28:18.government shall be upon his shoulder; and his name shall be called, Wonderful, Counselor, The Titus 2:13; Mosiah 7:27. TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah; Jesus Christ, Power of.Mighty God, The 2 Ne. 26:12; Mosiah 3:5; Alma 11:39 (38–39, 44); Moro. 7:22; 8:18.Everlasting Father, The Prince of Micah 5:5; D&C 27:16; 111:8.Peace.

Of the increase of TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.government and peace Dan. 2:44.there is no end, upon the throne of Ezek. 37:24.David, and upon his kingdom to order it, and to establish it with judgment and with justice from henceforth, even forever. The zeal of the Lord of Hosts will perform this.
The Lord sent his word unto Jacob and it hath lighted upon Israel.
And all the people shall know, even Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria, that say in the pride and stoutness of heart:
The bricks are fallen down, but we will build with hewn 1 Kgs. 5:17.stones; the sycamores are cut down, but we will change them into 1 Kgs. 5:6.cedars.
Therefore the Lord shall set up the adversaries of 2 Kgs. 16:9 (7–9).Rezin against him, and join his enemies together;

The Syrians before and the Philistines behind; and they shall 2 Kgs. 17:6 (1–18).devour Israel with open mouth. For all this his Isa. 5:25; 10:4; Jer. 4:8.anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
For the people turneth not unto Amos 4:10 (6–12).him that smiteth them, neither do they seek the Lord of Hosts.
Therefore will the Lord cut off from Israel head and tail, branch and rush Isa. 10:17.in one day.
The Isa. 9:15.ancient, he is the head; and the prophet that teacheth lies, he is the tail.
For the Isa. 1:23. TG Leadership.leaders of this people cause them to err; and they that are TG Trust Not in the Arm of Flesh.led of them are destroyed.

Therefore the Lord shall have no joy in their young men, neither shall have TG Mercy.mercy on their fatherless and TG Widows.widows; for Micah 7:2 (2–3).every one of them is a hypocrite and an Prov. 1:16; D&C 64:16.evildoer, and every mouth speaketh Eccl. 10:12 (1–3, 12); 2 Ne. 9:28 (28–29); D&C 35:7.folly. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his 2 Ne. 28:32; Jacob 5:47; 6:4.hand is stretched out still.
For Mal. 4:1.wickedness burneth as the fire; it shall devour the briers and thorns, and shall kindle in the thickets of the forests, and they shall mount up like the lifting up of smoke.
Through the wrath of the Lord of Hosts is the Isa. 8:22.land darkened, and the people shall be as the fuel of the fire; Micah 7:2 (2–6).no man shall spare his brother.

And he Lev. 26:26 (26, 29).shall snatch on the right hand and be hungry; and he shall Deut. 28:53 (53–57).eat on the left hand and they shall not be satisfied; they shall eat every man the flesh of his own arm—

Manasseh, TG Israel, Joseph, People of.Ephraim; and Ephraim, Manasseh; they together shall be against TG Israel, Judah, People of.Judah. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

div.p: The destruction of Assyria is a type of the destruction of the wicked at the Second Coming—Few people will be left after the Lord comes again—The remnant of Jacob will return in that day—Compare Isaiah 10. About 559–545 B.C.

chapter.verse: Isa. 10:1 (1–34).Wo unto them that decree TG Injustice.unrighteous decrees, and that write grievousness which they have prescribed;
To turn away the needy from judgment, and to take away the right from the Amos 4:1.poor of my people, that TG Widows.widows may be their prey, and that they may rob the fatherless!
And what will ye do in the day of visitation, and in the desolation which shall come from far? to whom will ye flee for help? and where will ye leave your glory?
Without me they shall bow down under the prisoners, and they shall fall under the slain. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
O Assyrian, the rod of mine anger, and the staff in their hand is Isa. 10:5.their indignation.

I will send him IE against Israel. TG Hypocrisy.against a hypocritical nation, and against the people of my wrath will I give him a charge to take the spoil, and to take the prey, and to tread them down like the mire of the streets.
Howbeit he meaneth not so, neither doth his heart think so; but in his heart it is to destroy and cut off nations not a few.
For he saith: Are not my 2 Kgs. 18:33 (33–35); 19:10 (10–13).princes altogether kings?
Is not Amos 6:2 (1–2).Calno as 2 Chr. 35:20.Carchemish? Is not Hamath as Arpad? Is not Samaria as 2 Kgs. 16:9.Damascus?
As IE the king of Assyria’s hand (vv. 10–11).my hand hath founded the kingdoms of the idols, and whose graven images did excel them of Jerusalem and of Samaria;
Shall I not, as I have done unto Samaria and her Ezek. 36:18 (16–20).idols, so do to Jerusalem and to her idols?

Wherefore it shall come to pass that when the Lord hath performed his whole work upon Mount Zion and upon Jerusalem, I will punish the fruit of the stout heart of the king of 2 Kgs. 16:7 (7–18); Zeph. 2:13; 2 Ne. 18:4 (4–7).Assyria, and the glory of his high looks.

For IE the king of Assyria (vv. 13–14).he saith: By the strength of Isa. 37:24 (24–38).my hand and by my wisdom I have done these things; for I am prudent; and I have moved the borders of the people, and have robbed their treasures, and I have put down the inhabitants like a valiant man;

And my hand hath found as a nest the riches of the people; and as one gathereth eggs that are left have I gathered all the earth; and there was none that moved the wing, or opened the mouth, or peeped.

Shall the IE Can the king prosper against God?ax boast itself against him that heweth therewith? Shall the saw magnify itself against him that shaketh it? As if the rod should shake itself against them that lift it up, or as if the staff should lift up itself as if it were no wood!
Therefore shall the Lord, the Lord of Hosts, send among his fat ones, leanness; and under his glory he shall kindle a burning like the burning of a fire.
And the light of Israel shall be for a Obad. 1:18; 2 Ne. 15:24; 3 Ne. 20:16.fire, and his Holy One for a flame, and shall burn and shall devour his thorns and his briers in one day;
And shall consume the glory of his forest, and of his fruitful field, both soul and body; and they shall be as when a standard-bearer fainteth.
And the IE the remnants of the army of Assyria.rest of the trees of his forest shall be few, that a child may write them.

And it shall come to pass in that day, that the remnant of Israel, and such as are escaped of the Amos 9:8 (8–9); 2 Ne. 6:11 (10–11).house of Jacob, shall no more again IE depend upon. 2 Kgs. 16:8 (7–9); 2 Chr. 28:21 (20–21).stay upon him that smote them, but shall stay upon the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, in truth.
The Isa. 11:11. TG Israel, Remnant of.remnant shall return, yea, even the remnant of Jacob, unto the mighty God.

For though thy people Gen. 22:17; Rom. 9:27.Israel be as the sand of the sea, yet a remnant of them shall TG Israel, Gathering of.return; the Isa. 28:22. TG World, End of.consumption decreed shall overflow with righteousness.
