TG Hardheartedness.harden their TG Spiritual Blindness.hearts against the TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.Holy Spirit, that it hath no place in them; wherefore, they cast many things away which are written and esteem them as things of naught.

But I, Nephi, have written what I have written, and I esteem it as of great TG Scriptures, Value of.worth, and especially unto my people. For I Gen. 20:7; Num. 21:7; 1 Sam. 7:5; Jer. 42:4; Enos 1:9 (9–12); W of M 1:8; Moro. 9:22.pray continually for them by day, and mine Ps. 6:6; Jer. 13:17; Acts 20:19.eyes water my pillow by night, because of them; and I cry unto my God in faith, and I know that he will hear my cry.

And I know that the Lord God will consecrate my prayers for the gain of my people. And the words which I have written in weakness will be made strong unto them; for it Ether 8:26; Moro. 7:13 (12–17). TG Motivations.persuadeth them to do good; it maketh known unto them of their fathers; and it speaketh of Jesus, and persuadeth them to believe in him, and to endure to the end, which is life TG Eternal Life.eternal.

And it speaketh 1 Ne. 16:2 (1–3); 2 Ne. 9:40; 28:28; Enos 1:23; W of M 1:17.harshly against sin, according to the 2 Ne. 25:4; Jacob 2:11; 4:13.plainness of the truth; wherefore, no man will be angry at the words which I have written save he shall be of the spirit of the devil.
I Ps. 44:8 (4–8); D&C 76:61.glory in 2 Ne. 31:3.plainness; I glory in truth; I glory in my Jesus, for he hath Enos 1:27.redeemed my soul from hell.
I have TG Charity.charity for my people, and great faith in Christ that I shall meet many souls spotless at his judgment-seat.
I have charity for the TG Israel, Judah, People of. BD Judah, Kingdom of.Jew—I say Jew, because I mean them from whence I came.

I also have charity for the Gentiles. But behold, for none of Eph. 3:6 (1–7); 2 Ne. 10:18; 3 Ne. 21:14.these can I hope except they shall be TG Reconciliation.reconciled unto Christ, and enter into the 2 Ne. 9:41; Hel. 3:29 (29–30).narrow Matt. 7:14.gate, and TG Walking with God.walk in the D&C 132:22.strait path which leads to life, and continue in the path until the end of the day of TG Probation.probation.

And now, my beloved brethren, and also TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jew, and all ye ends of the earth, hearken unto these words and TG Believe.believe in Christ; and if ye believe not in these words believe in Christ. And if ye shall John 8:47.believe in Christ ye will believe in these TG Book of Mormon.words, for they are the Isa. 51:16; Moro. 10:27 (27–29); D&C 1:24.words of Christ, and he hath given them unto me; and they 1 Kgs. 8:36; 2 Ne. 25:28.teach all men that they should do good.

And if they are not the words of Christ, judge ye—for Christ will show unto you, with Ether 5:4 (4–6); Moro. 7:35.power and great TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory, that they are his words, at the last day; and you and I shall stand face to face before his bar; and ye shall know that I have been commanded of him to write these things, notwithstanding my weakness.
And I pray the Father in the name of Christ that many of us, if not all, may be saved in his TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven; Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom at that great and last day.

And now, my beloved brethren, all those who are of the house of Israel, and all ye ends of the earth, I speak unto you as the voice of one Isa. 29:4; 2 Ne. 27:13; Morm. 8:26.crying from the dust: Farewell until that great day shall come.

And you that will not partake of the goodness of God, and respect the words of the TG Bible.Jews, and also my TG Book of Mormon.words, and the words which shall proceed forth out of the mouth of the Lamb of God, behold, I bid you an everlasting farewell, for these words shall 2 Ne. 29:11; W of M 1:11.condemn you at the last day.

For what I seal on earth, shall be brought against you at the 2 Ne. 25:22; 3 Ne. 27:25 (23–27); Ether 4:10 (8–10).judgment bar; for thus hath the Lord commanded me, and I must TG Obedience.obey. Amen.

div.p: The words of his preaching unto his brethren. He confoundeth a man who seeketh to overthrow the doctrine of Christ. A few words concerning the history of the people of Nephi.
Jacob and Joseph seek to persuade men to believe in Christ and keep His commandments—Nephi dies—Wickedness prevails among the Nephites. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: For behold, it came to pass that fifty and five years had passed away from the time that Lehi left Jerusalem; wherefore, Nephi gave me, Jacob, a Jacob 7:27; Jarom 1:15 (1–2, 15); Omni 1:3.commandment concerning the 2 Ne. 5:31 (28–33); Jacob 3:13 (13–14).small plates, upon which these things are engraven.

And he gave me, Jacob, a commandment that I should TG Scribe; Scriptures, Writing of.write upon 1 Ne. 6:6.these plates a few of the things which I considered to be most precious; that I should not touch, save it were lightly, concerning the history of this people which are called the people of Nephi.

For he said that the history of his people should be engraven upon his 2 Ne. 5:33 (29–33); Jacob 3:13 (13–14).other plates, and that I should TG Scriptures, Preservation of.preserve these plates and hand them down unto my seed, from generation to generation.

And if there were preaching which was TG Sacred.sacred, or revelation which was great, or prophesying, that I should engraven the IE the dominant, important items.heads of them upon these plates, and touch upon them as much as it were possible, for Christ’s sake, and for the sake of our people.

For because of faith and great anxiety, it truly had been made manifest unto us concerning our people, what things should See 1 Ne. 12–15. See also 1 Ne. 22:7 (7–8); 2 Ne. 1:9 (5–10); 4:7 (7–11); 30:3 (3–6).happen unto them.
And we also had many revelations, and the spirit of much prophecy; wherefore, we knew of 1 Ne. 10:4 (4–11); 19:8 (8–14).Christ and his kingdom, which should come.

Wherefore we labored diligently among our people, that we might persuade them to 2 Ne. 9:41 (41, 45, 51); Omni 1:26 (25–26); Moro. 10:32.come unto Christ, and partake of the goodness of God, that they might enter into his TG Rest.rest, lest by any means he should swear in his wrath they should not Num. 14:23; Deut. 1:35 (35–37); D&C 84:24 (23–25).enter in, as in the Heb. 3:8.provocation in the days of temptation while the children of Israel were in the Num. 26:65; 1 Ne. 17:31 (23–31).wilderness.

Wherefore, we would to God that we could persuade all men TG Loyalty; Rebellion.not to rebel against God, to Num. 14:11 (11–12); 1 Kgs. 16:33; 1 Ne. 17:30 (23–31); Alma 12:37 (36–37); Hel. 7:18.provoke him to anger, but that all men would believe in Christ, and view his death, and suffer his Luke 14:27.cross and bear the shame of the world; wherefore, I, Jacob, take it upon me to fulfil the commandment of my brother Nephi.

Now Nephi began to be old, and he saw that he must soon Gen. 47:29 (28–29); 2 Ne. 1:14.die; wherefore, he TG Anointing.anointed a man to be a king and a ruler over his people now, according to the reigns of the 2 Ne. 6:2; Jarom 1:7 (7, 14).kings.

The people having loved Nephi exceedingly, he having been a great protector for them, having wielded the 1 Ne. 4:9; 2 Ne. 5:14; W of M 1:13; Mosiah 1:16; D&C 17:1.sword of Laban in their defence, and having labored in all his days for their welfare—

Wherefore, the people were desirous to retain in remembrance his name. And whoso should reign in his stead were called by the people, second Nephi, third Nephi, and so forth, according to the reigns of the kings; and thus they were called by the people, let them be of whatever name they would.
And it came to pass that Nephi died.

Now the people which were not Enos 1:13; Alma 23:17; D&C 3:18.Lamanites were Nephites; nevertheless, they were called Nephites, Jacobites, Josephites, 1 Ne. 4:35; Alma 54:23; 4 Ne. 1:36 (36–37).Zoramites, Lamanites, Lemuelites, and Ishmaelites.

But I, Jacob, shall not hereafter distinguish W of M 1:16.them by these names, but I shall Mosiah 25:12; Alma 2:11.call them Lamanites that seek to destroy the people of Nephi, and those who are friendly to Nephi I shall call 2 Ne. 4:11.Nephites, or the 2 Ne. 5:9.people of Nephi, according to the reigns of the kings.

And now it came to pass that the people of Nephi, under the reign of the second king, began to grow hard in their hearts, and indulge themselves somewhat in wicked practices, such as like unto David of old desiring many Deut. 17:17; 1 Sam. 25:43 (42–43); D&C 132:38 (38–39).wives and 2 Sam. 20:3; 1 Chr. 3:9.concubines, and also Solomon, his son.
Yea, and they also began to search much Mosiah 2:12.gold and silver, and began to be lifted up somewhat in pride.

Wherefore I, Jacob, gave unto them these words as I taught them in the 2 Ne. 5:16; Alma 16:13; Hel. 3:14 (9, 14); 3 Ne. 11:1. TG Temple.temple, having first obtained mine TG Called of God.errand from the Lord.
For I, Jacob, and my brother Joseph had been 2 Ne. 5:26. TG Delegation of Responsibility; Setting Apart.consecrated priests and TG Teacher.teachers of this people, by the hand of Nephi.

And we did Jacob 2:2; D&C 24:3. TG Leadership; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.magnify our office unto the Lord, taking upon us the Ezek. 34:10. TG Accountability; Stewardship.responsibility, answering the sins of the people upon our own heads if we did not 1 Sam. 8:9; Moro. 9:6.teach them the word of God with all diligence; wherefore, by laboring with our might their Lev. 20:27; Acts 20:26; 2 Ne. 9:44; Mosiah 2:27; D&C 88:85; 112:33.blood might not come upon our garments; otherwise their blood would come upon our garments, and we would not be found spotless at the last day.

div.p: Jacob denounces the love of riches, pride, and unchastity—Men may seek riches to help their fellowmen—The Lord commands that no man among the Nephites may have more than one wife—The Lord delights in the chastity of women. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: The 2 Ne. 6:1.words which Jacob, the brother of Nephi, spake unto the people of Nephi, after the death of Nephi:

Now, my beloved brethren, I, Jacob, according to the TG Stewardship.responsibility which I am under to God, to Rom. 11:13; Jacob 1:19; D&C 24:3.magnify mine office with TG Sincere.soberness, and that I might Mosiah 2:28.rid my garments of your sins, I come up into the temple this day that I might declare unto you the word of God.

And ye yourselves know that I have hitherto been diligent in the office of my calling; but I this day am weighed down with much more desire and anxiety for the 2 Ne. 6:3; Mosiah 25:11.welfare of your souls than I have hitherto been.
For behold, as yet, ye have been obedient unto the word of the Lord, which I have given unto you.

But behold, hearken ye unto me, and know that by the help of the all-powerful Creator of heaven and earth I can tell you concerning your Amos 4:13; Alma 12:3 (3–7); D&C 6:16. TG God, Omniscience of.thoughts, how that ye are beginning to labor in sin, which sin appeareth very abominable unto me, yea, and abominable unto God.
Yea, it grieveth my soul and causeth me to shrink with shame before the presence of my Maker, that I must testify unto you concerning the wickedness of your hearts.

And also it grieveth me that I must use so much Lev. 19:17; D&C 121:43.boldness of speech concerning you, before your wives and your children, many of whose feelings are exceedingly tender and TG Chastity.chaste and delicate before God, which thing is pleasing unto God;
And it supposeth me that they have come up hither to hear the pleasing Micah 2:7; Alma 31:5; 36:26; Hel. 3:29 (29–30). TG Gospel.word of God, yea, the word which healeth the wounded soul.

Wherefore, it burdeneth my soul that I should be constrained, because of the strict commandment which I have received from God, to TG Warn.admonish you according to your crimes, to enlarge the wounds of those who are already wounded, instead of consoling and healing their wounds; and those who have not been wounded, instead of feasting upon the pleasing word of God have daggers placed to pierce their souls and wound their delicate minds.

But, notwithstanding the greatness of the task, I must do according to the strict TG Commandments of God.commands of God, and tell you concerning your wickedness and abominations, in the presence of the pure in heart, and the broken heart, and under the glance of the 2 Ne. 9:44. TG God, Omniscience of.piercing eye of the Almighty God.

Wherefore, I must tell you the truth according to the 2 Ne. 25:4; 33:5; Jacob 4:13.plainness of the Jacob 7:5.word of God. For behold, as I inquired of the Lord, thus came the word unto me, saying: Jacob, get thou up into the temple on the morrow, and declare the word which I shall give thee unto this people.

And now behold, my brethren, this is the word which I declare unto you, that many of you have begun to search for gold, and for silver, and for all manner of precious 1 Ne. 18:25; 2 Ne. 5:15 (14–16); Hel. 6:9 (9–11); Ether 9:17; 10:23 (12, 23).ores, in the which this land, which is a 1 Ne. 4:14; 17:13 (13–14). TG Promised Lands.land of promise unto you and to your seed, doth abound most plentifully.

And the hand of providence hath smiled upon you most pleasingly, that you have obtained many riches; and because some of you have obtained more abundantly than that of your brethren ye are 2 Kgs. 14:10; Alma 1:32; 31:25; Morm. 8:28 (28, 36–40).lifted up in the pride of your hearts, and wear stiff necks and high heads because of the costliness of your apparel, and persecute your brethren because ye suppose that ye are better than they.

And now, my brethren, do ye suppose that God justifieth you in this thing? Behold, I say unto you, Nay. But he condemneth you, and if ye persist in these things his judgments must speedily come unto you.
O that he would show you that he can pierce you, and with one glance of his TG God, Indignation of; God, Omniscience of.eye he can smite you to the dust!
O that he would rid you from this iniquity and abomination. And, O that ye would listen unto the word of his commands, and let not this TG Pride.pride of your hearts destroy your souls!

Think of your James 5:3 (1–6). TG Love.brethren like unto yourselves, and be familiar with all and free with your TG Almsgiving; Generosity; Welfare.substance, that Alma 4:12; 5:55; 4 Ne. 1:3 (3, 24–26).they may be rich like unto you.
But Mark 10:24 (17–27).before ye seek for 1 Kgs. 3:11 (11–13); Prov. 27:24 (24–27); 2 Ne. 26:31; Alma 39:14; D&C 6:7. TG Worldliness.riches, seek ye for the Luke 12:31 (22–31).kingdom of God.

And after ye have obtained a hope in Christ ye shall obtain riches, if ye seek them; and ye will seek them for the intent to Mosiah 4:26; 3 Ne. 12:42; 4 Ne. 1:3. TG Good Works.do good—to clothe the naked, and to feed the hungry, and to liberate the captive, and administer relief to the sick and the afflicted.

And now, my brethren, I have spoken unto you concerning pride; and those of you which have afflicted your neighbor, and persecuted him because ye were proud in your hearts, of the things which God hath given you, what say ye of it?

Do ye not suppose that such things are abominable unto him who created all flesh? And the one being is as precious in his sight as the other. And all flesh is of the dust; and for the selfsame end hath he created them, that they should keep his D&C 11:20; Abr. 3:25 (25–26).commandments and glorify him forever.
And now I make an end of speaking unto you concerning this pride. And were it not that I must speak unto you concerning a grosser crime, my heart would rejoice exceedingly because of you.

But the word of God burdens me because of your grosser crimes. For behold, thus saith the Lord: This people begin to wax in iniquity; they understand not the scriptures, for they seek to excuse themselves in committing TG Whore.whoredoms, because of the things which were written concerning David, and Solomon his son.

Behold, David and 1 Kgs. 11:1; Neh. 13:26 (25–27).Solomon truly had many Deut. 17:17 (14–17); 2 Sam. 5:13; D&C 132:39 (38–39).wives and concubines, which thing was Deut. 7:3 (1–4); 1 Kgs. 11:3; Ezra 9:2 (1–2).abominable before me, saith the Lord.

Wherefore, thus saith the Lord, I have led this people forth out of the land of Jerusalem, by the power of mine arm, that I might raise up unto me a Gen. 49:22 (22–26); Ezek. 17:22 (22–24); Amos 5:15; 2 Ne. 3:5; 14:2; Alma 26:36. TG Israel, Joseph, People of.righteous branch from the fruit of the loins of Joseph.
Wherefore, I the Lord God will not suffer that this people shall do like unto them of old.

Wherefore, my brethren, hear me, and hearken to the word of the Lord: For there shall not any TG Marriage, Husbands.man among you have save it be Jacob 3:5 (5–7); D&C 49:16. TG Marriage, Plural.one TG Marriage, Wives.wife; and concubines he shall have none;
For I, the Lord God, delight in the TG Chastity.chastity of women. And TG Sexual Immorality; Whore.whoredoms are an abomination before me; thus saith the Lord of Hosts.
Wherefore, this people shall keep my commandments, saith the Lord of Hosts, or Ether 2:11 (8–12). TG Curse.cursed be the land for their sakes.
For if I will, saith the Lord of Hosts, raise up Mal. 2:15; D&C 132:63 (61–66).seed unto me, I will command my people; otherwise they shall hearken unto these things.

For behold, I, the Lord, have seen the sorrow, and heard the mourning of the daughters of my people in the land of Jerusalem, yea, and in all the lands of my people, because of the wickedness and TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.abominations of their TG Marriage, Husbands.husbands.

And I will not suffer, saith the Lord of Hosts, that the cries of the fair daughters of this people, which I have led out of the land of Jerusalem, shall come up unto me against the men of my people, saith the Lord of Hosts.

For they shall not lead away captive the daughters of my people because of their tenderness, save I shall visit them with a sore curse, even unto destruction; for they shall not commit Ezek. 16:25 (20–34). TG Sensuality.whoredoms, like unto them of old, saith the Lord of Hosts.

And now behold, my brethren, ye know that these commandments were given to our 1 Ne. 1:16 (16–17).father, Lehi; wherefore, ye have known them before; and ye have come unto great condemnation; for ye have done these things which ye ought not to have done.

Behold, ye have done Jacob 3:5 (5–7).greater iniquities than the Lamanites, our brethren. Ye have broken the hearts of your tender wives, and lost the confidence of your children, because of your bad examples before them; and the sobbings of their hearts ascend up to God against you. And because of the Gen. 2:24.strictness of the word of God, which cometh down against you, many hearts died, pierced with deep wounds.

div.p: The pure in heart receive the pleasing word of God—Lamanite righteousness exceeds that of the Nephites—Jacob warns against fornication, lasciviousness, and every sin. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: But behold, I, Jacob, would speak unto you that are pure in heart. Look unto God with firmness of mind, and 2 Ne. 32:8.pray unto him with exceeding faith, and he will TG Comfort; Consolation; Purity.console you in your TG Affliction.afflictions, and he will plead your cause, and send down TG Deliver; Protection, Divine.justice upon those who seek your destruction.

O all ye that are pure in heart, lift up your heads and receive the pleasing word of God, and feast upon his TG God, Love of.love; for ye may, if your TG Steadfastness.minds are Alma 57:27.firm, forever.

But, wo, wo, unto you that are not pure in heart, that are filthy this day before God; for except ye repent the land is TG Earth, Curse of.cursed for your sakes; and the Lamanites, which are not TG Filthiness.filthy like unto you, nevertheless they are 1 Ne. 12:23.cursed with a sore cursing, shall scourge you even unto destruction.
And the time speedily cometh, that except ye repent they shall possess the land of your inheritance, and the Lord God will Omni 1:12 (5–7, 12–13).lead away the righteous out from among you.

Behold, the Lamanites your brethren, whom ye hate because of their filthiness and the cursing which hath come upon their skins, are more righteous than you; for they have not Jacob 2:35.forgotten the commandment of the Lord, which was given unto our father—that they should have save it were Jacob 2:27.one wife, and Mosiah 11:2 (2–14); Ether 10:5.concubines they should have none, and there should not be TG Chastity.whoredoms committed among them.

And now, this commandment they observe to keep; wherefore, because of this observance, in keeping this commandment, the Lord God will not destroy them, but will be 1 Ne. 13:31; 2 Ne. 4:7; 10:18 (18–19); Hel. 15:12 (10–17); Morm. 5:20 (20–21).merciful unto them; and one day they shall 1 Ne. 15:14 (13–18); 22:8.become a blessed people.

Behold, their TG Marriage, Husbands.husbands TG Family, Love within; Marriage, Continuing Courtship in.love their TG Marriage, Wives.wives, and their wives love their husbands; and their husbands and their wives love their children; and their D&C 3:18. TG Unbelief.unbelief and their hatred towards you is because of the iniquity of their fathers; wherefore, how much better are you than they, in the sight of your great Creator?
O my brethren, I fear that unless ye shall repent of your sins that their skins will be 3 Ne. 2:15.whiter than yours, when ye shall be brought with them before the throne of God.

Wherefore, a commandment I give unto you, which is the word of God, that ye TG Reviling.revile no more against them because of the darkness of their skins; neither shall ye revile against them because of their filthiness; but ye shall remember your own filthiness, and remember that their filthiness came because of their fathers.

Wherefore, ye shall remember your TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward; Family, Love within.children, how that ye have grieved their hearts because of the TG Example.example that ye have set before them; and also, remember that ye may, because of your filthiness, bring your children unto destruction, and their sins be heaped upon your heads at the last day.

O my brethren, hearken unto my words; TG Apathy.arouse the faculties of your souls; shake yourselves that ye may 2 Ne. 28:21; Alma 5:7 (6–7).awake from the slumber of death; and loose yourselves from the pains of TG Hell.hell that ye may not become 2 Ne. 9:9 (8–9). TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.angels to the devil, to be cast into that lake of fire and brimstone which is the second TG Death, Spiritual, Second.death.

And now I, Jacob, spake many more things unto the people of Nephi, TG Warn.warning them against TG Fornication.fornication and TG Lust.lasciviousness, and every kind of sin, telling them the awful consequences of them.

And a hundredth part of the proceedings of this people, which now began to be numerous, cannot be written upon Jacob 1:1 (1–4); 4:1 (1–4).these plates; but many of their proceedings are written upon the Jarom 1:14.larger plates, and their wars, and their contentions, and the reigns of their kings.
Jarom 1:2 (1–2).These plates are called the plates of Jacob, and they were 1 Ne. 19:2 (2–3).made by the hand of Nephi. And I make an end of speaking these words.

div.p: All the prophets worshiped the Father in the name of Christ—Abraham’s offering of Isaac was in similitude of God and His Only Begotten—Men should reconcile themselves to God through the Atonement—The Jews will reject the foundation stone. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: Now behold, it came to pass that I, Jacob, having ministered much unto my people in word, (and I cannot write but a 1 Ne. 6:6; Jarom 1:14; Omni 1:30.little of my words, because of the Ether 12:24 (23–26).difficulty of engraving our words upon plates) and we know that the things which we write upon plates must remain;

But whatsoever things we write upon anything save it be upon TG Scriptures, Preservation of.plates must perish and vanish away; but we can write a few words upon plates, which will give our children, and also our beloved brethren, a small degree of knowledge concerning us, or concerning their fathers—

Now in this thing we do rejoice; and we labor diligently to engraven these words upon plates, hoping that our beloved brethren and our children will receive them with thankful hearts, and look upon them that they may learn with joy and not with sorrow, neither with contempt, concerning their first TG Scriptures, Value of.parents.

For, for this intent have we written these things, that they may know that we TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Testimony.knew of Christ, and we had a hope of his TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory many hundred years before his coming; and not only we ourselves had a hope of his glory, but also all the holy Luke 24:27; 1 Pet. 1:11; Jacob 7:11 (11–12); Mosiah 13:33 (33–35); D&C 20:26.prophets which were before us.

Behold, they believed in Christ and Moses 5:8.worshiped the Father in his name, and also we worship the Father in his Gen. 4:26; Hel. 8:16 (16–20). TG Name of the Lord.name. And for this intent we 2 Ne. 25:24; Jacob 7:7; Mosiah 13:30.keep the Jarom 1:11; Alma 25:15 (15–16). TG Law of Moses.law of Moses, it Gal. 3:24; Ether 12:19 (18–19).pointing our souls to him; and for this cause it is sanctified unto us for righteousness, even as it was accounted unto Abraham in the wilderness to be obedient unto the commands of God in offering up his son Isaac, which is a TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.similitude of God and his Gen. 22:2 (1–14); John 3:16 (16–21); Heb. 11:17. TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Only Begotten Son.

Wherefore, we search the prophets, and we have many revelations and the spirit of TG Prophecy.prophecy; and having all these TG Witness.witnesses we obtain a hope, and our faith becometh unshaken, insomuch that we truly can 3 Ne. 28:20 (19–22). TG God, Power of.command in the Acts 3:6 (6–16); 3 Ne. 8:1.name of Jesus and the very trees obey us, or the mountains, or the waves of the sea.

Nevertheless, the Lord God showeth us our Ether 12:27; D&C 66:3.weakness that we may know that it is by his TG Grace.grace, and his great condescensions unto the children of men, that we have power to do these things.

Behold, great and marvelous are the Ps. 106:2.works of the Lord. How Rom. 11:34 (33–36); Mosiah 4:9.unsearchable are the depths of the D&C 19:10; 76:114 (114–16). TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries of him; and it is impossible that man should find out all his ways. And no man Dan. 1:17; 1 Cor. 2:11 (9–16); Alma 26:21 (21–22). TG God, Knowledge about.knoweth of his Isa. 55:8 (8–9).ways save it be revealed unto him; wherefore, brethren, despise not the D&C 3:7.revelations of God.

For behold, by the power of his Morm. 9:17; Moses 1:32.word TG Man, Physical Creation of.man came upon the face of the earth, which earth was TG Creation; God, Creator; Jesus Christ, Creator.created by the power of his word. Wherefore, if God being able to speak and the world was, and to speak and man was created, O then, why not able to command the Hel. 12:16 (8–17).earth, or the workmanship of his hands upon the face of it, according to his will and pleasure?

Wherefore, brethren, seek not to Josh. 9:14; Prov. 15:22; Isa. 45:9; 2 Ne. 9:28–29; Alma 37:12, 37; D&C 3:4, 13; 22:4.counsel the Lord, but to take counsel from his hand. For behold, ye yourselves know that he counseleth in TG God, Justice of; God, Wisdom of.wisdom, and in justice, and in great mercy, over all his works.

Wherefore, beloved brethren, be Lev. 6:30. TG Jesus Christ, Mission of; Reconciliation.reconciled unto him through the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement of Christ, his TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Only Begotten Son, and ye may obtain a TG Resurrection.resurrection, according to the TG God, Power of.power of the resurrection which is in Christ, and be presented as the Mosiah 15:21 (21–23); 18:9; Alma 40:16 (16–21).first-fruits of Christ unto God, having faith, and obtained a good hope of glory in him before he manifesteth himself in the flesh.

And now, beloved, marvel not that I tell you these things; for why not 2 Ne. 25:26.speak of the atonement of Christ, and attain to a perfect knowledge of him, as to attain to the knowledge of a resurrection and the world to come?

Behold, my brethren, he that prophesieth, let him prophesy to the understanding of men; for the TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.Spirit speaketh the John 17:17. TG Honesty.truth and lieth not. Wherefore, it speaketh of things as they really D&C 93:24.are, and of things as they really will be; wherefore, these things are manifested unto us Neh. 8:8; Jacob 2:11; Alma 13:23.plainly, for the salvation of our souls. But behold, we are not witnesses alone in these things; for God also TG Witness of the Father.spake them unto prophets of old.

But behold, the Jews were a Deut. 9:13; Neh. 9:16; 2 Ne. 25:2. TG Stiffnecked.stiffnecked people; and they Num. 15:31 (30–31); Ezek. 20:13 (13–16); 1 Ne. 17:30 (30–31); 19:7; 2 Ne. 33:2; D&C 3:7 (4–13).despised the words of 2 Cor. 11:3.plainness, and Zech. 1:4 (2–5).killed the prophets, and sought for things that they could not understand. Wherefore, because of their Isa. 44:18.blindness, which Rom. 11:25. TG Spiritual Blindness.blindness came by looking beyond the John 7:47 (45–53).mark, they must needs fall; for God hath taken away his plainness from them, and delivered unto them many things which they 2 Ne. 25:1.cannot understand, because they desired it. And because they desired it God hath done it, that they may Isa. 57:14.stumble.

And now I, Jacob, am led on by the Spirit unto prophesying; for I perceive by the workings of the Spirit which is in me, that by the Isa. 8:14 (13–15); 1 Cor. 1:23; 2 Ne. 18:14 (13–15).stumbling of the TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jews they will Rom. 11:1, 20 (1–36); 1 Ne. 10:11; Morm. 5:14 (14–20).reject the TG Cornerstone; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Rock.stone upon which they might build and have safe foundation.
But behold, according to the scriptures, this Ps. 118:22 (22–23).stone shall become the great, and the last, and the only sure Isa. 28:16 (14–17); Hel. 5:12.foundation, upon which the Jews can build.

And now, my beloved, how is it possible that these, after having rejected the sure foundation, can Matt. 19:30; Jacob 5:63 (62–64); D&C 29:30.ever build upon it, that it may become the head of their corner?
Behold, my beloved brethren, I will unfold this mystery unto you; if I do not, by any means, get shaken from my firmness in the Spirit, and stumble because of my over anxiety for you.

div.p: Jacob quotes Zenos relative to the allegory of the tame and wild olive trees—They are a likeness of Israel and the Gentiles—The scattering and gathering of Israel are prefigured—Allusions are made to the Nephites and Lamanites and all the house of Israel—The Gentiles will be grafted into Israel—Eventually the vineyard will be burned. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: Behold, my brethren, do ye not remember to have read the words of the prophet 1 Ne. 19:12 (12, 16); Jacob 6:1. TG Scriptures, Lost.Zenos, which he spake unto the house of Israel, saying:
Hearken, O ye house of Israel, and hear the words of me, a prophet of the Lord.

For behold, thus saith the Lord, I will liken thee, O house of TG Israel, Twelve Tribes of.Israel, like unto a tame Ezek. 36:8 (8–15); Rom. 11:21 (1–36); 1 Ne. 10:12; Jacob 6:1 (1–7). TG Israel, Mission of; Vineyard of the Lord.olive tree, which a man took and nourished in his Matt. 21:33 (33–41); D&C 101:44.vineyard; and it grew, and waxed old, and began to TG Apostasy of Israel.decay.

And it came to pass that the master of the vineyard went forth, and he saw that his olive tree began to decay; and he said: I will TG Prophets, Mission of.prune it, and dig about it, and nourish it, that perhaps it may shoot forth young and tender branches, and it perish not.
And it came to pass that he 2 Kgs. 17:13 (13–18).pruned it, and digged about it, and nourished it according to his word.

And it came to pass that after many days it began to put forth somewhat a little, young and tender branches; but behold, the main Luke 3:9 (8–9); 2 Ne. 30:2. TG Chief Priest.top thereof began to perish.

And it came to pass that the master of the vineyard saw it, and he said unto his TG Servant.servant: It grieveth me that I should lose this tree; wherefore, go and pluck the branches from a Rom. 11:17 (17, 24).wild olive tree, and bring them hither unto me; and we will pluck off those main branches which are beginning to wither away, and we will cast them into the fire that they may be burned.

And behold, saith the Lord of the vineyard, I take TG Israel, Scattering of.away many of these young and tender branches, and I will graft them Ezek. 17:22 (4–10, 22).whithersoever I will; and it mattereth not that if it so be that the root of this tree will perish, I may preserve the fruit thereof unto myself; wherefore, I will take these young and tender branches, and I will graft them whithersoever I will.

Take thou the branches of the wild olive tree, and graft them in, in the Acts 9:15; 14:27; Rom. 1:13; Gal. 3:14.stead thereof; and these which I have plucked off I will cast into the fire and burn them, that they may not cumber the ground of my vineyard.
And it came to pass that the servant of the Lord of the vineyard did according to the word of the Lord of the vineyard, and grafted in the branches of the TG Gentiles.wild olive tree.

And the Lord of the vineyard caused that it should be digged about, and pruned, and nourished, saying unto his servant: It grieveth me that I should lose this tree; wherefore, that perhaps I might preserve the roots thereof that they perish not, that I might preserve them unto myself, I have done this thing.
Wherefore, go thy way; watch the tree, and nourish it, according to my words.

And these will I Hosea 8:8; 1 Ne. 10:12.place in the nethermost part of my vineyard, whithersoever I will, it mattereth not unto thee; and I do it that I may preserve unto myself the natural branches of the tree; and also, that I may lay up fruit thereof against the season, unto myself; for it grieveth me that I should lose this tree and the fruit thereof.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard went his way, and hid the natural TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.branches of the tame olive tree in the nethermost parts of the vineyard, some in one and some in another, according to his will and pleasure.

And it came to pass that a long time passed away, and the Lord of the vineyard said unto his servant: Come, let us go down into the vineyard, that we may TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.labor in the vineyard.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard, and also the servant, went down into the vineyard to labor. And it came to pass that the servant said unto his master: Behold, look here; behold the tree.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard looked and beheld the tree in the which the wild olive branches had been grafted; and it had sprung forth and begun to bear Matt. 12:33; John 15:16; Gal. 3:9 (7–9, 29); Col. 1:6 (3–8).fruit. And he beheld that it was good; and the fruit thereof was like unto the natural fruit.

And he said unto the servant: Behold, the branches of the wild tree have taken hold of the moisture of the root thereof, that the root thereof hath brought forth much strength; and because of the much strength of the root thereof the wild branches have brought forth tame fruit. Now, if we had not grafted in these branches, the tree thereof would have perished. And now, behold, I shall lay up much fruit, which the tree thereof hath brought forth; and the fruit thereof I shall lay up against the season, unto mine own self.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said unto the servant: Come, let us go to the nethermost part of the vineyard, and behold if the natural branches of the tree have not brought forth much fruit also, that I may lay up of the fruit thereof against the season, unto mine own self.

And it came to pass that they went forth whither the master had hid the natural branches of the tree, and he said unto the servant: Behold these; and he beheld the Jacob 5:39.first that it had TG Israel, Restoration of.brought forth much fruit; and he beheld also that it was good. And he said unto the servant: Take of the fruit thereof, and lay it up against the season, that I may preserve it unto mine own self; for behold, said he, this long time have I nourished it, and it hath brought forth much fruit.
And it came to pass that the servant said unto his master: How comest thou hither to plant this tree, or this branch of the tree? For behold, it was the poorest spot in all the land of thy vineyard.

And the Lord of the vineyard said unto him: Counsel me not; I knew that it was a poor spot of ground; wherefore, I said unto thee, I have nourished it this long time, and thou beholdest that it hath brought forth much fruit.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said unto his servant: Look hither; behold I have planted another branch of the tree also; and thou knowest that this spot of ground was poorer than the first. But, behold the tree. I have nourished it this long time, and it hath brought forth much fruit; therefore, gather it, and lay it up against the season, that I may preserve it unto mine own self.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said again unto his servant: Look hither, and behold another Ezek. 17:22 (22–24); Alma 16:17.branch also, which I have planted; behold that I have nourished it also, and it hath brought forth fruit.

And he said unto the servant: Look hither and behold the last. Behold, this have I planted in a Ezek. 17:8; 1 Ne. 2:20; Jacob 5:43.good spot of ground; and I have nourished it this long time, and only a Hel. 15:3 (3–4).part of the tree hath brought forth tame fruit, and the Alma 26:36.other part of the tree hath brought forth wild fruit; behold, I have nourished this tree like unto the others.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said unto the servant: Pluck off the branches that have not brought forth good Matt. 7:19 (15–20); Alma 5:36; D&C 97:7.fruit, and cast them into the fire.

But behold, the servant said unto him: Let us prune it, and dig about it, and nourish it a little Jacob 5:50 (50–51); Alma 42:4.longer, that perhaps it may bring forth good fruit unto thee, that thou canst lay it up against the season.
And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard and the servant of the Lord of the vineyard did nourish all the fruit of the vineyard.

And it came to pass that a TG Last Days.long time had passed away, and the Lord of the vineyard said unto his D&C 101:55; 103:21.servant: Come, let us go down into the vineyard, that we may labor again in the vineyard. For behold, the time draweth near, and the 2 Ne. 30:10; Jacob 6:2.end soon cometh; wherefore, I must lay up fruit against the season, unto mine own self.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard and the servant went down into the vineyard; and they came to the tree whose natural branches had been broken off, and the wild branches had been grafted in; and behold all TG Apostasy of Israel.sorts of fruit did cumber the tree.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard did TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment.taste of the fruit, every sort according to its number. And the Lord of the vineyard said: Behold, this long time have we nourished this tree, and I have laid up unto myself against the season much fruit.

But behold, this time it hath brought forth much Hosea 10:1.fruit, and there is JS—H 1:19.none of it which is good. And behold, there are all kinds of bad fruit; and it profiteth me nothing, notwithstanding all our labor; and now it grieveth me that I should lose this tree.
And the Lord of the vineyard said unto the servant: What shall we do unto the tree, that I may preserve again good fruit thereof unto mine own self?

And the servant said unto his master: Behold, because thou didst graft in the branches of the wild olive tree they have nourished the roots, that they are alive and they have not perished; wherefore thou beholdest that they are yet good.
And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said unto his servant: The tree profiteth me nothing, and the roots thereof profit me nothing so long as it shall bring forth evil fruit.
Nevertheless, I know that the roots are good, and for mine own purpose I have preserved them; and because of their much strength they have hitherto brought forth, from the wild branches, good fruit.

But behold, the wild branches have grown and have D&C 45:30.overrun the roots thereof; and because that the wild branches have overcome the roots thereof it hath brought forth much evil fruit; and because that it hath brought forth so much evil fruit thou beholdest that it beginneth to perish; and it will soon become ripened, that it may be cast into the fire, except we should do something for it to preserve it.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said unto his servant: Let us go down into the nethermost parts of the vineyard, and behold if the natural branches have also brought forth evil fruit.

And it came to pass that they went down into the nethermost parts of the vineyard. And it came to pass that they beheld that the fruit of the natural branches had become corrupt also; yea, the Jacob 5:20 (20, 23, 25).first and the second and also the last; and they had all become corrupt.
And the Hel. 15:4 (3–4).wild fruit of the last had overcome that part of the tree which brought forth good fruit, even that the branch had withered away and died.
And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard wept, and said unto the servant: Isa. 5:4; 2 Ne. 2:27; 26:24; Jacob 5:47; Alma 26:37.What could I have done more for my vineyard?

Behold, I knew that all the fruit of the vineyard, save it were these, had become TG Apostasy of Israel.corrupted. And now these which have once brought forth good fruit have also become corrupted; and now all the trees of my vineyard are good for nothing save it be to be Matt. 3:10.hewn down and cast into the fire.

And behold this last, whose branch hath withered away, I did plant in a Ezek. 17:8; Jacob 5:25.good spot of ground; yea, even that which was choice unto me above all other parts of the land of my vineyard.
And thou beheldest that I also cut down that which Moro. 9:23.cumbered this spot of ground, that I might plant this tree in the stead thereof.

And thou beheldest that a 1 Ne. 15:12 (12–17); 2 Ne. 3:5; 10:1; Alma 46:24 (24–25).part thereof brought forth good fruit, and a part thereof brought forth wild fruit; and because I plucked not the branches thereof and cast them into the fire, behold, they have overcome the good branch that it hath withered away.

And now, behold, notwithstanding all the care which we have taken of my vineyard, the trees thereof have become corrupted, that they bring forth no good Luke 3:9.fruit; and these I had hoped to preserve, to have laid up fruit thereof against the season, unto mine own self. But, behold, they have become like unto the wild olive tree, and they are of no worth but to be Alma 5:52; 3 Ne. 27:11.hewn down and cast into the fire; and it grieveth me that I should lose them.

But Jacob 5:41 (41, 49).what could I have done more in my vineyard? Have I slackened mine hand, that I have not nourished it? Nay, I have nourished it, and I have digged about it, and I have pruned it, and I have dunged it; and I have Isa. 9:12 (12, 17, 21).stretched forth mine 2 Ne. 19:17 (17–21); 28:32; Jacob 6:4.hand almost all the day long, and the TG World, End of.end draweth nigh. And it grieveth me that I should hew down all the trees of my vineyard, and cast them into the fire that they should be burned. Who is it that has corrupted my vineyard?

And it came to pass that the servant said unto his master: Is it not the TG Haughtiness; Pride.loftiness of thy vineyard—have not the branches thereof overcome the roots which are good? And because the branches have overcome the roots thereof, behold they grew faster than the strength of the roots, D&C 121:39. TG Unrighteous Dominion.taking strength unto themselves. Behold, I say, is not this the cause that the trees of thy vineyard have become corrupted?

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard said unto the servant: Let us go to and hew down the trees of the vineyard and cast them into the fire, that they shall not cumber the ground of my vineyard, for I have done all. What could I have done more for my vineyard?
But, behold, the servant said unto the Lord of the vineyard: Spare it a little Jacob 5:27.longer.
And the Lord said: Yea, I will spare it a little longer, for it grieveth me that I should lose the trees of my vineyard.

Wherefore, let us take of the TG Israel, Gathering of; Israel, Restoration of.branches of these which I have planted in the nethermost parts of my vineyard, and let us graft them into the tree from whence they came; and let us pluck from the tree those branches whose fruit is most bitter, and graft in the natural branches of the tree in the stead thereof.
And this will I do that the tree may not perish, that, perhaps, I may preserve unto myself the roots thereof for mine Ex. 19:6; Isa. 49:6.own purpose.

And, behold, the roots of the natural branches of the tree which I planted whithersoever I would are yet alive; wherefore, that I may preserve them also for mine own purpose, I will take of the 3 Ne. 21:6 (5–6); Morm. 5:15.branches of this tree, and I will 1 Ne. 15:16.graft them in unto them. Yea, I will graft in unto them the branches of their mother tree, that I may preserve the roots also unto mine own self, that when they shall be sufficiently strong perhaps they may bring forth good fruit unto me, and I may yet have glory in the fruit of my vineyard.
And it came to pass that they took from the natural tree which had become wild, and grafted in unto the natural trees, which also had become wild.
And they also took of the natural trees which had become wild, and Jer. 24:6.grafted into their mother tree.
And the Lord of the vineyard said unto the servant: Pluck not the wild branches from the trees, save it be those which are most bitter; and in them ye shall graft according to that which I have said.

And we will nourish again the trees of the vineyard, and we will trim up the Isa. 27:11.branches thereof; and we will pluck from the trees those branches which are ripened, that must perish, and cast them into the fire.
And this I do that, perhaps, the roots thereof may take strength because of their goodness; and because of the change of the branches, that the good may TG Triumph.overcome the evil.

And because that I have preserved the natural branches and the roots thereof, and that I have grafted in the natural branches again into their mother tree, and have preserved the roots of their mother tree, that, perhaps, the trees of my vineyard may bring forth again good Isa. 27:6.fruit; and that I may have joy again in the fruit of my vineyard, and, perhaps, that I may rejoice exceedingly that I have preserved the roots and the branches of the first fruit—

Wherefore, go to, and call Jacob 6:2.servants, that we may D&C 24:19; 39:17; 95:4.labor diligently with our might in the vineyard, that we may TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.prepare the way, that I may bring forth again the natural fruit, which natural fruit is good and the most precious above all other fruit.
Wherefore, let us go to and labor with our might this last time, for behold the end draweth nigh, and this is for the last time that I shall D&C 75:2.prune my vineyard.

Graft in the branches; begin at the Matt. 20:16; Mark 10:31; Luke 13:30; 1 Ne. 13:42; Ether 13:12 (10–12).last that they may be first, and that the first may be Matt. 19:30; Jacob 4:17; D&C 29:30.last, and dig about the trees, both old and young, the first and the last; and the last and the first, that all may be nourished once again for the last time.

Wherefore, dig about them, and prune them, and dung them once more, for the last time, for the end draweth nigh. And if it be so that these last grafts shall grow, and bring forth the natural fruit, then shall ye prepare the way for them, that they may grow.

And as they begin to grow ye shall D&C 86:6 (6–7).clear away the branches which bring forth bitter fruit, according to the strength of the good and the size thereof; and ye shall not clear away the bad thereof all at once, lest the roots thereof should be too strong for the graft, and the graft thereof shall perish, and I lose the trees of my vineyard.

For it grieveth me that I should lose the trees of my vineyard; wherefore ye shall clear away the bad according as the good shall grow, that the root and the top may be equal in strength, until the good shall overcome the bad, and the bad be hewn down and cast into the fire, that they cumber not the ground of my vineyard; and thus will I sweep away the bad out of my vineyard.
And the branches of the natural tree will I graft in again into the natural tree;

And the branches of the natural tree will I graft into the natural branches of the tree; and thus will I bring them together again, that they shall bring forth the natural TG Israel, Mission of.fruit, and they shall be one.
And the bad shall be 1 Ne. 22:23 (15–17, 23); 2 Ne. 30:10 (9–10).cast away, yea, even out of all the land of my vineyard; for behold, only this once will I prune my vineyard.

And it came to pass that the Lord of the vineyard sent his D&C 101:55; 103:21.servant; and the servant went and did as the Lord had commanded him, and brought other Matt. 9:37 (36–38).servants; and they were 1 Ne. 14:12.few.

And the Lord of the vineyard said unto them: Go to, and Matt. 21:28; Jacob 6:2 (2–3); D&C 33:3 (3–4).labor in the vineyard, with your might. For behold, this is the D&C 39:17; 43:28 (28–30).last time that I shall TG Millennium, Preparing a People for.nourish my vineyard; for the end is nigh at hand, and the season speedily cometh; and if ye labor with your might with me ye shall have joy in the fruit which I shall lay up unto myself against the time which will soon come.

And it came to pass that the servants did go and labor with their mights; and the Lord of the vineyard labored also with them; and they did obey the commandments of the Lord of the vineyard in all things.

And there began to be the natural fruit again in the vineyard; and the natural branches began to grow and thrive exceedingly; and the wild branches began to be plucked off and to be cast away; and they did keep the root and the top thereof equal, according to the strength thereof.

And thus they labored, with all diligence, according to the commandments of the Lord of the vineyard, even until the bad had been cast away out of the vineyard, and the Lord had preserved unto himself that the trees had become again the natural fruit; and they became like unto D&C 38:27.one body; and the fruits were equal; and the Lord of the vineyard had preserved unto himself the natural fruit, which was most precious unto him from the beginning.

And it came to pass that when the TG Jesus Christ, Millennial Reign.Lord of the vineyard saw that his fruit was good, and that his vineyard was no more corrupt, he called up his servants, and said unto them: Behold, for this last time have we nourished my vineyard; and thou beholdest that I have done according to my will; and I have preserved the natural fruit, that it is good, even like as it was in the beginning. And 1 Ne. 13:37; D&C 21:9.blessed art thou; for because ye have been diligent in laboring with me in my vineyard, and have kept my commandments, and have brought unto me again the TG Israel, Restoration of.natural fruit, that my vineyard is no more corrupted, and the bad is cast away, behold ye shall have D&C 6:31; 18:15 (15–16).joy with me because of the fruit of my vineyard.

For behold, for a 1 Ne. 22:26.long time will I lay up of the fruit of my vineyard unto mine own self against the season, which speedily cometh; and for the last time have I nourished my vineyard, and pruned it, and dug about it, and dunged it; wherefore I will lay up unto mine own self of the fruit, for a long time, according to that which I have spoken.

And when the time cometh that evil fruit shall again come into my vineyard, then will I cause the D&C 86:7.good and the bad to be gathered; and the good will I preserve unto myself, and the bad will I cast away into its own place. And then cometh the Rev. 20:3 (3–10); D&C 29:22; 43:31 (30–31); 88:111 (110–12).season and the end; and my vineyard will I cause to be TG World, End of.burned with Joel 1:19 (19–20); 2 Ne. 6:15 (14–15); Jacob 6:3.fire.

div.p: The Lord will recover Israel in the last days—The world will be burned with fire—Men must follow Christ to avoid the lake of fire and brimstone. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, behold, my brethren, as I said unto you that I would prophesy, behold, this is my prophecy—that the things which this prophet Jacob 5:1; Alma 33:13 (13–15). TG Scriptures, Lost.Zenos spake, concerning the house of Israel, in the which he likened them unto a tame Rom. 11:21 (1–36); 1 Ne. 10:12; Jacob 5:3 (3–77).olive tree, must surely come to pass.

And the day that he shall set his hand again the second time to 1 Ne. 22:12 (10–12); D&C 110:11; 137:6. TG Israel, Gathering of; Israel, Restoration of.recover his people, is the day, yea, even the last time, that the Jacob 5:61.servants of the Lord shall go forth in his 1 Ne. 14:1, 14.power, to Jacob 5:71; D&C 101:56.nourish and prune his Jer. 12:10; D&C 138:56. TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard; and after that the 2 Ne. 30:10; Jacob 5:29; D&C 43:17 (17–20, 28).end soon cometh.

And how Jacob 5:71.blessed are they who have labored TG Diligence; Perseverance.diligently in his vineyard; and how D&C 41:1.cursed are they who shall be cast out into their own place! And the TG World.world shall be Isa. 24:6; 2 Ne. 27:2; Jacob 5:77; 3 Ne. 25:1. TG World, End of.burned with fire.

And how merciful is our God unto us, for he remembereth the house of 2 Sam. 7:24.Israel, both roots and branches; and he stretches forth his Neh. 9:19 (18–26); 2 Ne. 19:17 (17–21); 28:32; Jacob 5:47.hands unto them all the day long; and they are a TG Stiffnecked.stiffnecked and a gainsaying people; but as many as will not harden their hearts shall be saved in the kingdom of God.

Wherefore, my beloved brethren, I beseech of you in words of soberness that ye would repent, and come with full purpose of heart, and Deut. 10:20; Josh. 23:8; 2 Kgs. 18:6; Hel. 4:25; D&C 11:19.cleave unto God as he cleaveth unto you. And while his Isa. 59:16; 2 Ne. 1:15; Alma 5:33; 3 Ne. 9:14.arm of mercy is extended towards you in the light of the day, harden not your hearts.
Yea, today, if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts; for why will ye Ezek. 18:28 (26–28, 32).die?

For behold, after ye have been nourished by the good Ps. 119:28.word of God all the day long, will ye bring forth evil fruit, that ye must be Alma 5:52; 3 Ne. 27:11 (11–12).hewn down and cast into the fire?

Behold, will ye reject these words? Will ye reject the words of the Jer. 26:5.prophets; and will ye reject all the words which have been spoken concerning Christ, after so many have spoken concerning him; and TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.deny the good word of Christ, and the power of God, and the TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.gift of the Holy Ghost, and quench the Holy Spirit, and make a TG Sacrilege.mock of the great plan of redemption, which hath been laid for you?

Know ye not that if ye will do these things, that the power of the redemption and the resurrection, which is in Christ, will bring you to stand with TG Shame.shame and Jacob 7:19; Mosiah 15:26.awful TG Guilt; Judgment, the Last.guilt before the bar of God?

And according to the power of TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice, for justice cannot be denied, ye must go away into that Rev. 19:20; 2 Ne. 28:23; Mosiah 3:27. TG Hell.lake of fire and brimstone, whose flames are unquenchable, and whose smoke ascendeth up forever and ever, which lake of fire and brimstone is Mosiah 2:33; D&C 19:11 (10–12).endless TG Damnation.torment.
O then, my beloved brethren, repent ye, and enter in at the 2 Ne. 9:41.strait gate, and 2 Ne. 31:15. TG Commitment.continue in the way which is narrow, until ye shall obtain eternal life.
O be Matt. 10:16; Morm. 9:28.wise; what can I say more?
Finally, I bid you farewell, until I shall meet you before the Moro. 10:34.pleasing bar of God, which bar striketh the wicked with Alma 40:14.awful dread and fear. Amen.

div.p: Sherem denies Christ, contends with Jacob, demands a sign, and is smitten of God—All of the prophets have spoken of Christ and His Atonement—The Nephites lived out their days as wanderers, born in tribulation, and hated by the Lamanites. About 544–421 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass after some years had passed away, there came a man among the people of Nephi, whose name was TG False Prophets.Sherem.

And it came to pass that he began to preach among the people, and to declare unto them that there should be Alma 21:8; 30:12 (12, 22).no Christ. And he preached many things which were flattering unto the people; and this he did that he might TG False Doctrine.overthrow the doctrine of Christ.

And he labored diligently that he might lead away the hearts of the people, insomuch that he did lead away many hearts; and he knowing that I, Jacob, had faith in Christ who should come, he sought much opportunity that he might come unto me.

And he was TG Learn.learned, that he had a perfect knowledge of the language of the people; wherefore, he could use much TG Flatter.flattery, and much power of speech, according to the TG False Priesthoods.power of the devil.

And he had hope to shake me from the faith, notwithstanding the many 2 Ne. 10:3; 11:3; Jacob 2:11.revelations and the many things which I had seen concerning these things; for I truly had seen 2 Ne. 2:4.angels, and they had ministered unto me. And also, I had Ex. 19:9 (9–13).heard the voice of the Lord speaking unto me in very word, from time to time; wherefore, I could not be shaken.

And it came to pass that he came unto me, and on this wise did he speak unto me, saying: Brother Jacob, I have sought much opportunity that I might speak unto you; for I have heard and also know that thou goest about much, preaching that which ye call the 2 Ne. 31:2.gospel, or the doctrine of Christ.

And ye have led away much of this people that they pervert the right way of God, and Jacob 4:5.keep not the law of Moses which is the right way; and convert the law of Moses into the worship of a being which ye say shall come many hundred years hence. And now behold, I, Sherem, declare unto you that this is TG Blaspheme.blasphemy; for no man knoweth of such things; for he cannot Alma 30:13.tell of things to come. And after this manner did Sherem contend against me.
But behold, the Lord God poured in his TG God, Spirit of; Holy Ghost, Mission of.Spirit into my soul, insomuch that I did Ps. 97:7. TG Confound.confound him in all his words.
And I said unto him: Deniest thou the Christ who shall come? And he said: If there should be a Christ, I would not deny him; but I know that there is no Christ, neither has been, nor ever will be.
And I said unto him: Believest thou the scriptures? And he said, Yea.

And I said unto him: Then ye do not understand them; for they truly testify of Christ. Behold, I say unto you that none of the 1 Ne. 10:5; 3 Ne. 20:24 (23–24). TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.prophets have written, nor 1 Pet. 1:11; Rev. 19:10; Jacob 4:4; Mosiah 13:33 (33–35); D&C 20:26.prophesied, save they have spoken concerning this Christ.

And this is not all—it has been made manifest unto me, for I have heard and seen; and it also has been made manifest unto me by the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.power of the Holy Ghost; wherefore, I know if there should be no atonement made all mankind must be 2 Ne. 2:21 (10–30).lost.
And it came to pass that he said unto me: Show me a John 6:30; Alma 30:43 (43–60); D&C 46:9 (8–9). TG Sign Seekers.sign by this power of the Holy Ghost, in the which ye know so much.

And I said unto him: What am I that I should TG Test.tempt God to show unto thee a sign in the thing which thou knowest to be Mosiah 12:30; Alma 30:42 (41–42).true? Yet thou wilt deny it, because thou art of the Alma 30:53.devil. Nevertheless, not my will be done; but if God shall smite thee, let that be a Num. 26:10; D&C 124:53 (50–53).sign unto thee that he has power, both in heaven and in earth; and also, that Christ shall come. And thy will, O Lord, be done, and not mine.

And it came to pass that when I, Jacob, had spoken these words, the power of the Lord came upon him, insomuch that he fell to the earth. And it came to pass that he was nourished for the space of many days.
And it came to pass that he said unto the people: Gather together on the morrow, for I shall die; wherefore, I desire to speak unto the people before I shall die.

And it came to pass that on the morrow the multitude were gathered together; and he spake plainly unto them and denied the things which he had taught them, and confessed the Christ, and the power of the Holy Ghost, and the ministering of angels.

And he spake plainly unto them, that he had been Gal. 3:1 (1–4); Alma 30:53 (53, 60).deceived by the power of the TG Deceit; Devil.devil. And he spake of hell, and of TG Eternity.eternity, and of eternal TG Punish.punishment.

And he said: I TG Despair.fear lest I have committed the TG Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against; Sons of Perdition.unpardonable sin, for I have lied unto God; for I denied the Christ, and said that I believed the scriptures; and they truly testify of him. And because I have thus lied unto God I greatly fear lest my case shall be Jacob 6:9; Mosiah 15:26.awful; but I confess unto God.
And it came to pass that when he had said these words he could say no more, and he Jer. 28:16 (15–17); Alma 30:59 (12–60).gave up the Gen. 49:33; Hel. 14:21.ghost.

And when the multitude had witnessed that he spake these things as he was about to give up the ghost, they were astonished exceedingly; insomuch that the power of God came down upon them, and they were Alma 19:6 (1–36).overcome that they fell to the earth.
Now, this thing was pleasing unto me, Jacob, for I had requested it of my Father who was in heaven; for he had heard my cry and answered my prayer.

And it came to pass that peace and the TG God, Love of.love of God was restored again among the people; and they Alma 17:2. TG Scriptures, Study of.searched the scriptures, and hearkened no more to the words of this wicked man.

And it came to pass that many means were devised to Enos 1:20.reclaim and restore the Lamanites to the knowledge of the truth; but it all was Enos 1:14.vain, for they delighted in Mosiah 1:5; 10:12 (11–18); Alma 3:8; 9:16; D&C 93:39.wars and Jarom 1:6; Alma 26:24 (23–25).bloodshed, and they had an eternal 2 Ne. 5:1 (1–3); Mosiah 28:2. TG Malice.hatred against us, their brethren. And they sought by the power of their arms to destroy us continually.

Wherefore, the people of Nephi did fortify against them with their arms, and with all their might, trusting in the God and TG Rock.rock of their salvation; wherefore, they became as yet, conquerors of their enemies.

And it came to pass that I, Jacob, began to be old; and the record of this people being kept on the 1 Ne. 19:1 (1–6); Jarom 1:14 (1, 14–15).other plates of Nephi, wherefore, I conclude this record, declaring that I have written according to the best of my knowledge, by saying that the time passed away with us, and also our James 4:14.lives passed away like as it were unto us a 1 Chr. 29:15; Ps. 144:4.dream, we being a Alma 13:23.lonesome and a solemn people, Alma 26:36.wanderers, cast out from Jerusalem, born in tribulation, in a wilderness, and hated of our brethren, which caused wars and contentions; wherefore, we did mourn out our days.

And I, Jacob, saw that I must soon go down to my grave; wherefore, I said unto my son Enos 1:1.Enos: Take these Omni 1:3.plates. And I told him the things which my brother Nephi had Jacob 1:1 (1–4).commanded me, and he promised obedience unto the commands. And I make an end of my writing upon these plates, which writing has been 1 Ne. 6:3 (1–6); Jarom 1:2 (2, 14).small; and to the reader I bid farewell, hoping that many of my brethren may read my words. Brethren, adieu.

div.p: Enos prays mightily and gains a remission of his sins—The voice of the Lord comes into his mind, promising salvation for the Lamanites in a future day—The Nephites sought to reclaim the Lamanites—Enos rejoices in his Redeemer. About 420 B.C.

chapter.verse: Behold, it came to pass that I, Jacob 7:27.Enos, knowing my father that 2 Ne. 2:3 (2–4).he was a just man—for he 1 Ne. 1:1; Mosiah 1:2.taught me in his language, and also in the Eph. 6:4.nurture and admonition of the Lord—and blessed be the name of my God for it—
And I will tell you of the Gen. 32:24 (24–32); Alma 8:10. TG Repent.wrestle which I had before God, before I received a TG Remission of Sins.remission of my sins.

Behold, I went to hunt beasts in the forests; and the words which I had often heard my father speak concerning eternal life, and the TG Joy.joy of the saints, 1 Ne. 10:17 (17–19); Alma 36:17. TG Teachable.sunk deep into my heart.

And my soul 2 Ne. 9:51; 3 Ne. 12:6. TG Meditation; Motivations.hungered; and I TG Reverence.kneeled down before my Maker, and I Ps. 138:3. TG Perseverance.cried unto him in mighty TG Prayer.prayer and supplication for mine own soul; and all the day long did I cry unto him; yea, and when the night came I did still raise my voice high that it reached the heavens.
And there came a TG Revelation.voice unto me, saying: Enos, thy sins are TG Forgive.forgiven thee, and thou shalt be blessed.
And I, Enos, knew that God TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.could not lie; wherefore, my guilt was swept away.
And I said: Lord, how is it done?

And he said unto me: Ether 3:13 (12–13).Because of thy TG Faith.faith in Christ, whom thou hast never before heard nor seen. And many years pass away before he shall manifest himself in the flesh; wherefore, go to, thy faith hath made thee Matt. 9:22. TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn; Steadfastness.whole.

Now, it came to pass that when I had heard these words I began to feel a 1 Ne. 8:12; Alma 36:24.desire for the Alma 19:29. TG Benevolence.welfare of my brethren, the Nephites; wherefore, I did Num. 21:7; 1 Sam. 1:15; 7:5; Jer. 42:4; 2 Ne. 33:3; Alma 34:26 (26–27).pour out my whole soul unto God for them.

And while I was thus struggling in the spirit, behold, the voice of the Lord came into my TG Inspiration; Mind.mind again, saying: I will visit thy brethren according to their diligence in keeping my commandments. I have 1 Ne. 2:20.given unto them this land, and it is a holy land; and I Gen. 8:21 (20–22); Ether 2:9 (7–12).curse it not save it be for the cause of iniquity; wherefore, I will visit thy brethren according as I have said; and their TG Transgress.transgressions will I bring down with sorrow upon their own heads.

And after I, Enos, had heard these words, my TG Faith.faith began to be TG Steadfastness.unshaken in the Lord; and I Gen. 20:7; 1 Sam. 7:5; 2 Ne. 33:3; W of M 1:8.prayed unto him with many long Eph. 6:18.strugglings for my brethren, the Lamanites.

And it came to pass that after I had Morm. 5:21; 8:25 (24–26); 9:36 (36–37).prayed and labored with all diligence, the Lord said unto me: I will grant unto thee according to thy Ps. 37:4; 1 Ne. 7:12; Hel. 10:5.desires, because of thy faith.

And now behold, this was the desire which I desired of him—that if it should so be, that my people, the Nephites, should fall into transgression, and by any means be 1 Ne. 15:5; Morm. 6:1.destroyed, and the Lamanites should not be Lev. 26:44.destroyed, that the Lord God would W of M 1:7 (6–11); Alma 37:2.preserve a record of my people, the Nephites; even if it so be by the power of his holy arm, that it might be 2 Ne. 3:15; Jacob 1:13; Alma 37:19; Morm. 7:9 (8–10); Ether 12:22; D&C 3:18.brought forth at some future day unto the Lamanites, that, perhaps, they might be Alma 9:17; Hel. 15:16.brought unto salvation—

For at the present our strugglings were Jacob 7:24.vain in restoring them to the true faith. And they swore in their wrath that, if it were possible, they would 2 Ne. 26:17; Morm. 6:6.destroy our records and us, and also all the traditions of our fathers.

Wherefore, I knowing that the Lord God was able to TG Scriptures, Preservation of.preserve our records, I cried unto him continually, for he had said unto me: Whatsoever thing ye shall ask in faith, believing that ye shall receive in the name of Christ, ye shall receive it.

And I had faith, and I did cry unto God that he would 3 Ne. 5:14 (13–15); D&C 3:19 (16–20); 10:47 (46–50).preserve the TG Book of Mormon.records; and he covenanted with me that he would 2 Ne. 25:8; 27:6; Morm. 5:12.bring 1 Ne. 19:19. TG Israel, Restoration of.them forth unto the Lamanites in his own due time.
And I, Enos, TG Trust in God.knew it would be according to the covenant which he had made; wherefore my soul did rest.
And the Lord said unto me: Thy fathers have also required of me this thing; and it shall be done unto them according to their faith; for their faith was like unto thine.
And now it came to pass that I, Enos, went about among the people of Nephi, prophesying of things to come, and testifying of the things which I had heard and seen.

And I bear record that the people of Nephi did seek diligently to Jacob 7:24.restore the Lamanites unto the true faith in God. But our Moro. 9:6.labors were vain; their 2 Ne. 5:1. TG Hate.hatred was fixed, and they were led by their evil nature that they became wild, and ferocious, and a Jarom 1:6.blood-thirsty people, full of Mosiah 9:12. TG Idolatry.idolatry and TG Filthiness.filthiness; feeding upon beasts of prey; dwelling in Gen. 25:27.tents, and wandering about in the wilderness with a short skin girdle about their loins and their heads shaven; and their skill was in the Mosiah 10:8; Alma 3:5 (4–5); 43:20 (18–21).bow, and in the cimeter, and the ax. And many of them did eat nothing save it was raw meat; and they were continually seeking to destroy us.

And it came to pass that the people of Nephi did till the land, and 1 Ne. 8:1; Mosiah 9:9.raise all manner of grain, and of fruit, and 1 Ne. 18:25; Ether 9:19 (18–19).flocks of herds, and flocks of all manner of cattle of every kind, and goats, and wild goats, and also many horses.
And there were exceedingly many W of M 1:16.prophets among us. And the people were a Jarom 1:3.stiffnecked people, hard to understand.

And there was nothing save it was exceeding 1 Ne. 16:2 (1–3); 2 Ne. 33:5; W of M 1:17.harshness, TG Preaching.preaching and prophesying of wars, and contentions, and destructions, and continually Hel. 12:3.reminding them of death, and the duration of eternity, and the judgments and the power of God, and all these things—stirring them up Jarom 1:12; Alma 4:19; 31:5.continually to keep them in the fear of the Lord. I say there was nothing short of these things, and exceedingly great plainness of speech, would keep them from going down speedily to destruction. And after this manner do I write concerning them.
And I saw 1 Ne. 12:2 (2–3); Morm. 8:7 (7–8). TG War.wars between the Nephites and Lamanites in the course of my days.
And it came to pass that I began to be old, and an hundred and seventy and nine years had passed away from the time that our father Lehi 1 Ne. 2:2 (2–4).left Jerusalem.

And I saw that I 1 Cor. 9:16; Ether 12:2. TG Duty.must soon go down to my grave, having been wrought upon by the power of God that I must preach and prophesy unto this people, and declare the word according to the truth which is in Christ. And I have declared it in all my days, and have rejoiced in it above that of the world.

And I soon go to the place of my TG Rest.rest, which is with my Redeemer; for I know that in him I shall 2 Ne. 33:6.rest. And I rejoice in the day when my TG Mortality.mortal shall put on TG Immortality.immortality, and shall stand before him; then shall I see his face with pleasure, and he will say unto me: Come unto me, ye blessed, there is a place prepared for you in the Ps. 65:4; John 14:2 (2–3); Ether 12:32 (32–34); D&C 72:4; 98:18.mansions of my Father. Amen.

div.p: The Nephites keep the law of Moses, look forward to the coming of Christ, and prosper in the land—Many prophets labor to keep the people in the way of truth. About 399–361 B.C.

chapter.verse: Now behold, I, Jarom, write a few words according to the commandment of my father, Enos, that our 1 Ne. 3:12; 5:14.genealogy may be kept.

And as Jacob 3:14 (13–14); Omni 1:1.these plates are 1 Ne. 6:3 (1–6); Jacob 7:27.small, and as these things are TG Scriptures, Writing of.written for the intent of the benefit of our brethren the 2 Ne. 27:6; Morm. 5:12 (12–13).Lamanites, wherefore, it must needs be that I write a little; but I shall not write the things of my prophesying, nor of my revelations. For what could I write more than my fathers have written? For have not they revealed the plan of salvation? I say unto you, Yea; and this sufficeth me.

Behold, it is expedient that much should be done among this people, because of the hardness of their hearts, and the deafness of their ears, and the blindness of their minds, and the Enos 1:22 (22–23).stiffness of their necks; nevertheless, God is exceedingly merciful unto them, and has not as yet Ether 2:8 (8–10).swept them off from the face of the land.

And there are many among us who have many Alma 26:22; Hel. 11:23; D&C 107:19 (18–19).revelations, for they are not all TG Stiffnecked.stiffnecked. And as many as are not stiffnecked and have faith, have TG Holy Ghost; Revelation.communion with the Holy Spirit, which maketh manifest unto the children of men, according to their faith.

And now, behold, two hundred years had passed away, and the people of Nephi had waxed strong in the land. They observed to 2 Ne. 25:24; Mosiah 2:3; Alma 30:3; 34:14 (13–14).keep the law of Moses and the Ex. 35:2. TG Sabbath.sabbath day holy unto the Lord. And they TG Profanity.profaned not; neither did they TG Blaspheme.blaspheme. And the Alma 1:1.laws of the land were exceedingly strict.

And they were scattered upon Hel. 11:20 (19–20).much of the face of the land, and the Lamanites also. And they were exceedingly more Alma 2:27.numerous than were they of the Nephites; and they loved Jacob 7:24; Enos 1:20; Alma 26:24 (23–25).murder and would drink the TG Blood, Eating of.blood of beasts.

And it came to pass that they came many times against us, the Nephites, to battle. But our 2 Ne. 5:18; 6:2; Jacob 1:9 (9, 11, 15); Mosiah 1:10.kings and our TG Leadership.leaders were mighty men in the faith of the Lord; and they taught the people the ways of the Lord; wherefore, we withstood the Lamanites and swept them away out of W of M 1:14.our lands, and began to fortify our cities, or whatsoever place of our inheritance.

And we multiplied exceedingly, and spread upon the face of the land, and became exceedingly rich in 2 Ne. 5:15.gold, and in silver, and in precious things, and in fine TG Art.workmanship of wood, in buildings, and in TG Skill.machinery, and also in iron and copper, and brass and steel, making all manner of tools of every kind to till the ground, and 2 Ne. 5:14; Mosiah 10:8.weapons of war—yea, the sharp pointed arrow, and the quiver, and the dart, and the javelin, and all preparations for war.

And thus being prepared to meet the Lamanites, they did not prosper against us. But the word of the Lord was verified, which he spake unto our fathers, saying that: 2 Ne. 1:20; Omni 1:6.Inasmuch as ye will keep my commandments ye shall Josh. 1:7; Ps. 122:6.prosper in the land.

And it came to pass that the prophets of the Lord did threaten the people of Nephi, according to the word of God, that if they did not keep the commandments, but should fall into transgression, they should be 1 Ne. 12:19 (19–20); Omni 1:5.destroyed from off the face of the land.

Wherefore, the prophets, and the priests, and the TG Teacher.teachers, did labor diligently, exhorting with all long-suffering the people to TG Diligence.diligence; teaching the Jacob 4:5; Alma 25:15 (15–16).law of Moses, and the intent for which it was given; persuading them to 2 Ne. 11:4; Ether 12:19 (18–19).look forward unto the Messiah, and believe in him to come 2 Ne. 25:26 (24–27); Mosiah 3:13; 16:6.as though he already was. And after this manner did they teach them.

And it came to pass that by so doing they kept them from being Ether 2:10 (8–10).destroyed upon the face of the land; for they did Alma 31:5.prick their hearts with the word, Enos 1:23.continually stirring them up unto repentance.
And it came to pass that two hundred and thirty and eight years had passed away—after the manner of wars, and TG Contention.contentions, and dissensions, for the space of Omni 1:3.much of the time.

And I, Jarom, do not write more, for the plates are Jacob 4:1 (1–2); Omni 1:30.small. But behold, my brethren, ye can go to the Jacob 7:26 (26–27); W of M 1:3.other plates of Nephi; for behold, upon them the records of our wars are engraven, according to the writings of the Omni 1:11; W of M 1:10.kings, or those which they caused to be written.
And I deliver these plates into the hands of my son Omni, that they may be kept according to the Jacob 1:1 (1–4); Omni 1:3.commandments of my fathers.

div.p: Omni, Amaron, Chemish, Abinadom, and Amaleki, each in turn, keep the records—Mosiah discovers the people of Zarahemla, who came from Jerusalem in the days of Zedekiah—Mosiah is made king over them—The descendants of Mulek at Zarahemla had discovered Coriantumr, the last of the Jaredites—King Benjamin succeeds Mosiah—Men should offer their souls as an offering to Christ. About 323–130 B.C.

chapter.verse: Behold, it came to pass that I, Omni, being commanded by my father, Jarom, that I should write somewhat upon Jarom 1:2 (1–2); Omni 1:9.these plates, to preserve our genealogy—

Wherefore, in my days, I would that ye should know that I fought much with the sword to preserve my people, the Nephites, from falling into the hands of their enemies, the Lamanites. But behold, I of myself TG Confession; Honesty; Humility.am a wicked man, and I have not kept the statutes and the commandments of the Lord as I ought to have done.

And it came to pass that two hundred and seventy and six years had passed away, and we had many seasons of peace; and we had many Jarom 1:13.seasons of serious war and bloodshed. Yea, and in fine, two hundred and eighty and two years had passed away, and I had kept these plates according to the Jacob 1:1 (1–4); 7:27; Jarom 1:15 (1–2, 15).commandments of my TG Patriarch.fathers; and I TG Delegation of Responsibility.conferred them upon my son Amaron. And I make an end.
And now I, Amaron, write the things whatsoever I write, which are few, in the book of my father.
Behold, it came to pass that three hundred and twenty years had passed away, and the more wicked part of the Nephites were Jarom 1:10.destroyed.

For the Lord would not suffer, after he had led them out of the land of Jerusalem and kept and preserved them from falling into the hands of their enemies, yea, he would not suffer that the words should not be verified, which he spake unto our fathers, saying that: Jarom 1:9; Mosiah 1:7.Inasmuch as ye will not keep my commandments ye shall not Deut. 28:29.prosper in the land.
