Wherefore, the Lord did visit them in great judgment; nevertheless, he did spare the righteous that they should not perish, but did deliver them out of the hands of their enemies.
And it came to pass that I did deliver the plates unto my brother Chemish.

Now I, Chemish, write what few things I write, in the same book with my brother; for behold, I saw the last which he wrote, that he wrote it with his own hand; and he wrote it in the day that he delivered them unto me. And after this manner we keep the Omni 1:1. TG Record Keeping.records, for it is according to the commandments of our fathers. And I make an end.

Behold, I, Abinadom, am the son of Chemish. Behold, it came to pass that I saw much war and contention between my people, the Nephites, and the Lamanites; and I, with my own sword, have taken the lives of many of the Lamanites in the defence of my brethren.

And behold, the W of M 1:10.record of this people is engraven upon plates which is had by the Jarom 1:14.kings, according to the generations; and I know of no revelation save that which has been written, neither prophecy; wherefore, that which is sufficient is written. And I make an end.

Behold, I am Amaleki, the son of Abinadom. Behold, I will speak unto you somewhat concerning Omni 1:19.Mosiah, who was made king over the Alma 4:1.land of Zarahemla; for behold, he being TG Warn.warned of the Lord that he should Mosiah 11:13.flee out of the Omni 1:27; W of M 1:13.land of 2 Ne. 5:8; Mosiah 7:6 (6–7).Nephi, and as many as would hearken unto the voice of the Lord should also Jacob 3:4.depart out of the land with him, into the wilderness—

And it came to pass that he did according as the Lord had commanded him. And they departed out of the land into the wilderness, as many as would hearken unto the voice of the Lord; and they were led by many preachings and prophesyings. And they were admonished continually by the word of God; and they were led by the power of his Isa. 33:2; Mosiah 12:24.arm, through the wilderness until they came down into the land which is called the Mosiah 1:1; 2:4.land of Zarahemla.

And they discovered a Mosiah 1:10.people, who were called the people of Zarahemla. Now, there was great rejoicing among the people of Zarahemla; and also Zarahemla did rejoice exceedingly, because the Lord had sent the people of Mosiah with the 1 Ne. 3:3 (3, 19–20); 5:10 (10–22).plates of brass which contained the record of the Jews.

Behold, it came to pass that Mosiah discovered that the people of Ezek. 17:22 (22–23); Mosiah 25:2 (2–4).Zarahemla came out from Jerusalem at the time that Jer. 39:4 (1–10); 52:11 (9–11); Hel. 8:21.Zedekiah, king of Judah, was carried away captive into Babylon.

And they 1 Ne. 16:9.journeyed in the wilderness, and were brought by the hand of the Lord across the great waters, into the land where Mosiah discovered them; and they had dwelt there from that time forth.

And at the time that Mosiah discovered them, they had become exceedingly numerous. Nevertheless, they had had many wars and serious contentions, and had fallen by the sword from time to time; and their 1 Ne. 3:19. TG Language.language had become corrupted; and they had brought no Mosiah 1:3 (2–6).records with them; and they denied the being of their Creator; and Mosiah, nor the people of Mosiah, could understand them.

But it came to pass that Mosiah caused that they should be taught in his Mosiah 24:4.language. And it came to pass that after they were taught in the language of Mosiah, Zarahemla gave a genealogy of his fathers, according to his memory; and they are written, but 1 Ne. 9:4; W of M 1:10.not in these plates.
And it came to pass that the people of Zarahemla, and of Mosiah, did Mosiah 25:13.unite together; and Omni 1:12.Mosiah was appointed to be their king.

And it came to pass in the days of Mosiah, there was a large Mosiah 21:28 (27–28); 28:13.stone brought unto him with engravings on it; and he did Mosiah 8:13 (13–19); 28:17. TG Urim and Thummim.interpret the engravings by the gift and power of God.

And they gave an account of one Ether 12:1 (1–2); 13:20 (13–31); 15:32.Coriantumr, and the slain of his people. And Coriantumr was discovered by the people of Zarahemla; and he dwelt with them for the space of nine moons.

It also spake a few words concerning his fathers. And his first parents came out from the Ether 1:3 (1–6).tower, at the time the Lord Gen. 11:7 (6–9); Mosiah 28:17; Ether 1:33.confounded the language of the people; and the severity of the Lord fell upon them according to his judgments, which are just; and their Mosiah 8:8 (8–12).bones lay scattered in the land northward.
Behold, I, Amaleki, was born in the days of Mosiah; and I have lived to see his death; and W of M 1:3.Benjamin, Mosiah 2:11.his son, reigneth in his stead.

And behold, I have seen, in the days of king Benjamin, a serious war and much bloodshed between the Nephites and the Lamanites. But behold, the Nephites did obtain much advantage over them; yea, insomuch that king Benjamin did drive them out of the land of Zarahemla.

And it came to pass that I began to be old; and, having no seed, and knowing king W of M 1:18 (17–18); Mosiah 29:13.Benjamin to be a just man before the Lord, wherefore, I shall W of M 1:10.deliver up 1 Ne. 10:1.these plates unto him, exhorting all men to come unto God, the Holy One of Israel, and believe in prophesying, and in revelations, and in the ministering of angels, and in the gift of speaking with tongues, and in the gift of interpreting languages, and in all things which are Alma 5:40; Ether 4:12; Moro. 7:16 (15–17).good; for there is nothing which is good save it comes from the Lord: and that which is evil cometh from the devil.

And now, my beloved brethren, I would that ye should Jacob 1:7; Alma 29:2; Moro. 10:32.come unto Christ, who is the Holy One of Israel, and partake of his salvation, and the power of his redemption. Yea, come unto him, and TG Commitment; Self-Sacrifice.offer your whole souls as an 3 Ne. 9:20.offering unto him, and continue in TG Fast, Fasting.fasting and praying, and endure to the end; and as the Lord liveth ye will be saved.

And now I would speak somewhat concerning a certain Mosiah 9:3 (1–4).number who went up into the wilderness to Mosiah 7:1.return to the Omni 1:12.land of Nephi; for there was a large number who were desirous to possess the land of their inheritance.

Wherefore, they went up into the wilderness. And their leader being a strong and mighty man, and a stiffnecked man, wherefore he caused a contention among them; and they were Mosiah 9:2 (1–4).all slain, save fifty, in the wilderness, and they returned again to the land of Zarahemla.
And it came to pass that they also took others to a considerable number, and took their journey again into the wilderness.

And I, Amaleki, had a brother, who also went with them; and I have not since known concerning them. And I am about to lie down in my grave; and 1 Ne. 6:3 (3–6); Jacob 4:1 (1–2); Jarom 1:14.these plates are full. And I make an end of my speaking.

div.p: Mormon abridges the large plates of Nephi—He puts the small plates with the other plates—King Benjamin establishes peace in the land. About A.D. 385.

chapter.verse: And now I, Mormon, being about to deliver up the 3 Ne. 5:12 (9–12); Morm. 1:4 (1–4); 2:17 (17–18); 8:5 (1, 4–5, 14).record which I have been making into the hands of my son Moroni, behold I have witnessed almost all the destruction of my people, the Nephites.

And it is Morm. 6:5 (5–6).many hundred years after the coming of Christ that I deliver these records into the hands of my son; and it supposeth me that he will witness the entire 1 Ne. 12:19.destruction of my people. But may God grant that he may survive them, that he may write somewhat concerning them, and somewhat concerning Christ, that perhaps some day it may D&C 3:19 (16–20).profit them.

And now, I speak somewhat concerning that which I have written; for after I had made an 1 Ne. 1:17 (16–17); D&C 10:44.abridgment from the Jarom 1:14; W of M 1:10; D&C 10:38 (38–40).plates of Nephi, down to the reign of this king Benjamin, of whom Amaleki spake, I searched among the Mosiah 1:6 (2–6); Hel. 3:13 (13–15); Morm. 4:23.records which had been delivered into my hands, and I found these plates, which contained this small account of the prophets, from Jacob down to the reign of this king Omni 1:23 (23–25).Benjamin, and also many of the words of Nephi.

And the things which are upon these plates 1 Ne. 6:5 (3–6).pleasing me, because of the prophecies of the coming of Christ; and my fathers knowing that many of them have been fulfilled; yea, and I also know that as many things as have been TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.prophesied concerning us down to this day have been fulfilled, and as many as go beyond this day must surely come to pass—

Wherefore, I chose IE the things pleasing to him, mentioned in v. 4.these things, to finish my 3 Ne. 5:17 (14–18); Morm. 1:1.record upon them, which remainder of my record I shall take from the 1 Ne. 9:2.plates of Nephi; and I cannot write the Alma 13:31; 3 Ne. 5:8 (8–11); 26:6 (6–12).hundredth part of the things of my people.

But behold, I shall take these plates, which contain these prophesyings and revelations, and put them with the remainder of my record, for they are choice unto me; and I know they will be choice unto my brethren.

And I do this for a 1 Ne. 9:5; 19:3; Enos 1:13 (13–18); Alma 37:2; D&C 3:19 (9–20); 10:34 (1–19, 30–47).wise D&C 10:40 (20–46).purpose; for thus it whispereth me, according to the workings of the Spirit of the Lord which is in me. And now, I do not know all things; but the Lord TG God, Foreknowledge of; God, Intelligence of; God, Omniscience of.knoweth all things which are to come; wherefore, he 2 Ne. 27:23.worketh in me to do according to his TG God, Will of.will.

And my 2 Ne. 33:3; Enos 1:11 (11–12); Moro. 9:22.prayer to God is concerning my brethren, that they may once again come to the knowledge of God, yea, the redemption of Christ; that they may once again be a 2 Ne. 30:6.delightsome people.
And now I, Mormon, proceed to finish out my record, which I take from the plates of Nephi; and I make it according to the knowledge and the TG Understanding.understanding which God has given me.

Wherefore, it came to pass that after Amaleki had Omni 1:25 (25, 30).delivered up these plates into the hands of king Benjamin, he took them and put them with the 1 Ne. 9:4; Omni 1:11, 18; W of M 1:3; Mosiah 28:11.other plates, which contained records which had been handed down by the Jarom 1:14.kings, from generation to generation until the days of king Benjamin.

And they were handed down from king Benjamin, from generation to generation until they have fallen into 3 Ne. 5:12 (8–12); Morm. 1:4 (1–5).my hands. And I, Mormon, pray to God that they may be preserved from this time henceforth. And I know that they will be preserved; for there are great things written upon them, out of which Hel. 15:3 (3–4).my people and their brethren shall be 2 Ne. 25:18; 29:11; 33:14 (11, 14–15); 3 Ne. 27:25 (23–27); Ether 5:4.judged at the great and last day, according to the word of God which is written.
And now, concerning this king Benjamin—he had somewhat of contentions among his own people.

And it came to pass also that the armies of the Lamanites came down out of the Omni 1:12.land of Nephi, to battle against his people. But behold, king Benjamin gathered together his armies, and he did stand against them; and he did fight with the strength of his own arm, with the 1 Ne. 4:9; 2 Ne. 5:14; Jacob 1:10; Mosiah 1:16; D&C 17:1.sword of Laban.

And in the TG Strength.strength of the Lord they did contend against their enemies, until they had slain many thousands of the Lamanites. And it came to pass that they did contend against the Lamanites until they had driven them out of all the lands of their Jarom 1:7.inheritance.
And it came to pass that after there had been false TG False Christs.Christs, and their mouths had been shut, and they punished according to their crimes;

And after there had been TG False Prophets.false prophets, and false preachers and teachers among the people, and all these having been punished according to their crimes; and after there having been much contention and many dissensions away Jacob 1:14 (13–14).unto the Lamanites, behold, it came to pass that king Benjamin, with the assistance of the holy Enos 1:22.prophets who were among his people—

For behold, king Benjamin was a Ex. 22:31; Alma 13:26; D&C 49:8; 107:29.holy man, and he did reign over his people in righteousness; and there were many holy men in the land, and they did speak the word of God with Alma 17:3 (2–3). TG God, Power of.power and with authority; and they did use much Enos 1:23; Moro. 9:4; D&C 121:43 (41–43).sharpness because of the stiffneckedness of the people—

Wherefore, with the help of these, king Omni 1:25; Mosiah 29:13.Benjamin, by laboring with all the might of his body and the 1 Ne. 15:25.faculty of his whole soul, and also the prophets, did once more establish peace in the land.

div.p: King Benjamin teaches his sons the language and prophecies of their fathers—Their religion and civilization have been preserved because of the records kept on the various plates—Mosiah is chosen as king and is given custody of the records and other things. About 130–124 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now there was no more contention in all the Omni 1:13; Alma 2:15.land of Zarahemla, among all the people who belonged to king Benjamin, so that king Benjamin had continual peace all the remainder of his days.

And it came to pass that he had three 1 Ne. 1:1; D&C 68:25 (25, 28).sons; and he called their names Mosiah, and Helorum, and Helaman. And he caused that they should be Enos 1:1; Mosiah 4:15 (14–15).taught in all the Morm. 9:32.language of his fathers, that thereby they might become men of understanding; and that they might know concerning the prophecies which had been spoken by the mouths of their fathers, which were delivered them by the hand of the Lord.

And he also taught them concerning the records which were engraven on the 2 Ne. 5:12; Mosiah 1:16; 28:20.plates of brass, saying: My sons, I would that ye should remember that were it not for these 1 Ne. 3:19 (19–20); Omni 1:17.plates, which contain these records and these commandments, we must have suffered in Alma 37:8.ignorance, even at this present time, not knowing the mysteries of God.

For it were not possible that our father, Lehi, could have remembered all these things, to have taught them to his children, except it were for the help of these plates; for he having been taught in the 1 Ne. 1:2; 3:19; Morm. 9:32 (32–33); JS—H 1:64.language of the Egyptians therefore he could read these engravings, and teach them to his children, that thereby they could teach them to their children, and so fulfilling the commandments of God, even down to this present time.

I say unto you, my sons, Alma 37:9.were it not for these things, which have been kept and TG Scriptures, Preservation of.preserved by the hand of God, that we might Deut. 6:6 (6–8); 2 Chr. 34:21; 1 Ne. 15:24 (23–24). TG Scriptures, Value of.read and understand of his TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries, and have his 1 Ne. 4:15.commandments always before our eyes, that even our fathers would have dwindled in unbelief, and we should have been like unto our brethren, the Lamanites, who know nothing concerning these things, or even do not believe them when they are taught them, because of the Jacob 7:24; Mosiah 10:12 (11–17).traditions of their fathers, which are not correct.

O my sons, I would that ye should remember that these sayings are true, and also that these records are 1 Ne. 1:3; 14:30; 2 Ne. 25:20; Alma 3:12; Ether 5:3 (1–3).true. And behold, also the plates of Nephi, which contain the records and the sayings of our fathers from the time they left Jerusalem until now, and they are true; and we can know of their surety because we have them before our eyes.

And now, my sons, I would that ye should remember to TG Scriptures, Study of.search them diligently, that ye may profit thereby; and I would that ye should Lev. 25:18 (18–19); Mosiah 2:22; Alma 50:20 (20–22).keep the commandments of God, that ye may Josh. 1:7; Ps. 1:3 (2–3); 122:6; 1 Ne. 2:20.prosper in the land according to the Omni 1:6; Alma 9:13 (13–14).promises which the Lord made unto our fathers.
And many more things did king Benjamin teach his sons, which are not written in this book.

And it came to pass that after king Benjamin had made an end of teaching his sons, that he waxed TG Old Age.old, and he saw that he must very soon go the way of all the earth; therefore, he thought it expedient that he should confer the kingdom upon one of his sons.

Therefore, he had Mosiah brought before him; and these are the words which he spake unto him, saying: My son, I would that ye should make a proclamation throughout all this land among all this Omni 1:14; Mosiah 27:35.people, or the people of Zarahemla, and the people of Mosiah who dwell in the land, that thereby they may be gathered together; for on the morrow I shall proclaim unto this my people out of mine own mouth that thou art a Gen. 41:43 (41–43); Jarom 1:7 (7, 14); Mosiah 2:30.king and a ruler over this people, whom the Lord our God hath given us.

And moreover, I shall give this people a Mosiah 5:8 (8–12). TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name, that thereby they may be distinguished above all the people which the Lord God hath brought out of the land of Jerusalem; and this I do because they have been a TG Diligence.diligent people in keeping the commandments of the Lord.
And I give unto them a name that never shall be blotted out, except it be through TG Transgress.transgression.

Yea, and moreover I say unto you, that if this highly favored people of the Lord should fall into Heb. 6:6 (4–6).transgression, and become a wicked and an adulterous people, that the Lord will deliver them up, that thereby they become Jer. 46:15 (15–17); Hel. 4:24 (24, 26).weak like unto their brethren; and he will no more D&C 103:8.preserve them by his matchless and marvelous power, as he has hitherto preserved our fathers.
For I say unto you, that if he had not extended his arm in the preservation of our fathers they must have fallen into the hands of the Lamanites, and become victims to their hatred.
And it came to pass that after king Benjamin had made an end of these sayings to his son, that he gave him 1 Kgs. 2:1; Ps. 72:1 (1–4).charge concerning all the affairs of the kingdom.

And moreover, he also gave him charge concerning the records which were engraven on the Mosiah 1:3.plates of brass; and also the plates of Nephi; and also, the Jacob 1:10; W of M 1:13; D&C 17:1.sword of Laban, and the 2 Ne. 5:12.ball or director, which led our fathers through the wilderness, which was prepared by the hand of the Lord that thereby they might be led, every one according to the heed and diligence which they gave unto him.

Therefore, as they were TG Disobedience.unfaithful they did not prosper nor progress in their journey, but were 1 Ne. 18:13 (12–13).driven back, and incurred the displeasure of God upon them; and therefore they were smitten with famine and sore Lam. 1:5. TG Affliction.afflictions, to stir them up in Judg. 13:1; Hel. 12:3 (2–3).remembrance of their duty.

And now, it came to pass that Mosiah went and did as his father had commanded him, and proclaimed unto all the people who were in the land of Zarahemla that thereby they might gather themselves together, to go up to the 2 Ne. 5:16; Mosiah 2:1.temple to hear the words which his father should speak unto them.

div.p: King Benjamin addresses his people—He recounts the equity, fairness, and spirituality of his reign—He counsels them to serve their Heavenly King—Those who rebel against God will suffer anguish like unquenchable fire. About 124 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that after Mosiah had done as his father had commanded him, and had made a proclamation throughout all the land, that the people TG Assembly for Worship.gathered themselves together throughout all the land, that they might go up to the Mosiah 1:18.temple to 2 Chr. 34:30 (29–33).hear the Mosiah 26:1.words which king Benjamin should speak unto them.
And there were a great number, even so many that they did not number them; for they had multiplied exceedingly and waxed great in the land.

And they also took of the Gen. 4:4 (2–7); Ex. 13:12 (12–13); Deut. 12:6; Moses 5:20 (5, 19–23).firstlings of their flocks, that they might offer Ezra 6:10. TG Sacrifice.sacrifice and Lev. 1:3 (2–9); Deut. 33:10.burnt Ezra 3:2 (2–5); 1 Ne. 5:9.offerings 2 Ne. 25:24; Jarom 1:5; Alma 30:3; 34:14 (13–14).according to the law of Moses;

And also that they might give thanks to the Lord their God, who had brought them out of the land of Jerusalem, and who had delivered them out of the hands of their enemies, and had TG Called of God.appointed just men to be their Mosiah 18:18 (18–22).teachers, and also a just man to be their king, who had established peace in the Omni 1:13 (12–15).land of Zarahemla, and who had taught them to John 15:10; D&C 95:12.keep the commandments of God, that they might rejoice and be filled with Deut. 11:1. TG Love.love towards God and all men.

And it came to pass that when they came up to the temple, they pitched their tents round about, every man according to his TG Family, Patriarchal.family, consisting of his wife, and his sons, and his daughters, and their sons, and their daughters, from the eldest down to the youngest, every family being separate one from another.

And they pitched their tents round about the temple, every man having his Ex. 33:8 (8–10).tent with the door thereof towards the temple, that thereby they might remain in their tents and hear the words which king Benjamin should speak unto them;

For the multitude being so great that king Benjamin could not teach them all within the walls of the temple, therefore he caused a Gen. 35:21; Neh. 8:4 (4–5); Mosiah 11:12 (12–13).tower to be erected, that thereby his people might hear the words which he should speak unto them.

And it came to pass that he began to speak to his people from the tower; and they could not all hear his words because of the greatness of the multitude; therefore he caused that the words which he spake should be written and sent forth among those that were not under the sound of his voice, that they might also receive his words.

And these are the words which he Mosiah 8:3.spake and caused to be written, saying: My brethren, all ye that have assembled yourselves together, you that can hear my words which I shall speak unto you this day; for I have not commanded you to come up hither to D&C 6:12; 32:5. TG Mocking.trifle with the words which I shall speak, but that you should TG Teachable.hearken unto me, and open your ears that ye may hear, and your Prov. 8:5; Mosiah 12:27; 3 Ne. 19:33.hearts that ye may understand, and your TG Mind.minds that the TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries of God may be unfolded to your view.
I have not commanded you to come up hither that ye should fear TG Humility.me, or that ye should think that I of myself am more than a mortal man.

But I am like as yourselves, subject to all manner of infirmities in body and mind; yet I have been chosen by this people, and TG Setting Apart.consecrated by Omni 1:23 (23–24). TG Serve; Service.my father, and was suffered by the hand of the Lord that I should be a ruler and a king over this people; and have been kept and preserved by his matchless power, to serve you with all the might, mind and strength which the Lord hath granted unto me.

I say unto you that as I have been suffered to 1 Sam. 12:2 (1–25).spend my days in your service, even up to this time, and have not sought 2 Kgs. 5:16; Acts 20:33 (33–34); Jacob 1:16.gold nor silver nor any manner of riches of you;

Neither have I suffered that ye should be confined in dungeons, nor that ye should make slaves one of another, nor that ye should murder, or plunder, or steal, or commit adultery; nor even have I suffered that ye should commit any manner of wickedness, and have taught you that ye should keep the commandments of the Lord, in all things which he hath commanded you—

And even I, myself, have Deut. 17:17; Neh. 5:14 (14–15); 1 Cor. 9:18 (4–18). TG Self-Sacrifice; Work, Value of.labored with mine own Acts 20:34 (33–35).hands that I might serve you, and that ye should not be Ezek. 46:18.laden with taxes, and that there should nothing come upon you which was grievous to be borne—and of all these things which I have spoken, ye yourselves are witnesses this day.

Yet, my brethren, I have not done these things that I might TG Boast.boast, neither do I tell these things that thereby I might accuse you; but I tell you these things that ye may know that I can answer a clear TG Conscience.conscience before God this day.
Behold, I say unto you that because I said unto you that I had spent my days in your service, I do not desire to boast, for I have only been in the service of God.

And behold, I tell you these things that ye may learn TG Wisdom.wisdom; that ye may learn that when ye are in the Matt. 25:40; D&C 42:31 (30–31). TG Service.service of your TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood; Fellowshipping; Neighbor.fellow beings ye are only in the service of your God.
Behold, ye have called me your king; and if I, whom ye call your king, do labor to Luke 22:26.serve you, then ought not ye to labor to serve one another?

And behold also, if I, whom ye call your king, who has spent his days in your service, and yet has been in the service of God, do merit any thanks from you, O how you ought to 1 Chr. 16:8. TG Thanksgiving.thank your heavenly TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.King!

I say unto you, my brethren, that if you should render all the Job 1:21; Ps. 34:1 (1–3); D&C 59:21; 62:7; 78:19.thanks and 1 Sam. 12:24; 1 Ne. 18:16; D&C 136:28.praise which your whole soul has power to possess, to that God who has created you, and has kept and D&C 63:3.preserved you, and has caused that ye should Neh. 12:43.rejoice, and has granted that ye should live in peace one with another—

I say unto you that if ye should Job 22:3 (3–4).serve him who has created you from the beginning, and is Neh. 9:6.preserving you from day to day, by lending you 2 Ne. 9:26.breath, that ye may live and move and do according to your own TG Agency.will, and even supporting you from one moment to another—I say, if ye should serve him with all your TG Dedication.whole souls yet ye would be Luke 17:10 (7–10); Rom. 3:12.unprofitable servants.

And behold, all that he TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.requires of you is to Gen. 4:7; Lev. 25:18 (18–19); Mosiah 1:7; Alma 50:20 (20–22).keep his commandments; and he has 1 Ne. 4:14; Omni 1:6; Ether 2:7 (7–12).promised you that if ye would keep his commandments ye should prosper in the land; and he never doth TG God, Perfection of.vary from that which he hath said; therefore, if ye do Ps. 19:11 (9–11); 2 Ne. 1:20; D&C 14:7; 58:2.keep his TG Commandments of God.commandments he doth bless you and prosper you.
And now, in the first place, he hath created you, and granted unto you your lives, for which ye are indebted unto him.

And secondly, he doth TG Duty.require that ye should do as he hath commanded you; for which if ye do, he doth immediately Prov. 22:4 (4–5); 2 Ne. 1:20.bless you; and therefore he hath paid you. And ye are still indebted unto him, and are, and will be, forever and ever; therefore, of what have ye to boast?

And now I ask, can ye say aught of yourselves? I answer you, Nay. Ye cannot say that ye are even as much as the dust of the earth; yet ye were TG Man, Physical Creation of.created of the Jacob 2:21; Alma 42:2; Hel. 12:7 (7–8); Morm. 9:17.dust of the earth; but behold, it 1 Chr. 29:12; Mosiah 4:22.belongeth to him who created you.

And I, even I, whom ye call your king, am TG Equal.no better than ye yourselves are; for I am also of the dust. And ye behold that I am old, and am about to yield up this mortal frame to its mother earth.

Therefore, as I said unto you that I had TG Serve; Service.served you, TG Walking with God.walking with a clear conscience before God, even so I at this time have caused that ye should assemble yourselves together, that I might be found blameless, and that your Jacob 1:19.blood should not come upon me, when I shall stand to be judged of God of the things whereof he hath commanded me concerning you.

I say unto you that I have caused that ye should assemble yourselves together that I might 2 Ne. 9:44; Jacob 2:2 (2, 16); D&C 61:34.rid my garments of your blood, at this period of time when I am about to go down to my grave, that I might go down in peace, and my immortal TG Spirit Body.spirit may join the Morm. 7:7.choirs above in singing the praises of a just God.
And moreover, I say unto you that I have caused that ye should assemble yourselves together, that I might declare unto you that I can no longer be your teacher, nor your king;

For even at this time, my whole frame doth tremble exceedingly while attempting to speak unto you; but the Lord God doth support me, and hath suffered me that I should speak unto you, and hath commanded me that I should declare unto you this day, that my son Mosiah is a Mosiah 1:10; 6:3 (3–4). TG Kings, Earthly.king and a ruler over you.

And now, my brethren, I would that ye should do as ye have hitherto done. As ye have kept my commandments, and also the commandments of my father, and have prospered, and have been kept from falling into the hands of your enemies, even so if ye shall keep the commandments of my son, or the commandments of God which shall be delivered unto you by him, ye shall prosper in the land, and your enemies shall have no power over you.

But, O my people, beware lest there shall arise Eph. 4:27 (26–27); 3 Ne. 11:29. TG Contention.contentions among you, and ye Alma 3:27 (26–27); 5:42 (41–42); 30:60; D&C 29:45.list to 2 Ne. 32:8; Mosiah 4:14; Alma 5:20.obey the evil spirit, which was spoken of by my father Mosiah.

For behold, there is a wo pronounced upon him who listeth to TG Agency.obey that spirit; for if he listeth to obey him, and remaineth and dieth in his TG Accountability.sins, the same drinketh TG Damnation.damnation to his own soul; for he receiveth for his wages an Jacob 6:10; D&C 19:11 (10–12).everlasting TG Punish.punishment, having transgressed the law of God contrary to his own knowledge.

I say unto you, that there are not any among you, except it be your little children that have not been taught concerning these things, but what knoweth that ye are eternally Philip. 3:8 (7–10). TG Debt.indebted to your heavenly Father, to render to him Rom. 12:1 (1–2).all that you have and are; and also have been taught concerning the 1 Ne. 5:14; Alma 33:2.records which contain the prophecies which have been spoken by the holy prophets, even down to the time our father, Lehi, left Jerusalem;
And also, all that has been spoken by our fathers until now. And behold, also, they spake that which was commanded them of the Lord; therefore, they are Rom. 7:12; Rev. 15:3.just and true.

And now, I say unto you, my brethren, that after ye have known and have been taught all these things, if ye should transgress and go TG Disobedience.contrary to that which has been spoken, that ye do TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.withdraw yourselves from the Spirit of the Lord, that it may have no place in you to guide you in wisdom’s paths that ye may be blessed, prospered, and preserved—

I say unto you, that the man that doeth this, the same cometh out in open Mosiah 3:12; Hel. 8:25 (24–25). TG Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against; Rebellion.rebellion against God; therefore he Prov. 19:27; 1 Ne. 8:34 (33–34).listeth to obey the evil spirit, and becometh an enemy to all righteousness; therefore, the Lord has no place in him, for he dwelleth not in Alma 7:21; 34:36; Hel. 4:24.unholy temples.

Therefore if that man TG Repent.repenteth not, and remaineth and dieth an enemy to God, the demands of divine TG God, Justice of.justice do awaken his immortal soul to a lively sense of his own Mosiah 27:29 (25–29). TG Guilt.guilt, which doth cause him to shrink from the TG God, Presence of.presence of the Lord, and doth fill his breast with guilt, and TG Pain.pain, and TG Sorrow.anguish, which is like an unquenchable TG Hell.fire, whose flame ascendeth up forever and ever.
And now I say unto you, that TG Mercy.mercy hath no claim on that man; therefore his final doom is to endure a never-ending TG Damnation; Punish.torment.

O, all ye TG Old Age.old men, and also ye young men, and you little children who can understand my words, for I have spoken plainly unto you that ye might understand, I pray that ye should awake to a Alma 5:18 (7–18).remembrance of the awful situation of those that have fallen into transgression.

And moreover, I would desire that ye should consider on the blessed and Matt. 11:29 (28–30); Alma 50:23; 4 Ne. 1:16 (15–18). TG Happiness; Joy.happy state of those that keep the commandments of God. For behold, they are Gen. 39:3 (1–6); Ps. 37:25; Matt. 6:33; 1 Ne. 17:3 (1–5, 12–14).blessed in all things, both temporal and spiritual; and if they hold out Ps. 31:23; Ether 4:19; D&C 6:13; 63:47.faithful to the end they are received into TG Heaven.heaven, that thereby they may dwell with God in a state of never-ending happiness. O remember, remember that these things are true; for the Lord God hath spoken it.

div.p: King Benjamin continues his address—The Lord Omnipotent will minister among men in a tabernacle of clay—Blood will come from every pore as He atones for the sins of the world—His is the only name whereby salvation comes—Men can put off the natural man and become Saints through the Atonement—The torment of the wicked will be as a lake of fire and brimstone. About 124 B.C.

chapter.verse: And again my brethren, I would call your attention, for I have somewhat more to speak unto you; for behold, I have things to tell you concerning that which is to come.
And the things which I shall tell you are made known unto me by an Mosiah 4:1; 5:5. TG Angels.angel from God. And he said unto me: Zech. 4:1 (1–2).Awake; and I awoke, and behold he stood before me.

And he said unto me: Awake, and hear the words which I shall tell thee; for behold, I am come to declare unto you the Isa. 52:7 (7–10); Luke 2:10 (10–11); Rom. 10:15; D&C 31:3.glad tidings of great TG Joy.joy.

For the Lord hath heard thy prayers, and hath judged of thy TG Righteousness.righteousness, and hath sent me to declare unto thee that thou mayest rejoice; and that thou mayest declare unto thy people, that they may also be filled with joy.

For behold, the time cometh, and is not far distant, that with power, the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Lord Rev. 1:8 (7–8). TG Jesus Christ, Power of.Omnipotent who TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.reigneth, who was, and is from all 2 Ne. 26:12; Mosiah 16:15.eternity to all eternity, shall come down from heaven among the children of men, and shall dwell in a Mosiah 7:27; 15:2 (1–7); Alma 7:9 (9–13).tabernacle of clay, and shall go forth amongst men, working mighty Matt. 4:24 (23–24); 9:35; Acts 2:22; 1 Ne. 11:31. TG Death, Power over; Heal; Miracle.miracles, such as healing the sick, raising the dead, causing the lame to walk, the Matt. 9:28 (28–31); 20:30 (30–34); John 9:1 (1–4); 3 Ne. 17:9 (7–10); D&C 84:69.blind to receive their sight, and the deaf to hear, and curing all manner of diseases.
And he shall cast out Mark 1:34 (32–34); 1 Ne. 11:31.devils, or the TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.evil spirits which dwell in the hearts of the children of men.

And lo, he shall Luke 12:50. TG Suffering.suffer TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Temptation.temptations, and pain of body, Matt. 4:2 (1–2).hunger, thirst, and fatigue, even more than man can D&C 19:16 (15–18).suffer, except it be unto death; for behold, Matt. 26:39 (38–39); Luke 22:44.blood cometh from every pore, so great shall be his Isa. 53:4 (4–5).anguish for the wickedness and the abominations of his people.

And he shall be called TG Foreordination; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Jesus TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Christ, the Mosiah 15:3; Alma 7:10; 3 Ne. 1:14.Son of God, the Mosiah 15:4; Hel. 14:12; 3 Ne. 9:15; Ether 4:7.Father of heaven and earth, the TG Jesus Christ, Creator.Creator of all things from the beginning; and his Matt. 1:16; 1 Ne. 11:18 (14–21).mother shall be called Mary.

And lo, he cometh unto his own, that TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.salvation might come unto the children of men even through TG Faith.faith on his name; and even after all this they shall consider him a man, and say that he hath a Luke 11:15 (14–22); John 8:48; 12:37; Hel. 13:26 (26–27).devil, and shall Luke 18:33; 1 Ne. 11:33; 2 Ne. 10:3.scourge him, and shall 1 Ne. 19:10 (10, 13); 2 Ne. 6:9; 3 Ne. 11:14 (14–15, 33). TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucify him.

And he shall TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.rise the 2 Ne. 25:13; Hel. 14:20 (20–27).third day from the dead; and behold, he standeth to TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge the world; and behold, all these things are done that a righteous judgment might come upon the children of men.

For behold, and also his TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Redemption.atoneth for the sins of those who have TG Fall of Man.fallen by the transgression of Adam, who have died not knowing the TG God, Will of.will of God concerning them, or who have Lev. 4:13 (13–35); Num. 15:27 (2–29); 2 Ne. 9:26 (25–26); Alma 9:16 (15–16); 42:21; 3 Ne. 6:18. TG Accountability; Ignorance.ignorantly sinned.

But wo, wo unto him who knoweth that he Mosiah 2:37 (36–38); Hel. 8:25 (24–25). TG Rebellion.rebelleth against God! For salvation cometh to none such except it be through repentance and faith on the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord Jesus Christ.

And the Lord God hath sent his holy TG Prophets, Mission of.prophets among all the children of men, to declare these things to every kindred, nation, and tongue, that thereby whosoever should believe that Christ should come, the same might receive TG Remission of Sins.remission of their sins, and rejoice with exceedingly great joy, even 2 Ne. 25:26 (24–27); Jarom 1:11; Mosiah 16:6.as though he had already come among them.

Yet the Lord God saw that his people were a 2 Ne. 25:28; Alma 9:31. TG Stiffnecked.stiffnecked people, and he appointed unto them a Josh. 1:8; Mosiah 13:29 (29–32); Alma 25:15 (15–16); D&C 41:5 (4–5).law, even the TG Law of Moses.law of Moses.

And many signs, and wonders, and TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Passover; Symbolism.types, and shadows showed he unto them, concerning his coming; and also holy prophets spake unto them concerning his coming; and yet they TG Hardheartedness.hardened their hearts, and understood not that the Heb. 10:1; Mosiah 12:31; 13:28 (27–32); Alma 25:16.law of Moses availeth nothing 2 Ne. 11:6.except it were through the Lev. 4:20; Matt. 26:54 (51–56).atonement of his blood.

And even if it were possible that little TG Conceived in Sin.children could sin they could not be saved; but I say unto you they are TG Salvation of Little Children.blessed; for behold, as in Adam, or by nature, they fall, even so the blood of Christ Moro. 8:8 (8–9); Moses 6:54 (54–56). TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atoneth for their sins.

And moreover, I say unto you, that there shall be Acts 4:12 (10–12); 2 Ne. 31:21; Mosiah 4:8 (7–8); 3 Ne. 9:17.no other name given nor any other way nor means whereby Matt. 7:14 (13–14). TG Jesus Christ, Savior; Salvation, Plan of.salvation can come unto the children of men, only in and through the name of Christ, the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord Omnipotent.

For behold he judgeth, and his judgment is just; and the infant perisheth not that dieth in his infancy; but men drink 1 Cor. 11:29.damnation to their own souls except they humble themselves and Matt. 18:3.become as little children, and believe that TG Salvation.salvation was, and is, and is to come, in and through the Mosiah 4:2; Hel. 5:9.atoning blood of Christ, the Lord Omnipotent.

For the Gen. 8:21; 1 Cor. 2:14 (11–14); 2 Pet. 2:12; Mosiah 16:3; Alma 41:11; Ether 3:2. TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn; Worldliness.natural TG Mortality.man is an James 4:4. TG Enemies.enemy to God, and has been from the TG Fall of Man.fall of Adam, and will be, forever and ever, unless he 2 Chr. 30:8; Rom. 6:13 (12–14).yields to the enticings of the Rom. 8:4 (1–9). TG Guidance, Divine.Holy Spirit, and Alma 19:6.putteth off the Col. 3:9; D&C 67:12.natural man and becometh a Luke 22:32 (31–38). TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn; Saints; Spirituality.saint through the atonement of Christ the Lord, and becometh as a Matt. 18:3; 1 Pet. 2:2 (1–3); 3 Ne. 9:22.child, TG Self-Mastery; Submissiveness.submissive, meek, humble, patient, full of love, willing to submit to all things which the Lord seeth fit to inflict upon him, even as a child doth submit to his father.

And moreover, I say unto you, that the time shall come when the D&C 3:16.knowledge of a TG Jesus Christ, Savior.Savior shall spread throughout Mosiah 16:1. TG Missionary Work.every nation, kindred, tongue, and people.

And behold, when that time cometh, none shall be found Col. 1:22; D&C 4:2. TG Accountability.blameless before God, except it be little children, only through repentance and faith on the name of the Lord God Omnipotent.

And even at this time, when thou shalt have taught thy people the things which the Lord thy God hath commanded thee, even then are they found no more blameless in the sight of God, only according to the words which I have spoken unto thee.
And now I have spoken the words which the Lord God hath commanded me.

And thus saith the Lord: They shall stand as a bright testimony against this people, at the judgment day; whereof they shall be judged, every man according to his TG Good Works.works, whether they be good, or whether they be evil.

And if they be evil they are consigned to an awful 2 Ne. 9:14, 46; Alma 5:18; 11:43; 12:15 (14–15).view of their own guilt and abominations, which doth cause them to shrink from the presence of the Lord into a state of Rom. 3:16; Morm. 8:38.misery and TG Punish.endless torment, from whence they can no more return; therefore they have drunk damnation to their own souls.

Therefore, they have drunk out of the Ps. 75:8; Jer. 25:15; Lam. 4:21.cup of the wrath of God, which justice could no more deny unto them than it could deny that Morm. 9:12; Moro. 8:8.Adam should fall because of his partaking of the forbidden Gen. 3:6; 2 Ne. 2:15 (15–19); Alma 12:22 (21–23).fruit; therefore, TG Mercy.mercy could have claim on them no more forever.

And their TG Hell.torment is as a 2 Ne. 9:16; Jacob 6:10; Alma 12:17; D&C 76:36.lake of fire and brimstone, whose flames are unquenchable, and whose smoke ascendeth up Mosiah 5:5.forever and ever. Thus hath the Lord commanded me. Amen.

div.p: King Benjamin continues his address—Salvation comes because of the Atonement—Believe in God to be saved—Retain a remission of your sins through faithfulness—Impart of your substance to the poor—Do all things in wisdom and order. About 124 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, it came to pass that when king Benjamin had made an end of speaking the words which had been delivered unto him by the 1 Chr. 21:18; Mosiah 3:2.angel of the Lord, that he cast his eyes round about on the multitude, and behold they had Neh. 8:9; Alma 19:17.fallen to the earth, for the Jer. 36:16; Heb. 12:28. TG Reverence.fear of the Lord had come upon them.

And they had TG Poor in Spirit.viewed themselves in their own Neh. 9:1 (1–3). TG Carnal Mind.carnal state, even Gen. 18:27.less than the dust of the earth. And they all cried aloud with one voice, saying: O have mercy, and apply the Mosiah 3:18; Hel. 5:9.atoning blood of Christ that we may receive forgiveness of our sins, and our hearts may be TG Purification.purified; for we believe in Jesus Christ, the Son of God, who TG Jesus Christ, Creator.created heaven and earth, and all things; who shall come down among the children of men.

And it came to pass that after they had spoken these words the Spirit of the Lord came upon them, and they were filled with joy, having received a TG Remission of Sins.remission of their sins, and having peace of TG Conscience.conscience, because of the exceeding TG Faith.faith which they had in Jesus Christ who should come, according to the Neh. 8:12.words which king Benjamin had spoken unto them.

And king Benjamin again opened his mouth and began to speak unto them, saying: My friends and my brethren, my kindred and my people, I would again call your attention, that ye may hear and understand the remainder of my words which I shall speak unto you.
For behold, if the knowledge of the goodness of God at this time has awakened you to a sense of your Moses 1:10.nothingness, and your worthless and fallen state—

I say unto you, if ye have come to a TG God, Knowledge about; God, Perfection of.knowledge of the goodness of God, and his matchless power, and his wisdom, and his patience, and his long-suffering towards the children of men; and also, the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement which has been prepared from the Mosiah 15:19; 18:13.foundation of the world, that thereby salvation might come to him that should put his Ps. 36:7 (7–8); 2 Ne. 22:2; Hel. 12:1. TG Trust in God.trust in the Lord, and should be diligent in keeping his commandments, and continue in the faith even unto the end of his life, I mean the life of the mortal body—

I say, that this is the man who receiveth salvation, through the atonement which was prepared from the foundation of the world for all mankind, which ever were since the TG Fall of Man.fall of Adam, or who are, or who ever shall be, even unto the end of the world.

And this is the means whereby salvation cometh. And there is Acts 4:12; 2 Ne. 31:21; Mosiah 3:17.none other salvation save this which hath been spoken of; neither are there any conditions whereby man can be saved except the conditions which I have told you.

Believe in Deut. 4:39.God; believe that he is, and that he TG God, Creator; Jesus Christ, Creator.created all things, both in heaven and in earth; believe that he has all Rom. 11:34 (33–36); Jacob 4:8 (8–13). TG God, Perfection of.wisdom, and all power, both in heaven and in earth; believe that man doth not Isa. 55:9.comprehend all the things which the Lord can comprehend.

And again, believe that ye must TG Repent.repent of your sins and forsake them, and humble yourselves before God; and ask in TG Sincere.sincerity of heart that he would Ps. 41:4; D&C 61:2.forgive you; and now, if you Matt. 7:24 (24–27); Acts 16:31 (30–31).believe all these things see that ye 2 Ne. 31:19 (19–21).do them.

And again I say unto you as I have said before, that as ye have come to the knowledge of the glory of God, or if ye have known of his goodness and have Ps. 34:8; Alma 36:24 (24–26).tasted of his love, and have received a TG Remission of Sins.remission of your sins, which causeth such exceedingly great joy in your souls, even so I would that ye should remember, and always retain in remembrance, the greatness of God, and your own Rom. 5:8 (6–8); Moses 1:10.nothingness, and his Ex. 34:6 (5–7); Moro. 8:3.goodness and long-suffering towards you, unworthy creatures, and humble yourselves even in the depths of TG Humility.humility, TG Prayer.calling on the name of the Lord daily, and standing TG Steadfastness.steadfastly in the faith of that which is to come, which was spoken by the mouth of the angel.

And behold, I say unto you that if ye do this ye shall always rejoice, and be filled with the TG God, Love of.love of God, and always Mosiah 4:26; Alma 4:14 (13–14); 5:26 (26–35); D&C 20:32 (31–34).retain a remission of your sins; and ye shall grow in the TG God, Knowledge about.knowledge of the glory of him that created you, or in the knowledge of that which is just and true.
And ye will not have a TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.mind to injure one another, but to live TG Peacemakers.peaceably, and to render to every man according to that which is his due.

And ye will not suffer your 1 Tim. 5:8; D&C 83:4. TG Marriage, Fatherhood.children that they go hungry, or naked; neither will ye Prov. 13:24.suffer that they transgress the laws of God, and fight and TG Contention.quarrel one with another, and serve the devil, who is the master of sin, or who is the 2 Ne. 32:8; Mosiah 2:32.evil spirit which hath been spoken of by our fathers, he being an enemy to all righteousness.

But ye will Mosiah 1:2; Moses 6:58 (58–63). TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.teach them to TG Walking with God.walk in the ways of truth and TG Sincere.soberness; ye will teach them to 1 Sam. 18:1; Mosiah 18:21. TG Family, Love within.love one another, and to serve one another.

And also, ye yourselves will Prov. 19:17. TG Charity; Service; Welfare.succor those that stand in need of your succor; ye will administer of your substance unto him that standeth in need; and ye will not suffer that the Prov. 21:13; Isa. 10:2; Luke 3:11; D&C 38:16.beggar putteth up his petition to you in vain, and turn him out to perish.

Perhaps thou shalt Prov. 17:5.say: The man has brought upon himself his misery; therefore I will stay my hand, and will not give unto him of my food, nor impart unto him of my substance that he may not suffer, for his punishments are just—

But I say unto you, O man, whosoever doeth this the same hath great cause to repent; and except he repenteth of that which he hath done he perisheth forever, and hath no interest in the kingdom of God.

For behold, are we not all Prov. 22:2; 1 Cor. 4:7.beggars? Do we not all depend upon the same Being, even God, for all the substance which we have, for both food and raiment, and for gold, and for silver, and for all the riches which we have of every kind?

And behold, even at this time, ye have been calling on his name, and begging for a Rom. 2:4 (1–4).remission of your sins. And has he suffered that ye have begged in vain? Nay; he has poured out his TG God, Spirit of.Spirit upon you, and has caused that your hearts should be filled with TG Joy.joy, and has caused that your mouths should be stopped that ye could not find utterance, so exceedingly great was your joy.

And now, if God, who has created you, on whom you are dependent for your lives and for all that ye have and are, doth grant unto you whatsoever ye ask that is right, in faith, believing that ye shall receive, O then, how ye ought to Dan. 4:27. TG Generosity; Welfare.impart of the substance that ye have one to another.

And if ye Matt. 7:2 (1–2); John 7:24.judge the man who putteth up his petition to you for your substance that he perish not, and condemn him, how much more just will be your 1 Jn. 3:17.condemnation for withholding your substance, which doth not belong to you but to God, to whom also your life Mosiah 2:25.belongeth; and yet ye put up no petition, nor repent of the thing which thou hast done.
I say unto you, wo be unto that man, for his substance shall perish with him; and now, I say these things unto those who are Luke 21:1 (1–4); D&C 56:16.rich as pertaining to the things of this world.

And again, I say unto the poor, ye who have not and yet have sufficient, that ye remain from day to day; I mean all you who deny the beggar, because ye have not; I would that ye say in your hearts that: I Deut. 16:17.give not because I Acts 3:6 (5–7).have not, but if I had I would Mark 12:44.give.
And now, if ye say this in your hearts ye remain guiltless, otherwise ye are D&C 56:17.condemned; and your condemnation is just for ye covet that which ye have not received.

And now, for the sake of these things which I have spoken unto you—that is, for the sake of retaining a remission of your sins from day to day, that ye may TG Walking with God.walk guiltless before God—I would that ye should Jacob 2:19 (17–19); Mosiah 21:17; Alma 35:9.impart of your substance to the Job 29:12; Zech. 7:10; Luke 18:22; Alma 1:27. TG Almsgiving.poor, every man according to that which he hath, such as Isa. 58:10 (9–11); Alma 4:12 (12–13); 3 Ne. 12:42.feeding the hungry, clothing the naked, visiting the sick and administering to their relief, both spiritually and temporally, according to their wants.

And see that all these things are done in wisdom and TG Order.order; for it is not requisite that a man should run Eccl. 9:11; Alma 1:26; D&C 10:4.faster than he has strength. And again, it is expedient that he should be diligent, that thereby he might win the prize; therefore, all things must be done in order.

And I would that ye should remember, that whosoever among you TG Borrow; Debt; Honesty.borroweth of his neighbor should return the thing that he borroweth, according as he doth agree, or else thou shalt commit sin; and perhaps thou shalt cause thy neighbor to commit sin also.
And finally, I cannot tell you all the things whereby ye may commit sin; for there are divers ways and means, even so many that I cannot number them.

But this much I can tell you, that if ye do not Deut. 4:9; Alma 12:14. TG Watch.watch yourselves, and your Matt. 5:28 (27–28); Mark 7:23 (15–23).thoughts, and your Matt. 15:18 (18–20). TG Gossip.words, and your deeds, and observe the commandments of God, and TG Steadfastness.continue in the faith of what ye have heard concerning the coming of our Lord, even unto the end of your lives, ye must perish. And now, O man, remember, and perish not.

div.p: The Saints become the sons and daughters of Christ through faith—They are then called by the name of Christ—King Benjamin exhorts them to be steadfast and immovable in good works. About 124 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, it came to pass that when king Benjamin had thus spoken to his people, he sent among them, desiring to know of his people if they 1 Ne. 11:4 (1–5).believed the words which he had spoken unto them.

And they all cried with one voice, saying: Yea, we believe all the words which thou hast spoken unto us; and also, we know of their surety and truth, because of the Spirit of the Lord Omnipotent, which has wrought a mighty Rom. 8:2 (1–4); Alma 5:14; 13:12. TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.change in us, or in our hearts, that we have no more disposition to do Alma 19:33.evil, but to do good continually.

And we, ourselves, also, through the infinite Ex. 34:6 (5–7).goodness of God, and the manifestations of his Spirit, have great views of that which is to come; and were it expedient, we could prophesy of all things.
And it is the faith which we have had on the things which our king has spoken unto us that has brought us to this great knowledge, whereby we do rejoice with such exceedingly great joy.

And we are willing to enter into a 2 Chr. 15:12 (12–15); Neh. 10:29; Mosiah 6:3. TG Commitment.covenant with our God to do his will, and to be obedient to his commandments in all things that he shall command us, all the remainder of our days, that we may not bring upon ourselves a Mosiah 3:27 (25–27).never-ending torment, as has been spoken by the Mosiah 3:2.angel, that we may not drink out of the cup of the wrath of God.

And now, these are the words which king Benjamin desired of them; and therefore he said unto them: Ye have spoken the words that I desired; and the covenant which ye have made is a righteous covenant.

And now, because of the covenant which ye have made ye shall be called the 1 Jn. 2:12; Mosiah 15:2; 27:25 (24–26); Moses 6:68 (64–68).children of Christ, his sons, and his daughters; for behold, this day he hath spiritually begotten you; for ye say that your hearts are TG Sanctification.changed through faith on his name; therefore, ye are John 1:13 (12–13); Mosiah 15:10. TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.born of him and have become his Matt. 12:49 (49–50); D&C 11:30.sons and his daughters.

And under this head ye are made Rom. 6:18 (14–22); 1 Cor. 7:22; Gal. 5:1; D&C 88:86.free, and there is Acts 4:12; Mosiah 4:8; Alma 21:9.no other head whereby ye can be made free. There is no other Gal. 3:27; Mosiah 1:11; 26:18.name given whereby salvation cometh; therefore, I would that ye should Acts 11:26; Alma 46:15.take upon you the name of Christ, all you that have entered into the covenant with God that ye should be obedient unto the end of your lives.
And it shall come to pass that whosoever doeth this shall be found at the right hand of God, for he shall know the name by which he is called; for he shall be called by the name of Christ.

And now it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall not take upon him the name of Christ must be called by some Alma 5:39 (38–42).other name; therefore, he findeth himself on the Matt. 25:33.left hand of God.

And I would that ye should remember also, that this is the Mosiah 1:11 (11–12). TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name that I said I should give unto you that never should be blotted out, except it be through transgression; therefore, take heed that ye do not transgress, that the name be not blotted out of your hearts.

I say unto you, I would that ye should remember to Num. 6:27; Ps. 119:55; D&C 18:25.retain the name written always in your hearts, that ye are not found on the left hand of God, but that ye hear and know the voice by which ye shall be called, and also, the name by which he shall call you.
For how Jer. 4:22; Mosiah 26:25 (24–27).knoweth a man the master whom he has not served, and who is a stranger unto him, and is far from the thoughts and intents of his heart?

And again, doth a man take an ass which belongeth to his neighbor, and keep him? I say unto you, Nay; he will not even suffer that he shall feed among his flocks, but will drive him away, and cast him out. I say unto you, that even so shall it be among you if ye know not the name by which ye are called.

Therefore, I would that ye should be steadfast and immovable, always abounding in TG Good Works.good works, that Christ, the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah; Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord God Omnipotent, may TG Election.seal you his, that you may be brought to heaven, that ye may have everlasting salvation and eternal life, through the wisdom, and power, and justice, and mercy of him who Col. 1:16; Mosiah 4:2; Alma 11:39.created all things, in heaven and in earth, who is God above all. Amen.

div.p: King Benjamin records the names of the people and appoints priests to teach them—Mosiah reigns as a righteous king. About 124–121 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, king Benjamin thought it was expedient, after having finished speaking to the people, that he should D&C 128:8.take the names of all those who had entered into a covenant with God to keep his commandments.
And it came to pass that there was not one soul, except it were little children, but who had entered into the covenant and had taken upon them the name of Christ.

And again, it came to pass that when king Benjamin had made an end of all these things, and had consecrated his son Mosiah 1:10; 2:30.Mosiah to be a ruler and a king over his people, and had given him all the charges concerning the kingdom, and also had TG Priesthood, Ordination.appointed Mosiah 29:42.priests to Alma 4:7.teach the people, that thereby they might hear and know the commandments of God, and to stir them up in remembrance of the Mosiah 5:5 (5–7).oath which they had made, he dismissed the multitude, and they returned, every one, according to their Num. 1:2; Ether 1:41; D&C 48:6.families, to their own houses.

And Mosiah began to reign in his father’s stead. And he began to reign in the thirtieth year of his age, making in the whole, about four hundred and seventy-six years from the 1 Ne. 1:4; 2:4.time that Lehi left Jerusalem.
And king Benjamin lived three years and he died.

And it came to pass that king Mosiah did TG Walking with God.walk in the ways of the Lord, and did observe his judgments and his statutes, and did keep his commandments in all things whatsoever he commanded him.

And king Mosiah did cause his people that they should till the earth. And he also, himself, did till the earth, that thereby he might 2 Cor. 11:9.not become burdensome to his people, that he might do according to that which his father had done in all things. And there was no contention among all his people for the space of three years.

div.p: Ammon finds the land of Lehi-Nephi, where Limhi is king—Limhi’s people are in bondage to the Lamanites—Limhi recounts their history—A prophet (Abinadi) had testified that Christ is the God and Father of all things—Those who sow filthiness reap the whirlwind, and those who put their trust in the Lord will be delivered. About 121 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, it came to pass that after king Mosiah had had continual peace for the space of three years, he was desirous to know concerning the people who Omni 1:27 (27–30).went up to dwell in the land of 2 Ne. 5:8; Mosiah 9:6.Lehi-Nephi, or in the city of Lehi-Nephi; for his people had heard nothing from them from the time they left the land of Omni 1:13.Zarahemla; therefore, they wearied him with their teasings.
And it came to pass that king Mosiah granted that sixteen of their strong men might go up to the land of Lehi-Nephi, to inquire concerning their brethren.

And it came to pass that on the morrow they started to go up, having with them one Mosiah 8:2.Ammon, he being a strong and mighty man, and a Omni 1:14.descendant of Zarahemla; and he was also their leader.

And now, they knew not the course they should travel in the wilderness to go up to the land of Lehi-Nephi; therefore they wandered many days in the wilderness, even Num. 13:25 (17–25).forty days did they wander.

And when they had wandered forty days they came to a Mosiah 11:13.hill, which is north of the land of Mosiah 9:14 (6, 8, 14); 11:12 (12–13); 22:11 (8, 11); Alma 23:12.Shilom, and there they pitched their tents.

And Mosiah 21:22, 26.Ammon took three of his brethren, and their names were Amaleki, Helem, and Hem, and they went down into the land of 2 Ne. 5:8; Omni 1:12 (12, 27); Mosiah 9:1 (1, 3–4, 14); 28:1 (1, 5); Alma 50:8 (8, 11).Nephi.

And behold, they met the king of the people who were in the land of Nephi, and in the land of Mosiah 22:8.Shilom; and they were surrounded by the king’s guard, and were Mosiah 21:21.taken, and were Alma 17:20.bound, and were committed to Mosiah 21:23 (22–24).prison.

And it came to pass when they had been in prison two days they were again brought before the king, and their bands were loosed; and they stood before the king, and were permitted, or rather Alma 14:19.commanded, that they should answer the questions which he should ask them.

And he said unto them: Behold, I am Mosiah 11:1; 19:16.Limhi, the son of Noah, who was the son of Zeniff, who came up out of the Omni 1:13.land of Zarahemla to inherit this land, which was the land of their fathers, who was made a TG Kings, Earthly.king by the Mosiah 19:26.voice of the people.
And now, Mosiah 21:23 (23–24).I desire to know the cause whereby ye were so bold as to come near the walls of the city, when I, myself, was with my guards without the Josh. 20:4.gate?
And now, for this cause have I suffered that ye should be preserved, that I might inquire of you, or else I should have caused that my guards should have put you to death. Ye are permitted to speak.

And now, when Ammon saw that he was permitted to speak, he went forth and Alma 47:22 (22–23).bowed himself before the king; and rising again he said: O king, I am very thankful before God this day that I am yet alive, and am permitted to speak; and I will endeavor to speak with boldness;

For I am assured that if ye had known me ye would not have suffered that I should have worn these bands. For I am Ammon, and am a Omni 1:14.descendant of Zarahemla, and have come up out of the Omni 1:13.land of Zarahemla to inquire concerning our brethren, whom Mosiah 8:2; 9:1.Zeniff brought up out of that land.

And now, it came to pass that after Limhi had heard the words of Ammon, he was exceedingly Mosiah 21:24.glad, and said: Now, I know of a surety that my brethren who were in the land of Zarahemla are Mosiah 21:25 (25–26).yet alive. And now, I will rejoice; and on the morrow I will cause that my people shall rejoice also.

For behold, we are in bondage to the Lamanites, and are Mosiah 19:15.taxed with a tax which is grievous to be borne. And now, behold, our brethren will deliver us out of our TG Bondage, Physical.bondage, or out of the hands of the Lamanites, and we will be their TG Slavery.slaves; for it is better that we be slaves to the Nephites than to pay tribute to the king of the Lamanites.

And now, king Limhi commanded his guards that they should no more bind Ammon nor his brethren, but caused that they should go to the hill which was north of Shilom, and bring their brethren into the city, that thereby they might eat, and drink, and rest themselves from the labors of their journey; for they had suffered many things; they had suffered hunger, thirst, and fatigue.

And now, it came to pass on the morrow that king Limhi sent a proclamation among all his people, that thereby they might gather themselves together to the 2 Ne. 5:16.temple, to hear the words which he should speak unto them.

And it came to pass that when they had gathered themselves together that he Mosiah 8:1.spake unto them in this wise, saying: O ye, my people, lift up your heads and be comforted; for behold, the time is at hand, or is not far distant, when we shall no longer be in subjection to our enemies, notwithstanding our many strugglings, which have been in vain; yet I trust there Mosiah 22:1 (1–16).remaineth an effectual struggle to be made.

Therefore, lift up your heads, and rejoice, and put your TG Trust in God.trust in Ex. 3:6 (2–10); 1 Ne. 19:10; D&C 136:21.God, in that God who was the God of Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob; and also, that God who Ex. 12:51; 1 Ne. 17:40; Mosiah 12:34; Alma 36:28.brought the children of TG Israel, Origins of.Israel out of the land of Egypt, and caused that they should walk through the Josh. 2:10; 1 Ne. 4:2; 17:26.Red Sea on dry ground, and fed them with Ex. 16:15, 35; Num. 11:7 (7–8); Josh. 5:12; John 6:49; 1 Ne. 17:28.manna that they might not perish in the wilderness; and many more things did he do for them.

And again, that same God has brought our fathers 1 Ne. 2:2 (1–4).out of the land of Jerusalem, and has kept and preserved his people even until now; and behold, it is Deut. 28:15 (1–2, 15–68).because of our iniquities and abominations that he has brought us into bondage.

And ye all are witnesses this day, that Zeniff, who was made king over this people, he being Mosiah 9:3 (1–3).over-zealous to inherit the land of his fathers, therefore being deceived by the cunning and craftiness of king Laman, who having entered into a treaty with king Zeniff, and having yielded up into his hands the possessions of a part of the land, or even the city of Lehi-Nephi, and the city of Shilom; and the land round about—

And all this he did, for the sole purpose of Mosiah 9:10; 10:18.bringing this people into subjection or into bondage. And behold, we at this time do pay TG Tribute.tribute to the king of the Lamanites, to the amount of one half of our corn, and our barley, and even all our grain of every kind, and one half of the increase of our flocks and our herds; and even one half of all we have or possess the king of the Lamanites doth exact of us, or our lives.
And now, is not this grievous to be borne? And is not this, our affliction, great? Now behold, how great reason we have to Prov. 29:2.mourn.

Yea, I say unto you, great are the reasons which we have to Ezek. 24:23; Hel. 9:22.mourn; for behold how many of our brethren have been slain, and their blood has been spilt in vain, and all because of iniquity.

For if this people had not fallen into transgression the Lord would not have suffered that this great evil should come upon them. But behold, they would not hearken unto his words; but there arose contentions among them, even so much that they did shed blood among themselves.

And a Mosiah 17:20 (12–20).prophet of the Lord have they TG Prophets, Rejection of.slain; yea, a chosen man of God, who told them of their wickedness and abominations, and prophesied of many things which are to come, yea, even the coming of Christ.

And because he said unto them that Christ was the Isa. 9:6; 2 Ne. 26:12; Mosiah 27:31 (30–31).God, the Father of all things, and said that he should take upon him the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.image of man, and it should be the Gen. 1:26 (26–28); Ether 3:15 (14–17); D&C 20:18 (17–18).image after which man was created in the beginning; or in other words, he said that man was created after the image of Mosiah 13:34; 15:2 (1–7); Alma 7:9 (9–13).God, and that God should come down among the children of men, and take upon him flesh and blood, and go forth upon the face of the earth—

And now, because he said this, they did Mosiah 17:13.put him to death; and many more things did they do which brought down the wrath of God upon them. Therefore, who wondereth that they are in bondage, and that they are smitten with sore afflictions?

For behold, the Lord hath said: I will not Josh. 24:20; 1 Sam. 12:15; 2 Chr. 24:20.succor my people in the day of their transgression; but I will hedge up their ways that they prosper not; and their doings shall be as a Jer. 6:21.stumbling block before them.

And again, he saith: If my people shall sow TG Filthiness.filthiness they shall Hosea 8:7; Gal. 6:8 (7–8); D&C 6:33. TG Harvest.reap the Luke 3:17; 2 Ne. 15:24.chaff thereof in the whirlwind; and the effect thereof is poison.
And again he saith: If my people shall sow filthiness they shall reap the Gen. 41:23; Jer. 18:17; Ezek. 27:26; Jonah 4:8; Mosiah 12:6.east wind, which bringeth immediate destruction.
And now, behold, the promise of the Lord is fulfilled, and ye are smitten and afflicted.

But if ye will Deut. 30:10; Lam. 5:21; Morm. 9:6; D&C 98:47.turn to the Lord with full purpose of heart, and put your trust in him, and serve him with all TG Diligence.diligence of mind, if ye do this, he will, according to his own will and pleasure, deliver you out of bondage.

div.p: Ammon teaches the people of Limhi—He learns of the twenty-four Jaredite plates—Ancient records can be translated by seers—No gift is greater than seership. About 121 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that after king Limhi had made an end of Mosiah 7:18 (18–33).speaking to his people, for he spake many things unto them and only a few of them have I written in this book, he told his people all the things concerning their brethren who were in the land of Zarahemla.

And he caused that Ammon should stand up before the multitude, and rehearse unto them all that had happened unto their brethren from the time that Mosiah 7:13.Zeniff went up out of the land even until the time that he Mosiah 7:3.himself came up out of the land.

And he also rehearsed unto them the last words which king Benjamin had Mosiah 2:9.taught them, and explained them to the people of king Limhi, so that they might understand all the words which he spake.
And it came to pass that after he had done all this, that king Limhi dismissed the multitude, and caused that they should return every one unto his own house.

And it came to pass that he caused that the See Mosiah 9–22.plates which contained the Mosiah 9:1; 22:14.record of his people from the time that they left the Omni 1:13.land of Zarahemla, should be brought before Ammon, that he might read them.
Now, as soon as Ammon had read the record, the king inquired of him to know if he could 1 Cor. 12:10; Mosiah 21:28.interpret languages, and Ammon told him that he could not.

And the king said unto him: Being grieved for the afflictions of my people, I caused that Mosiah 21:25.forty and three of my people should take a journey into the wilderness, that thereby they might find the land of Zarahemla, that we might appeal unto our brethren to deliver us out of bondage.

And they were lost in the wilderness for the space of Alma 50:29; Hel. 3:4 (3–4); Morm. 6:4.many days, yet they were diligent, and found not the land of Zarahemla but returned to this land, having traveled in a land among many waters, having discovered a land which was covered with Omni 1:22; Mosiah 21:26 (26–27).bones of men, and of beasts, and was also covered with ruins of buildings of every kind, having discovered a land which had been peopled with a people who were as numerous as the hosts of Israel.
And for a testimony that the things that they had said are true they have brought Mosiah 21:27; 22:14.twenty-four plates which are filled with engravings, and they are of pure gold.
And behold, also, they have brought Ex. 25:7; Ether 15:15.breastplates, which are large, and they are of Ether 10:23.brass and of copper, and are perfectly sound.

And again, they have brought swords, the hilts thereof have perished, and the blades thereof were cankered with rust; and there is no one in the land that is able to interpret the language or the engravings that are on the plates. Therefore I said unto thee: Canst thou translate?

And I say unto thee again: Knowest thou of any one that can translate? For I am desirous that these records should be translated into our language; for, perhaps, they will give us a knowledge of a remnant of the people who have been destroyed, from whence these records came; or, perhaps, they will give us a knowledge of this very people who have been destroyed; and I am desirous to know the cause of their destruction.

Now Ammon said unto him: I can assuredly tell thee, O king, of a man that can Dan. 5:16; Omni 1:20 (20–22); Mosiah 28:17 (11–17).translate the records; for he has wherewith that he can look, and translate all records that are of ancient date; and it is a gift from God. And the things are called Ex. 28:30; Mosiah 21:28 (27–28). TG Urim and Thummim.interpreters, and no man can look in them except he be commanded, lest he should look for that he ought not and he should perish. And whosoever is commanded to look in them, the same is called TG Seer.seer.
And behold, the king of the people who are in the land of Zarahemla is the man that is commanded to do these things, and who has this high gift from God.
And the king said that a 1 Sam. 9:9; D&C 21:1.seer is greater than a prophet.

And Ammon said that a seer is a revelator and a prophet also; and a gift which is greater can no man have, except he should possess the power of God, which no man can; yet a man may have great power given him from God.

But a seer can know of things which are past, and also of things which are to come, and by them shall all things be revealed, or, rather, shall secret things be made manifest, and hidden things shall come to light, and things which are not known shall be made known by them, and also things shall be made known by them which otherwise could not be known.
Thus God has provided a means that man, through faith, might work mighty miracles; therefore he becometh a great benefit to his fellow beings.

And now, when Ammon had made an end of speaking these words the king rejoiced exceedingly, and gave thanks to God, saying: Doubtless a Ether 3:26 (21–28); 4:4 (1–8).great mystery is contained within these plates, and these interpreters were doubtless prepared for the purpose of unfolding all such mysteries to the children of men.

O how marvelous are the works of the Lord, and how long doth he suffer with his people; yea, and how TG Spiritual Blindness.blind and impenetrable are the understandings of the children of men; for they will not seek wisdom, neither do they desire that IE Wisdom, a feminine noun in Hebrew and Greek. Prov. 9:1; Matt. 11:19.she should rule over them!
Yea, they are as a wild flock which fleeth from the shepherd, and scattereth, and are driven, and are devoured by the beasts of the forest.

div.p: The Record of Zeniff—An account of his people, from the time they left the land of Zarahemla until the time that they were delivered out of the hands of the Lamanites.
Comprising chapters 9 through 22.
Zeniff leads a group from Zarahemla to possess the land of Lehi-Nephi—The Lamanite king permits them to inherit the land—There is war between the Lamanites and Zeniff’s people. About 200–187 B.C.

chapter.verse: Mosiah 8:5; 22:14.I, Mosiah 7:13.Zeniff, having been taught in all the language of the Nephites, and having had a knowledge of the land of 2 Ne. 5:8; Omni 1:12 (12, 27); Mosiah 7:6 (6–7); 28:1 (1, 5).Nephi, or of the land of our fathers’ first inheritance, and having been sent as a spy among the Lamanites that I might spy out their forces, that our army might come upon them and destroy them—but when I saw that which was good among them I was desirous that they should not be destroyed.

Therefore, I contended with my brethren in the wilderness, for I would that our ruler should make a treaty with them; but he being an austere and a blood-thirsty man commanded that I should be slain; but I was rescued by the shedding of much blood; for father fought against father, and brother against brother, until the greater number of our army was destroyed in the wilderness; and we returned, those of us that were spared, to the land of Zarahemla, to relate that tale to their wives and their children.

And yet, I being Omni 1:27 (27–29); Mosiah 7:21.over-zealous to inherit the land of our fathers, collected as many as were desirous to go up to possess the land, and started again on our Mosiah 25:5.journey into the wilderness to go up to the land; but we were smitten with famine and sore afflictions; for we were slow to remember the Lord our God.
Nevertheless, after many days’ wandering in the wilderness we pitched our tents in the place where our brethren were slain, which was near to the land of our fathers.

And it came to pass that I went again with four of my men into the city, in unto the king, that I might know of the disposition of the king, and that I might know if I might go in with my people and possess the land in peace.
And I went in unto the king, and he covenanted with me that I might possess the 2 Ne. 5:8; Mosiah 7:1 (1–4, 21).land of Lehi-Nephi, and the land of Shilom.
And he also commanded that his people should depart out of the land, and I and my people went into the land that we might possess it.
And we began to build buildings, and to repair the walls of the city, yea, even the walls of the city of Lehi-Nephi, and the city of Shilom.

And we began to till the ground, yea, even with all manner of 1 Ne. 8:1; Enos 1:21; Mosiah 10:4.seeds, with seeds of corn, and of wheat, and of barley, and with neas, and with sheum, and with seeds of all manner of fruits; and we did begin to multiply and prosper in the land.
Now it was the cunning and the craftiness of king Mosiah 24:3.Laman, to Mosiah 7:22; 10:18.bring my people into bondage, that he yielded up the land that we might possess it.

Therefore it came to pass, that after we had dwelt in the land for the space of twelve years that king Laman began to grow uneasy, lest by any means my people should Ex. 1:9 (9–10).wax strong in the land, and that they could not overpower them and bring them into bondage.

Now they were a TG Laziness.lazy and an Enos 1:20. TG Idolatry.idolatrous people; therefore they were desirous to bring us into bondage, that they might glut themselves with the labors of our hands; yea, that they might feast themselves upon the flocks of our fields.
Therefore it came to pass that king Laman began to stir up his people that they should contend with my people; therefore there began to be wars and contentions in the land.
