For, in the thirteenth year of my reign in the land of Nephi, away on the south of the land of Mosiah 7:5; 11:12 (12–13).Shilom, when my people were watering and Mosiah 10:21.feeding their flocks, and tilling their lands, a numerous host of Lamanites came upon them and began to slay them, and to take off their flocks, and the corn of their fields.
Yea, and it came to pass that they fled, all that were not overtaken, even into the city of Nephi, and did call upon me for protection.

And it came to pass that I did arm them with bows, and with arrows, with swords, and with cimeters, and with clubs, and with slings, and with all manner of weapons which we could invent, and I and my people did go forth against the Lamanites to battle.

Yea, in the TG Strength.strength of the Lord did we go forth to battle against the Lamanites; for I and my people did cry mightily to the Lord that he would Josh. 21:44; Alma 46:7.deliver us out of the hands of our enemies, for we were awakened to a remembrance of the deliverance of our fathers.

And God did Ex. 2:24 (23–24); Ps. 4:1 (1, 3); Dan. 10:12; D&C 35:3; Abr. 1:15 (15–16).hear our cries and did answer our prayers; and we did go forth in his might; yea, we did go forth against the Lamanites, and in one day and a night we did slay three thousand and forty-three; we did slay them even until we had driven them out of our land.
And I, myself, with mine own hands, did help to bury their dead. And behold, to our great sorrow and lamentation, two hundred and seventy-nine of our brethren were slain.

div.p: King Laman dies—His people are wild and ferocious and believe in false traditions—Zeniff and his people prevail against them. About 187–160 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that we again began to establish the kingdom and we again began to possess the land in peace. And I caused that there should be TG Weapon.weapons of war made of every kind, that thereby I might have weapons for my people against the time the Lamanites should come up again to war against my people.

And I set guards round about the land, that the Lamanites might not come upon us again unawares and destroy us; and thus I did guard my people and my flocks, and keep them from falling into the hands of our enemies.
And it came to pass that we did inherit the land of our fathers for many years, yea, for the space of twenty and two years.
And I did cause that the men should till the ground, and raise all manner of Mosiah 9:9.grain and all manner of fruit of every kind.

And I did cause that the women should spin, and toil, and work, and work all manner of fine linen, yea, and Alma 1:29; Hel. 6:13. TG Clothing.cloth of every kind, that we might clothe our nakedness; and thus we did prosper in the land—thus we did have continual peace in the land for the space of twenty and two years.

And it came to pass that king Mosiah 9:10 (10–11); 24:3.Laman died, and his son began to reign in his stead. And he began to stir his people up in rebellion against my people; therefore they began to prepare for war, and to come up to battle against my people.

But I had sent my spies out round about the land of Mosiah 11:12.Shemlon, that I might discover their preparations, that I might guard against them, that they might not come upon my people and destroy them.

And it came to pass that they came up upon the north of the land of Shilom, with their numerous hosts, men 2 Ne. 5:14; Jarom 1:8; Alma 2:12.armed with Enos 1:20; Alma 3:5 (4–5).bows, and with arrows, and with swords, and with cimeters, and with stones, and with slings; and they had their heads shaved that they were naked; and they were girded with a leathern girdle about their loins.

And it came to pass that I caused that the women and children of my people should be hid in the wilderness; and I also caused that all my old men that could bear arms, and also all my young men that were able to bear arms, should gather themselves together to go to battle against the Lamanites; and I did place them in their ranks, every man according to his age.

And it came to pass that we did go up to battle against the Lamanites; and I, even I, in my old age, did go up to battle against the Lamanites. And it came to pass that we did go up in the TG Strength; Trust in God.strength of the Lord to battle.

Now, the Lamanites knew nothing concerning the Lord, nor the strength of the Lord, therefore they depended upon their own strength. Yet they were a strong people, as to the TG Trust Not in the Arm of Flesh.strength of men.

They were a Alma 17:14.wild, and ferocious, and a blood-thirsty people, believing in the 2 Ne. 5:1 (1–3); Mosiah 1:5; Alma 3:8.tradition of their fathers, which is this—Believing that they were driven out of the land of Jerusalem because of the iniquities of their fathers, and that they were TG Injustice.wronged in the wilderness by their brethren, and they were also wronged while crossing the sea;

And again, that they were wronged while in the land of their 1 Ne. 18:23.first inheritance, after they had crossed the sea, and all this because that Nephi was more faithful in keeping the commandments of the Lord—therefore 2 Ne. 5:5 (5–9).he was favored of the Lord, for the Lord heard his prayers and answered them, and he took the lead of their journey in the wilderness.

And his brethren were 1 Ne. 18:10 (10–11).wroth with him because they 1 Ne. 15:8 (8–11); 2 Ne. 1:25 (24–27).understood not the dealings of the Lord; they were also wroth with him upon the waters because they hardened their hearts against the Lord.

And again, they were 2 Ne. 5:1.wroth with him when they had arrived in the promised land, because they said that he had taken the 2 Ne. 5:3 (1–4).ruling of the people out of their hands; and they sought to kill him.

And again, they were wroth with him because he departed into the wilderness as the Lord had commanded him, and took the 2 Ne. 5:12; Mosiah 28:11.records which were engraven on the plates of brass, for they said that he Alma 20:10 (10, 13).robbed them.

And thus they have taught their children that they should hate them, and that they should murder them, and that they should rob and plunder them, and do all they could to destroy them; therefore they have an eternal hatred towards the children of Nephi.

For this very cause has king Laman, by his Mosiah 9:10; 19:28 (26, 28).cunning, and lying craftiness, and his fair promises, deceived me, that I have brought this my people up into this land, that they may destroy them; yea, and we have suffered these many years in the land.

And now I, Zeniff, after having told all these things unto my people concerning the Lamanites, I did stimulate them to go to battle with their might, putting their trust in the Lord; therefore, we did contend with them, face to face.
And it came to pass that we did drive them again out of our land; and we slew them with a great slaughter, even so many that we did not number them.
And it came to pass that we returned again to our own land, and my people again began to Mosiah 9:14 (9, 14).tend their flocks, and to till their ground.
And now I, being old, did confer the kingdom upon one of my sons; therefore, I say no more. And may the Lord Num. 6:24 (22–27).bless my people. Amen.

div.p: King Noah rules in wickedness—He revels in riotous living with his wives and concubines—Abinadi prophesies that the people will be taken into bondage—His life is sought by King Noah. About 160–150 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that Zeniff conferred the kingdom upon Noah, one of his sons; therefore Noah began to reign in his stead; and he did not walk in the ways of his father.

For behold, he did not keep the commandments of God, but he did walk after the desires of his own heart. And he had many wives and Jacob 3:5; Ether 10:5.concubines. And he did 1 Kgs. 14:16; 15:26; 16:2; 21:22; 2 Kgs. 21:2 (1–9); Mosiah 23:12; 29:31.cause his people to commit sin, and do that which was Mosiah 29:18.abominable in the sight of the Lord. Yea, and they did commit 2 Ne. 28:15.whoredoms and Mosiah 23:9.all manner of wickedness.

And he laid a Gen. 47:24; Ether 10:5 (5–6).tax of one fifth part of all they possessed, a fifth part of their gold and of their silver, and a fifth part of their HEB related words: adjective, “shining”; verb, “to overlay or plate with metal.”ziff, and of their copper, and of their brass and their iron; and a fifth part of their fatlings; and also a fifth part of all their grain.

And all this did he take to Prov. 29:3.support himself, and his wives and his TG Concubine.concubines; and also his priests, and their wives and their concubines; thus he had changed the affairs of the kingdom.

For he put down all the priests that had been consecrated by his father, and consecrated new 1 Kgs. 12:31; 2 Chr. 11:14 (13–14); Mosiah 11:11; 12:25 (17, 25).ones in their stead, such as were lifted up in the pride of their hearts.

Yea, and thus they were supported in their laziness, and in their idolatry, and in their whoredoms, by the taxes which king Noah had put upon his people; thus did the people labor exceedingly to support iniquity.
Yea, and they also became idolatrous, because they were deceived by the vain and flattering words of the king and priests; for they did speak flattering things unto them.

And it came to pass that king Noah built many elegant and spacious buildings; and he ornamented them with fine work of wood, and of all manner of Esth. 1:4.precious things, of gold, and of silver, and of iron, and of brass, and of ziff, and of copper;
And he also built him a spacious palace, and a throne in the midst thereof, all of which was of fine wood and was ornamented with gold and silver and with precious things.
And he also caused that his workmen should work all manner of fine work within the walls of the 2 Ne. 5:16.temple, of fine wood, and of copper, and of brass.

And the seats which were set apart for the Mosiah 11:5 (5, 14); 12:17.high priests, which were above all the other seats, he did ornament with pure gold; and he caused a breastwork to be built before them, that they might rest their bodies and their arms upon while they should speak lying and vain words to his people.

And it came to pass that he built a Gen. 35:21; Mosiah 2:7 (7–8); 19:5 (5–6).tower near the temple; yea, a very high tower, even so high that he could stand upon the top thereof and overlook the land of Mosiah 9:14 (6, 8, 14); 22:11 (8, 11).Shilom, and also the land of Mosiah 10:7.Shemlon, which was possessed by the Lamanites; and he could even look over all the land round about.

And it came to pass that he caused many buildings to be built in the land Shilom; and he caused a great tower to be built on the Mosiah 7:5.hill north of the land Shilom, which had been a resort for the children of Nephi at the time they Omni 1:12 (12–13).fled out of the land; and thus he did do with the riches which he obtained by the taxation of his people.

And it came to pass that he placed his heart upon his riches, and he spent his time in TG Rioting and Reveling.riotous living with his wives and his concubines; and so did also his priests spend their time with harlots.

And it came to pass that he planted vineyards round about in the land; and he built wine-presses, and made TG Drunkenness; Wine.wine in abundance; and therefore he became a wine-bibber, and also his people.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites began to come in upon his people, upon small numbers, and to slay them in their fields, and while they were tending their flocks.

And king Noah sent guards round about the land to keep them off; but he did not send a sufficient number, and the Lamanites came upon them and killed them, and drove many of their flocks out of the land; thus the Lamanites began to destroy them, and to exercise their hatred upon them.
And it came to pass that king Noah sent his armies against them, and they were driven back, or they drove them back for a time; therefore, they returned rejoicing in their spoil.

And now, because of this great victory they were lifted up in the pride of their hearts; they did Amos 6:13; D&C 3:4. TG Boast.boast in their own strength, saying that their fifty could stand against thousands of the Lamanites; and thus they did boast, and did delight in blood, and the shedding of the blood of their brethren, and this because of the wickedness of their king and priests.

And it came to pass that there was a man among them whose name was See accounts of Abinadi in Mosiah 11–17.Abinadi; and he went forth among them, and began to prophesy, saying: Behold, thus saith the Lord, and thus hath he commanded me, saying, Go forth, and say unto this people, thus saith the Lord—Wo be unto this people, for I have seen their abominations, and their wickedness, and their whoredoms; and except they repent I will TG Punish; Reproof.visit them in mine anger.

And except they repent and turn to the Lord their God, behold, I will deliver them into the hands of their enemies; yea, and they shall be brought into Mosiah 12:2; D&C 101:79.bondage; and they shall be afflicted by the hand of their enemies.

And it shall come to pass that they shall Ezek. 26:6; 1 Ne. 21:26 (25–26); D&C 43:25.know that I am the Lord their God, and am a Ex. 20:5; Deut. 6:15; 32:21; Mosiah 13:13.jealous God, visiting the iniquities of my people.

And it shall come to pass that except this people repent and turn unto the Lord their God, they shall be brought into bondage; and none shall Hosea 13:10 (4, 10).deliver them, except it be the Lord the Almighty God.

Yea, and it shall come to pass that when they shall Micah 3:4.cry unto me I will be Ps. 10:1; Jer. 2:27; Mosiah 21:15.slow to hear their cries; yea, and I will suffer them that they be smitten by their enemies.

And except they repent in TG Sackcloth.sackcloth and ashes, and cry mightily to the Lord their God, I will not Isa. 1:15 (15–17); 59:2.hear their prayers, neither will I deliver them out of their afflictions; and thus saith the Lord, and thus hath he commanded me.

Now it came to pass that when Abinadi had spoken these words unto them they were wroth with him, and sought to take away his life; but the Lord TG Prophets, Rejection of; Protection, Divine.delivered him out of their hands.

Now when king Noah had heard of the words which Abinadi had spoken unto the people, he was also wroth; and he said: Alma 9:6 (5–6).Who is Abinadi, that I and my people should be judged of him, or Ex. 5:2; Mosiah 12:13.who is the Lord, that shall bring upon my people such great affliction?

I command you to bring Abinadi hither, that I may slay him, for he has said these things that he might TG Provoking.stir up my people to anger one with another, and to raise contentions among my people; therefore I will slay him.

Now the eyes of the people were 1 Kgs. 15:26 (26–34). TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded; therefore they TG Hardheartedness.hardened their hearts against the words of Abinadi, and they sought from that time forward to take him. And king Noah hardened his heart against the word of the Lord, and he did not repent of his evil doings.

div.p: Abinadi is imprisoned for prophesying the destruction of the people and the death of King Noah—The false priests quote the scriptures and pretend to keep the law of Moses—Abinadi begins to teach them the Ten Commandments. About 148 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that after the space of two years that Abinadi came among them in disguise, that they knew him not, and began to TG Missionary Work.prophesy among them, saying: Thus has the Lord commanded me, saying—Abinadi, go and prophesy unto this my people, for they have hardened their hearts against my words; they have repented not of their evil doings; therefore, I will Isa. 65:6 (6–7, 11); Jer. 9:9.visit them in my anger, yea, in my fierce anger will I visit them in their iniquities and abominations.

Yea, wo be unto this generation! And the Lord said unto me: Stretch forth thy hand and prophesy, saying: Thus saith the Lord, it shall come to pass that this generation, because of their iniquities, shall be brought into 1 Kgs. 8:46; Mosiah 11:21; 20:21.bondage, and shall be smitten on the Lam. 3:30; Mosiah 21:3 (3–4, 13).cheek; yea, and shall be driven by men, and shall be slain; and the vultures of the air, and the dogs, yea, and the wild beasts, shall devour their Deut. 28:26.flesh.
And it shall come to pass that the Amos 7:11 (10–11); Mosiah 12:10 (10–12).life of king Noah shall be valued even as a garment in a hot Mosiah 19:20.furnace; for he shall know that I am the Lord.

And it shall come to pass that I will smite this my people with sore afflictions, yea, with famine and with Luke 21:11 (10–13); 2 Ne. 6:15; D&C 97:26 (22–26).pestilence; and I will cause that they shall Mosiah 21:9 (1–15).howl all the day long.
Yea, and I will cause that they shall have Mosiah 21:3.burdens lashed upon their backs; and they shall be driven before like a dumb ass.

And it shall come to pass that I will send forth Ex. 9:18 (13–35); Ezek. 13:13.hail among them, and it shall smite them; and they shall also be smitten with the Jer. 18:17; Ezek. 27:26; Mosiah 7:31.east wind; and Ex. 10:4 (1–12).insects shall pester their land also, and devour their grain.
And they shall be smitten with a great pestilence—and all this will I do because of their D&C 3:18.iniquities and abominations.

And it shall come to pass that except they repent I will utterly Gen. 6:13; Isa. 42:14 (14–15); 2 Ne. 26:10 (10–11); Alma 45:11 (9–14).destroy them from off the face of the earth; yet they shall leave a Morm. 8:14 (14–16).record behind them, and I will preserve them for other nations which shall possess the land; yea, even this will I do that I may discover the abominations of this people to other nations. And many things did Abinadi prophesy against this people.

And it came to pass that they were angry with him; and they took him and carried him bound before the king, and said unto the king: Behold, we have brought a man before thee who has prophesied evil concerning thy people, and saith that God will destroy them.
And he also prophesieth evil concerning thy Amos 7:11 (10–11); Mosiah 12:3.life, and saith that thy life shall be as a garment in a furnace of fire.
And again, he saith that thou shalt be as a stalk, even as a dry stalk of the field, which is run over by the beasts and trodden under foot.

And again, he saith thou shalt be as the blossoms of a thistle, which, when it is fully ripe, if the wind bloweth, it is driven forth upon the face of the land. And he pretendeth the Lord hath spoken it. And he saith all this shall come upon thee except thou repent, and this because of thine iniquities.
And now, O king, what great evil hast thou done, or what great sins have thy people committed, that we should be Mosiah 11:27.condemned of God or judged of this man?
And now, O king, behold, we are Jer. 2:35; Alma 21:6.guiltless, and thou, O king, hast not sinned; therefore, this man has Hel. 13:26 (24–28).lied concerning you, and he has prophesied in vain.
And behold, we are strong, we shall not come into bondage, or be taken captive by our enemies; yea, and thou hast prospered in the land, and thou shalt also prosper.
Behold, here is the man, we deliver him into thy hands; thou mayest do with him as seemeth thee good.

And it came to pass that king Noah caused that Abinadi should be cast into prison; and he commanded that the Mosiah 11:11.priests should gather themselves together that he might hold a council with them what he should do with him.
And it came to pass that they said unto the king: Bring him hither that we may question him; and the king commanded that he should be brought before them.

And they began to question him, that they might cross him, that thereby they might have wherewith to John 8:6.accuse him; but he answered them boldly, and withstood all their questions, yea, to their astonishment; for he did D&C 100:5 (5–6).withstand them in all their questions, and did confound them in all their words.
And it came to pass that one of them said unto him: Mosiah 13:3.What meaneth the words which are written, and which have been taught by our fathers, saying:

Isa. 52:7 (7–10); Nahum 1:15.How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him Mosiah 15:14; 27:37 (36–37).that bringeth good tidings; that publisheth peace; that bringeth good tidings of good; that publisheth salvation; that saith unto Zion, Thy God reigneth;
Mosiah 15:29.Thy watchmen shall lift up the voice; with the voice together shall they sing; for they shall see eye to eye when the Lord shall bring again Zion;
Break forth into joy; sing together ye waste places of Jerusalem; for the Lord hath comforted his people, he hath redeemed Jerusalem;
The Lord hath made bare his holy Isa. 33:2; Omni 1:13.arm in the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our God?

And now Abinadi said unto them: Are you John 3:10 (7–10); Mosiah 11:5.priests, and pretend to teach this people, and to understand the spirit of prophesying, and yet desire to know of me what these things mean?
I say unto you, wo be unto you for perverting the ways of the Lord! For if ye understand these things ye have not taught them; therefore, ye have perverted the ways of the Lord.
Ye have not applied your Prov. 8:5; Mosiah 2:9.hearts to TG Understanding.understanding; therefore, ye have not been wise. Therefore, what teach ye this people?
And they said: We teach the law of Moses.

And again he said unto them: If ye teach the TG Law of Moses.law of Moses why do ye not keep it? Why do ye set your hearts upon Jer. 48:7.riches? Why do ye commit whoredoms and TG Sexual Immorality.spend your strength with harlots, yea, and cause this people to commit sin, that the Lord has cause to send me to prophesy against this people, yea, even a great evil against this people?
Know ye not that I speak the Jacob 7:14; Alma 30:42 (41–42).truth? Yea, ye know that I speak the truth; and you ought to tremble before God.

And it shall come to pass that ye shall be smitten for your iniquities, for ye have said that ye teach the law of Moses. And what know ye concerning the law of Moses? Mosiah 3:15; 13:28 (27–32); Alma 25:16.Doth salvation come by the law of Moses? What say ye?
And they answered and said that salvation did come by the law of Moses.

But now Abinadi said unto them: I know if ye Deut. 27:1; Neh. 9:13; 3 Ne. 25:4.keep the commandments of God ye shall be saved; yea, if ye keep the commandments which the Lord delivered unto Moses in the mount of Ex. 19:18 (9, 16–20); Mosiah 13:5.Sinai, saying:
Ex. 20:2 (2–4).I am the Lord thy God, who hath Ex. 12:51; 1 Ne. 17:40; Mosiah 7:19.brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
Thou shalt have no Hosea 13:4. TG Idolatry.other God before me.
Mosiah 13:12.Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness of any thing in heaven above, or things which are in the earth beneath.

Now Abinadi said unto them, Have ye done all this? I say unto you, Nay, ye have not. And have ye Mosiah 13:25 (25–26).taught this people that they should do all these things? I say unto you, Nay, ye have not.

div.p: Abinadi is protected by divine power—He teaches the Ten Commandments—Salvation does not come by the law of Moses alone—God Himself will make an atonement and redeem His people. About 148 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now when the king had heard these words, he said unto his priests: Away with this fellow, and slay him; for what have we to do with him, for he is John 10:20 (19–20).mad.
And they stood forth and attempted to lay their hands on him; but he withstood them, and said unto them:

1 Ne. 17:48.Touch me not, for God shall smite you if ye lay your hands upon me, for I have not delivered the message which the Lord sent me to deliver; neither have I told you that which ye Mosiah 12:20 (20–24).requested that I should tell; therefore, God will not suffer that I shall be destroyed at this time.

But I must fulfil the commandments wherewith God has commanded me; and because I have told you the truth ye are angry with me. And again, because I have spoken the word of God ye have judged me that I am mad.

Now it came to pass after Abinadi had spoken these words that the people of king Noah durst not lay their hands on him, for the Spirit of the Lord was upon him; and his face Ex. 34:29 (29–35).shone with exceeding luster, even as Moses’ did while in the mount of Sinai, while speaking with the Lord.
And he spake with TG Priesthood, Power of; Teaching with the Spirit.power and authority from God; and he continued his words, saying:

Ye see that ye have not power to slay me, therefore I finish my Prov. 15:10; 1 Ne. 16:2 (1–3); 2 Ne. 9:40.message. Yea, and I perceive that it cuts you to your hearts because I tell you the truth concerning your iniquities.
Yea, and my words fill you with wonder and amazement, and with anger.
But I finish my message; and then it Dan. 3:16 (16–18).matters not whither I go, if it so be that I am saved.
But this much I tell you, what you Alma 25:10.do with me, after this, shall be as a Jer. 26:14; Mosiah 17:18 (13–19).type and a shadow of things which are to come.

And now I read unto you the remainder of the Ex. 20:1 (1–17).commandments of God, for I perceive that they are not written in your hearts; I perceive that ye have studied and taught Micah 2:1 (1–2).iniquity the most part of your lives.

And now, ye remember that I Mosiah 12:36.said unto you: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness of things which are in heaven above, or which are in the earth beneath, or which are in the water under the earth.

And again: Thou shalt not Ex. 20:5.bow down thyself unto them, nor serve them; for I the Lord thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquities of the fathers upon the children, unto the third and fourth generations of them that hate me;
And showing mercy unto thousands of them that love me and keep my commandments.
Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain; for the Lord will not hold him Ex. 20:7; Morm. 7:7; D&C 58:30.guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
Remember the Mosiah 18:23. TG Sabbath.sabbath day, to keep it holy.
Six days shalt thou labor, and do all thy work;

But the seventh day, the sabbath of the Lord thy God, thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy man-servant, nor thy maid-servant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates;
For in Gen. 1:31; Ex. 20:11.six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is; wherefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
TG Honoring Father and Mother.Honor thy Prov. 20:20.father and thy mother, that thy days may be long upon the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
Thou shalt not commit TG Adulterer.adultery. Thou shalt not TG Stealing.steal.
Thou shalt not bear Prov. 24:28.false witness against thy neighbor.
Thou shalt not TG Covet.covet thy neighbor’s house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbor’s wife, nor his man-servant, nor his maid-servant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor anything that is thy neighbor’s.

And it came to pass that after Abinadi had made an end of these sayings that he said unto them: Have ye Mosiah 12:37.taught this people that they should observe to do all these things for to keep these commandments?
I say unto you, Nay; for if ye had, the Lord would not have caused me to come forth and to prophesy evil concerning this people.

And now ye have said that salvation cometh by the law of Moses. I say unto you that it is expedient that ye should 2 Ne. 25:24.keep the law of Moses as yet; but I say unto you, that the time shall come when it shall 3 Ne. 9:19 (19–20); 15:4 (2–10).no more be expedient to keep the law of Moses.

And moreover, I say unto you, that Gal. 2:16; Mosiah 12:31; Alma 25:16. TG Redemption; Salvation, Plan of.salvation doth not come by the Rom. 7:4 (4–25); Gal. 2:21; 3:2; Heb. 10:1; 2 Ne. 2:5; Mosiah 3:15 (14–15).law alone; and were it not for the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.atonement, which God himself shall make for the sins and iniquities of his people, that they must unavoidably perish, notwithstanding the law of Moses.

And now I say unto you that it was expedient that there should be a law given to the children of Israel, yea, even a very Josh. 1:8; Heb. 9:10 (8–10); Mosiah 3:14 (14–15); Alma 25:15 (15–16); D&C 41:5 (4–5).strict law; for they were a stiffnecked people, 1 Ne. 17:45; Alma 46:8.quick to do iniquity, and slow to remember the Lord their God;

Therefore there was a Rom. 7:1 (1–3).law given them, yea, a law of performances and of TG Ordinance.ordinances, a law which they were to 2 Ne. 25:24; Jacob 4:5.observe strictly from day to day, to keep them in remembrance of God and their duty towards him.
But behold, I say unto you, that all these things were Mosiah 16:14; Alma 25:15 (15–16). TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Symbolism.types of things to come.

And now, did they Ps. 111:10.understand the law? I say unto you, Nay, they did not all understand the law; and this because of the hardness of their hearts; for they understood not that there could not any man be saved 2 Ne. 25:23 (23–25).except it were through the redemption of God.

For behold, did not Moses prophesy unto them concerning the coming of the Messiah, and that God should redeem his people? Yea, and even 1 Pet. 1:11; 1 Ne. 10:5; Jacob 4:4; 7:11 (11–12); Alma 25:16 (10–16); 30:44. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.all the prophets who have prophesied ever since the world began—have they not spoken more or less concerning these things?

Have they not said that Mosiah 7:27; 15:1; 17:8; Alma 10:21. TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.God himself should come down among the children of men, and take upon him the form of man, and go forth in mighty power upon the face of the earth?
Yea, and have they not said also that he should bring to pass the Isa. 26:19; 2 Ne. 2:8.resurrection of the dead, and that he, himself, should be oppressed and afflicted?

div.p: Isaiah speaks messianically—The Messiah’s humiliation and sufferings are set forth—He makes His soul an offering for sin and makes intercession for transgressors—Compare Isaiah 53. About 148 B.C.

chapter.verse: Yea, even doth not Isaiah say: Who hath Isa. 53:1 (1–12).believed our report, and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?
For he shall grow up before him as a tender plant, and as a root out of dry ground; he hath no form nor comeliness; and when we shall see him there is no beauty that we should desire him.
He is Ps. 22:6; 1 Ne. 19:14.despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief; and we hid as it were our faces from him; he was despised, and we esteemed him not.
Surely he has Alma 7:11.borne our Matt. 8:17.griefs, and carried our sorrows; yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.

But he was TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.wounded for our Mosiah 15:9; Alma 11:40.transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities; the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are 1 Pet. 2:24 (24–25).healed.
All we, like Matt. 9:36; 2 Ne. 12:5; 28:14; Alma 5:37.sheep, have gone astray; we have turned every one to his own way; and the Lord hath laid on him the iniquities of us all.

He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he Isa. 53:7 (7–8); Mark 15:3 (2–14); John 19:9 (9–10); 1 Pet. 2:23 (22–23); Mosiah 15:6. TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.opened not his mouth; he is brought as a Jer. 11:19. TG Passover.lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb so he opened not his mouth.
He was taken from prison and from judgment; and who shall declare his generation? For he was cut off out of the land of the living; for the transgressions of my people was he stricken.

And he made his grave with the wicked, and with the Matt. 27:57 (57–60); Mark 15:46 (27, 43–46). TG Jesus Christ, Death of.rich in his death; because he had done no John 19:4.evil, neither was any deceit in his mouth.

Yet it pleased the Lord to Gen. 3:15; Rom. 16:20.bruise him; he hath put him to grief; when thou shalt make his soul an offering for sin he shall see his Mosiah 15:10 (10–13).seed, he shall prolong his days, and the pleasure of the Lord shall prosper in his hand.

He shall see the travail of his soul, and shall be satisfied; by his knowledge shall my righteous servant justify many; for he shall Lev. 16:22 (21–22); 1 Pet. 3:18; D&C 19:16 (16–19).bear their iniquities.

Therefore will I divide him a portion with the Luke 24:26.great, and Mosiah 15:12.he shall divide the spoil with the strong; because he hath poured out his soul unto death; and he was numbered with the transgressors; and he bore the sins of many, and made 2 Ne. 2:9; Mosiah 15:8; Moro. 7:28 (27–28).intercession for the transgressors.

div.p: How Christ is both the Father and the Son—He will make intercession and bear the transgressions of His people—They and all the holy prophets are His seed—He brings to pass the Resurrection—Little children have eternal life. About 148 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now Abinadi said unto them: I would that ye should understand that Isa. 54:5; 1 Tim. 3:16; Mosiah 13:34 (33–34).God himself shall TG God, Manifestations of.come down among the children of men, and shall TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.redeem his people.

And because he Mosiah 3:5; 7:27; Alma 7:9 (9–13).dwelleth in TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.flesh he shall be called the John 19:7. TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son of God, and having subjected the flesh to the TG God, Will of.will of the Isa. 9:6; 64:8; John 10:30; 14:10 (8–10); Mosiah 5:7; Alma 11:39 (38–39); Ether 3:14.Father, being the Father and the Son—

The Father, D&C 93:4.because he was Luke 1:32 (31–33); Mosiah 3:8 (8–9); Alma 7:10; 3 Ne. 1:14. TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.conceived by the power of God; and the Son, because of the flesh; thus becoming the Father and Son—
And they are Deut. 6:4. TG Godhead.one God, yea, the very Alma 11:39.Eternal Mosiah 3:8; Hel. 14:12; 3 Ne. 9:15; Ether 4:7.Father of heaven and of earth.

And thus the flesh becoming subject to the Spirit, or the Son to the Father, being one God, Luke 4:2; Heb. 4:15. TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of.suffereth temptation, and yieldeth not to the temptation, but suffereth himself to be mocked, and John 19:1.scourged, and cast out, and disowned by his Matt. 21:42; Mark 8:31; Luke 17:25; 23:38.people.

And after all this, after working many mighty miracles among the children of men, he shall be led, yea, even Isa. 53:7.as Isaiah said, as a sheep before the shearer is dumb, so he Luke 23:9; John 19:9 (9–10); Mosiah 14:7. TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.opened not his mouth.

Yea, even so he shall be led, TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified, and slain, the Isa. 53:10.flesh becoming subject even unto death, the Luke 22:42; John 6:38; 3 Ne. 11:11.will of the Son being swallowed up in the will of the Father.

And thus God breaketh the Alma 5:7.bands of death, having gained the Hosea 13:14; 1 Cor. 15:57 (55–57); Mosiah 16:7.victory over death; giving the Son power to make 2 Ne. 2:9; Mosiah 14:12; Moro. 7:28 (27–28).intercession for the children of men—

Having ascended into heaven, having the bowels of mercy; being filled with compassion towards the children of men; standing betwixt them and justice; having broken the bands of death, taken upon Mosiah 14:5 (5–12). TG Self-Sacrifice.himself their iniquity and their transgressions, having redeemed them, and TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.satisfied the demands of justice.

And now I say unto you, who shall declare his Isa. 53:8.generation? Behold, I say unto you, that when his soul has been made an offering for Lev. 6:25 (25–26).sin he shall see his Isa. 53:10; Mosiah 5:7; 27:25; Moro. 7:19. TG God the Father, Jehovah.seed. And now what say ye? And who shall be his seed?

Behold I say unto you, that whosoever has heard the words of the Luke 10:16; D&C 84:36 (36–38).prophets, yea, all the holy prophets who have prophesied concerning the coming of the Lord—I say unto you, that all those who have hearkened unto their words, and believed that the Lord would redeem his people, and have looked forward to that day for a remission of their sins, I say unto you, that these are his seed, or they are the heirs of the TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven; Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of God.
For these are they whose sins Mosiah 14:12; Alma 7:13; 11:40 (40–41).he has borne; these are they for whom he has died, to redeem them from their transgressions. And now, are they not his seed?

Yea, and are not the Zech. 7:12; Matt. 11:13; 1 Ne. 3:20. TG Sons and Daughters of God.prophets, every one that has opened his mouth to prophesy, that has not fallen into transgression, I mean all the holy prophets ever since the world began? I say unto you that they are his seed.

And these are Mosiah 12:21 (21–24); 27:37.they who have published peace, who have brought good Isa. 52:7.tidings of good, who have TG Missionary Work.published salvation; and said unto Zion: Thy God reigneth!
And O how beautiful upon the mountains were their feet!
And again, how beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of those that are still publishing peace!
And again, how beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of those who shall hereafter publish peace, yea, from this time henceforth and forever!

And behold, I say unto you, this is not all. For O how beautiful upon the mountains are the Nahum 1:15; 3 Ne. 20:40; D&C 128:19.feet of him that bringeth good tidings, that is the founder of Micah 5:5 (4–7); John 16:33. TG Peace of God.peace, yea, even the Lord, who has redeemed his people; yea, him who has granted salvation unto his people;

For were it not for the redemption which he hath made for his people, which was prepared from the Mosiah 4:6.foundation of the world, I say unto you, were it not for this, all mankind must have 2 Ne. 9:7 (6–13).perished.
But behold, the bands of death shall be broken, and the Son reigneth, and hath power over the dead; therefore, he bringeth to pass the resurrection of the dead.

And there cometh a resurrection, even a Jacob 4:11; Alma 40:16 (16–21). TG Firstfruits.first resurrection; yea, even a resurrection of those that have been, and who are, and who shall be, even until the resurrection of Christ—for so shall he be called.

And now, the resurrection of all the prophets, and all those that have believed in their words, or all those that have kept the commandments of God, shall come forth in the first resurrection; therefore, they are the first resurrection.

They are raised to Ps. 15:1 (1–5); 24:3 (3–4); 1 Ne. 15:33 (33–36); D&C 76:62 (50–70).dwell with God who has redeemed them; thus they have eternal life through Christ, who has TG Death, Power over.broken the bands of death.

And these are those who have part in the first resurrection; and these are they that have died before Christ came, in their ignorance, not having 2 Ne. 9:26 (25–26); D&C 137:7.salvation declared unto them. And thus the Lord bringeth about the restoration of these; and they have a part in the first resurrection, or have eternal life, being redeemed by the Lord.
And little D&C 29:46; 137:10. TG Salvation of Little Children.children also have eternal life.

But behold, and Deut. 5:29; Jacob 6:9; 7:19.fear, and tremble before God, for ye ought to tremble; for the Lord redeemeth none such that Josh. 22:16; Job 24:13; Ps. 5:10; 1 Ne. 2:23 (21–24).rebel against him and Ezek. 18:26; 1 Ne. 15:33 (32–33); Moro. 10:26.die in their sins; yea, even all those that have perished in their sins ever since the world began, that have wilfully rebelled against God, that have known the commandments of God, and would not keep them; Alma 40:19.these are they that have D&C 76:85.no part in the first TG Telestial Glory.resurrection.

Therefore ought ye not to tremble? For salvation cometh to none such; for the Lord hath redeemed none such; yea, neither can the Lord redeem such; for he cannot deny himself; for he cannot deny Alma 12:32; 34:16 (15–16); 42:1.justice when it has its claim.
And now I say unto you that the time shall come that the Ps. 67:2 (1–2). TG Missionary Work.salvation of the Lord shall be declared to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people.

Yea, Lord, Isa. 52:8 (8–10); Mosiah 12:22 (22–24).thy TG Watchman.watchmen shall lift up their voice; with the voice together shall they sing; for they shall see eye to eye, when the Lord shall bring again Zion.
Break forth into joy, sing together, ye waste places of Jerusalem; for the Lord hath comforted his people, he hath redeemed Jerusalem.
The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the TG Salvation.salvation of our God.

div.p: God redeems men from their lost and fallen state—Those who are carnal remain as though there were no redemption—Christ brings to pass a resurrection to endless life or to endless damnation. About 148 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, it came to pass that after Abinadi had spoken these words he stretched forth his hand and said: The time shall come when all shall see the TG Salvation.salvation of the Lord; when Mosiah 3:20 (20–21).every nation, kindred, tongue, and people shall see eye to eye and shall Mosiah 27:31; D&C 88:104.confess before God that his TG Justice.judgments are just.

And then shall the Ps. 91:8; Jer. 12:1; D&C 1:9 (9–10).wicked be Ps. 52:5; D&C 63:54.cast out, and they shall have cause to howl, and Matt. 13:42 (41–42); Luke 13:28; Alma 40:13; Moses 1:22.weep, and wail, and gnash their teeth; and this because they would not Jer. 44:16; Ether 11:13.hearken unto the voice of the Lord; therefore the Lord redeemeth them not.

For they are Gal. 5:19 (16–26); Mosiah 3:19.carnal and devilish, and the devil has power over them; yea, even that old serpent that did Gen. 3:13; 2 Ne. 9:9; Ether 8:25; Moses 4:19 (5–19).beguile our first parents, which was the Moses 5:13.cause of their fall; which was the cause of Ps. 14:3. TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.all mankind becoming carnal, sensual, devilish, Gen. 3:5; 2 Ne. 2:18 (18, 26); Alma 29:5; Moro. 7:16 (15–19).knowing evil from good, Alma 5:41 (41–42); D&C 29:40.subjecting themselves to the devil.

Thus all mankind were 2 Ne. 9:7; Alma 11:45; 12:36; 42:6 (6, 9, 14). TG Fall of Man.lost; and behold, they would have been endlessly lost were it not that God redeemed his people from their lost and fallen state.

But remember that he that persists in his own TG Carnal Mind; Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.carnal nature, and goes on in the ways of sin and rebellion against God, remaineth in his fallen state and the TG Bondage, Spiritual.devil hath all power over him. Therefore he is as though there was no 1 Ne. 10:6; 2 Ne. 2:6 (5–8); 25:20; Alma 12:22 (22–25).redemption made, being an enemy to God; and also is the TG Devil.devil an enemy to God.
And now if Christ had not come into the world, speaking of things to come 2 Ne. 25:26 (24–27); Jarom 1:11; Mosiah 3:13.as though they had already come, there could have been no redemption.

And if Christ had not risen from the dead, or have broken the bands of death that the grave should have no victory, and that death should have no Hosea 13:14; Mosiah 15:8 (8, 20).sting, there could have been no resurrection.
But there is a 2 Ne. 9:10; Alma 42:15 (6–15).resurrection, therefore the grave hath no victory, and the sting of Isa. 25:8; 1 Cor. 15:54–55; Morm. 7:5.death is swallowed up in Christ.

He is the Ether 3:14; Moro. 7:18; D&C 88:13 (7–13). TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World.light and the life of the world; yea, a light that is endless, that can never be darkened; yea, and also a life which is endless, that there can be no more death.

Even this mortal shall put on Alma 40:2. TG Immortality.immortality, and this 1 Cor. 15:42.corruption shall put on incorruption, and shall be brought to 3 Ne. 26:4. TG Jesus Christ, Judge.stand before the bar of God, to be judged of him according to their works whether they be good or whether they be evil—

If they be good, to the resurrection of Dan. 12:2 (2–3); John 5:29 (28–29).endless life and TG Happiness.happiness; and if they be evil, to the resurrection of Alma 9:11. TG Damnation.endless damnation, being delivered up to the devil, who hath subjected them, which is damnation—

Having gone according to their own carnal wills and desires; having never called upon the Lord while the arms of mercy were extended towards them; for the arms of mercy were extended towards them, and they would TG Prophets, Rejection of.not; they being warned of their iniquities and yet they would not depart from them; and they were commanded to repent and yet they would not repent.
And now, ought ye not to tremble and repent of your sins, and remember that only in and through Christ ye can be saved?
Therefore, if ye teach the TG Law of Moses.law of Moses, also teach that it is a Mosiah 13:31; Alma 25:15 (15–16). TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.shadow of those things which are to come—
Teach them that redemption cometh through Christ the Lord, who is the very Mosiah 3:8.Eternal Father. Amen.

div.p: Alma believes and writes the words of Abinadi—Abinadi suffers death by fire—He prophesies disease and death by fire upon his murderers. About 148 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that when Abinadi had finished these sayings, that the king commanded that the TG False Priesthoods.priests should take him and cause that he should be put to Jer. 26:11.death.

But there was one among them whose name was Mosiah 23:9.Alma, he also being a descendant of Nephi. And he was a young man, and he Mosiah 26:15; Alma 5:11.believed the words which Abinadi had spoken, for he knew concerning the iniquity which Abinadi had testified against them; therefore he began to plead with the king that he would not be angry with Abinadi, but suffer that he might depart in peace.
But the king was more wroth, and caused that Alma should be cast out from among them, and sent his servants after him that they might slay him.

But he fled from before them and 1 Kgs. 17:3 (1–16); Ether 13:13 (13, 22).hid himself that they found him not. And he being concealed for many days did TG Scriptures, Writing of.write all the words which Abinadi had spoken.
And it came to pass that the king caused that his guards should surround Abinadi and take him; and they bound him and cast him into prison.
And after three days, having counseled with his Mosiah 11:5.priests, he caused that he should again be brought before him.
And he said unto him: Abinadi, we have found an accusation against thee, and thou art worthy of death.

For thou hast said that Mosiah 7:27; 13:34.God himself should come down among the children of men; and now, for this cause thou shalt be put to death unless thou wilt recall all the words which thou hast spoken evil concerning me and my people.

Now Abinadi said unto him: I say unto you, I will TG Courage; Integrity.not recall the words which I have spoken unto you concerning this people, for they are true; and that ye may know of their surety I have suffered myself that I have fallen into your hands.

Yea, and I will TG Persecution.suffer even until death, and I will not recall my words, and they shall stand as a TG Testimony.testimony against you. And if ye slay me ye will shed Jer. 26:15 (14–15); Lam. 4:13; Alma 14:11; 60:13.innocent blood, and this shall also stand as a testimony against you at the last day.
And now king Noah was about to release him, for he feared his word; for he feared that the judgments of God would come upon him.

But the Mosiah 11:5; 12:25 (17, 25).priests lifted up their voices against him, and began to accuse him, saying: He has reviled the king. Therefore the king was stirred up in Prov. 20:2.anger against him, and he delivered him up that he might be slain.
And it came to pass that they took him and bound him, and Dan. 3:6; James 5:10 (10–11); Alma 14:26 (20–27).scourged his skin with faggots, yea, even unto Mosiah 7:28; 21:30; Alma 25:11.death.
And now when the flames began to scorch him, he cried unto them, saying:

Behold, even as ye have done unto me, so shall it come to pass that thy Mosiah 13:10; Alma 25:12 (7–12).seed shall cause that many shall suffer the pains that I do suffer, even the pains of Alma 25:5.death by fire; and this because they believe in the salvation of the Lord their God.
And it will come to pass that ye shall be afflicted with all manner of Deut. 28:60 (25–60).diseases because of your iniquities.
Yea, and ye shall be smitten on every hand, and shall be driven and scattered to and fro, even as a wild flock is driven by wild and ferocious beasts.

And in that day ye shall be Alma 25:8.hunted, and ye shall be taken by the hand of your enemies, and then ye shall suffer, as I suffer, the pains of Mosiah 13:10; 19:20; Alma 25:11 (7–12).death by fire.
Thus God executeth Ps. 125:3.vengeance upon those that destroy his people. O God, Luke 23:46; Acts 7:59.receive my soul.

And now, when Mosiah 7:26.Abinadi had said these words, he fell, having suffered death by fire; yea, having been put to death because he would not deny the commandments of God, having sealed the truth of his words by his Heb. 9:16 (16–17).death.

div.p: Alma preaches in private—He sets forth the covenant of baptism and baptizes at the waters of Mormon—He organizes the Church of Christ and ordains priests—They support themselves and teach the people—Alma and his people flee from King Noah into the wilderness. About 147–145 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now, it came to pass that Alma, who had fled from the servants of king Noah, Mosiah 23:9.repented of his sins and iniquities, and went about privately among the people, and began to teach the words of Abinadi—

Yea, concerning that which was to come, and also concerning the resurrection of the dead, and the TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.redemption of the people, which was to be brought to pass through the power, and sufferings, and TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of; Jesus Christ, Death of.death of Christ, and his resurrection and ascension into heaven.
And as many as would hear his word he did teach. And he taught them privately, that it might not come to the knowledge of the king. And many did believe his words.

And it came to pass that as many as did believe him did go forth to a Alma 5:3; 3 Ne. 5:12.place which was called Mormon, having received its name from the king, being in the Mosiah 18:31.borders of the land having been infested, by times or at seasons, by wild beasts.

Now, there was in Mormon a fountain of pure water, and Alma resorted thither, there being near the water a thicket of small trees, where he did hide himself in the daytime from the searches of the king.
And it came to pass that as many as believed him went thither to hear his words.

And it came to pass after many days there were a goodly number gathered together at the place of Mormon, to hear the words of Alma. Yea, all were gathered together that believed on his word, to hear him. And he did Alma 5:13.teach them, and did preach unto them repentance, and redemption, and faith on the Lord.

And it came to pass that he said unto them: Behold, here are the waters of Mormon (for thus were they called) and now, as ye are D&C 20:36–37, 77.desirous to come into the TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood; Conversion.fold of God, and to be called his people, and are willing to bear one another’s burdens, that they may be light;

Yea, and are TG Baptism, Qualifications for.willing to mourn with those that TG Comfort; Compassion.mourn; yea, and comfort those that stand in need of comfort, and to stand as TG Missionary Work; Witness.witnesses of God at all times and in all things, and in all places that ye may be in, even until death, that ye may be redeemed of God, and be numbered with those of the Jacob 4:11.first resurrection, that ye may have eternal life—

Now I say unto you, if this be the desire of your hearts, what have you against being 2 Ne. 31:17; Alma 4:4.baptized in the TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name of the Lord, as a witness before him that ye have entered into a Neh. 10:29. TG Commitment.covenant with him, that ye will serve him and keep his commandments, that he may pour out his Spirit more abundantly upon you?
And now when the people had heard these words, they clapped their hands for joy, and exclaimed: This is the desire of our hearts.

And now it came to pass that Alma took Helam, he being one of the first, and went and stood forth in the water, and cried, saying: O Lord, pour out thy Spirit upon thy servant, that he may do this work with holiness of heart.

And when he had said these words, the TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.Spirit of the Lord was upon him, and he said: Helam, I baptize thee, having Mosiah 21:33; Alma 5:3; 3 Ne. 11:25.authority from the Almighty God, as a testimony that ye have entered into a Mosiah 21:31. TG Covenants.covenant to serve him until you are dead as to the mortal body; and may the Spirit of the Lord be poured out upon you; and may he grant unto you eternal life, through the redemption of Christ, whom he has prepared from the Mosiah 4:6; Alma 12:30 (25, 30).foundation of the world.
And after Alma had said these words, both Alma and Helam were TG Baptism, Immersion.buried in the water; and they arose and came forth out of the water rejoicing, being filled with the Spirit.
And again, Alma took another, and went forth a second time into the water, and baptized him according to the first, only he did not bury JS—H 1:71 (70–71).himself again in the water.

And after this manner he did baptize every one that went forth to the place of Mormon; and they were in number about two hundred and four souls; yea, and they were Mosiah 25:18.baptized in the waters of Mormon, and were filled with the TG Grace.grace of God.

And they were called the church of God, or the Mosiah 21:34; 25:22 (18–23); 26:4; Alma 4:5 (4–5); 3 Ne. 26:21. TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church of Christ, from that time forward. And it came to pass that whosoever was baptized by the power and authority of God was added to his church.

And it came to pass that Alma, having Mosiah 23:16. TG Priesthood, Authority.authority from God, TG Priesthood, History of.ordained priests; even one priest to every fifty of their number did he ordain to preach unto them, and to Mosiah 2:4; 23:14; 24:4 (4–8).teach them concerning the things pertaining to the kingdom of God.
And he commanded them that Mosiah 23:14.they should D&C 5:10.teach nothing save it were the things which he had taught, and which had been spoken by the mouth of the holy prophets.
Yea, even he commanded them that they should TG Preaching; Repent.preach nothing save it were repentance and faith on the Lord, who had redeemed his people.

And he commanded them that there should be no TG Contention.contention one with another, but that they should look forward with Matt. 6:22; Morm. 8:15; D&C 4:5; 88:68.one eye, having one faith and one baptism, having their hearts 1 Sam. 18:1; Rom. 15:5 (1–7); Mosiah 4:15; 23:15.knit together in unity and in love one towards another.
And thus he commanded them to preach. And thus they became the Moses 6:68.children of God.
And he commanded them that they should observe the Ex. 35:2; Mosiah 13:16 (16–19).sabbath day, and keep it holy, and also every day they should give thanks to the Lord their God.

And he also commanded them that the priests whom he had ordained Acts 20:34 (33–35); Mosiah 27:5 (3–5); Alma 1:3, 26.should 1 Cor. 9:18 (16–19); Alma 30:32.labor with their own hands for their support.

And there was Alma 32:11.one day in every week that was set apart that they should TG Meetings.gather themselves together to teach the people, and to worship the Lord their God, and also, as often as it was in their power, to TG Assembly for Worship.assemble themselves together.

And the priests were not to depend upon the people for their support; but for their labor they were to receive the TG Blessing; Reward; Wages.grace of God, that they might wax strong in the Spirit, having the Neh. 10:28 (28–31). TG God, Knowledge about.knowledge of God, that they might teach with power and authority from God.

And again Alma commanded that the people of the church should impart of their substance, Alma 16:16; 4 Ne. 1:3.every one according to that which he had; if he have more abundantly he should impart more abundantly; and of him that had but little, but little should be required; and to him that had not should be given.

And thus they should impart of their TG Generosity; Initiative.substance of their own free will and good desires towards God, and to those priests that stood in need, yea, and to every needy, naked soul.

And this he said unto them, having been commanded of God; and they did TG Walking with God; Welfare.walk uprightly before God, imparting to one another both temporally and spiritually according to their needs and their wants.

And now it came to pass that all this was done in Mormon, yea, by the Mosiah 26:15.waters of Mormon, in the forest that was near the waters of Mormon; yea, the place of Mormon, the waters of Mormon, the forest of Mormon, how beautiful are they to the eyes of them who there came to the knowledge of their Redeemer; yea, and how blessed are they, for they shall TG Praise; Singing.sing to his praise forever.
And these things were done in the Mosiah 18:4.borders of the land, that they might not come to the knowledge of the king.

But behold, it came to pass that the king, having discovered a movement among the people, sent his servants to watch them. Therefore on the day that they were assembling themselves together to hear the word of the Lord they were discovered unto the king.
And now the king said that Alma was stirring up the people to rebellion against him; therefore he sent his Mosiah 19:1.army to destroy them.

And it came to pass that Alma and the people of the Lord were Mosiah 23:1.apprised of the coming of the king’s army; therefore they took their tents and their families and Mosiah 21:30; 23:13, 36.departed into the wilderness.
And they were in number about Mosiah 23:10.four hundred and fifty souls.

div.p: Gideon seeks to slay King Noah—The Lamanites invade the land—King Noah suffers death by fire—Limhi rules as a tributary monarch. About 145–121 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that the Mosiah 18:33.army of the king returned, having searched in vain for the people of the Lord.
And now behold, the forces of the king were small, having been reduced, and there began to be a division among the remainder of the people.
And the lesser part began to Acts 9:1.breathe out threatenings against the king, and there began to be a great contention among them.
And now there was a man among them whose name was Gideon, and he being a strong man and an enemy to the king, therefore he drew his sword, and swore in his wrath that he would slay the king.

And it came to pass that he fought with the king; and when the king saw that he was about to overpower him, he fled and ran and got upon the Judg. 9:51 (50–55); Mosiah 11:12.tower which was near the temple.

And Gideon pursued after him and was about to get upon the tower to slay the king, and the king cast his eyes round about towards the land of Mosiah 10:7; 11:12; 20:1.Shemlon, and behold, the army of the Lamanites were within the borders of the land.
And now the king cried out in the anguish of his soul, saying: Gideon, Deut. 13:8 (6–9).spare me, for the Lamanites are upon us, and they will destroy us; yea, they will destroy my people.
And now the king was not so much concerned about his people as he was about his TG Selfishness.own life; nevertheless, Gideon did spare his life.
And the king commanded the people that they should flee before the Lamanites, and he himself did go before them, and they did flee into the wilderness, with their women and their children.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites did pursue them, and did overtake them, and began to slay them.
Now it came to pass that the king commanded them that all the men should Mosiah 19:19 (19–23).leave their wives and their children, and flee before the Lamanites.
Now there were many that would not leave them, but had rather stay and perish with them. And the rest left their wives and their children and fled.
And it came to pass that those who tarried with their wives and their children caused that their fair daughters should stand forth and plead with the Lamanites that they would not slay them.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites had compassion on them, for they were charmed with the beauty of their women.

Therefore the Lamanites did spare their lives, and took them captives and carried them back to the land of Nephi, and granted unto them that they might possess the land, under the conditions that they would deliver up king Noah into the hands of the Lamanites, and deliver up their property, even Mosiah 7:15.one half of all they possessed, one half of their gold, and their silver, and all their precious things, and thus they should pay tribute to the king of the Lamanites from year to year.
And now there was one of the sons of the king among those that were taken captive, whose name was Mosiah 7:9; 11:1.Limhi.
And now Limhi was desirous that his father should not be destroyed; nevertheless, Limhi was not ignorant of the iniquities of his father, he himself being a just man.

And it came to pass that Gideon sent men into the wilderness secretly, to search for the king and those that were with him. And it came to pass that they met the people in the wilderness, all save the king and his priests.

Now they had sworn in their hearts that they would return to the land of Nephi, and if their Mosiah 19:11.wives and their children were slain, and also those that had tarried with them, that they would seek revenge, and also perish with them.

And the king commanded them that they should not return; and they were angry with the king, and caused that he should suffer, even unto Mosiah 12:3; 13:10; 17:18 (13–19); Alma 25:11 (7–12).death by fire.
And they were about to take the priests also and Mosiah 20:3.put them to death, and they fled before them.

And it came to pass that they were about to return to the land of Nephi, and they met the men of Gideon. And the men of Gideon told them of all that had happened to their wives and their children; and that the Lamanites had granted unto them that they might possess the land by paying a tribute to the Lamanites of one half of all they possessed.
And the people told the men of Mosiah 20:17.Gideon that they had slain the king, and his Mosiah 17:12 (1, 6, 12–18); 20:23 (3, 18, 23).priests had fled from them farther into the wilderness.

And it came to pass that after they had ended the ceremony, that they returned to the land of Nephi, rejoicing, because their wives and their children were not slain; and they told Gideon what they had done to the king.
And it came to pass that the king of the Lamanites made an Mosiah 21:3.oath unto them, that his people should not slay them.

And also Limhi, being the son of the king, having the kingdom conferred upon him Mosiah 7:9.by the people, made Mosiah 20:14, 22.oath unto the king of the Lamanites that his people should pay Mosiah 22:7.tribute unto him, even one half of all they possessed.
And it came to pass that Limhi began to establish the kingdom and to establish TG Peacemakers.peace among his people.

And the king of the Lamanites set Mosiah 21:5; 22:6 (6–10).guards round about the land, that he might Mosiah 7:22; 9:10.keep the people of Limhi in the land, that they might not depart into the wilderness; and he did support his guards out of the tribute which he did receive from the Nephites.
And now king Limhi did have continual peace in his kingdom for the space of two years, that the Lamanites did not molest them nor seek to destroy them.

div.p: Some Lamanite daughters are abducted by the priests of Noah—The Lamanites wage war upon Limhi and his people—The Lamanite hosts are repulsed and pacified. About 145–123 B.C.

chapter.verse: Now there was a place in Mosiah 19:6.Shemlon where the daughters of the Lamanites did gather themselves together to sing, and to Judg. 21:21.dance, and to make themselves merry.
And it came to pass that there was one day a small number of them gathered together to sing and to dance.

And now the priests of king Noah, being ashamed to return to the city of Nephi, yea, and also fearing that the people would Mosiah 19:21.slay them, therefore they durst not return to their wives and their Mosiah 25:12.children.
And having tarried in the wilderness, and having discovered the daughters of the Lamanites, they laid and watched them;

And when there were but few of them gathered together to dance, they came forth out of their secret places and took them and carried them into the wilderness; yea, twenty and four of the Mosiah 21:20; 23:33 (30–35).daughters of the Lamanites they carried into the wilderness.
And it came to pass that when the Lamanites found that their daughters had been missing, they were angry with the people of Limhi, for they thought it was the people of Limhi.
Therefore they sent their armies forth; yea, even the king himself went before his people; and they went up to the land of Nephi to destroy the people of Limhi.

And now Limhi had discovered them from the Mosiah 11:12.tower, even all their preparations for war did he discover; therefore he gathered his people together, and laid wait for them in the fields and in the forests.
And it came to pass that when the Lamanites had come up, that the people of Limhi began to fall upon them from their waiting places, and began to slay them.
And it came to pass that the battle became exceedingly sore, for they fought like lions for their prey.

And it came to pass that the people of Limhi began to drive the Lamanites before them; yet they were not half so numerous as the Lamanites. But they Alma 43:45.fought for their lives, and for their Alma 46:12.wives, and for their children; therefore they exerted themselves and like dragons did they fight.

And it came to pass that they found the king of the Lamanites among the number of their dead; yet he was not dead, having been wounded and left upon the ground, so speedy was the flight of his people.

And they took him and bound up his wounds, and brought him before Limhi, and said: Behold, here is the king of the Lamanites; he having received a wound has fallen among their dead, and they have left him; and behold, we have brought him before you; and now let us slay him.

But Limhi said unto them: Ye shall not slay him, but bring him hither that I may see him. And they brought him. And Limhi said unto him: What cause have ye to come up to war against my people? Behold, my people have not broken the Mosiah 19:26 (25–26).oath that I made unto you; therefore, why should ye break the oath which ye made unto my people?
And now the king said: I have broken the oath because thy people did carry away the daughters of my people; therefore, in my anger I did cause my people to come up to war against thy people.

And now Limhi had heard nothing concerning this matter; therefore he said: I will search among my people and whosoever has done this thing shall perish. Therefore he caused a search to be made among his people.

Now when Mosiah 19:23 (4–8, 23); 22:3; Alma 1:8 (8–9).Gideon had heard these things, he being the king’s captain, he went forth and said unto the king: I pray thee forbear, and do not search this people, and lay not this thing to their charge.
For do ye not remember the priests of thy father, whom this people sought to destroy? And are they not in the wilderness? And are not they the ones who have stolen the daughters of the Lamanites?

And now, behold, and tell the king of these things, that he may tell his people that they may be pacified towards us; for behold they are already preparing to come against us; and behold also there are but few of us.
And behold, they come with their numerous hosts; and except the king doth pacify them towards us we must perish.
For are not the words of Abinadi Mosiah 12:2 (1–8); 21:4.fulfilled, which he prophesied against us—and all this because we would not hearken unto the words of the Lord, and turn from our iniquities?

And now let us pacify the king, and we fulfil the Mosiah 19:26.oath which we have made unto him; for it is better that we should be in bondage than that we should lose our TG Life, Sanctity of.lives; therefore, let us put a stop to the shedding of so much blood.

And now Limhi told the king all the things concerning his father, and the Mosiah 19:23 (21, 23); 23:9 (9, 12, 31).priests that had fled into the wilderness, and attributed the carrying away of their daughters to them.

And it came to pass that the king was pacified towards his people; and he said unto them: Let us go forth to meet my people, without arms; and I swear unto you with an Mosiah 21:3.oath that my people shall not slay thy people.

And it came to pass that they followed the king, and went forth without arms to meet the Lamanites. And it came to pass that they did meet the Lamanites; and the king of the Lamanites did bow himself down before them, and did plead in behalf of the people of Limhi.

And when the Lamanites saw the people of Limhi, that they were without arms, they had TG Compassion.compassion on them and were pacified towards them, and returned with their king in peace to their own land.

div.p: Limhi’s people are smitten and defeated by the Lamanites—Limhi’s people meet Ammon and are converted—They tell Ammon of the twenty-four Jaredite plates. About 122–121 B.C.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that Limhi and his people returned to the city of Nephi, and began to dwell in the land again in peace.
And it came to pass that after many days the Lamanites began again to be stirred up in anger against the Nephites, and they began to come into the borders of the land round about.

Now they durst not slay them, because of the Mosiah 19:25; 20:24.oath which their king had made unto Limhi; but they would smite them on their Lam. 3:30; Mosiah 12:2.cheeks, and exercise authority over them; and began to put heavy Ex. 1:11 (10–11); Mosiah 12:5; 24:9.burdens upon their backs, and drive them as they would a dumb ass—
Yea, all this was done that the D&C 3:19.word of the Lord might be Mosiah 20:21.fulfilled.

And now the afflictions of the Nephites were great, and there was no way that they could deliver themselves out of their hands, for the Lamanites had Mosiah 19:28; 22:6 (6–10).surrounded them on every side.

And it came to pass that the people began to murmur with the king because of their afflictions; and they began to be desirous to go against them to battle. And they did afflict the king sorely with their complaints; therefore he granted unto them that they should do according to their desires.
And they gathered themselves together again, and put on their armor, and went forth against the Lamanites to drive them out of their land.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites did beat them, and drove them back, and Mosiah 21:29.slew many of them.

And now there was a great Mosiah 12:4. TG Mourning.mourning and lamentation among the people of Limhi, the widow mourning for her husband, the son and the daughter mourning for their father, and the brothers for their brethren.
Now there were a great many TG Widows.widows in the land, and they did cry mightily from day to day, for a great fear of the Lamanites had come upon them.

And it came to pass that their continual cries did stir up the remainder of the people of Limhi to anger against the Lamanites; and they went again to battle, but they were driven back again, suffering much loss.
Yea, they went again even the third time, and suffered in the like manner; and those that were not slain returned again to the city of Nephi.

And they did humble themselves even to the dust, subjecting themselves to the Mosiah 19:28 (26, 28); 21:36.yoke of bondage, TG Submissiveness.submitting themselves to be smitten, and to be driven to and fro, and burdened, according to the desires of their enemies.

And they did Mosiah 29:20.humble themselves even in the depths of humility; and they did cry mightily to God; yea, even all the day long did they cry unto their God that he would TG Deliver; Protection, Divine.deliver them out of their afflictions.

And now the Lord was 1 Sam. 8:18; Prov. 15:29; Mosiah 11:24 (23–25); D&C 101:7 (7–9).slow to hear their cry because of their iniquities; nevertheless the Lord did hear their Ex. 3:9 (7, 9); 2 Ne. 26:15; D&C 109:49.cries, and began to soften the hearts of the Lamanites that they began to ease their burdens; yet the Lord did not see fit to deliver them out of bondage.
And it came to pass that they began to prosper by degrees in the land, and began to raise grain more abundantly, and flocks, and herds, that they did not suffer with hunger.

Now there was a great number of women, more than there was of men; therefore king Limhi commanded that every man should Mosiah 4:26 (16, 26).impart to the support of the TG Widows.widows and their children, that they might not perish with hunger; and this they did because of the greatness of their number that had been slain.
Now the people of Limhi kept together in a body as much as it was possible, and secured their grain and their flocks;
And the king himself did not trust his person without the walls of the city, unless he took his guards with him, fearing that he might by some means fall into the hands of the Lamanites.

And he caused that his people should watch the land round about, that by some means they might take those priests that fled into the wilderness, who had stolen the Mosiah 20:5.daughters of the Lamanites, and that had caused such a great destruction to come upon them.

For they were desirous to take them that they might Mosiah 7:7 (7–11).punish them; for they had come into the land of Nephi by night, and carried off their grain and many of their precious things; therefore they laid wait for them.
And it came to pass that there was no more disturbance between the Lamanites and the people of Limhi, even until the time that Mosiah 7:6 (6–13).Ammon and his brethren came into the land.

And the king having been without the gates of the city with his guard, Mosiah 7:10.discovered Ammon and his brethren; and supposing them to be priests of Noah therefore he caused that they should be taken, and bound, and cast into Mosiah 7:7 (6–8); Hel. 5:21.prison. And had they been the priests of Noah he would have caused that they should be put to death.
But when he found that they were not, but that they were his brethren, and had come from the Omni 1:13.land of Zarahemla, he was filled with exceedingly great joy.

Now king Limhi had sent, previous to the coming of Ammon, a Mosiah 8:7.small number of men to Mosiah 7:14.search for the land of Zarahemla; but they could not find it, and they were lost in the wilderness.

Nevertheless, they did find a land which had been peopled; yea, a land which was covered with dry Mosiah 8:8; Hel. 3:6 (3–12).bones; yea, a land which had been peopled and which had been destroyed; and they, having supposed it to be the land of Zarahemla, returned to the land of Nephi, having arrived in the borders of the land not many days before the Mosiah 7:6 (6–11).coming of Ammon.
And they brought a Mosiah 8:9; 28:11.record with them, even a record of the people whose bones they had found; and it was engraven on plates of ore.

And now Limhi was again filled with joy on learning from the mouth of Ammon that king Mosiah had a Omni 1:20 (20–22); Mosiah 28:13 (11–19). TG God, Gifts of.gift from God, whereby he could 1 Cor. 12:10; Mosiah 8:6 (6, 12–13).interpret such engravings; yea, and Ammon also did rejoice.
Yet Ammon and his brethren were filled with sorrow because so many of their brethren had been Mosiah 21:8 (7–14); 25:9.slain;

And also that king Noah and his priests had caused the people to commit so many sins and iniquities against God; and they also did mourn for the Mosiah 17:13 (12–20).death of Abinadi; and also for the Mosiah 18:34 (34–35).departure of Alma and the people that went with him, who had formed a church of God through the strength and power of God, and faith on the words which had been spoken by Abinadi.

Yea, they did mourn for their departure, for they knew not whither they had fled. Now they would have gladly joined with them, for they themselves had entered into a Mosiah 18:13.covenant with God to serve him and keep his commandments.
And now since the coming of Ammon, king Limhi had also entered into a covenant with God, and also many of his people, to serve him and keep his commandments.

And it came to pass that king Limhi and many of his people were desirous to be baptized; but there was none in the land that had TG Baptism, Essential; Priesthood, Authority.authority from God. And Ammon declined doing this thing, considering himself an unworthy servant.

Therefore they did not at that time form themselves into a Mosiah 18:17.church, waiting upon the Spirit of the Lord. Now they were desirous to become even as Alma and his brethren, who had fled into the wilderness.

They were desirous to be baptized as a witness and a testimony that they were willing to serve God with all their hearts; nevertheless they did prolong the time; and an account of their baptism shall be Mosiah 25:18 (17–18).given hereafter.
And now all the study of Ammon and Mosiah 7:3 (2–3).his people, and king Limhi and his people, was to deliver themselves out of the hands of the Lamanites and from Mosiah 21:13.bondage.

div.p: Plans are made for the people to escape from Lamanite bondage—The Lamanites are made drunk—The people escape, return to Zarahemla, and become subject to King Mosiah. About 121–120 B.C.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that Ammon and king Limhi began to consult with the people how they should Mosiah 7:18.deliver themselves out of bondage; and even they did cause that all the people should gather themselves together; and this they did that they might have the voice of the people concerning the matter.

And it came to pass that they could find no way to deliver themselves out of bondage, except it were to take their women and children, and their flocks, and their herds, and their tents, and depart into the wilderness; for the Lamanites being so numerous, it was impossible for the people of Limhi to contend with them, thinking to deliver themselves out of bondage by the sword.

Now it came to pass that Mosiah 20:17; Alma 1:8 (8–9).Gideon went forth and stood before the king, and said unto him: Now O king, thou hast hitherto hearkened unto my words many times when we have been contending with our brethren, the Lamanites.

And now O king, if thou hast not found me to be an unprofitable servant, or if thou hast hitherto listened to my words in any degree, and they have been of service to thee, even so I desire that thou wouldst listen to my words at this time, and I will be thy servant and deliver this people out of bondage.
And the king granted unto him that he might speak. And Gideon said unto him:

Behold the back pass, through the back wall, on the back side of the city. The Lamanites, or the Mosiah 19:28; 21:5.guards of the Lamanites, by night are Alma 55:14 (8–17).drunken; therefore let us send a proclamation among all this people that they gather together their flocks and herds, that they may drive them into the wilderness by night.
