And if any man will sue thee at the law and take away thy coat, TG Charity; Initiative.let him have thy cloak also;
And whosoever shall compel thee to TG Generosity.go a mile, go with him twain.
Jacob 2:19 (17–19); Mosiah 4:26.Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would TG Borrow.borrow of thee turn thou not away.
And behold it is written also, that thou shalt love thy neighbor and hate thine enemy;

But behold I say unto you, love your Prov. 24:17; 25:21 (21–22); Alma 48:23. TG Enemies.enemies, bless them that curse you, do TG Benevolence.good to them that hate you, and Acts 7:60 (59–60); 2 Tim. 4:16.pray for them who despitefully use you and persecute you;
That ye may be the children of your Father who is in heaven; for he maketh his sun to rise Matt. 5:45.on the evil and on the good.
Therefore those things which were of old time, which were under the law, in me are all Heb. 8:13.fulfilled.
3 Ne. 9:17; 15:2 (2, 7); D&C 22:1.Old things are done away, and all things have become Jer. 31:31 (31–33); Ether 13:9.new.

Therefore I would that ye should be Matt. 5:48; 3 Ne. 19:29 (28–29); 27:27. TG God, Perfection of; God, the Standard of Righteousness; Jesus Christ, Exemplar; Man, New, Spiritually Reborn; Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.perfect even as I, or your Father who is in heaven is perfect.

div.p: Jesus teaches the Nephites the Lord’s Prayer—They are to lay up treasures in heaven—The twelve disciples in their ministry are commanded to take no thought for temporal things—Compare Matthew 6. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: Matt. 6:1 (1–34). TG Almsgiving.Verily, verily, I say that I would that ye should do alms unto the poor; but take heed that ye do not your alms before men to be seen of them; otherwise ye have no reward of your Father who is in heaven.

Therefore, when ye shall do your alms do not sound a trumpet before you, as will hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have D&C 121:35 (34–35).glory of men. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.
But when thou doest alms let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth;
That thine alms may be in secret; and thy Father who seeth in secret, himself shall reward thee openly.

And when thou TG Prayer.prayest thou shalt not do as the TG Hypocrisy.hypocrites, for they love to pray, standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.

But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast 2 Kgs. 4:33.shut thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret; and thy Father, who TG God, Omniscience of.seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
But when ye pray, use not TG Sincere.vain repetitions, as the TG Heathen.heathen, for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.
Be not ye therefore like unto them, for your Father D&C 84:83.knoweth what things ye have need of before ye Ezek. 36:37.ask him.
After this Matt. 6:9 (9–13).manner therefore TG Prayer.pray ye: Our TG God the Father, Elohim.Father who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name.
Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.
And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.
And JST Matt. 6:14 (Matt. 6:13 note a).lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.
For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, forever. Amen.
For, if ye Mosiah 26:31. TG Forgive.forgive men their trespasses your heavenly Father will also forgive you;
But if ye forgive not men their trespasses neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

Moreover, when ye Isa. 58:5 (5–7); Zech. 7:5 (5–6). TG Fast, Fasting.fast be not as the TG Hypocrisy.hypocrites, of a sad countenance, for they disfigure their faces that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, they have their reward.
But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thy head, and TG Wash.wash thy face;
That thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father, who is in Isa. 45:15; D&C 38:7.secret; and thy Father, who seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where 3 Ne. 27:32.moth and rust doth corrupt, and thieves break through and steal;
But lay up for yourselves Hel. 5:8; 8:25. TG Treasure.treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal.
For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
The Ezra 9:8.light of the body is the Matt. 6:22 (20–25).eye; if, therefore, thine eye be D&C 88:67. TG Dedication.single, thy whole body shall be full of light.
But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If, therefore, the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!

No man can 1 Sam. 7:3; Alma 5:41 (39–42); Moses 1:15.serve Hosea 10:2.two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or else he will hold to the one and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and Mammon.

And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words he looked upon the Matt. 6:25; 3 Ne. 12:1; 15:11.twelve whom he had chosen, and said unto them: Remember the words which I have spoken. For behold, ye are they whom I have chosen to TG Church Organization; Delegation of Responsibility.minister unto this people. Therefore I say unto you, Alma 31:37; D&C 84:81 (79–85).take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than Job 27:16 (16–17).raiment?
Behold the D&C 117:6.fowls of the air, for they sow not, neither do they reap nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?
Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?
And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the TG Nature, Earth.lilies of the field how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin;
And yet I say unto you, that even Solomon, in all his glory, was not arrayed like one of these.
Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which today is, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, even so will he clothe you, if ye are not of little faith.
Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?
For your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.
But TG Commitment.seek ye first the Luke 12:31 (22–34). TG Objectives.kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things shall be added unto you.
Take therefore no thought for the morrow, for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Matt. 6:34.Sufficient is the day unto the evil thereof.

div.p: Jesus commands: Judge not; ask of God; beware of false prophets—He promises salvation to those who do the will of the Father—Compare Matthew 7. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: Matt. 7:1 (1–27); JST Matt. 7:1–2 (Matt. 7:1 note a).And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words he turned again to the multitude, and did open his mouth unto them again, saying: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Judge not, that ye be not judged.
Morm. 8:19.For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged; and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.
And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
Or how wilt thou say to thy brother: Let me pull the mote out of thine eye—and behold, a beam is in thine own eye?
Thou TG Hypocrisy.hypocrite, first cast the John 8:7 (3–11).beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast the mote out of thy brother’s eye.
Give not that which is TG Holiness.holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.
3 Ne. 27:29. TG Prayer.Ask, and it shall be given unto you; TG Initiative; Objectives.seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
For every one that asketh, receiveth; and he that seeketh, findeth; and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.
Or what man is there of you, who, if his son ask bread, will give him a stone?
Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?
If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father who is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?
Therefore, all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, TG Benevolence; Compassion; Courtesy.do ye even so to them, for this is the law and the prophets.
Enter ye in at the Luke 13:24; 3 Ne. 27:33.strait gate; for wide is the gate, and D&C 132:25.broad is the way, which leadeth to destruction, and many there be who go in thereat;
Because strait is the 2 Ne. 9:41; 31:9 (9, 17–18); D&C 22:4.gate, and 1 Ne. 8:20.narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and Matt. 7:14; 1 Ne. 14:12.few there be that find it.
Beware of Jer. 23:21 (21–32); 2 Ne. 28:12 (9, 12, 15). TG False Prophets.false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.
Ye shall know them by their Col. 1:6; Alma 32:42 (28–42); D&C 52:34 (18, 34).fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?
Even so every Alma 5:41.good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.
A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
Every tree that Matt. 3:10; Alma 5:36 (36–41); D&C 97:7.bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
Wherefore, by their Matt. 7:17 (16–20); 12:33; Luke 6:43 (43–45); Moro. 7:5.fruits ye shall know them.
Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall 1 Jn. 2:17.enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father who is in heaven.
Many will Alma 5:17.say to me in that day: Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name, and in thy name have cast out devils, and in thy name done many wonderful works?
And then will Matt. 7:23.I profess unto them: I never Mosiah 5:13; 26:25 (24–27); D&C 112:26.knew you; Ps. 119:115; Luke 13:27.depart from me, ye that work iniquity.
Therefore, whoso heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, who built his house upon a TG Rock.rock—
And the Alma 26:6; Hel. 5:12.rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it Prov. 12:7.fell not, for it was founded upon a rock.
And every one that heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them not shall be likened unto a TG Foolishness.foolish man, who built his house upon the 3 Ne. 11:40.sand—
And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell, and great was the fall of it.

div.p: Jesus announces that the law of Moses is fulfilled in Him—The Nephites are the other sheep of whom He spoke in Jerusalem—Because of iniquity, the Lord’s people in Jerusalem do not know of the scattered sheep of Israel. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that when Jesus had ended these sayings he cast his eyes round about on the multitude, and said unto them: Behold, ye have heard the things which I IE in Galilee and Judea.taught before I ascended to my Father; therefore, whoso remembereth these sayings of mine and James 1:22 (22–24).doeth them, him will I John 6:39; 1 Ne. 13:37; Mosiah 23:22; Alma 26:7; D&C 5:35.raise up at the last day.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had said these words he perceived that there were some among them who marveled, and wondered what he would concerning the law of Moses; for they understood not the saying that 3 Ne. 12:47 (46–47).old things had passed away, and that all things had become new.
And he said unto them: Marvel not that I said unto you that old things had passed away, and that all things had become Heb. 8:13; Ether 13:9.new.
Behold, I say unto you that the Mosiah 13:27 (27–31); 3 Ne. 9:17 (17–20).law is fulfilled that was given unto Moses.

Behold, 1 Cor. 10:4 (1–4); 3 Ne. 11:14. TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.I am he that gave the law, and I am he who covenanted with my people Israel; therefore, the law in me is fulfilled, for I have come to Alma 34:13. TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.fulfil the law; therefore it hath an end.
Behold, I do 3 Ne. 20:11 (11–12); 23:3 (1–3).not destroy the prophets, for as many as have not been fulfilled in me, verily I say unto you, shall all be fulfilled.
And because I said unto you that old things have passed away, I do not destroy that which hath been spoken concerning things which are to come.

For behold, the 3 Ne. 5:25 (24–26); 16:5. TG Covenants; Restoration of the Gospel.covenant which I have made with my people is not all fulfilled; but the law which was given unto Moses hath an end in me.

Behold, I am the 2 Ne. 26:1.law, and the TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World.light. Look unto me, and endure to the end, and ye shall Lev. 18:5; John 11:25; D&C 84:44.live; for unto him that Matt. 10:22 (22–33); Mark 13:13; 2 Ne. 31:20; Alma 32:13 (13–15); 3 Ne. 27:6 (6–17).endureth to the end will I give eternal life.
Behold, I have given unto you the 3 Ne. 12:20.commandments; therefore keep my commandments. And this is the law and the prophets, for they truly Mosiah 13:33.testified of me.
And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words, he 3 Ne. 13:25; Moro. 2:1.said unto those twelve whom he had chosen:
Ye are my TG Church Organization.disciples; and ye are a TG Example; Leadership.light unto this people, who are a remnant of the house of TG Israel, Joseph, People of.Joseph.
And behold, this is the 1 Ne. 18:23; 3 Ne. 16:16.land of your inheritance; and the Father hath given it unto you.
And not at any time hath the Father given me commandment that I should 3 Ne. 5:20.tell it unto your brethren at Jerusalem.

Neither at any time hath the Father given me commandment that I should tell unto them concerning the 2 Ne. 21:12; 3 Ne. 16:1 (1–4). TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.other tribes of the house of Israel, whom the Father hath led away out of the land.
This much did the Father John 15:15; 16:12.command me, that I should tell unto them:
That other sheep I have which are not of this fold; them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one TG Shepherd.shepherd.

And now, because of TG Stiffnecked.stiffneckedness and TG Doubt; Unbelief.unbelief they D&C 10:59.understood not my word; therefore I was commanded to say no more of the John 12:50.Father concerning this thing unto them.

But, verily, I say unto you that the Father hath commanded me, and I tell it unto you, that ye were 1 Kgs. 8:53; John 17:6 (6–22).separated from among them because of their iniquity; therefore it is because of their iniquity that they know not of you.
And verily, I say unto you again that the other tribes hath the Father separated from them; and it is because of their iniquity that they know not of them.

And verily I say unto you, that ye are they of whom I said: John 10:16 (14–16).Other sheep I have which are not of this fold; them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd.shepherd.
And they understood me not, for they supposed it had been the TG Gentiles.Gentiles; for they understood not that the Gentiles should be Acts 10:45 (34–48).converted through their preaching.

And they understood me not that I said they shall hear my voice; and they understood me not that the Matt. 15:24.Gentiles should not at any time hear my voice—that I should not manifest myself unto them save it were by the 1 Ne. 10:11. TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.Holy Ghost.
But behold, ye have both heard Alma 5:38; 3 Ne. 16:3 (1–5); 18:31.my voice, and seen me; and ye are my sheep, and ye are numbered among those whom the Father hath John 6:37; D&C 27:14.given me.

div.p: Jesus will visit others of the lost sheep of Israel—In the latter days the gospel will go to the Gentiles and then to the house of Israel—The Lord’s people will see eye to eye when He brings again Zion. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And verily, verily, I say unto you that I have 1 Ne. 19:10; 2 Ne. 21:12; 3 Ne. 15:15. TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.other sheep, which are not of this land, neither of the land of Jerusalem, neither in any parts of that land round about whither I have been to minister.
For they of whom I speak are they who have not as yet heard my voice; neither have I at any time manifested myself unto them.

But I have received a 3 Ne. 18:27.commandment of the Father that I shall go unto them, and that they shall 3 Ne. 17:4.hear my voice, and shall be numbered among my sheep, that there may be one fold and one shepherd; therefore I go to show myself unto them.

And I command you that ye shall 2 Ne. 25:18. TG Scriptures to Come Forth.write these sayings after I am gone, that if it so be that my people at Jerusalem, they who have seen me and been with me in my ministry, do not ask the Father in my name, that they may receive a knowledge of you by the Holy Ghost, and also of the other tribes whom they know not of, that these sayings which ye shall write shall be kept and shall be manifested unto the 1 Ne. 10:14; 3 Ne. 21:6 (1–11).Gentiles, that through the fulness of the Gentiles, the remnant of their seed, who shall be scattered forth upon the face of the earth because of their TG Unbelief.unbelief, may be brought in, or may be brought to a Ezek. 20:42 (42–44); Micah 7:9 (8–9); 3 Ne. 20:13. TG Israel, Restoration of.knowledge of me, their Redeemer.

And then will I TG Israel, Gathering of.gather them in from the four quarters of the earth; and then will I fulfil the 3 Ne. 5:25 (24–26); 15:8.covenant which the Father hath made unto all the people of the 1 Ne. 22:9; 3 Ne. 21:27 (26–29).house of Israel.

And blessed are the 1 Ne. 13:39 (23, 30–42); 2 Ne. 30:3; 3 Ne. 20:27.Gentiles, because of their belief in me, in and of the Holy Ghost, which 2 Ne. 32:5; 3 Ne. 11:32 (32, 35–36). TG Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.witnesses unto them of me and of the Father.

Behold, because of their belief in me, saith the Father, and because of the unbelief of you, O house of Israel, in the TG Restoration of the Gospel.latter day shall the truth come unto the D&C 19:27; 107:33. TG Mission of Latter-day Saints.Gentiles, that the fulness of these things shall be made known unto them.

But wo, saith the Father, unto the 2 Ne. 6:15; 28:15 (15–32); Ether 2:9 (8–11).unbelieving of the Gentiles—for notwithstanding they have come forth upon the face of this land, and have 1 Ne. 13:14; 2 Ne. 26:19; Morm. 5:9 (9, 15).scattered my people who are of the house of Israel; and my people who are of the house of Israel have been 3 Ne. 20:28.cast out from among them, and have been trodden under feet by them;

And because of the mercies of the Father unto the Gentiles, and also the judgments of the Father upon my people who are of the house of Israel, verily, verily, I say unto you, that after all this, and I have caused my people who are of the house of Israel to be smitten, and to be afflicted, and to be Amos 9:1 (1–4).slain, and to be cast out from among them, and to become Jer. 23:40; Lam. 2:16 (15–16); Joel 2:17; 1 Ne. 19:14.hated by them, and to become a hiss and a byword among them—

And thus commandeth the Father that I should say unto you: At that day when the Gentiles shall 3 Ne. 20:15.sin against my gospel, and shall reject the fulness of my gospel, and shall be Morm. 8:36 (35–41).lifted up in the pride of their hearts above all nations, and above all the people of the whole earth, and shall be filled with all manner of lyings, and of deceits, and of mischiefs, and all manner of hypocrisy, and 3 Ne. 30:2; Morm. 8:31.murders, and 2 Ne. 26:29. TG Priestcraft.priestcrafts, and whoredoms, and of secret abominations; and if they shall do all those things, and shall 3 Ne. 20:28 (27–28); D&C 6:31.reject the fulness of my gospel, behold, saith the Father, I will bring the fulness of my gospel from among them.
And then will I Isa. 44:21; 3 Ne. 20:29 (28–31); 21:4 (1–11); Morm. 5:20.remember my covenant which I have made unto my people, O house of Israel, and I will bring my gospel unto them.

And I will show unto thee, O house of Israel, that the Gentiles shall not have power over you; but I will remember my covenant unto you, O house of Israel, and ye shall come unto the Hel. 15:13. TG Israel, Restoration of; Knowledge.knowledge of the fulness of my gospel.

But if the Gentiles will repent and return unto me, saith the Father, behold they shall be Gal. 3:7 (7, 29); 1 Ne. 15:13 (13–17); 22:9 (5–10); 2 Ne. 10:18 (18–19); 3 Ne. 30:2; Abr. 2:10 (9–11).numbered among my people, O house of Israel.
And I will not suffer my people, who are of the house of Israel, to go through among them, and tread them down, saith the Father.

But if they will not turn unto me, and hearken unto my voice, I will suffer them, yea, I will suffer my people, O house of Israel, that they shall go through among them, and shall Micah 5:8 (8–15); 3 Ne. 20:16; 21:12 (12–21); D&C 87:5.tread them down, and they shall be as salt that hath lost its savor, which is thenceforth good for nothing but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of my people, O house of Israel.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, thus hath the Father commanded me—that I should give unto 3 Ne. 15:13.this people this land for their inheritance.
And then the 3 Ne. 20:11.words of the prophet Isaiah shall be fulfilled, which say:

Isa. 52:8 (8–10); 3 Ne. 20:32.Thy Ezek. 33:2 (2, 7); D&C 101:45 (45, 53–54). TG Watchman.watchmen shall lift up the voice; with the voice together shall they sing, for they shall see eye to eye when the Lord shall bring again Zion.
Break forth into joy, sing together, ye waste places of Jerusalem; for the Lord hath comforted his people, he hath redeemed Jerusalem.
The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of God.

div.p: Jesus directs the people to ponder His words and pray for understanding—He heals their sick—He prays for the people, using language that cannot be written—Angels minister to and fire encircles their little ones. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: Behold, now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words he looked round about again on the multitude, and he said unto them: Behold, my IE to return to the Father. See v. 4.time is at hand.

I TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.perceive that ye are weak, that ye cannot John 16:12; D&C 50:40; 78:18 (17–18).understand all my words which I am commanded of the Father to speak unto you at this time.

Therefore, go ye unto your homes, and TG Meditation.ponder upon the things which I have said, and ask of the Father, in my name, that ye may understand, and Ezra 7:10; D&C 29:8; 132:3.prepare your minds for the 3 Ne. 19:2.morrow, and I come unto you again.

But now I 3 Ne. 18:39.go unto the Father, and also to 3 Ne. 16:3. TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.show myself unto the lost tribes of Israel, for they are not TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.lost unto the Father, for he knoweth whither he hath taken them.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had thus spoken, he cast his eyes round about again on the multitude, and beheld they were TG God, Love of.in tears, and did look steadfastly upon him as if they would ask him to tarry a little longer with them.
And he said unto them: Behold, my bowels are filled with TG Compassion.compassion towards you.

Have ye any that are TG Sickness.sick among you? Bring them hither. Have ye any that are lame, or blind, or halt, or maimed, or TG Leprosy.leprous, or that are withered, or that are deaf, or that are afflicted in any manner? Bring them hither and I will TG Administrations to the Sick; Heal.heal them, for I have compassion upon you; my bowels are filled with mercy.

For I perceive that ye desire that I should show unto you what I have done unto your brethren at Jerusalem, for I see that your Matt. 8:10 (1–17); Luke 18:42.faith is 2 Ne. 27:23; Ether 12:12.sufficient that I should heal you.

And it came to pass that when he had thus spoken, all the multitude, with one accord, did go forth with their sick and their afflicted, and their lame, and with their Matt. 9:28 (28–31); Mosiah 3:5; 3 Ne. 26:15; D&C 84:69.blind, and with their dumb, and with all them that were afflicted in any manner; and he did heal them every one as they were brought forth unto him.

And they did all, both they who had been healed and they who were whole, bow down at his feet, and did worship him; and as many as could come for the multitude did Luke 7:38 (38, 45); 3 Ne. 11:19.kiss his feet, insomuch that they did bathe his feet with their tears.
And it came to pass that he commanded that their Matt. 19:13 (13–14); Mark 10:13; 3 Ne. 26:14 (14, 16). TG Children.little children should be brought.
So they brought their little children and set them down upon the ground round about him, and Jesus stood in the midst; and the multitude gave way till they had all been brought unto him.
And it came to pass that when they had all been brought, and Jesus stood in the midst, he commanded the multitude that they should Acts 9:40; 20:36; 3 Ne. 19:6 (6, 16–17).kneel down upon the ground.

And it came to pass that when they had knelt upon the ground, Jesus groaned within himself, and said: Father, I am Mosiah 28:3; Alma 31:2; 3 Ne. 17:20; 27:32; Moses 7:41.troubled because of the wickedness of the people of the house of Israel.

And when he had said these words, he himself also 3 Ne. 19:19 (19, 27).knelt upon the earth; and behold he TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.prayed unto the Father, and the things which he prayed cannot be written, and the multitude did bear record who heard him.

And after this manner do they bear record: The Isa. 64:4; 1 Cor. 2:9; 3 Ne. 19:32 (30–36); D&C 76:10, 116 (114–19).eye hath never seen, neither hath the ear heard, before, so great and marvelous things as we saw and heard Jesus speak unto the Father;

And no 2 Cor. 12:4; 3 Ne. 19:34 (32–34).tongue can speak, neither can there be written by any man, neither can the hearts of men conceive so great and marvelous things as we both saw and heard Jesus speak; and no one can conceive of the joy which filled our souls at the time we heard him pray for us unto the Father.
And it came to pass that when Jesus had made an end of praying unto the Father, he arose; but so great was the TG Joy.joy of the multitude that they were overcome.
And it came to pass that Jesus spake unto them, and bade them arise.
And they arose from the earth, and he said unto them: Blessed are ye because of your faith. And 3 Ne. 17:14.now behold, my joy is full.

And when he had said these words, he John 11:35. TG Sincere.wept, and the multitude bare record of it, and he took their little children, one by one, and Mark 10:16 (14–16).blessed them, and prayed unto the Father for them.
And when he had done this he wept again;
And he spake unto the multitude, and said unto them: Behold your little ones.

And as they looked to behold they cast their eyes towards heaven, and they saw the heavens open, and they saw angels descending out of heaven as it were in the midst of fire; and they came down and Hel. 5:43 (23–24, 43–45); 3 Ne. 19:14. TG Transfiguration.encircled those little ones about, and they were encircled about with fire; and the angels did minister unto them.

And the multitude did see and Ex. 19:9 (9–13).hear and bear record; and they know that their record is true for they all of them did see and hear, every man for himself; and they were in number about two thousand and five hundred souls; and they did consist of men, women, and children.

div.p: Jesus institutes the sacrament among the Nephites—They are commanded to pray always in His name—Those who eat His flesh and drink His blood unworthily are damned—The disciples are given power to confer the Holy Ghost. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that Jesus commanded his disciples that they should bring forth some Matt. 26:26; 3 Ne. 20:3 (3–9); 26:13.bread and wine unto him.
And while they were gone for bread and wine, he commanded the multitude that they should sit themselves down upon the earth.

And when the disciples had come with TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.bread and wine, he took of the bread and brake and blessed it; and he gave unto the disciples and commanded that they should eat.
And when they had eaten and were filled, he commanded that they should give unto the multitude.

And when the multitude had eaten and were filled, he said unto the disciples: Behold there shall one be TG Church Organization.ordained among you, and to him will I give power that he shall Moro. 4:1.break Matt. 14:19 (19–21); 3 Ne. 20:6 (6–7).bread and bless it and give it unto the people of my TG Church.church, unto all those who shall believe and be baptized in my name.
And this shall ye always observe to TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.do, even as I have done, even as I have broken bread and blessed it and given it unto you.

And this shall ye do in 3 Ne. 20:8; Moro. 4:3. TG Sacrament.remembrance of my TG Bread of Life.body, which I have shown unto you. And it shall be a testimony unto the Father that ye do always remember me. And if ye do always remember me ye shall have my Spirit to be with you.

And it came to pass that when he said these words, he commanded his disciples that they should take of the Matt. 26:27 (27–29). TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.wine of the cup and drink of it, and that they should also give unto the multitude that they might drink of it.
And it came to pass that they did so, and did drink of it and were filled; and they gave unto the multitude, and they did drink, and they were filled.

And when the disciples had done this, Jesus said unto them: Blessed are ye for this thing which ye have done, for this is fulfilling my commandments, and this doth witness unto the Father that ye are TG Commitment.willing to do that which I have commanded you.

And this shall ye always do to those who repent and are baptized in my name; and ye shall do it in Moro. 5:1.remembrance of my TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood, which I have shed for you, that ye may witness unto the Father that ye do always remember me. And if ye do always remember me ye shall have my Spirit to be with you.
And I give unto you a commandment that ye shall do these things. And if ye shall always do these things blessed are ye, for ye are built upon my TG Rock.rock.

But whoso among you shall do Josh. 1:7; D&C 3:2.more or less than these are not built upon my rock, but are built upon a sandy foundation; and when the rain descends, and the floods come, and the winds blow, and beat upon them, they shall TG Apostasy of Individuals.fall, and the Matt. 16:18; 3 Ne. 11:39.gates of hell are ready open to receive them.
Therefore blessed are ye if ye shall keep my commandments, which the Father hath commanded me that I should give unto you.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, ye must watch and Alma 34:23; D&C 93:49.pray always, lest ye be tempted by the devil, and ye be led away captive by him.

And as I have prayed among you even so shall ye pray in my TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church, among my people who do repent and are baptized in my name. Behold I am the TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World.light; I have set an TG God, the Standard of Righteousness; Jesus Christ, Exemplar.example for you.
And it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words unto his disciples, he turned again unto the multitude and said unto them:

Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, ye must watch and pray always lest ye enter into temptation; for Luke 22:31; 2 Ne. 2:18 (17–18); D&C 10:22 (22–27).Satan desireth to have you, that he may sift you as wheat.
Therefore ye must always pray unto the Father in my name;

And Isa. 58:9 (8–9); Matt. 21:22 (21–22); Mark 11:24; Hel. 10:5; Morm. 9:21; Moro. 7:26; D&C 88:64 (63–65).whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, which is right, believing that ye shall receive, behold it shall be given unto you.
Alma 34:21. TG Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.Pray in your families unto the Father, always in my name, that your wives and your children may be blessed.
And behold, ye shall TG Meetings.meet together oft; and ye shall not forbid any man from coming unto you when ye shall meet together, but suffer them that they may come unto you and forbid them not;
But ye shall 3 Ne. 18:30. TG Missionary Work.pray for them, and shall not cast them out; and if it so be that they come unto you oft ye shall pray for them unto the Father, in my name.

Therefore, hold up your TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World.light that it may shine unto the world. Behold I am the Matt. 5:16; Mark 4:21; 3 Ne. 12:16.light which ye shall hold up—that which ye have seen me do. Behold ye see that I have prayed unto the Father, and ye all have witnessed.

And ye see that I have commanded that Alma 5:33; 19:36.none of you should go away, but rather have commanded that ye should come unto me, that ye might 3 Ne. 11:15 (14–16).feel and see; even so shall ye do unto the world; and whosoever breaketh this commandment suffereth himself to be led into temptation.
And now it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words, he turned his eyes again upon the 3 Ne. 13:25.disciples whom he had chosen, and said unto them:

Behold verily, verily, I say unto you, I give unto you another commandment, and then I must go unto my TG God the Father, Elohim.Father that I may fulfil 3 Ne. 16:3.other commandments which he hath given me.

And now behold, this is the commandment which I give unto you, that ye shall not suffer any one knowingly to Ex. 12:43; 1 Cor. 11:27 (27–30); 4 Ne. 1:27.partake of my flesh and blood Lev. 7:18; Morm. 9:29.unworthily, when ye shall minister it;

For whoso eateth and drinketh my flesh and TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood 3 Ne. 12:24 (23–26); D&C 46:4.unworthily eateth and drinketh damnation to his soul; therefore if ye know that a man is unworthy to eat and drink of my flesh and blood ye shall forbid him.

Nevertheless, ye shall not D&C 46:3.cast him out from among you, but ye shall 3 Ne. 18:23.minister unto him and shall pray for him unto the Father, in my name; and if it so be that he repenteth and is baptized in my name, then shall ye receive him, and shall minister unto him of my flesh and blood.
But if he repent not he shall not be numbered among my people, that he may not destroy my people, for behold I D&C 27:14.know John 10:14; Alma 5:38; 3 Ne. 15:24.my sheep, and they are numbered.

Nevertheless, ye shall not cast him out of your Alma 16:13; Moro. 7:1.synagogues, or your places of worship, for unto such shall ye continue to minister; for ye know not but what they will return and repent, and come unto me with full purpose of heart, and I shall Jer. 3:22; 3 Ne. 9:13 (13–14); D&C 112:13.heal them; and ye shall be the means of bringing salvation unto them.
Therefore, keep these sayings which I have commanded you that ye come not under TG Condemnation.condemnation; for wo unto him whom the Father condemneth.
And I give you these commandments because of the disputations which have been among you. And blessed are ye if ye have 3 Ne. 11:28 (28–30). TG Disputations.no disputations among you.
And now I go unto the Father, because it is expedient that I should go unto the Father 1 Jn. 2:1; 2 Ne. 2:9; Moro. 7:28 (27–28); D&C 29:5.for your sakes.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had made an end of these sayings, he touched with his TG Hands, Laying on of.hand the 1 Ne. 12:7; 3 Ne. 15:11; 19:4 (4–12).disciples whom he had chosen, one by one, even until he had touched them all, and spake unto them as he touched them.

And the multitude heard not the words which he spake, therefore they did not bear record; but the disciples bare record that he gave them TG Holy Ghost, Gift of; Priesthood, Authority.power to give the Alma 31:36.Holy Ghost. And I will show unto you 3 Ne. 26:17; 28:18; Moro. 2:2 (2–3).hereafter that this record is true.
And it came to pass that when Jesus had touched them all, there came a Ex. 19:9 (9, 16); 2 Chr. 5:14 (11–14).cloud and overshadowed the multitude that they could not see Jesus.
And while they were overshadowed he 3 Ne. 17:4.departed from them, and ascended into heaven. And the disciples saw and did bear record that he ascended again into heaven.

div.p: The twelve disciples minister unto the people and pray for the Holy Ghost—The disciples are baptized and receive the Holy Ghost and the ministering of angels—Jesus prays using words that cannot be written—He attests to the exceedingly great faith of these Nephites. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that when Jesus had ascended into heaven, the multitude did disperse, and every man did take his wife and his children and did return to his own home.

And it was noised abroad among the people immediately, before it was yet dark, that the multitude had seen Jesus, and that he had ministered unto them, and that he would also show himself on the 3 Ne. 17:3.morrow unto the multitude.

Yea, and even all the night it was noised abroad concerning Jesus; and insomuch did they send forth unto the people that there were many, yea, an exceedingly great number, did labor exceedingly all that night, that they might be on the morrow in the place where Jesus should show himself unto the multitude.

And it came to pass that on the morrow, when the multitude was gathered together, behold, Nephi and his 3 Ne. 7:19.brother whom he had raised from the TG Death, Power over.dead, whose name was Timothy, and also his son, whose name was Jonas, and also Mathoni, and Mathonihah, his brother, and Kumen, and Kumenonhi, and Jeremiah, and Shemnon, and Jonas, and Zedekiah, and Isaiah—now these were the 3 Ne. 28:25.names of the 3 Ne. 18:36 (36–37); 26:17.disciples whom Jesus had chosen—and it came to pass that they went forth and stood in the midst of the multitude.
And behold, the multitude was Mosiah 2:7.so great that they did cause that they should be separated into twelve bodies.
And the twelve did teach the multitude; and behold, they did cause that the multitude should 3 Ne. 17:13.kneel down upon the face of the earth, and should pray unto the Father in the name of Jesus.
And the disciples did pray unto the Father also in the name of Jesus. And it came to pass that they arose and ministered unto the people.
And when they had ministered those same words which Jesus had spoken—nothing varying from the words which Jesus had spoken—behold, they knelt again and prayed to the Father in the name of Jesus.
And they did pray for that which they most desired; and they desired that the 3 Ne. 9:20.Holy Ghost should be given unto them.
And when they had thus prayed they went down unto the water’s edge, and the multitude followed them.
And it came to pass that Nephi went down 3 Ne. 11:23.into the water and was Matt. 3:14 (13–15); JS—H 1:71 (70–71).baptized.
And he came up out of the water and began to baptize. And he baptized all those whom Jesus had chosen.

And it came to pass when they were all baptized and had come TG Baptism, Immersion.up out of the water, the 3 Ne. 12:2; Morm. 7:10. TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.Holy Ghost did fall upon them, and they were filled with the Holy Ghost and with fire.

And behold, they were Hel. 5:43 (23–24, 43–45); 3 Ne. 17:24.encircled about as if it were by fire; and it came down from heaven, and the multitude did witness it, and did bear record; and angels did come down out of heaven and did minister unto them.
And it came to pass that while the angels were ministering unto the disciples, behold, Jesus came and stood in the midst and ministered unto them.
And it came to pass that he spake unto the multitude, and commanded them that they should kneel down again upon the earth, and also that his disciples should kneel down upon the earth.
And it came to pass that when they had all knelt down upon the earth, he commanded his disciples that they should pray.
And behold, they began to pray; and they did pray unto Jesus, calling him their Lord and their God.
And it came to pass that Jesus departed out of the midst of them, and went a little way off from them and 3 Ne. 17:15.bowed himself to the earth, and he said:
Father, I thank thee that thou hast given the Holy Ghost unto these whom I have TG Church Organization.chosen; and it is because of their belief in me that I have chosen them out of the world.
Father, I pray thee that thou wilt give the Holy Ghost unto all them that shall believe in their words.

Father, thou hast given them the Holy Ghost because they believe in Acts 7:59.me; and thou seest that they believe in me because thou hearest them, and they pray unto me; and they pray unto me because I am with them.

And now Father, I TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.pray unto thee for them, and also for all those who shall believe on their words, that they may believe in me, that I may be in them 3 Ne. 9:15; 11:27.as thou, Father, art in me, that we may be John 17:22 (1–22); 1 Cor. 6:17.one.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had thus prayed unto the Father, he came unto his disciples, and behold, they did still continue, without ceasing, to pray unto him; and they did not Matt. 6:7.multiply many words, for it was given unto them what they should Hel. 10:5; D&C 46:30.pray, and they were filled with desire.

And it came to pass that Jesus blessed them as they did pray unto him; and his Num. 6:25 (23–27).countenance did smile upon them, and the light of his Ps. 4:6.countenance did Dan. 9:17.shine upon them, and behold they were as TG Transfiguration.white as the countenance and also the garments of Jesus; and behold the whiteness thereof did exceed all the whiteness, yea, even there could be nothing upon earth so white as the whiteness thereof.
And Jesus said unto them: Pray on; nevertheless they did not cease to pray.
And he turned from them again, and went a little way off and bowed himself to the earth; and he prayed again unto the Father, saying:

Father, I thank thee that thou hast Neh. 12:30; Moro. 7:48; D&C 50:29 (28–29); 88:74 (74–75). TG Purity.purified those whom I have chosen, because of their faith, and I pray for them, and also for them who shall believe on their words, that they may be purified in me, through faith on their words, even as they are purified in me.

Father, I pray not for the world, but for those whom thou hast given me John 17:6.out of the world, because of their faith, that they may be purified in me, that I may be in them as thou, Father, art in me, that we may be one, that I may be glorified in them.

And when Jesus had spoken these words he came again unto his disciples; and behold they did pray steadfastly, without ceasing, unto him; and he did smile upon them again; and behold they were Matt. 17:2.white, even as Jesus.
And it came to pass that he went again a little way off and prayed unto the Father;
And tongue cannot speak the words which he prayed, neither can be 3 Ne. 5:18; D&C 76:116.written by man the words which he prayed.
And the multitude did hear and do bear record; and their Prov. 2:2; 8:5; Isa. 44:18; Mosiah 2:9; 12:27.hearts were open and they did understand in their hearts the words which he prayed.
Nevertheless, so great and marvelous were the words which he prayed that they cannot be written, neither can they be 2 Cor. 12:4; 3 Ne. 17:17.uttered by man.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had made an end of praying he came again to the disciples, and said unto them: Matt. 8:10.So great TG Faith.faith have I never seen among all the Jews; wherefore I could not show unto them so great John 11:47 (47–48).miracles, because of their Matt. 13:58. TG Doubt; Unbelief.unbelief.
Verily I say unto you, there are none of them that have seen so great things as ye have seen; neither have they heard so great things as ye have heard.

div.p: Jesus provides bread and wine miraculously and again administers the sacrament unto the people—The remnant of Jacob will come to the knowledge of the Lord their God and will inherit the Americas—Jesus is the prophet like unto Moses, and the Nephites are children of the prophets—Others of the Lord’s people will be gathered to Jerusalem. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that he commanded the multitude that they should 1 Sam. 7:8; 2 Ne. 32:9.cease to Mosiah 24:12.pray, and also his disciples. And he commanded them that they should not cease to pray in their hearts.
And he commanded them that they should arise and stand up upon their feet. And they arose up and stood upon their feet.
And it came to pass that he Mark 6:41 (36–44).brake 3 Ne. 18:1; 26:13.bread again and blessed it, and gave to the disciples to eat.
And when they had eaten he commanded them that they should break bread, and give unto the multitude.
And when they had given unto the multitude he also gave them wine to drink, and commanded them that they should give unto the multitude.
Now, there had been no Matt. 14:19 (19–21).bread, neither wine, brought by the disciples, neither by the multitude;
But he truly John 6:14.gave unto them bread to eat, and also wine to drink.

And he said unto them: He that eateth this bread eateth of John 6:51 (50–58); 1 Cor. 11:24 (20–26); 3 Ne. 18:7; Moro. 4:3.my body to his soul; and he that drinketh of this wine drinketh of my blood to his soul; and his soul shall never hunger nor thirst, but shall be filled.
Now, when the multitude had all eaten and drunk, behold, they were filled with the Spirit; and they did cry out with one voice, and gave glory to Jesus, whom they both saw and heard.

And it came to pass that when they had all given glory unto Jesus, he said unto them: Behold now I finish the commandment which the Father hath commanded me concerning this people, who are a remnant of the house of Israel.

Ye remember that I spake unto you, and said that when the 3 Ne. 16:17 (17–20); 23:3 (1–3).words of 2 Ne. 25:5 (1–5); Morm. 8:23.Isaiah should be fulfilled—behold they are written, ye have them before you, therefore search them—

And verily, verily, I say unto you, that when they shall be fulfilled then is the fulfilling of the Gen. 17:11 (9–12); 3 Ne. 15:7.covenant which the Father hath made unto his people, O house of Israel.

And then shall the 3 Ne. 16:13 (6–13); 21:3 (2–7).remnants, which shall be TG Israel, Scattering of.scattered abroad upon the face of the earth, be Jer. 46:27 (2–28); 3 Ne. 22:7 (6–17). TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered in from the east and from the west, and from the south and from the north; and they shall be brought to the Ezek. 20:42 (42–44); 3 Ne. 16:4 (4–5).knowledge of the Lord their God, who hath redeemed them.
And the Father hath TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.commanded me that I should give unto you this TG Promised Lands.land, for your inheritance.
And I say unto you, that if the Gentiles do not 3 Ne. 16:10 (10–14).repent after the 3 Ne. 20:27.blessing which they shall receive, after they have scattered my people—

Then shall ye, who are a TG Israel, Remnant of.remnant of the house of Jacob, go forth among them; and ye shall be in the midst of them who shall be many; and ye shall be among them as a lion among the beasts of the forest, and as a young Gen. 49:9; Morm. 5:24; D&C 87:5 (4–5); 109:65 (65–67). TG Israel, Deliverance of.lion among the flocks of sheep, who, if he goeth through both Micah 5:8 (8–9); 3 Ne. 16:15 (14–15); 21:12 (11–21).treadeth down and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.
Thy hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.
And I will Micah 4:12.gather my people together as a man gathereth his sheaves into the floor.

For I will make my Lev. 26:12; D&C 63:1 (1–6).people with whom the Father hath covenanted, yea, I will make thy TG Last Days.horn iron, and I will make thy hoofs brass. And thou shalt Micah 4:13.beat in pieces many people; and I will consecrate their gain unto the Lord, and their substance unto the Lord of the whole earth. And behold, I am he who doeth it.

And it shall come to pass, saith the Father, that the 1 Ne. 14:17; 22:16 (15–16); 3 Ne. 29:4.sword of my justice shall hang over them at that day; and except they repent it shall fall upon them, saith the Father, yea, even upon all the nations of the Gentiles.
And it shall come to pass that I will establish my 1 Kgs. 8:51; 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–15); 21:23 (12–24).people, O house of Israel.

And behold, this TG Israel, Joseph, People of.people will I establish in this land, unto the fulfilling of the Gen. 49:26 (22–26).covenant which I made with your father Jacob; and it shall be a Isa. 2:3 (2–5); 3 Ne. 21:23 (23–24); Ether 13:3 (1–12); D&C 84:2 (2–4). TG Jerusalem, New.New Jerusalem. And the 3 Ne. 21:25.powers of heaven shall be in the midst of this people; yea, even Isa. 59:20 (20–21); 3 Ne. 24:1.I will be in the midst of you.

Behold, I am he of whom Moses spake, saying: Deut. 18:15 (15–19); Acts 3:22 (22–23); 1 Ne. 22:20 (20–21); D&C 133:63.A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you. And it shall come to pass that every soul who will not hear that prophet shall be cut off from among the people.
Verily I say unto you, yea, and Acts 3:24 (24–26); 1 Ne. 10:5; Jacob 7:11.all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have testified of me.

And behold, ye are the Rom. 4:24 (23–24).children of the prophets; and ye are of the house of Israel; and ye are of the TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenant which the Father made with your fathers, saying unto Abraham: And Gen. 12:3 (1–3); 22:18 (9, 18). TG Seed of Abraham.in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.

The Father having raised me up unto you first, and sent me to TG Israel, Blessings of.bless you in Prov. 16:6; Alma 19:33.turning away every one of you from his iniquities; and this because ye are the children of the covenant—

And after that ye were blessed then fulfilleth the Father the covenant which he made with Abraham, saying: Gen. 12:2 (1–3); Gal. 3:8 (7–29); 2 Ne. 29:14; Abr. 2:9.In thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed—unto the pouring out of the Holy Ghost through me upon the Gentiles, which 3 Ne. 20:15.blessing upon the 3 Ne. 16:6 (6–7); Morm. 5:19.Gentiles shall make them mighty above all, unto the 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–9); Morm. 5:9.scattering of my people, O house of Israel.

And they shall be a Josh. 23:13; 1 Ne. 2:24; 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–10).scourge unto the people of this land. Nevertheless, when they shall have received the fulness of my gospel, then if they shall harden their hearts against me I will return their Isa. 51:23.iniquities upon their own heads, saith the Father.

And I will Isa. 44:21; 3 Ne. 16:11 (11–12).remember the covenant which I have made with my people; and I have covenanted with them that I would TG Israel, Gathering of.gather them together in mine own due time, that I would give unto them again the Amos 9:15; Alma 7:10; D&C 133:24. TG Israel, Land of.land of their fathers for their inheritance, which is the land of Jerusalem, which is the promised land unto them forever, saith the Father.
And it shall come to pass that the time cometh, when the fulness of my gospel shall be preached unto them;
And they shall 3 Ne. 5:26 (21–26); 21:26 (26–29).believe in me, that I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God, and shall pray unto the Father in my name.
Then shall their Isa. 52:8 (8–9); 3 Ne. 16:18 (18–20). TG Watchman.watchmen lift up their voice, and with the voice together shall they sing; for they shall see eye to eye.

Then will the Father gather them together again, and give unto them Isa. 18:7; D&C 84:2. TG Jerusalem.Jerusalem for the Deut. 11:11. TG Israel, Land of; Lands of Inheritance.land of their inheritance.
Then shall they break forth into joy—Isa. 54:1.Sing together, ye waste places of Jerusalem; for the Father hath comforted his people, he hath redeemed Jerusalem.
The Father hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of the Father; and the Father and I are one.

And then shall be brought to pass that which is written: Isa. 52:1 (1–3); D&C 113:7 (7–10). TG Priesthood, Power of.Awake, awake again, and put on thy strength, O Zion; put on thy beautiful garments, O Jerusalem, the holy city, for henceforth there shall no more come into thee the uncircumcised and the unclean.
Shake thyself from the dust; arise, sit down, O Jerusalem; loose thyself from the bands of thy neck, O captive daughter of Zion.
For thus saith the Lord: Ye have sold yourselves for naught, and ye shall be redeemed without money.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, that my people shall know my name; yea, in that day they shall know that I am he that doth speak.

And then shall they say: Isa. 52:7; Nahum 1:15; Mosiah 15:18 (13–18); D&C 128:19.How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him that bringeth good tidings unto them, that Mark 13:10; 1 Ne. 13:37.publisheth peace; that bringeth good tidings unto them of good, that publisheth salvation; that saith unto Zion: Thy God reigneth!

And then shall a cry go forth: Isa. 52:11 (11–15).Depart ye, depart ye, go ye out from thence, touch not that which is TG Uncleanness.unclean; go ye out of the midst of her; be ye D&C 133:5. TG Cleanliness.clean that bear the vessels of the Lord.
For ye shall 3 Ne. 21:29.not go out with TG Haste; Rashness.haste nor go by flight; for the Lord will go before you, and the God of Israel shall be your rearward.
Behold, my servant shall deal prudently; he shall be exalted and extolled and be very high.
As many were astonished at thee—his visage was so marred, more than any man, and his form more than the sons of men—

So shall he Lev. 1:5; Isa. 52:15; Ezek. 36:25.sprinkle many nations; the kings shall shut their mouths at him, for that which had not been told them shall they see; and that which they had not heard shall they 3 Ne. 21:8; D&C 101:94.consider.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, all these things shall surely come, even as the Father hath commanded me. Then shall this covenant which the Father hath covenanted with his people be fulfilled; and then shall Joel 2:18; Ether 13:5 (5, 11).Jerusalem be inhabited again with my people, and it shall be the land of their inheritance.

div.p: Israel will be gathered when the Book of Mormon comes forth—The Gentiles will be established as a free people in America—They will be saved if they believe and obey; otherwise, they will be cut off and destroyed—Israel will build the New Jerusalem, and the lost tribes will return. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And verily I say unto you, I give unto you a Isa. 66:19.sign, that ye may know the TG Last Days.time when these things shall be about to take place—that I shall gather in, from their long dispersion, my people, O house of Israel, and shall establish again among them my Zion;

And behold, this is the thing which I will give unto you for a sign—for verily I say unto you that 1 Ne. 10:14; Ether 4:17; D&C 20:9 (8–11); JS—H 1:34.when these things which I declare unto you, and which I shall declare unto you hereafter of myself, and by the power of the Holy Ghost which shall be given unto you of the Father, shall be made known unto the Gentiles that they may know concerning this people who are a remnant of the house of Jacob, and concerning this my people who shall be scattered by them;

Verily, verily, I say unto you, when these things shall be made TG Witness of the Father.known unto them of the Father, and shall come forth of the Father, 3 Ne. 20:13; Morm. 5:15 (10–21).from them unto you;

For it is wisdom in the Father that they should be established in this land, and be set up as a John 8:32 (32–36); 1 Ne. 13:19 (17–19); D&C 101:77 (77–80).free people by the power of the Father, that these things might come forth from them unto a remnant of your seed, that the Ps. 89:35; 3 Ne. 16:11 (8–12); Morm. 5:20. TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenant of the Father may be fulfilled which he hath covenanted with his people, O house of Israel;

Therefore, when these works and the works which shall be wrought among you hereafter shall come forth 3 Ne. 26:8.from the Gentiles, unto your 2 Ne. 30:5; Morm. 5:15; D&C 3:18.seed which shall dwindle in unbelief because of iniquity;

For thus it behooveth the Father that it should come forth from the 1 Ne. 10:14; Jacob 5:54; 3 Ne. 16:4 (4–7); 21:24 (24–26); Morm. 5:15.Gentiles, that he may show forth his power unto the Gentiles, for this cause that the Gentiles, if they will not harden their hearts, that they may repent and come unto me and be baptized in my name and know of the true points of my doctrine, that they may be Gal. 3:7 (7, 29); 2 Ne. 30:3; 3 Ne. 16:13; Abr. 2:10 (9–11).numbered among my people, O house of Israel;

And when these things come to pass that thy 3 Ne. 5:23 (21–26); 21:26.seed shall begin to know these things—it shall be a sign unto them, that they may know that the work of the Father hath already commenced unto the fulfilling of the covenant which he hath made unto the people who are of the house of Israel.

And when that day shall come, it shall come to pass that kings shall shut their mouths; for that which had not been told them shall they see; and that which they had not heard shall they 3 Ne. 20:45.consider.

For in that day, for my sake shall the Father TG God, Works of.work a work, which shall be a great and a 1 Ne. 22:8. TG Restoration of the Gospel.marvelous Acts 13:41 (40–41).work among them; and there shall be among them those who will not believe it, although a man shall declare it unto them.

But behold, the life of my servant shall be in my hand; therefore they shall not hurt him, although he shall be D&C 135:1 (1–3).marred because of them. Yet I will heal him, for I will show unto them that D&C 10:43.my wisdom is greater than the cunning of the devil.

Therefore it shall come to pass that whosoever will not believe in my words, who am Jesus Christ, which the Father shall cause 2 Ne. 3:11 (6–15); Morm. 8:16 (16, 25); Ether 3:28 (21–28).him to bring forth unto the 1 Ne. 21:6.Gentiles, and shall give unto him power that he shall bring them forth unto the Gentiles, (it shall be done even as Moses said) they shall be D&C 1:14; 133:63.cut off from among my people who are of the covenant.

And my people who are a remnant of Jacob shall be among the Gentiles, yea, in the midst of them as a Isa. 5:29; Micah 5:8 (8–15); Mal. 4:3; 2 Ne. 15:29; 3 Ne. 16:15 (7–15); 20:16; 25:3; D&C 87:5.lion among the beasts of the forest, as a young lion among the flocks of sheep, who, if he go through both treadeth down and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.
Their hand shall be lifted up upon their 1 Ne. 21:17 (17–19).adversaries, and all their enemies shall be cut off.

Yea, wo be unto the Gentiles except they Eph. 3:6 (1–7); 2 Ne. 10:18; 33:9.repent; for it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Father, that I will cut off thy horses out of the midst of thee, and I will destroy thy Lev. 26:22 (21–22); Hel. 14:24.chariots;
And I will cut off the cities of thy land, and throw down all thy 2 Ne. 12:15.strongholds;
And I will cut off TG Sorcery; Superstitions.witchcrafts out of thy land, and thou shalt have no more soothsayers;

Thy Ex. 20:4 (3–4, 23); Isa. 41:29 (24, 29); Mosiah 13:12; D&C 1:16. TG Idolatry.graven images I will also cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee, and thou shalt no more worship the works of thy hands;
And I will pluck up thy 1 Kgs. 16:33 (32–33).groves out of the midst of thee; so will I destroy thy cities.
And it shall come to pass that all 3 Ne. 30:2; D&C 109:30.lyings, and deceivings, and envyings, and strifes, and priestcrafts, and whoredoms, shall be done away.

For it shall come to pass, saith the Father, that at that Amos 5:18; Alma 29:2; Morm. 9:27.day whosoever will not repent and come unto my Beloved Son, them will I Jer. 44:8 (6–8).cut off from among my people, O house of Israel;
And I will execute Isa. 34:8; 61:2; Jer. 23:19 (19–20); Mal. 4:1 (1, 3); D&C 97:26 (25–28).vengeance and Ezek. 21:17 (14–17).fury upon them, even as upon the heathen, such as they have not heard.

But if they will repent and hearken unto my words, and TG Hardheartedness.harden not their hearts, I will 1 Ne. 14:12 (12, 14). TG Dispensations; Millennium, Preparing a People for.establish my church among them, and they shall come in unto the covenant and be 2 Ne. 10:18 (18–19); 3 Ne. 16:13; 30:2.numbered among this the remnant of Jacob, unto whom I have given this land for their TG Lands of Inheritance.inheritance;

And they shall assist my 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–15).people, the remnant of Jacob, and also as many of the house of Israel as shall come, that they may build a city, which shall be called the 3 Ne. 20:22 (21–22, 39); Ether 13:3 (1–12). TG Jerusalem, New.New Jerusalem.
And then shall 3 Ne. 21:6.they assist my people that they may be gathered in, who are scattered upon all the face of the land, in unto the New Jerusalem.
And then shall the 1 Ne. 13:37; 3 Ne. 20:22.power of heaven come down among them; and Isa. 2:2 (2–4); 59:20 (20–21); 3 Ne. 24:1.I also will be in the midst.

And then shall the work of the Father commence at that day, even 3 Ne. 20:31 (29–34); Morm. 5:14.when this gospel shall be preached among the remnant of 1 Ne. 14:17; 3 Ne. 21:6 (6–7).this people. Verily I say unto you, at that day shall the work of the Father commence among all the dispersed of my people, yea, even the tribes which have been TG Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.lost, which the Father hath led away out of Jerusalem.

Yea, the work shall commence among all the 3 Ne. 16:5 (4–5).dispersed of my people, with the Father to prepare the way whereby they may TG Israel, Restoration of.come unto me, that they may call on the Father in my name.
Yea, and then shall the work commence, with the Father among all nations in preparing the way whereby his people may be TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered home to the land of their inheritance.
And they shall go out from all nations; and they shall 3 Ne. 20:42.not go out in Isa. 52:12 (11–12).haste, nor go by flight, for I will go before them, saith the Father, and I will be their rearward.

div.p: In the last days, Zion and her stakes will be established, and Israel will be gathered in mercy and tenderness—They will triumph—Compare Isaiah 54. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And then shall that which is written come to pass: Sing, O Isa. 54:1 (1–17).barren, thou that didst not bear; break forth into TG Singing.singing, and cry aloud, thou that didst not travail with child; for more are the children of the Isa. 49:21.desolate than the children of the married wife, saith the Lord.
Enlarge the place of thy tent, and let them stretch forth the curtains of thy habitations; spare not, lengthen thy cords and strengthen thy TG Stake.stakes;
For thou shalt break forth on the right hand and on the left, and thy seed shall Obad. 1:19 (19–21).inherit the TG Gentiles.Gentiles and make the desolate cities to be inhabited.

Fear not, for thou shalt not be ashamed; neither be thou confounded, for thou shalt not be put to Joel 2:26 (26–27); 2 Ne. 6:13 (7, 13).shame; for thou shalt forget the TG Shame.shame of thy youth, and shalt not remember the TG Reproach.reproach of thy youth, and shalt not remember the reproach of thy widowhood any more.
For thy maker, thy Hosea 3:5 (4–5).husband, the Lord of Hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel—the God of the whole earth shall he be called.
For the Lord hath called thee Isa. 62:4.as a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit, and a wife of youth, when thou wast refused, saith thy God.
For a small moment have I Jer. 46:27 (2–28); 3 Ne. 20:13 (11–13).forsaken thee, but with great mercies will I gather thee.
In a little wrath I hid my face from thee for a moment, but with everlasting TG Kindness.kindness will I have TG God, Mercy of.mercy on thee, saith the Lord thy Redeemer.

For Isa. 54:9.this, the Gen. 8:21; Matt. 24:37 (36–38); Alma 10:22. TG Earth, Cleansing of.waters of Noah unto me, for as I have sworn that the waters of Noah should no more go over the earth, so have I sworn that I would not be wroth with thee.

For the Isa. 40:4 (4–5). TG Earth, Renewal of.mountains shall depart and the hills be removed, but my TG Israel, Blessings of.kindness shall not Ps. 94:14; D&C 35:25.depart from thee, neither shall the covenant of my peace be removed, saith the Lord that hath mercy on thee.
O thou afflicted, tossed with tempest, and not comforted! Behold, I will lay thy Rev. 21:19 (18–21). TG Rock.stones with fair colors, and lay thy foundations with sapphires.
And I will make thy windows of agates, and thy gates of carbuncles, and all thy borders of pleasant stones.
And Isa. 60:21; Jer. 31:34 (33–34).all thy children shall be taught of the Lord; and great shall be the 1 Ne. 22:17 (15–22); 2 Ne. 30:10; Moses 7:61.peace of thy children.
In TG Righteousness.righteousness shalt thou be established; thou shalt be far from oppression for thou shalt not fear, and from terror for it shall not come near thee.
Behold, they shall surely gather together 1 Ne. 22:14.against thee, not by me; whosoever shall gather together against thee shall fall for thy sake.
Behold, I have created the smith that bloweth the coals in the fire, and that bringeth forth an instrument for his work; and I have created the waster to destroy.

No weapon that is formed against thee shall prosper; and every tongue that shall revile against thee in judgment thou shalt condemn. This is the heritage of the TG Servant.servants of the Lord, and their righteousness is of me, saith the Lord.

div.p: Jesus approves the words of Isaiah—He commands the people to search the prophets—The words of Samuel the Lamanite concerning the Resurrection are added to their records. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And now, behold, I say unto you, that ye ought to TG Scriptures, Study of.search these things. Yea, a commandment I give unto you that ye search these things diligently; for great are the words of 2 Ne. 25:5 (1–5); 3 Ne. 20:11; Morm. 8:23.Isaiah.
For surely he spake as touching all things concerning my people which are of the house of Israel; Isa. 49:6.therefore it must needs be that he must speak also to the Gentiles.
And all things that he spake have been and 3 Ne. 15:6; 20:11 (11–12).shall be, even according to the words which he spake.
Therefore give heed to my words; write the things which I have told you; and according to the time and the will of the Father Morm. 8:26.they shall go forth unto the Gentiles.
And whosoever will hearken unto my words and repenteth and is baptized, the same shall be saved. Search the Luke 24:27 (25–27).prophets, for many there be that testify of these things.

And now it came to pass that when Jesus had said these words he said unto them again, after he had expounded all the scriptures unto them which they had received, he said unto them: Behold, other scriptures I would that ye should write, that ye have not.
And it came to pass that he said unto 3 Ne. 8:1; 4 Ne. 1:19.Nephi: Bring forth the record which ye have kept.
And when Nephi had brought forth the records, and laid them before him, he cast his eyes upon them and said:

Verily I say unto you, I commanded my servant Hel. 13:2.Samuel, the Lamanite, that he should testify unto this people, that at the day that the Father should glorify his name in me that there were Hel. 14:25 (1, 21–26).many TG Saints.saints who should TG Resurrection.arise from the dead, and should appear unto many, and should minister unto them. And he said unto them: Was it not so?
And his disciples answered him and said: Yea, Lord, Samuel did prophesy according to thy words, and they were all fulfilled.

And Jesus said unto them: How be it that ye have not TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.written this thing, that many Matt. 27:52 (52–53).saints did arise and appear unto many and did minister unto them?
And it came to pass that Nephi remembered that this thing had not been written.
And it came to pass that Jesus commanded that it should be TG Record Keeping.written; therefore it was written according as he commanded.

And now it came to pass that when Jesus had Luke 24:44 (27, 44).expounded all the scriptures in one, which they had written, he commanded them that they should TG Scriptures, Study of; Teaching.teach the things which he had expounded unto them.

div.p: The Lord’s messenger will prepare the way for the Second Coming—Christ will sit in judgment—Israel is commanded to pay tithes and offerings—A book of remembrance is kept—Compare Malachi 3. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that he commanded them that they should write the words which the Father had given unto Malachi, which he should tell unto them. And it came to pass that after they were written he expounded them. And these are the words which he did tell unto them, saying: Thus said the Father unto Malachi—Behold, I will Mal. 3:1 (1–18).send my D&C 45:9.messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me, and the Lord whom ye seek shall suddenly Isa. 59:20 (20–21); 3 Ne. 20:22; 21:25.come to his temple, even the TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in; behold, he shall come, saith the Lord of Hosts.

But who may 3 Ne. 25:1.abide the day of his coming, and who shall stand when he appeareth? For he is like a Deut. 4:24; Zech. 13:9; D&C 128:24. TG Earth, Cleansing of; Jesus Christ, Second Coming.refiner’s fire, and like fuller’s soap.

And he shall sit as a refiner and purifier of silver; and he shall TG Purification.purify the Deut. 10:8; D&C 84:31 (31–34); 128:24.sons of Levi, and purge them as gold and silver, that they may D&C 13.offer unto the Lord an offering in righteousness.
Then shall the offering of Judah and Jerusalem be pleasant unto the Lord, as in the days of old, and as in former years.

And I will come Ezek. 43:7 (1–7).near to you to judgment; and I will be a swift witness against the TG Sorcery.sorcerers, and against the adulterers, and against false Hosea 10:4; D&C 104:5 (4–5). TG Swearing.swearers, and against those that Ps. 94:6. TG Oppression.oppress the hireling in his wages, the widow and the Ps. 10:14; 68:5; James 1:27; D&C 136:8.fatherless, and that turn aside the TG Stranger.stranger, and fear not me, saith the Lord of Hosts.
For 2 Ne. 6:7; Moses 1:6.I am the Lord, I change not; therefore ye sons of Jacob are not consumed.

Even from the days of your fathers ye are gone away from mine TG Ordinance.ordinances, and have not kept them. 1 Sam. 7:3; Hel. 13:11; 3 Ne. 10:6 (5–7); Moro. 9:22.Return unto me and I will return unto you, saith the Lord of Hosts. But ye say: Wherein shall we return?
Will a man rob God? Yet ye have robbed me. But ye say: Wherein have we robbed thee? In TG Tithing.tithes and Neh. 10:32. TG Sacrifice.offerings.
Ye are cursed with a curse, for ye have robbed me, even this whole nation.

Bring ye all the D&C 64:23; 119:4 (1–7).tithes into the storehouse, that there may be TG Food.meat in my house; and prove me now herewith, saith the Lord of Hosts, if I will not open you the Gen. 7:11.windows of heaven, and pour you out a TG Blessing.blessing that there shall not be room enough to receive it.

And I will rebuke the D&C 86:3.devourer for your sakes, and he shall not destroy the fruits of your ground; neither shall your vine cast her fruit before the time in the fields, saith the Lord of Hosts.
And all nations shall call you blessed, for ye shall be a delightsome land, saith the Lord of Hosts.
Your words have been stout against me, saith the Lord. Yet ye say: What have we spoken against thee?

Ye have Job 16:11 (11–17); Jer. 20:7 (7–8); Hab. 1:2 (1–4).said: It is Mal. 3:14 (14–15).vain to serve God, and what doth it profit that we have kept his TG Ordinance.ordinances and that we have walked mournfully before the Lord of Hosts?
And now we call the proud happy; yea, they that work wickedness are set up; yea, they that tempt God are even delivered.

Then they that feared the Lord 4 Ne. 1:12; Moro. 6:5 (5–6).spake often one to another, and the Lord hearkened and heard; and a book of D&C 85:9 (7–9); Moses 6:5. TG Book of Remembrance.remembrance was written before him for them that feared the Lord, and that thought upon his name.
And they shall be Lev. 20:26.mine, saith the Lord of Hosts, in that day when I D&C 101:3.make up my jewels; and I will spare them as a man spareth his own son that serveth him.
Then shall ye return and TG Discernment, Spiritual.discern between the righteous and the wicked, between him that serveth God and him that serveth him not.

div.p: At the Second Coming, the proud and wicked will be burned as stubble—Elijah will return before that great and dreadful day—Compare Malachi 4. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: Mal. 4:1 (1–6).For behold, the day cometh that shall Ps. 21:9 (8–10); Isa. 24:6; 66:16; 1 Ne. 22:15; 3 Ne. 24:2; D&C 29:9; 64:23 (23–24); 133:64; JS—H 1:37. TG Earth, Cleansing of.burn as an oven; and all the Ps. 18:27; 2 Ne. 20:33. TG Pride.proud, yea, and all that do wickedly, shall be stubble; and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of Hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch.

But unto you that fear my name, shall the Ps. 84:11; Mal. 4:2; Ether 9:22.Son of Righteousness arise with healing in his wings; and ye shall go forth and D&C 45:58.grow up as Amos 6:4; 1 Ne. 22:24.calves in the stall.
And ye shall 3 Ne. 21:12.tread down the wicked; for they shall be ashes under the soles of your feet in the day that I shall do this, saith the Lord of Hosts.
Remember ye the law of Moses, my servant, which I commanded unto him in Ex. 3:1; 19:18 (9, 16–20); 1 Kgs. 19:8; Neh. 9:13; Mosiah 12:33; 13:5.Horeb for all Israel, with the statutes and judgments.

Behold, I will send you 2 Kgs. 2:2; D&C 2:1; 35:4; 110:13 (13–16); 128:17 (17–18). TG Genealogy and Temple Work.Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful TG Day of the Lord.day of the Lord;

And he shall D&C 98:16 (16–17).turn the heart of the TG Family, Eternal; Salvation for the Dead.fathers to the children, and the heart of the children to their fathers, lest I come and Mal. 4:6; D&C 110:15 (13–16).smite the earth with a curse.

div.p: Jesus expounds all things from the beginning to the end—Babes and children utter marvelous things that cannot be written—Those in the Church of Christ have all things in common among them. About A.D. 34.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that when Jesus had told these things he expounded them unto the multitude; and he did expound all things unto them, both great and small.

And he saith: IE Mal. 3–4, quoted in 3 Ne. 24–25.These scriptures, which ye had not with you, the Father commanded that I should give unto you; for it was wisdom in him that they should be given unto future generations.

And he did expound all things, even from the beginning until the TG Day of the Lord.time that he should come in his Ps. 72:19. TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory—yea, even all things which should come upon the face of the earth, even until the Amos 9:13; 2 Pet. 3:10 (10, 12); Morm. 9:2. TG Earth, Cleansing of; Earth, Destiny of; World, End of.elements should melt with fervent heat, and the earth should be Morm. 5:23.wrapt together as a scroll, and the heavens and the earth should pass away;

And even unto the Mal. 4:5; Hel. 12:25; 3 Ne. 28:31.great and last day, when all people, and all kindreds, and all nations and tongues shall Mosiah 16:10 (1–2, 10–11). TG Judgment, the Last.stand before God, to be judged of their works, whether they be good or whether they be evil—

If they be good, to the Dan. 12:2.resurrection of everlasting life; and if they be evil, to the resurrection of damnation; being on a parallel, the one on the one hand and the other on the other hand, according to the mercy, and the TG God, Justice of; Justice.justice, and the holiness which is in Christ, who was 3 Ne. 1:14; Ether 3:14. TG Man, Antemortal Existence of.before the world began.
And now there cannot be written in this book even a John 21:25; W of M 1:5; 3 Ne. 5:8 (8–11); Ether 15:33.hundredth part of the things which Jesus did truly teach unto the people;
But behold the Jarom 1:14; 4 Ne. 1:19.plates of Nephi do contain the more part of the things which he taught the people.

And these things have I written, which are a 3 Ne. 28:33; D&C 11:22.lesser part of the things which he taught the people; and I have written them to the intent that they may be brought again unto this people, 3 Ne. 21:5 (5–6).from the Gentiles, according to the words which Jesus hath spoken.

And when they shall have received this, which is expedient that they should have first, to try their faith, and if it shall so be that they shall believe these things then shall the John 16:12; 2 Ne. 27:8 (7–11, 21); Morm. 8:12; Ether 4:8 (4–10).greater things be made manifest unto them.
And if it so be that they will not believe these things, then shall the Ether 4:7 (1–8).greater things be 2 Ne. 28:27; Alma 12:10 (9–11); D&C 6:26 (26–27).withheld from them, unto their condemnation.
Behold, I was about to write them, all which were engraven upon the plates of Nephi, but the Lord 3 Ne. 26:18.forbade it, saying: I will Ether 12:6.try the faith of my people.

Therefore I, 3 Ne. 28:24.Mormon, do write the things which have been commanded me of the Lord. And now I, W of M 1:1 (1–2).Mormon, make an end of my sayings, and proceed to write the things which have been commanded me.

Therefore, I would that ye should behold that the Lord truly did teach the people, for the space of three days; and after that he did John 21:14; 3 Ne. 27:2.show himself unto them oft, and did break 3 Ne. 18:1; 20:3 (3–9). TG Bread; Sacrament.bread oft, and bless it, and give it unto them.

And it came to pass that he did teach and minister unto the Luke 10:21; Alma 32:23; 3 Ne. 17:11; D&C 128:18.children of the multitude of whom hath been spoken, and he did D&C 23:3.loose their 3 Ne. 19:32; 28:14 (14, 16).tongues, and they did speak unto their fathers great and marvelous things, even greater than he had revealed unto the people; and he loosed their tongues that they could utter.

And it came to pass that after he had ascended into heaven—the second time that he showed himself unto them, and had gone unto the Father, after having 3 Ne. 17:9. TG Administrations to the Sick; Heal.healed all their sick, and their lame, and opened the eyes of their blind and unstopped the ears of the deaf, and even had done all manner of cures among them, and raised a man from the TG Death, Power over.dead, and had shown forth his power unto them, and had ascended unto the Father—

Behold, it came to pass on the morrow that the multitude gathered themselves together, and they both saw and heard these children; yea, even Matt. 11:25.babes did open their mouths and utter marvelous things; and the things which they did utter were 3 Ne. 27:23.forbidden that there should not any man write them.

And it came to pass that the 3 Ne. 19:4 (4–12); 4 Ne. 1:14.disciples whom Jesus had chosen began Ether 12:31.from that time forth to 4 Ne. 1:1.baptize and to teach as many as did come unto them; and as many as were baptized in the name of Jesus were filled with the Holy Ghost.
And many of them saw and heard unspeakable things, which are 3 Ne. 26:11.not lawful to be written.
And they taught, and did TG Benevolence.minister one to another; and they had 4 Ne. 1:3 (3, 25–26).all things TG Consecration.common among them, every man dealing justly, one with another.
And it came to pass that they did do all things even as Jesus had commanded them.
