And they who were baptized in the name of Jesus were called the Mosiah 18:17; Alma 4:5 (4–5). TG Church.church of Christ.

div.p: Jesus commands that the Church be called in His name—His mission and atoning sacrifice constitute His gospel—Men are commanded to repent and be baptized that they may be sanctified by the Holy Ghost—They are to be even as Jesus is. About A.D. 34–35.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that as the disciples of Jesus were journeying and were preaching the things which they had both heard and seen, and were baptizing in the name of Jesus, it came to pass that the disciples were gathered together and were D&C 29:6; 84:1.united in Alma 8:10; D&C 5:24; 29:2.mighty prayer and Mosiah 27:22; Alma 5:46; 6:6. TG Fast, Fasting.fasting.

And Jesus again 3 Ne. 26:13. TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.showed himself unto them, for they were praying unto the Father in his name; and Jesus came and stood in the midst of them, and said unto them: What will ye that I shall give unto you?
And they said unto him: Lord, we will that thou wouldst tell us the D&C 1:1; 20:1.name whereby we shall call this church; for there are disputations among the people concerning this matter.
And the Lord said unto them: Verily, verily, I say unto you, why is it that the people should murmur and dispute because of this thing?
Have they not read the scriptures, which say ye must take upon you the TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name of Christ, which is my name? For by this name shall ye be called at the last day;
And whoso taketh upon him my name, and Alma 32:13 (13–15); 38:2; 3 Ne. 15:9.endureth to the end, the same shall be saved at the last day.
Therefore, whatsoever ye shall do, ye shall do it in my name; therefore ye shall call the church in my name; and ye shall call upon the Father in my name that he will bless the church for my sake.

And how be it 1 Cor. 1:12 (11–13); D&C 115:4.my TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church save it be called in my name? For if a church be called in Moses’ name then it be Moses’ church; or if it be called in the name of a man then it be the church of a man; but if it be called in my name then it is my church, if it so be that they are built upon my gospel.

Verily I say unto you, that ye are built upon my gospel; therefore ye shall call whatsoever things ye do call, in my name; therefore if ye call upon the Father, for the church, if it be in my name the Father will hear you;
And if it so be that the church is built upon my gospel then will the Father show forth his own works in it.

But if it be not built upon my gospel, and is built upon the works of men, or upon the works of the devil, verily I say unto you they have joy in their works for a season, and by and by the end cometh, and they are Jacob 5:46; 6:7; Alma 5:52; D&C 132:13.hewn down and cast into the TG Hell.fire, from whence there is no return.
For their works do Rev. 14:13; D&C 59:2.follow them, for it is because of their works that they are hewn down; therefore remember the things that I have told you.

Behold I have given unto you my Acts 10:36 (36–40); D&C 76:40 (40–42). TG Gospel.gospel, and this is the gospel which I have given unto you—that I came into the world to do the John 6:39. TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.will of my Father, because my Father sent me.

And my Father sent me that I might be 3 Ne. 15:1; Morm. 2:19.lifted up upon the Moses 7:55.cross; and after that I had been lifted up upon the Luke 9:44 (44–45); 1 Ne. 11:33.cross, that I might John 6:44; 2 Ne. 9:5; D&C 17:8; 27:18.draw all men unto me, that as I have been lifted up by men even so should men be lifted up by the Father, to stand before me, to be TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judged of their works, whether they be good or whether they be evil—

And for this cause have I been TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.lifted up; therefore, according to the power of the Father I will draw all men unto me, that they may be judged according to their 1 Sam. 2:3.works.

And it shall come to pass, that whoso TG Repent.repenteth and is baptized in my TG Baptism, Qualifications for.name shall be filled; and if he 1 Ne. 13:37. TG Endure; Steadfastness.endureth to the end, behold, him will I hold guiltless before my Father at that day when I shall stand to judge the world.
And he that endureth not unto the end, the same is he that is also hewn down and cast into the fire, from whence they can no more return, because of the TG God, Justice of.justice of the Father.
And this is the word which he hath given unto the children of men. And for this cause he fulfilleth the words which he hath given, and he lieth not, but fulfilleth all his words.

And Alma 11:37. TG Uncleanness.no unclean thing can enter into his kingdom; therefore nothing entereth into his D&C 84:24. TG Rest.rest save it be those who have Rev. 1:5 (1–6); 7:14; 1 Ne. 12:10; Alma 5:21 (21–27); 13:11 (11–13).washed their garments in my blood, because of their faith, and the repentance of all their sins, and their faithfulness unto the end.

Now this is the commandment: Ether 4:18; Moro. 7:34.Repent, all ye ends of the earth, and come unto me and be TG Baptism, Essential.baptized in my name, that ye may be TG Sanctification.sanctified by the reception of the Holy Ghost, that ye may stand 1 Cor. 1:8; D&C 4:2.spotless before me at the last day.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, this is my 1 Ne. 13:36. TG Gospel; Salvation, Plan of.gospel; and ye know the things that ye must TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.do in my church; for the works which ye have seen me do that shall ye also do; for that which ye have seen me do even that shall ye do;
Therefore, if ye do these things blessed are ye, for ye shall be lifted up at the last day.
TG Record Keeping.Write the things which ye have seen and heard, save it be those which are 3 Ne. 26:16 (16, 18).forbidden.
Write the works of this people, which shall be, even as hath been written, of that which hath been.

For behold, out of the books which have been written, and which shall be written, shall this people be 2 Ne. 33:15 (10–15); W of M 1:11.judged, for by them shall their Ps. 33:15 (13–15); 1 Ne. 15:33 (26–36).works be known unto men.
And behold, all things are 3 Ne. 24:16. TG Book of Life.written by the Father; therefore out of the books which shall be written shall the world be judged.

And know ye that 1 Ne. 12:10 (9–10); Morm. 3:19.ye shall be Rev. 20:4 (4–6).judges of this people, according to the judgment which I shall give unto you, which shall be just. Therefore, what 2 Pet. 3:11. TG Godliness; Jesus Christ, Exemplar; Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.manner of men ought ye to be? Verily I say unto you, even Matt. 5:48; 3 Ne. 12:48.as I am.
And now I John 16:10; 20:17.go unto the Father. And verily I say unto you, whatsoever things ye shall ask the Father in my name shall be given unto you.
Therefore, Matt. 7:7; 3 Ne. 14:7.ask, and ye shall receive; knock, and it shall be opened unto you; for he that asketh, receiveth; and unto him that knocketh, it shall be opened.

And now, behold, my joy is great, even unto fulness, because of you, and also this generation; yea, and even the Father rejoiceth, and also all the holy angels, because of you and this generation; for John 17:12.none of them are lost.

Behold, I would that ye should understand; for I mean them who are 3 Ne. 9:13 (11–13); 10:12.now alive of 3 Ne. 28:23.this generation; and none of them are lost; and in them I have fulness of TG Joy.joy.

But behold, it Gen. 6:6; 3 Ne. 17:14.sorroweth me because of the 1 Ne. 12:11 (11–12); 2 Ne. 26:9 (9–10); Alma 45:12 (10, 12); Hel. 13:6 (5–19).fourth generation from this generation, for they are led away captive by him even as was the John 17:12; 3 Ne. 29:7.son of perdition; for they will sell me for silver and for gold, and for that which Matt. 6:19; 3 Ne. 13:19 (19–21).moth doth corrupt and which thieves can break through and steal. And in that day will I visit them, even in turning their works upon their own heads.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had ended these sayings he said unto his disciples: Enter ye in at the Matt. 7:13 (13–14); Luke 13:24; 3 Ne. 14:13; D&C 22:4 (1–4).strait gate; for strait is the gate, and narrow is the way that leads to life, and few there be that find it; but wide is the gate, and broad the way which leads to death, and many there be that travel therein, until the night cometh, wherein no man can work.

div.p: Nine of the twelve disciples desire and are promised an inheritance in Christ’s kingdom when they die—The Three Nephites desire and are given power over death so as to remain on the earth until Jesus comes again—They are translated and see things not lawful to utter, and they are now ministering among men. About A.D. 34–35.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass when Jesus had said these words, he spake unto his disciples, one by one, saying unto them: What is it that ye 2 Chr. 1:7 (7–12); D&C 7:1 (1–8).desire of me, after that I am gone to the Father?

And they all spake, save it were three, saying: We desire that after we have lived unto the age of man, that our ministry, wherein thou hast called us, may have an end, that we may speedily come unto thee in thy kingdom.

And he said unto them: Blessed are ye because ye desired this thing of me; therefore, after that ye are 4 Ne. 1:14.seventy and two years old ye shall come unto me in my TG Election.kingdom; and with me ye shall find TG Rest.rest.
And when he had spoken unto them, he turned himself unto the three, and said unto them: What will ye that I should do unto you, when I am gone unto the Father?
And they sorrowed in their hearts, for they durst not speak unto him the thing which they desired.

And he said unto them: Behold, I Amos 4:13; Alma 18:32.know your thoughts, and ye have desired the thing which John 21:22 (21–23); D&C 7:3 (1–8).John, my beloved, who was with me in my ministry, before that I was lifted up by the Jews, desired of me.

Therefore, more blessed are ye, for ye shall 4 Ne. 1:37 (14, 37); Morm. 8:10 (10–12); Ether 12:17.never taste of Luke 9:27. TG Translated Beings.death; but ye shall live to behold all the doings of the Father unto the children of men, even until all things shall be fulfilled according to the will of the Father, when I shall come in my glory with the 3 Ne. 20:22.powers of heaven.

And ye shall never endure the pains of death; but when I shall come in my glory ye shall be changed in the twinkling of an eye from 3 Ne. 28:36 (36–40). TG Mortality.mortality to TG Immortality.immortality; and then shall ye be blessed in the kingdom of my Father.

And again, ye shall not have pain while ye shall dwell in the flesh, neither sorrow save it be for the 4 Ne. 1:44; Morm. 8:10.sins of the world; and all this will I do because of the thing which ye have desired of me, for ye have desired that ye might Philip. 1:24 (23–24); 3 Ne. 28:27; D&C 7:5 (1–8).bring the souls of men unto me, while the world shall stand.

And for this cause ye shall have John 16:15; D&C 76:59; 84:38 (37–38).fulness of joy; and ye shall sit down in the kingdom of my Father; yea, your joy shall be full, even as the Father hath given me fulness of joy; and ye shall be even as I am, and I am even as the Father; and the Father and I are Deut. 6:4; Gal. 3:20; 2 Ne. 31:21; 3 Ne. 11:27 (27–28, 36); Morm. 7:7; D&C 20:28.one;
And the 2 Ne. 31:18 (17–21); 3 Ne. 11:32; Ether 5:4; Moses 6:66.Holy Ghost beareth record of the Father and me; and the Father giveth the Holy Ghost unto the children of men, because of me.

And it came to pass that when Jesus had spoken these words, he touched every one of them with his finger save it were the 4 Ne. 1:14 (14, 37); Morm. 1:13.three who were to tarry, and then he departed.
And behold, the heavens were opened, and they were 2 Cor. 12:4 (2–4).caught up into heaven, and saw and heard unspeakable things.
And it was D&C 76:115.forbidden them that they should utter; neither was it given unto them 3 Ne. 19:32; 26:14.power that they could utter the things which they saw and heard;

And whether they were in the body or out of the body, they could not tell; for it did seem unto them like a Moses 1:11. TG Transfiguration.transfiguration of them, that they were changed from this body of flesh into an immortal state, that they could behold the things of God.

But it came to pass that they did again minister upon the face of the earth; nevertheless they did not minister of the things which they had heard and seen, because of the commandment which was given them in heaven.
And now, whether they were mortal or immortal, from the day of their transfiguration, I know not;

But this much I know, according to the record which hath been given—they did go forth upon the face of the land, and did minister unto all the people, uniting as many to the church as would believe in their preaching; baptizing them, and as many as were baptized did receive the Holy Ghost.
And they were cast into prison by them who did not belong to the church. And the Acts 16:26; Alma 14:27 (26–28); 4 Ne. 1:30; Morm. 8:24.prisons could not hold them, for they were rent in twain.

And they were cast down into the earth; but they did smite the earth with the word of God, insomuch that by his 1 Ne. 7:17 (17–18); Jacob 4:6.power they were delivered out of the depths of the earth; and therefore they could not dig pits sufficient to hold them.
And thrice they were cast into a Dan. 3:25; 4 Ne. 1:32; Morm. 8:24.furnace and received no harm.
And twice were they cast into a Dan. 6:16 (16–27); 4 Ne. 1:33.den of wild beasts; and behold they did play with the beasts as a child with a suckling lamb, and received no harm.

And it came to pass that thus they did go forth among all the people of Nephi, and did preach the TG Gospel.gospel of Christ unto all people upon the face of the land; and they were converted unto the Lord, and were united unto the church of Christ, and thus the people of 3 Ne. 27:31 (30–31).that generation were blessed, according to the word of Jesus.
And now I, 3 Ne. 26:12.Mormon, make an end of speaking concerning these things for a time.
Behold, I was about to write the 3 Ne. 19:4.names of those who were never to taste of death, but the Lord forbade; therefore I write them not, for they are hid from the world.
But behold, Morm. 8:11.I have seen them, and they have ministered unto me.
And behold they will be 3 Ne. 28:9.among the Gentiles, and the Gentiles shall know them not.
They will also be among the Jews, and the Jews shall know them not.

And it shall come to pass, when the Lord seeth fit in his wisdom that they shall minister unto all the TG Israel, Scattering of; Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of.scattered tribes of Israel, and unto all nations, kindreds, tongues and people, and shall bring out of them unto Jesus many souls, that their desire may be fulfilled, and also because of the convincing power of God which is in them.
And they are as the TG Angels.angels of God, and if they shall pray unto the Father in the name of Jesus they can show themselves unto whatsoever man it seemeth them good.

Therefore, great and marvelous works shall be wrought by them, before the Mal. 4:5; Hel. 12:25; 3 Ne. 26:4; Morm. 9:2.great and coming day when all people must surely stand before the judgment-seat of Christ;
Yea even among the Gentiles shall there be a 2 Ne. 25:17.great and marvelous work wrought by them, before that judgment day.
And if ye had 3 Ne. 26:8 (6–12).all the scriptures which give an account of all the marvelous works of Christ, ye would, according to the words of Christ, know that these things must surely come.

And wo be unto him that will Ether 4:8 (8–12).not hearken unto the words of Jesus, and also to them whom he hath chosen and Matt. 10:5 (5–42).sent among them; for whoso TG Prophets, Rejection of.receiveth not the words of Jesus and the words of those whom he hath sent receiveth not him; and therefore he will not receive them at the last day;

And it would be better for them if they had not been born. For do ye suppose that ye can get rid of the justice of an TG Blaspheme.offended God, who hath been Hel. 12:2.trampled under feet of men, that thereby salvation might come?

And now behold, as I spake concerning those whom the Lord hath chosen, yea, even three who were caught up into the heavens, that I knew not whether they were TG Purification.cleansed from 3 Ne. 28:8 (8–9). TG Immortality; Mortality.mortality to immortality—

But behold, since I wrote, I have inquired of the Lord, and he hath made it manifest unto me that there must needs be a change wrought upon their bodies, or else it needs be that they must taste of death;

Therefore, that they might not taste of death there was a TG Translated Beings.change wrought upon their bodies, that they might not TG Suffering.suffer pain nor sorrow save it were for the sins of the world.

Now this change was not equal to that which shall take place at the last day; but there was a change wrought upon them, insomuch that Satan could have no power over them, that he could not TG Temptation; Test.tempt them; and they were TG Sanctification.sanctified in the flesh, that they were TG Holiness.holy, and that the powers of the earth could not hold them.

And in this state they were to remain until the judgment day of Christ; and at that day they were to receive a greater change, and to be received into the kingdom of the Father to go no more out, but to dwell with God eternally in the heavens.

div.p: The coming forth of the Book of Mormon is a sign that the Lord has commenced to gather Israel and fulfill His covenants—Those who reject His latter-day revelations and gifts will be cursed. About A.D. 34–35.

chapter.verse: And now behold, I say unto you that when the Lord shall see fit, in his wisdom, that these sayings shall 2 Ne. 30:3 (3–8); Morm. 3:17.come unto the Gentiles according to his word, then ye may know that the Ezek. 20:37; Morm. 5:14 (14, 20).covenant which the Father hath made with the children of Israel, concerning their restoration to the TG Lands of Inheritance.lands of their inheritance, is already beginning to be fulfilled.

And ye may know that the words of the Lord, which have been spoken by the holy prophets, shall all be fulfilled; and ye need not say that the Lord Matt. 24:48; Luke 12:45.delays his coming unto the children of Israel.
And ye need not imagine in your hearts that the words which have been spoken are vain, for behold, the Lord will remember his covenant which he hath made unto his people of the house of Israel.

And when ye shall see these sayings coming forth among you, then ye need not any longer spurn at the doings of the Lord, for the 3 Ne. 20:20.sword of his TG Justice.justice is in his right hand; and behold, at that day, if ye shall spurn at his doings he will cause that it shall soon overtake you.
2 Ne. 28:15; Morm. 9:26.Wo unto him that Morm. 8:17; Ether 4:8 (8–10).spurneth at the doings of the Lord; yea, wo unto him that shall Matt. 10:33 (32–33); Moro. 1:3.deny the Christ and his works!

Yea, Morm. 9:7 (7–11, 15).wo unto him that shall deny the revelations of the Lord, and that shall say the Lord no longer worketh by revelation, or by prophecy, or by TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.gifts, or by tongues, or by healings, or by the power of the Holy Ghost!

Yea, and wo unto him that shall say at that day, to get TG Priestcraft.gain, that there can be 2 Ne. 28:6 (4–6); Morm. 8:26; 9:15 (15–26).no miracle wrought by Jesus Christ; for he that doeth this shall become 2 Ne. 9:9; 3 Ne. 27:32. TG Sons of Perdition.like unto the son of perdition, for whom there was no mercy, according to the word of Christ!

Yea, and ye need not any longer 1 Ne. 19:14.hiss, nor 2 Ne. 29:5 (4–5). TG Backbiting.spurn, nor make game of the TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jews, nor any of the remnant of the house of Israel; for behold, the Lord remembereth his covenant unto them, and he will do unto them according to that which he hath sworn.

Therefore ye need not suppose that ye can turn the right hand of the Lord unto the left, that he may not execute judgment unto the fulfilling of the covenant which he hath made unto the house of Israel.

div.p: The latter-day Gentiles are commanded to repent, come unto Christ, and be numbered with the house of Israel. About A.D. 34–35.

chapter.verse: Hearken, O ye Gentiles, and hear the words of Jesus Christ, the Son of the living God, which he hath 3 Ne. 5:13 (12–13).commanded me that I should speak concerning you, for, behold he commandeth me that I should write, saying:

Turn, all ye Rom. 15:10 (8–21).Gentiles, from your wicked ways; and TG Repent.repent of your evil doings, of your Alma 16:18; 3 Ne. 21:19 (19–21).lyings and deceivings, and of your whoredoms, and of your secret abominations, and your idolatries, and of your 3 Ne. 16:10; Morm. 8:31.murders, and your TG Priestcraft.priestcrafts, and your TG Envy.envyings, and your strifes, and from all your wickedness and abominations, and come unto me, and be baptized in my name, that ye may receive a remission of your sins, and be filled with the Holy Ghost, that ye may be Gal. 3:29 (27–29); 2 Ne. 10:18 (18–19); 3 Ne. 21:22 (22–25); Abr. 2:10.numbered with my people who are of the house of Israel.

div.p: An account of the people of Nephi, according to his record.

The Nephites and the Lamanites are all converted unto the Lord—They have all things in common, work miracles, and prosper in the land—After two centuries, divisions, evils, false churches, and persecutions arise—After three hundred years, both the Nephites and the Lamanites are wicked—Ammaron hides up the sacred records. About A.D. 35–321.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that the thirty and fourth year passed away, and also the thirty and fifth, and behold the disciples of Jesus had formed a church of Christ in all the lands round about. And as many as did come unto them, and did truly repent of their sins, were 3 Ne. 26:17.baptized in the name of Jesus; and they did also receive the Holy Ghost.

And it came to pass in the thirty and sixth year, the people were all converted unto the Lord, upon all the face of the land, both Nephites and Lamanites, and there were no contentions and disputations among them, and every man did deal justly one with another.

And they had Jacob 2:19 (17–19); Mosiah 4:26; 18:27 (19–29); Alma 16:16; 3 Ne. 12:42; 26:19. TG Consecration.all things common among them; therefore there were not rich and poor, bond and free, but they were all made free, and partakers of the heavenly TG God, Gifts of.gift.
And it came to pass that the thirty and seventh year passed away also, and there still continued to be TG Peace.peace in the land.

And there were great and marvelous works wrought by the disciples of Jesus, insomuch that they did TG Heal.heal the sick, and TG Death, Power over.raise the dead, and cause the lame to walk, and the blind to receive their sight, and the deaf to hear; and all manner of John 14:12 (12–14). TG Miracle.miracles did they work among the children of men; and in nothing did they work miracles save it were in the name of Jesus.

And thus did the thirty and eighth year pass away, and also the thirty and ninth, and forty and first, and the forty and second, yea, even until forty and nine years had passed away, and also the fifty and first, and the fifty and second; yea, and even until fifty and nine years had passed away.
And the Lord did prosper them exceedingly in the land; yea, insomuch that they did build cities again where there had been cities burned.
Yea, even that great 3 Ne. 8:8 (8, 24).city Zarahemla did they cause to be built again.
But there were many cities which had been 3 Ne. 9:4 (4, 7).sunk, and waters came up in the stead thereof; therefore these cities could not be renewed.
And now, behold, it came to pass that the people of Nephi did wax strong, and did multiply exceedingly fast, and became an exceedingly 1 Ne. 13:15; 2 Ne. 5:21; Morm. 9:6.fair and delightsome people.
And they were married, and given in marriage, and were blessed according to the multitude of the TG Promise.promises which the Lord had made unto them.

And they did not walk any more after the 2 Ne. 25:30.performances and TG Ordinance.ordinances of the 3 Ne. 9:19; 15:4 (2–8). TG Law of Moses.law of Moses; but they did walk after the commandments which they had received from their Lord and their God, continuing in Moro. 6:5; D&C 88:76.fasting and prayer, and in meeting together oft both to pray and to hear the word of the Lord.
And it came to pass that there was no contention among all the people, in all the land; but there were mighty miracles wrought among the disciples of Jesus.

And it came to pass that the seventy and first year passed away, and also the seventy and second year, yea, and in fine, till the seventy and ninth year had passed away; yea, even an hundred years had passed away, and the 3 Ne. 28:3; Morm. 3:19.disciples of Jesus, whom he had chosen, had all gone to the TG Paradise.paradise of God, save it were the 3 Ne. 28:12. TG Translated Beings.three who should tarry; and there were other TG Apostles.disciples TG Priesthood, History of.ordained in their stead; and also many of that 1 Ne. 12:12.generation had passed away.
And it came to pass that there was no TG Contention.contention in the land, because of the TG God, Love of.love of God which did dwell in the hearts of the people.

And there were no TG Envy.envyings, nor TG Strife.strifes, nor TG Rioting and Reveling.tumults, nor whoredoms, nor lyings, nor murders, nor any manner of TG Lust.lasciviousness; and surely there could not be a Prov. 14:34; Mosiah 2:41; Alma 50:23. TG Happiness.happier people among all the people who had been created by the hand of God.
There were no robbers, nor murderers, neither were there Lamanites, nor any manner of -ites; but they were in John 17:21 (21–23). TG Zion.one, the children of Christ, and heirs to the kingdom of God.

And how blessed were they! For the Lord did bless them in all their doings; yea, even they were blessed and prospered until an hundred and ten years had passed away; and the first generation from Christ had passed away, and there was no contention in all the land.

And it came to pass that 4 Ne. heading.Nephi, he that kept this last record, (and he kept it upon the 3 Ne. 26:7.plates of Nephi) died, and his son Amos kept it in his stead; and he kept it upon the plates of Nephi also.

And he kept it eighty and four years, and there was still peace in the land, save it were a small part of the people who had revolted from the church and taken upon them the name of Lamanites; therefore there began to be 3 Ne. 10:18; Morm. 1:9.Lamanites again in the land.

And it came to pass that 4 Ne. 1:47.Amos died also, (and it was an hundred and ninety and four years from the coming of Christ) and his son Amos kept the record in his stead; and he also kept it upon the plates of Nephi; and it was also written in the book of Nephi, which is this book.
And it came to pass that two hundred years had passed away; and the second generation had all passed away save it were a few.

And now I, Mormon, would that ye should know that the people had multiplied, insomuch that they were spread upon all the face of the land, and that they had become exceedingly TG Treasure.rich, because of their prosperity in Christ.

And now, in this two hundred and first year there began to be among them those who were lifted up in TG Pride; Selfishness.pride, such as the wearing of costly apparel, and all manner of fine pearls, and of the fine things of the world.
And from that time forth they did have their goods and their substance no more TG Consecration.common among them.

And they began to be divided into classes; and they began to build up 1 Ne. 22:23; 2 Ne. 28:3 (3–32); Morm. 8:28 (28, 32–38).churches unto themselves to get Ezek. 22:27; D&C 10:56. TG Priestcraft.gain, and began to deny the true church of Christ.

And it came to pass that when two hundred and ten years had passed away there were many churches in the land; yea, there were many churches which professed to know the Christ, and yet they did TG Apostasy of Individuals.deny the more parts of his gospel, insomuch that they did receive all manner of wickedness, and did administer that which was sacred unto him to whom it had been 3 Ne. 18:28 (28–29).forbidden because of unworthiness.
And this church did multiply exceedingly because of iniquity, and because of the power of TG Devil, Church of.Satan who did get hold upon their TG Hardheartedness.hearts.

And again, there was another church which denied the Christ; and they did TG Persecution.persecute the true TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church of Christ, because of their humility and their belief in Christ; and they did despise them because of the many miracles which were wrought among them.

Therefore they did exercise power and authority over the disciples of Jesus who did tarry with them, and they did cast them into 3 Ne. 28:19 (19–20).prison; but by the power of the word of God, which was in them, the prisons were rent in twain, and they went forth doing mighty miracles among them.
Nevertheless, and notwithstanding all these miracles, the people did harden their hearts, and did seek to kill them, even as the Jews at Jerusalem sought to kill Jesus, according to his word.
And they did cast them into 3 Ne. 28:21.furnaces of Dan. 3:27.fire, and they came forth receiving no harm.
And they also cast them into 3 Ne. 28:22.dens of wild beasts, and they did play with the wild beasts even as a child with a lamb; and they did come forth from among them, receiving no harm.

Nevertheless, the people did harden their hearts, for they were led by many priests and TG False Prophets.false prophets to build up many churches, and to do all manner of iniquity. And they did 3 Ne. 6:13; 12:39; D&C 98:23 (23–27).smite upon the people of Jesus; but the people of Jesus did not smite again. And thus they did dwindle in unbelief and wickedness, from year to year, even until two hundred and thirty years had passed away.
And now it came to pass in this year, yea, in the two hundred and thirty and first year, there was a great division among the people.

And it came to pass that in this year there arose a people who were called the Morm. 1:8.Nephites, and they were true believers in Christ; and among them there were those who were called by the Lamanites—Jacobites, and Josephites, and Jacob 1:13.Zoramites;

Therefore the true believers in Christ, and the true worshipers of Christ, (among whom were the 3 Ne. 28:7; Morm. 8:10 (10–12).three disciples of Jesus who should tarry) were called Nephites, and Jacobites, and Josephites, and Zoramites.

And it came to pass that they who rejected the gospel were called Lamanites, and Lemuelites, and Ishmaelites; and they did not dwindle in TG Unbelief.unbelief, but they did Josh. 22:18; Morm. 1:16.wilfully rebel against the gospel of Christ; and they did teach their children that they should not believe, even as their fathers, from the beginning, did dwindle.

And it was because of the wickedness and abomination of their fathers, even as it was in the beginning. And they were Mosiah 10:17.taught to hate the children of God, even as the Lamanites were taught to TG Hate; Malice.hate the children of Nephi from the beginning.

And it came to pass that two hundred and forty and four years had passed away, and thus were the affairs of the people. And the Hel. 5:2.more wicked part of the people did wax strong, and became exceedingly more numerous than were the people of God.

And they did still continue to build up churches unto themselves, and adorn them with all manner of precious things. And thus did two hundred and fifty years pass away, and also two hundred and sixty years.
And it came to pass that the wicked part of the people began again to build up the secret oaths and TG Secret Combinations.combinations of Gadianton.
And also the people who were called the people of Nephi began to be proud in their hearts, because of their exceeding riches, and become Hel. 16:22.vain like unto their brethren, the Lamanites.
And from this time the disciples began to sorrow for the Eccl. 3:16 (16–17); 3 Ne. 28:9; Morm. 8:10 (9–10).sins of the world.
And it came to pass that when three hundred years had passed away, both the people of Nephi and the Lamanites had become exceedingly wicked one like unto another.

And it came to pass that the robbers of Morm. 2:8; Ether 8:20.Gadianton did spread over all the face of the land; and there were none that were righteous save it were the disciples of Jesus. And gold and silver did they lay up in store in abundance, and did Mosiah 24:7; Ether 10:22.traffic in all manner of traffic.

And it came to pass that after three hundred and five years had passed away, (and the people did still remain in wickedness) 4 Ne. 1:21.Amos died; and his brother, Ammaron, did keep the record in his stead.

And it came to pass that when three hundred and twenty years had passed away, Morm. 1:2.Ammaron, being constrained by the Holy Ghost, did Morm. 2:17.hide up the TG Scriptures, Preservation of.records which were Hel. 3:15 (13, 15–16).sacred—yea, even all the sacred records which had been handed down from generation to generation, which were sacred—even until the three hundred and twentieth year from the coming of Christ.

And he did hide them up unto the Lord, that they might Enos 1:13; Morm. 5:9.come again unto the remnant of the house of Jacob, according to the prophecies and the promises of the Lord. And thus is the end of the record of Ammaron.

div.p: Ammaron instructs Mormon concerning the sacred records—War commences between the Nephites and the Lamanites—The Three Nephites are taken away—Wickedness, unbelief, sorceries, and witchcraft prevail. About A.D. 321–26.

chapter.verse: And now I, Mormon, make a 3 Ne. 5:11 (11–18); Morm. 8:5.record of the things which I have both seen and heard, and call it the W of M 1:5; Morm. 5:9.Book of Mormon.

And about the time that 4 Ne. 1:48 (47–49).Ammaron hid up the records unto the Lord, he came unto me, (I being about ten years of age, and I began to be Enos 1:1; Mosiah 1:3 (3–5).learned somewhat after the manner of the learning of my people) and Ammaron said unto me: I perceive that thou art a TG Sobriety; Trustworthiness.sober child, and art quick to observe;

Therefore, when ye are about twenty and four years old I would that ye should remember the things that ye have observed concerning this people; and when ye are of that age go to the Morm. 2:17.land Antum, unto a hill which shall be called Morm. 4:23; Ether 9:3.Shim; and there have I deposited unto the Lord all the sacred engravings concerning this people.

And behold, ye shall take the W of M 1:1 (1, 11); 3 Ne. 5:10 (9–12); Morm. 2:17 (17–18); 8:5 (1, 4–5, 14).plates of Nephi unto yourself, and the remainder shall ye leave in the place where they are; and ye shall engrave on the plates of Nephi all the things that ye have observed concerning this people.
And I, Mormon, being a descendant of 3 Ne. 5:20 (12, 20).Nephi, (and my father’s name was Mormon) I remembered the things which Ammaron commanded me.
And it came to pass that I, being eleven years old, was carried by my father into the land southward, even to the land of Zarahemla.
The whole face of the land had become covered with buildings, and the people were as numerous almost, as it were the sand of the sea.

And it came to pass in this year there began to be a war between the 4 Ne. 1:36.Nephites, who consisted of the Nephites and the Jacobites and the Josephites and the Zoramites; and this war was between the Nephites, and the Lamanites and the Lemuelites and the Ishmaelites.
Now the 4 Ne. 1:20.Lamanites and the Lemuelites and the Ishmaelites were called Lamanites, and the two parties were Nephites and Lamanites.
And it came to pass that the war began to be among them in the borders of Zarahemla, by the waters of Sidon.

And it came to pass that the Nephites had gathered together a great number of men, even to exceed the number of thirty thousand. And it came to pass that they did have in this same year a number of 2 Ne. 1:12; Morm. 4:1 (1–23).battles, in which the Nephites did beat the Lamanites and did slay many of them.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites withdrew their design, and there was peace settled in the land; and peace did remain for the space of about four years, that there was no bloodshed.

But wickedness did prevail upon the face of the whole land, insomuch that the Lord did take away his 3 Ne. 28:12 (2, 12).beloved disciples, and the work of miracles and of healing did cease because of the iniquity of the people.
And there were no 1 Sam. 3:1.gifts from the Lord, and the TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.Holy Ghost did not come upon any, because of their wickedness and TG Unbelief.unbelief.

And I, being Morm. 2:1 (1–2).fifteen years of age and being somewhat of a TG Sobriety.sober mind, therefore I was Ex. 3:16; 2 Ne. 4:26; Alma 9:21.visited of the Lord, and Ps. 34:8.tasted and knew of the goodness of Jesus.

And I did endeavor to preach unto this people, but my mouth was shut, and I was forbidden that I should preach unto them; for behold they had 4 Ne. 1:38. TG Rebellion.wilfully rebelled against their God; and the beloved disciples were Morm. 8:10.taken away out of the land, because of their iniquity.

But I did remain among them, but I was forbidden to Micah 3:6 (5–7).preach unto them, because of the hardness of their hearts; and because of the hardness of their hearts the land was 2 Ne. 1:7; Alma 45:16 (10–14, 16); Ether 2:11 (8–12).cursed for their sake.

And these Gadianton robbers, who were among the Lamanites, did infest the land, insomuch that the inhabitants thereof began to Hel. 12:18; Morm. 2:10 (10–14); Ether 14:1 (1–2).hide up their Hel. 13:18–23, 30–37. TG Treasure.treasures in the earth; and they became slippery, because the Lord had cursed the land, that they could not hold them, nor retain them again.

And it came to pass that there were TG Sorcery.sorceries, and witchcrafts, and magics; and the power of the evil one was wrought upon all the face of the land, even unto the fulfilling of all the words of Abinadi, and also Morm. 2:10 (10–15).Samuel the Lamanite.

div.p: Mormon leads the Nephite armies—Blood and carnage sweep the land—The Nephites lament and mourn with the sorrowing of the damned—Their day of grace is passed—Mormon obtains the plates of Nephi—Wars continue. About A.D. 327–50.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass in that same year there began to be a war again between the Nephites and the Lamanites. And notwithstanding I being Morm. 1:15 (12, 15–16).young, was large in stature; therefore the people of Nephi appointed me that I should be their leader, or the leader of their armies.
Therefore it came to pass that in my sixteenth year I did go forth at the head of an army of the Nephites, against the Lamanites; therefore three hundred and twenty and six years had passed away.

And it came to pass that in the three hundred and twenty and seventh year the Lamanites did come upon us with Morm. 4:13 (13–17); 5:6.exceedingly great power, insomuch that they did frighten my armies; therefore they would not fight, and they began to retreat towards the Alma 50:11; Morm. 2:29.north countries.

And it came to pass that we did come to the city of Angola, and we did take possession of the city, and make preparations to defend ourselves against the Lamanites. And it came to pass that we did 3 Ne. 3:14 (14, 25); Morm. 2:21.fortify the city with our might; but notwithstanding all our fortifications the Lamanites did come upon us and did drive us out of the city.
And they did also drive us forth out of the land of David.
And we marched forth and came to the land of Joshua, which was in the borders west by the seashore.
And it came to pass that we did gather in our people as fast as it were possible, that we might get them together in 3 Ne. 3:22 (22–25).one body.

But behold, the land was 3 Ne. 2:11.filled with 4 Ne. 1:46; Morm. 8:9; Ether 8:20.robbers and with Lamanites; and notwithstanding the great destruction which hung over my people, they did not repent of their evil doings; therefore there was blood and carnage spread throughout all the face of the land, both on the part of the Nephites and also on the part of the Lamanites; and it was one complete revolution throughout all the face of the land.

And now, the Lamanites had a king, and his name was Moro. 9:17.Aaron; and he came against us with an army of forty and four thousand. And behold, I withstood him with forty and two thousand. And it came to pass that I beat him with my army that he fled before me. And behold, all this was done, and three hundred and thirty years had passed away.

And it came to pass that the Nephites began to repent of their iniquity, and began to cry even as had been prophesied by Samuel the prophet; for behold no man could Hel. 12:18; 13:18 (17–23); Morm. 1:18 (17–19); Ether 14:1 (1–2).keep that which was his own, for the thieves, and the robbers, and the murderers, and the magic art, and the witchcraft which was in the land.
Thus there began to be a 3 Ne. 12:4.mourning and a lamentation in all the land because of these things, and more especially among the people of Nephi.

And it came to pass that when I, Mormon, saw their lamentation and their TG Mourning.mourning and their sorrow before the Lord, my heart did begin to rejoice within me, knowing the mercies and the long-suffering of the Lord, therefore supposing that he would be merciful unto them that they would Hel. 11:9 (8–17).again become a righteous people.

But behold this my joy was vain, for their 2 Cor. 7:10; Alma 42:29.sorrowing was not unto repentance, because of the goodness of God; but it was rather the Hosea 7:14; Ether 8:7.sorrowing of the TG Damnation.damned, because the Lord would not always suffer them to take Alma 41:10.happiness in sin.

And they did not come unto Jesus with broken TG Contrite Heart.hearts and contrite spirits, but they did TG Blaspheme.curse God, and wish to die. Nevertheless they would struggle with the sword for their lives.

And it came to pass that my sorrow did return unto me again, and I saw that the Hel. 13:38.day of TG Grace.grace Jer. 8:20.was passed with them, both temporally and spiritually; for I saw thousands of them hewn down in open TG Rebellion.rebellion against their God, and heaped up as Jer. 8:2 (1–3).dung upon the face of the land. And thus three hundred and forty and four years had passed away.

And it came to pass that in the three hundred and forty and fifth year the Nephites did begin to flee before the Lamanites; and they were pursued until they came even to the land of Jashon, before it was possible to stop them in their retreat.

And now, the city of Jashon was near the Morm. 1:3 (1–4).land where Ammaron had 4 Ne. 1:48 (48–49).deposited the records unto the Lord, that they might not be destroyed. And behold I had gone according to the word of Ammaron, and taken the Morm. 8:5 (1, 4–5, 14).plates of Nephi, and did make a record according to the words of Ammaron.

And upon the plates of Nephi I did make a full account of all the wickedness and abominations; but upon 3 Ne. 5:15 (8–20).these plates I did forbear to make a full account of their wickedness and abominations, for behold, a continual scene of wickedness and abominations has been before mine eyes ever since I have been sufficient to behold the ways of man.

And wo is me because of their wickedness; for my heart has been filled with sorrow because of their wickedness, all my days; nevertheless, I know that I shall be Mosiah 23:22; Ether 4:19.lifted up at the last day.
And it came to pass that in this year the people of Nephi again were hunted and driven. And it came to pass that we were driven forth until we had come northward to the land which was called Shem.
And it came to pass that we did Morm. 2:4.fortify the city of Shem, and we did gather in our people as much as it were possible, that perhaps we might save them from destruction.
And it came to pass in the three hundred and forty and sixth year they began to come upon us again.

And it came to pass that I did speak unto my people, and did urge them with great energy, that they would stand boldly before the Lamanites and Alma 58:12.fight for their Ether 14:2.wives, and their children, and their houses, and their homes.
And my words did arouse them somewhat to vigor, insomuch that they did not flee from before the Lamanites, but did stand with boldness against them.

And it came to pass that we did contend with an army of thirty thousand against an army of fifty thousand. And it came to pass that we did stand before them with such firmness that they did flee from before us.

And it came to pass that when they had fled we did pursue them with our armies, and did meet them again, and did Morm. 3:8 (7–8, 13).beat them; nevertheless the TG God, Spirit of; Strength.strength of the Lord was not with us; yea, we were left to ourselves, that the Spirit of the Lord did not abide in us; therefore we had become weak like unto our brethren.

And my heart did sorrow because of this the great calamity of my people, because of their wickedness and their abominations. But behold, we did go forth against the Lamanites and the robbers of Gadianton, until we had again taken possession of the lands of our inheritance.

And the three hundred and forty and ninth year had passed away. And in the three hundred and fiftieth year we made a treaty with the Lamanites and the robbers of Gadianton, in which we did get the lands of our inheritance divided.

And the Lamanites did give unto us the land Morm. 2:3.northward, yea, even to the Alma 22:32; 52:9; 63:5.narrow passage which led into the land southward. And we did give unto the Lamanites all the land southward.

div.p: Mormon cries repentance unto the Nephites—They gain a great victory and glory in their own strength—Mormon refuses to lead them, and his prayers for them are without faith—The Book of Mormon invites the twelve tribes of Israel to believe the gospel. About A.D. 360–62.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that the Lamanites did not come to battle again until ten years more had passed away. And behold, I had employed my people, the Nephites, in preparing their lands and their arms against the time of battle.

And it came to pass that the Lord did say unto me: Cry unto this people—Repent ye, and come unto me, and be ye baptized, and build up again my church, and ye shall be Hel. 15:17 (16–17); Morm. 6:11 (11–15).spared.

And I did cry unto this people, but it was Morm. 4:18.in vain; and they did TG Hardheartedness; Ingratitude.not realize that it was the Lord that had spared them, and granted unto them a chance for repentance. And behold they did harden their hearts against the Lord their God.

And it came to pass that after this tenth year had passed away, making, in the whole, three hundred and sixty years from the coming of Christ, the king of the Lamanites sent an epistle unto me, which gave unto me to know that they were preparing to come again to battle against us.

And it came to pass that I did cause my people that they should gather themselves together at the land Alma 50:34; Morm. 4:3 (1–3).Desolation, to a city which was in the borders, by the narrow pass which led into the land Ether 9:31 (31–32); 10:21.southward.
And there we did place our armies, that we might stop the armies of the Lamanites, that they might not get possession of any of our lands; therefore we did fortify against them with all our force.

And it came to pass that in the three hundred and sixty and first year the Lamanites did come down to the 3 Ne. 3:23; Morm. 4:2 (2, 19).city of Desolation to battle against us; and it came to pass that in that year we did beat them, insomuch that they did return to their own lands again.

And in the three hundred and sixty and second year they did come down Morm. 2:26; 3:13.again to battle. And we did beat them again, and did slay a great number of them, and their dead were cast into the sea.

And now, because of this great thing which my people, the Nephites, had done, they began to Prov. 14:16; 2 Ne. 4:34; Morm. 4:8.boast in their own strength, and began to swear before the heavens that they would avenge themselves of the blood of their brethren who had been slain by their enemies.

And they did TG Swearing.swear by the heavens, and also by the throne of God, that they Alma 43:46 (46–47); 48:14; 3 Ne. 3:21 (20–21); Morm. 4:4.would go up to battle against their enemies, and would cut them off from the face of the land.
And it came to pass that I, Mormon, did utterly Morm. 5:1.refuse from this time forth to be a commander and a leader of this people, because of their wickedness and abomination.

Behold, I had led them, notwithstanding their wickedness I had led them many times to battle, and had loved them, according to the TG God, Love of.love of God which was in me, with all my heart; and my soul had been poured out in prayer unto my God all the day long for them; nevertheless, it was James 1:6; Morm. 5:2.without faith, because of the Neh. 9:16 (16–17).hardness of their hearts.
And Morm. 2:26; 3:8 (7–8).thrice have I delivered them out of the hands of their enemies, and they have repented not of their sins.

And when they had sworn by all that had been 3 Ne. 12:34.forbidden them by our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, that they would go up unto their enemies to battle, and avenge themselves of the blood of their brethren, behold the voice of the Lord came unto me, saying:

Isa. 35:4; Rom. 12:19. TG Vengeance.Vengeance is mine, and I will 2 Sam. 16:12 (9–12); D&C 82:23. TG God, Justice of.repay; and because this people repented not after I had delivered them, behold, they shall be cut off from the face of the earth.

And it came to pass that I utterly refused to go up against mine enemies; and I did even as the Lord had commanded me; and I did stand as an idle witness to manifest unto the world the things which I saw and heard, according to the manifestations of the Spirit which had testified of things to come.

Therefore I write 2 Ne. 30:3 (3–8); 3 Ne. 29:1.unto you, Gentiles, and also unto you, house of Israel, when the work shall commence, that ye shall be about to prepare to return to the land of your inheritance;

Yea, behold, I write unto all the ends of the earth; yea, unto you, twelve tribes of Israel, who shall be Matt. 19:28; Luke 22:30 (29–30); D&C 29:12.judged according to your works by the twelve whom Jesus chose to be his disciples in the land of Jerusalem.

And I write also unto the remnant of this people, who shall also be judged by the 1 Ne. 12:10 (9–10); 3 Ne. 27:27.twelve whom Jesus chose in this land; and they shall be judged by the other twelve whom Jesus chose in the land of Jerusalem.

And these things doth the Spirit manifest unto me; therefore I write unto you all. And for this cause I write unto you, that ye may know that ye must all stand before the TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment, the Last.judgment-seat of Christ, yea, every soul who belongs to the whole human D&C 27:11.family of Adam; and ye must stand to be judged of your works, whether they be good or evil;

And also that ye may D&C 3:20.believe the gospel of Jesus Christ, which ye shall 1 Ne. 13:23 (20–29, 41).have among you; and also that the TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jews, the covenant people of the Lord, shall have other 2 Ne. 25:18.witness besides him whom they saw and heard, that Jesus, whom they slew, was the 2 Ne. 26:12; Mosiah 7:27.very Christ and the very God.
And I would that I could persuade Alma 29:1.all ye ends of the earth to repent and prepare to stand before the judgment-seat of Christ.

div.p: War and carnage continue—The wicked punish the wicked—Greater wickedness prevails than ever before in all Israel—Women and children are sacrificed to idols—The Lamanites begin to sweep the Nephites before them. About A.D. 363–75.

chapter.verse: And now it came to pass that in the three hundred and sixty and third year the Nephites did go up with their armies to 2 Ne. 1:12; Morm. 1:11 (11–19).battle against the Lamanites, out of the land Desolation.

And it came to pass that the armies of the Nephites were driven back again to the land of Desolation. And while they were yet weary, a fresh army of the Lamanites did come upon them; and they had a sore battle, insomuch that the Lamanites did take possession of the 3 Ne. 3:23; Morm. 3:7.city Desolation, and did slay many of the Nephites, and did take many prisoners.
And the remainder did flee and join the inhabitants of the city Teancum. Now the city Teancum lay in the borders by the seashore; and it was also near the city Morm. 3:5.Desolation.
And it was Morm. 3:10 (10–11).because the armies of the Nephites went up unto the Lamanites that they began to be smitten; for were it not for that, the Lamanites could have had no power over them.

But, behold, the judgments of God will overtake the Nahum 1:3.wicked; and it is by the wicked that the wicked are 2 Pet. 2:12; D&C 63:33.punished; for it is the wicked that stir up the hearts of the children of men unto bloodshed.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites did make preparations to come against the city Teancum.
And it came to pass in the three hundred and sixty and fourth year the Lamanites did come against the city Teancum, that they might take possession of the city Teancum also.

And it came to pass that they were repulsed and driven back by the Nephites. And when the Nephites saw that they had driven the Lamanites they did again Morm. 3:9.boast of their own strength; and they went forth in their own might, and took possession again of the city Desolation.
And now all these things had been done, and there had been thousands slain on both sides, both the Nephites and the Lamanites.

And it came to pass that the three hundred and sixty and sixth year had passed away, and the Lamanites came again upon the Nephites to battle; and yet the Nephites repented not of the evil they had done, but persisted in their wickedness continually.

And it is impossible for the tongue to describe, or for man to write a perfect description of the horrible scene of the blood and carnage which was among the people, both of the Nephites and of the Lamanites; and every heart was hardened, so that they Moro. 9:5 (5, 23). TG Blood, Shedding of.delighted in the shedding of blood continually.

And there never had been so great Gen. 6:5 (5–6); 3 Ne. 9:9; D&C 112:23; Moses 7:36 (36–37); 8:22 (22, 28–30).wickedness among all the children of Lehi, nor even among all the house of Israel, according to the words of the Lord, as was among this people.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites did take possession of the city Desolation, and this because their Morm. 2:3; 5:6.number did exceed the number of the Nephites.

And they did also march forward against the city Teancum, and did drive the inhabitants forth out of her, and did take many prisoners both women and children, and did offer them up as Jer. 19:5; Alma 17:15; Abr. 1:8 (6–14). TG Idolatry; Sacrifice.sacrifices unto their idol gods.

And it came to pass that in the three hundred and sixty and seventh year, the Nephites being angry because the Lamanites had sacrificed their women and their children, that they did go against the Lamanites with exceedingly great anger, insomuch that they did beat again the Lamanites, and drive them out of their lands.
And the Lamanites did not come again against the Nephites until the three hundred and seventy and fifth year.
And in this year they did come down against the Nephites with all their powers; and they were not numbered because of the greatness of their number.
And Morm. 3:3.from this time forth did the Nephites gain no power over the Lamanites, but began to be swept off by them even as a dew before the sun.

And it came to pass that the Lamanites did come down against the Morm. 3:7.city Desolation; and there was an exceedingly sore battle fought in the land Desolation, in the which they did beat the Nephites.

And they fled again from before them, and they came to the city Boaz; and there they did stand against the Lamanites with exceeding boldness, insomuch that the Lamanites did not beat them until they had come again the second time.

And when they had come the second time, the Nephites were driven and slaughtered with an exceedingly great slaughter; their women and their 2 Kgs. 17:31. TG Idolatry.children were again sacrificed unto idols.
And it came to pass that the Nephites did again flee from before them, taking all the inhabitants with them, both in towns and villages.

And now I, Mormon, seeing that the Lamanites were about to overthrow the land, therefore I did go to the hill Morm. 1:3; Ether 9:3.Shim, and did take up all the W of M 1:3.records which Ammaron had hid up unto the Lord.

div.p: Mormon again leads the Nephite armies in battles of blood and carnage—The Book of Mormon will come forth to convince all Israel that Jesus is the Christ—Because of their unbelief, the Lamanites will be scattered, and the Spirit will cease to strive with them—They will receive the gospel from the Gentiles in the latter days. About A.D. 375–84.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass that I did go forth among the Nephites, and did repent of the Morm. 3:11 (11, 16). TG Vow.oath which I had made that I would no more assist them; and they gave me command again of their armies, for they looked upon me as though I could deliver them from their afflictions.

But behold, I was Morm. 3:12.without hope, for I knew the judgments of the Lord which should come upon them; for they repented not of their iniquities, but did struggle for their lives without calling upon that Being who created them.
And it came to pass that the Lamanites did come against us as we had fled to the city of Jordan; but behold, they were driven back that they did not take the city at that time.

And it came to pass that they came against us again, and we did maintain the city. And there were also other cities which were maintained by the Nephites, which strongholds did cut them off that they could not get into the country which lay before us, to destroy the inhabitants of our land.

But it came to pass that whatsoever lands we had passed by, and the inhabitants thereof were not gathered in, were destroyed by the Lamanites, and their towns, and villages, and cities were burned with fire; and thus three hundred and seventy and nine years passed away.

And it came to pass that in the three hundred and eightieth year the Lamanites did come again against us to battle, and we did stand against them boldly; but it was all in vain, for so Morm. 2:3; 4:13 (13–17).great were their numbers that they did tread the people of the Nephites under their feet.

And it came to pass that we did again take to flight, and those whose flight was swifter than the Lamanites’ did escape, and those whose flight did not exceed the Lamanites’ were swept down and destroyed.

And now behold, I, Mormon, do not desire to harrow up the souls of men in casting before them such an awful scene of blood and carnage as was laid before mine eyes; but I, knowing that these things must surely be made known, and that all things which are hid must be Matt. 10:26 (26–33); Luke 12:3; 2 Ne. 27:11; D&C 1:3; 88:108.revealed upon the house-tops—

And also that a knowledge of these things must 4 Ne. 1:49.come unto the remnant of these people, and also unto the Gentiles, who the Lord hath said should 3 Ne. 16:8 (8–9).scatter this people, and this people should be counted as naught among them—therefore Alma 43:3.I write a Morm. 1:1.small abridgment, daring not to give a full account of the things which I have seen, because of the commandment which I have received, and also that ye might not have too great sorrow because of the wickedness of this people.
And now behold, this I speak unto their seed, and also to the Gentiles who have care for the house of Israel, that realize and know from whence their blessings come.

For I know that such will sorrow for the calamity of the house of Israel; yea, they will sorrow for the destruction of this people; they will sorrow that this people had not repented that they might have been clasped in the arms of Jesus.

Now 1 Ne. 19:19; Enos 1:16; Hel. 15:11 (11–13); Morm. 7:1 (1, 9–10).these things are TG Book of Mormon; Scriptures to Come Forth.written unto the 2 Ne. 25:8; 27:6; Jarom 1:2; D&C 3:20 (16–20).remnant of the house of Jacob; and they are written after this manner, because it is known of God that wickedness will not bring them forth unto them; and they are to be Morm. 8:4 (4, 13–14); Moro. 10:2 (1–2). TG Scriptures, Preservation of.hid up unto the Lord that they may come forth in his own due time.
And this is the commandment which I have received; and behold, they 2 Ne. 3:18.shall come forth according to the commandment of the Lord, when he shall see fit, in his wisdom.

And behold, they shall go unto the Rom. 11:20 (1–36); 1 Ne. 10:11; Jacob 4:15 (15–18). TG Unbelief.unbelieving of the 2 Ne. 26:12; 29:13; 30:7 (7–8). TG Israel, Judah, People of.Jews; and for this intent shall they go—that they may be John 20:31; 2 Ne. 25:16 (16–17). TG Israel, Restoration of.persuaded that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the living God; that the Father may bring about, through his most Beloved, his great and eternal purpose, in restoring the Jews, or all the house of Israel, to the TG Lands of Inheritance.land of their inheritance, which the Lord their God hath given them, unto the fulfilling of his Ezek. 20:37; 3 Ne. 29:1 (1–3).covenant;

And also that the seed of 2 Ne. 30:5; 3 Ne. 21:5 (3–7, 24–26).this people may more fully believe his gospel, which shall 1 Ne. 13:38 (20–29, 38); Morm. 7:8 (8–9).go forth unto them from the Gentiles; for this people shall be 1 Ne. 10:12 (12–14); 3 Ne. 16:8.scattered, and shall 2 Ne. 26:33.become a dark, a filthy, and a loathsome people, beyond the description of that which ever hath been amongst us, yea, even that which hath been among the Lamanites, and this because of their unbelief and idolatry.

For behold, the Spirit of the Lord hath already ceased to Gen. 6:3; Ether 2:15; Moro. 8:28.strive with their fathers; and they are without Christ and God in the world; and they are driven about as Ps. 1:4 (1–4); Hosea 13:3 (1–4).chaff before the wind.
They were once a delightsome people, and they had Christ for their TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd.shepherd; yea, they were led even by God the Father.

But now, behold, they are 2 Ne. 28:21.led about by Satan, even as chaff is driven before the wind, or as a vessel is tossed about upon the waves, without sail or anchor, or without anything wherewith to steer her; and even as she is, so are they.
And behold, the Lord hath reserved their blessings, which they might have received in the land, for the 3 Ne. 20:27.Gentiles who shall possess the land.

But behold, it shall come to pass that they shall be driven and scattered by the Gentiles; and after they have been driven and scattered by the Gentiles, behold, then will the Lord 1 Ne. 13:31; 3 Ne. 16:11 (8–12).remember the TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenant which he made unto Abraham and unto all the house of Israel.
And also the Lord will remember the Enos 1:12 (12–18); Morm. 8:25 (24–26); 9:36 (36–37).prayers of the righteous, which have been put up unto him for them.
And then, O ye Gentiles, how can ye stand before the power of God, except ye shall repent and turn from your evil ways?

Know ye not that ye are in the Ether 1:1; D&C 87:6 (6–7).hands of God? Know ye not that he hath all power, and at his great command the Hel. 12:11 (8–18); Morm. 9:2.earth shall be 3 Ne. 26:3.rolled together as a scroll?

Therefore, repent ye, and humble yourselves before him, lest he shall come out in justice against you—lest a TG Israel, Remnant of.remnant of the seed of Jacob shall go forth among you as a 3 Ne. 20:16 (15–16).lion, and tear you in pieces, and there is none to deliver.

div.p: The Nephites gather to the land of Cumorah for the final battles—Mormon hides the sacred records in the hill Cumorah—The Lamanites are victorious, and the Nephite nation is destroyed—Hundreds of thousands are slain with the sword. About A.D. 385.

chapter.verse: And now I finish my record concerning the 1 Ne. 12:19 (19–20); Jarom 1:10; Alma 45:11 (9–14); Hel. 13:5 (5–11).destruction of my people, the Nephites. And it came to pass that we did march forth before the Lamanites.

And I, Mormon, wrote an epistle unto the king of the Lamanites, and desired of him that he would grant unto us that we might gather together our people unto the Ether 9:3.land of Morm. 8:2; D&C 128:20.Cumorah, by a hill which was called Cumorah, and there we could give them battle.
And it came to pass that the king of the Lamanites did grant unto me the thing which I desired.

And it came to pass that we did march forth to the land of Cumorah, and we did pitch our tents around about the hill Cumorah; and it was in a land of Mosiah 8:8; Alma 50:29; Hel. 3:4 (3–4).many waters, rivers, and fountains; and here we had hope to gain advantage over the Lamanites.
And when W of M 1:2.three hundred and eighty and four years had passed away, we had gathered in all the remainder of our people unto the land of Cumorah.

And it came to pass that when we had gathered in all our people in one to the land of Cumorah, behold I, Mormon, began to be old; and knowing it to be the last struggle of my people, and having been commanded of the Lord that I should not suffer the records which had been handed down by our fathers, which were TG Sacred.sacred, to fall into the hands of the Lamanites, (for the Lamanites would 2 Ne. 26:17; Enos 1:14.destroy them) therefore I made Morm. 2:18.this record out of the plates of Nephi, and Ether 15:11.hid up in the hill Cumorah all the records which had been entrusted to me by the hand of the Lord, save it were Moro. 9:24; 10:2; D&C 17:1; JS—H 1:52.these few plates which I gave unto my son Morm. 8:1.Moroni.

And it came to pass that my people, with their wives and their children, did now behold the 1 Ne. 12:15.armies of the Lamanites marching towards them; and with that awful TG Fearful.fear of death which fills the breasts of all the wicked, did they await to receive them.
And it came to pass that they came to battle against us, and every soul was filled with terror because of the greatness of their numbers.
And it came to pass that they did fall upon my people with the sword, and with the bow, and with the arrow, and with the ax, and with all manner of weapons of war.
And it came to pass that my men were hewn down, yea, even my Judg. 1:4.ten thousand who were with me, and I fell wounded in the midst; and they passed by me that they did not put an end to my life.

And when they had gone through and hewn down 1 Ne. 12:19 (19–20); Hel. 15:17 (16–17); Morm. 3:2.all my people save it were twenty and four of us, (among whom was my son Moroni) and we having survived the dead of our people, did behold on the morrow, when the Lamanites had returned unto their camps, from the top of the hill Cumorah, the ten thousand of my people who were hewn down, being led in the front by me.
And we also beheld the ten thousand of my people who were led by my son Moroni.
And behold, the ten thousand of Gidgiddonah had fallen, and he also in the midst.

And Lamah had fallen with his ten thousand; and Gilgal had fallen with his ten thousand; and Limhah had fallen with his ten thousand; and Jeneum had fallen with his ten thousand; and Cumenihah, and Moronihah, and Antionum, and Shiblom, and Shem, and Josh, had fallen with their ten thousand each.

And it came to pass that there were ten more who did fall by the sword, with their ten thousand each; yea, even Alma 9:24.all my people, save it were those twenty and four who were with me, and also a Morm. 8:2.few who had escaped into the south countries, and a few who had deserted over unto the Lamanites, had fallen; and their flesh, and bones, and blood lay upon the face of the earth, being left by the hands of those who slew them to molder upon the land, and to crumble and to return to their mother earth.
And my soul was rent with TG Despair; Mourning; Sorrow.anguish, because of the slain of my people, and I cried:
2 Ne. 26:7.O ye fair ones, how could ye have departed from the ways of the Lord! O ye fair ones, how could ye have rejected that Jesus, who stood with open arms to receive you!
Behold, if ye had not done this, ye would not have fallen. But behold, ye are fallen, and I Lam. 2:11.mourn your loss.
O ye Ether 13:17.fair sons and daughters, ye fathers and mothers, ye husbands and wives, ye fair ones, how is it that ye could have 1 Ne. 13:15.fallen!
But behold, ye are gone, and my sorrows cannot bring your return.

And the day soon cometh that your mortal must put on immortality, and these bodies which are now moldering in corruption must soon become 1 Cor. 15:53 (53–54).incorruptible bodies; and then ye must stand before the judgment-seat of Christ, to be judged according to your works; and if it so be that ye are righteous, then are ye blessed with your fathers who have gone before you.

O that ye had repented before this great 2 Sam. 1:27 (17–27).destruction had come upon you. But behold, ye are gone, and the Father, yea, the Eternal Father of heaven, 2 Sam. 7:20; D&C 6:16.knoweth your state; and he doeth with you according to his TG God, Justice of.justice and Ps. 36:5 (5–6); Alma 26:16; D&C 97:6.mercy.

div.p: Mormon invites the Lamanites of the latter days to believe in Christ, accept His gospel, and be saved—All who believe the Bible will also believe the Book of Mormon. About A.D. 385.

chapter.verse: And now, behold, I would speak somewhat unto the Hel. 15:11 (11–13); Morm. 5:12 (9, 12).remnant of this people who are spared, if it so be that God may give unto them my words, that they may know of the things of their fathers; yea, I speak unto you, ye remnant of the house of Israel; and these are the words which I speak:
Know ye that ye are of the 1 Ne. 5:14; Alma 10:3; Hel. 6:10; 8:21.house of Israel.
Know ye that ye must come unto repentance, or ye cannot be saved.
Know ye that ye must lay down your weapons of war, and delight no more in the shedding of blood, and take them not again, save it be that God shall Alma 43:47.command you.

Know ye that ye must come to the 2 Ne. 3:12; 3 Ne. 5:23.knowledge of your fathers, and repent of all your sins and iniquities, and TG Faith.believe in Jesus Christ, that he is the Son of God, and that he was slain by the Jews, and by the power of the Father he hath risen again, whereby he hath gained the Isa. 25:8; Mosiah 16:8 (7–8); Alma 24:23.victory over the grave; and also in him is the sting of death swallowed up.
And he bringeth to pass the TG Resurrection.resurrection of the dead, whereby man must be raised to stand before his TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment-seat.

And he hath brought to pass the TG Redemption.redemption of the TG World.world, whereby he that is found Mosiah 13:15; D&C 58:30. TG Justification.guiltless before him at the judgment day hath it given unto him to Ps. 27:4; 1 Ne. 10:21; D&C 76:62 (50–62); Moses 6:57.dwell in the presence of God in his kingdom, to sing ceaseless praises with the Mosiah 2:28.choirs above, unto the Father, and unto the Son, and unto the Holy Ghost, which are Deut. 6:4; Gal. 3:20; D&C 20:28.one God, in a state of TG Happiness.happiness which hath no end.

Therefore repent, and be baptized in the name of Jesus, and lay hold upon the TG Gospel.gospel of Christ, which shall be set before you, not only in this record but also in the record which shall come unto the Gentiles 2 Ne. 29:4 (4–13).from the Jews, which record shall come from the Gentiles 1 Ne. 13:38 (20–29, 38); Morm. 5:15.unto you.

For behold, 1 Ne. 19:19; Enos 1:16 (12–18); 3 Ne. 5:15 (12–17); Morm. 5:12. TG Israel, Restoration of.this is TG Book of Mormon.written for the intent that ye may 1 Ne. 13:40 (38–42).believe that; and if 2 Ne. 3:15 (12–15); Alma 37:19 (1–20).ye believe that ye will believe this also; and if ye believe this ye will know concerning your fathers, and also the marvelous works which were wrought by the power of God among them.

And ye will also know that ye are a TG Israel, Remnant of.remnant of the seed of Jacob; therefore ye are numbered among the people of the first covenant; and if it so be that ye believe in Christ, and are baptized, first 3 Ne. 19:13 (13–14); Ether 12:14.with water, then with fire and with the Holy Ghost, following the TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.example of our Savior, according to that which he hath commanded us, it shall be well with you in the day of judgment. Amen.

div.p: The Lamanites seek out and destroy the Nephites—The Book of Mormon will come forth by the power of God—Woes pronounced upon those who breathe out wrath and strife against the work of the Lord—The Nephite record will come forth in a day of wickedness, degeneracy, and apostasy. About A.D. 400–421.

chapter.verse: Behold I, Morm. 6:6; Moro. 9:24.Moroni, do finish the TG Record Keeping.record of my father, Mormon. Behold, I have but few things to write, which things I have been commanded by my father.

And now it came to pass that after the Morm. 6:15 (2–15).great and tremendous battle at Cumorah, behold, the Nephites who had escaped into the country southward were hunted by the D&C 3:18.Lamanites, until they were all destroyed.

And my father also was killed by them, and I even Moro. 9:22.remain Ether 4:3.alone to write the sad tale of the destruction of my people. But behold, they are gone, and I fulfil the commandment of my father. And whether they will slay me, I know not.
Therefore I will write and Morm. 5:12; Moro. 10:2 (1–2).hide up the records in the earth; and whither I go it mattereth not.

Behold, my father hath made Morm. 2:17 (17–18).this record, and he hath written the intent thereof. And behold, I would write it also if I had room upon the Morm. 6:6.plates, but I have not; and ore I have none, for I am alone. My father hath been slain in battle, and all my kinsfolk, and I have not friends nor whither to go; and Moro. 1:1; 10:1 (1–2).how long the Lord will suffer that I may live I know not.
Behold, Alma 45:10.four hundred years have passed away since the coming of our Lord and Savior.

And behold, the Lamanites have hunted my people, the Nephites, down from city to city and from place to place, even until they are no more; and great has been their 1 Ne. 12:2 (2–3); Enos 1:24.fall; yea, great and marvelous is the destruction of my people, the Nephites.

And behold, it is the hand of the Lord which hath done it. And behold also, the Lamanites are at 1 Ne. 12:21 (20–23).war one with another; and the whole face of this land is one continual round of murder and bloodshed; and no one knoweth the end of the war.
And now, behold, I say no more concerning them, for there are none save it be the Lamanites and 4 Ne. 1:46; Morm. 2:8 (8, 28); Ether 8:20.robbers that do exist upon the face of the land.

And there are none that do know the true God save it be the 3 Ne. 28:7; 4 Ne. 1:37 (14, 37); Ether 12:17.disciples of Jesus, who did tarry in the land until the wickedness of the people was so great that the Lord would not suffer them to Morm. 1:16.remain with the people; and whether they be upon the face of the land no man knoweth.
But behold, my 3 Ne. 28:26.father and I have seen TG Translated Beings.them, and they have ministered unto us.

And whoso receiveth 3 Ne. 5:15 (8–18).this record, and shall not condemn it because of the imperfections which are in it, the same shall know of John 16:12; 3 Ne. 26:9 (6–11); D&C 42:15.greater things than these. Behold, I am Moroni; and were it possible, I would make all things known unto you.
Behold, I make an end of speaking concerning this people. I am the son of Mormon, and my father was a Alma 10:3; 3 Ne. 5:20.descendant of Nephi.

And I am the same who Ether 4:3; Moro. 10:2 (1–2).hideth up this record unto the Lord; the plates thereof are of no worth, because of the commandment of the Lord. For he truly saith that no one shall have them JS—H 1:53. TG Scriptures, Preservation of.to get gain; but the record thereof is of 2 Ne. 3:7 (6–9).great worth; and whoso shall bring it to light, him will the Lord bless.

For none can have power to bring it to light save it be given him of God; for God wills that it shall be done with an Matt. 6:22; D&C 4:5.eye single to his glory, or the welfare of the ancient and long dispersed covenant people of the Lord.

And blessed be 3 Ne. 21:11 (8–11); Ether 3:28 (21–28).he that shall bring this thing to light; for it shall be TG Scriptures to Come Forth.brought out of darkness unto light, according to the word of God; yea, it shall be brought out of the earth, and it shall shine forth out of darkness, and come unto the knowledge of the people; and it shall be done by the power of God.

And if there be 1 Ne. 19:6; Morm. 9:31 (31, 33); Ether 12:23 (22–28, 35).faults they be the faults of a man. But behold, we know no fault; nevertheless God knoweth all things; therefore, he that 3 Ne. 29:5 (1–9); Ether 4:8 (8–10).condemneth, let him be aware lest he shall be in danger of hell fire.
And he that saith: Show unto me, or ye shall be JS—H 1:60 (60–61).smitten—let him beware lest he commandeth that which is forbidden of the Lord.

For behold, the same that 3 Ne. 14:2; Moro. 7:14. TG Gossip.judgeth TG Rashness.rashly shall be judged rashly again; for according to his works shall his wages be; therefore, he that smiteth shall be smitten again, of the Lord.
Behold what the scripture says—man shall not TG Violence.smite, neither shall he James 4:12 (11–12).judge; for judgment is mine, saith the Lord, and vengeance is mine also, and I will repay.

And he that shall breathe out Prov. 19:19.wrath and TG Strife.strifes against the work of the Lord, and against the 1 Ne. 14:17.covenant people of the Lord who are the house of Israel, and shall say: We will destroy the work of the Lord, and the Lord will not remember his covenant which he hath made unto the house of Israel—the same is in danger to be hewn down and cast into the fire;
For the eternal D&C 3:3.purposes of the Lord shall roll on, until all his promises shall be fulfilled.

Search the prophecies of Isa. 29:4; 3 Ne. 20:11; 23:1 (1–3).Isaiah. Behold, I cannot write them. Yea, behold I say unto you, that those saints who have gone before me, who have possessed this land, shall 2 Ne. 3:20; 26:16.cry, yea, even from the dust will they cry unto the Lord; and as the Lord liveth he will remember the covenant which he hath made with them.

And he knoweth their Enos 1:13 (12–18); Morm. 9:36 (34–37); D&C 10:46 (46–49).prayers, that they were in behalf of their brethren. And he knoweth their faith, for in his name could they remove Jacob 4:6; Hel. 10:9.mountains; and in his name could they cause the earth to shake; and by the power of his word did they cause 3 Ne. 28:19 (19–21).prisons to tumble to the earth; yea, even the fiery furnace could not harm them, neither wild beasts nor poisonous serpents, because of the power of his word.
And behold, their Morm. 5:21.prayers were also in behalf of him that the Lord should suffer to bring these things forth.
